area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Hewg
Smithing Master Hewg is an NPC in Elden Ring . Hewg is an imprisoned Misbegotten blacksmith that can be found at the Roundtable Hold . He will upgrade your Weapons beyond the +3 level, as well as duplicating Ashes of War for a fee, provided you have the proper Materials .
See Smithing Master Hewg Lore
You're a new face. No matter, it's all the same.
Lay out your arms. Let's get smithing.
Smithing Master Hewg can be found at the Roundtable Hold .
When speaking with Smithing Master Hewg , you are presented with the following options:
Strengthen Armament : Access the Smithing screen .
Ashes of War : Access the Ashes of War screen .
Ash of War duplication : Access the Ashes of War Duplication screen .
Sell : Access the selling screen.
Dialogue : Learn about Hewg’s background, which will progress both his and Roderika's questlines.

I'm a prisoner, and these are my chains. I'm trapped by the Hold, undying, smithing for you fools. That's all there is to it". [You're a prisoner?] "Nah, don't read too much into it. I've no grudge against you. My being a prisoner is no fault of yours. Besides, I don't mind smithing. Despite my differences, the weapons get stronger, all the same. Given time, technique never fails. Besides, it helps me forget. The sheer terror of her..."

§ After completing Crumbling Farum Azula
Who are you? Oh, I must be a blacksmith. Now, let's get smithing...
Could you tell me what happened? Why is the Roundtable burning, in ruins? Why does that girl weep for me? Have I forgotten something of dire importance?
He is a Misbegotten , and he shares many similarities with them. He uses the same model as the Scaly Misbegotten, although his head is altered. This currently makes him the only Misbegotten capable of speaking to the player, not unlike Iji , the only Troll that can speak.
If he is somehow killed in any way, he will drop the same items as the axe-wielding Misbegotten.
His hammer can be found in the real world, inside the Fortified Manor in Leyndell .
Hammer comprised of a large stone affixed to a metal handle. Originally a blacksmith's tool.
The art of smithing is said to have originated among the giants. This weapon's striking attacks boast ample weight behind them.
Ashen remains in which spirits yet dwell. Use to summon the spirit of a winged misbegotten.
A spirit with the aspect of wings which takes flight to loose arrows from its bow. The misbegotten are held to be a punishment for making contact with the Crucible, and from birth they are treated as slaves, or worse .
Smithing stone made polishing a golden Gravel Stone. A scale of the Ancient Dragonlord, and hidden treasure of Farum Azula.
Strengthens armaments to +25.
The Ancient Dragonlord's seat is said to lie beyond time. This stone lightly twists time, allowing the creation of a weapon capable of slaying a god.
"Which is why I need your help persuading Master Hewg to leave. His roots are so knotted in this place. He won't last much longer if he stays here... His shackles are broken. He's a free man, now. It's high time he put the Roundtable behind him"
"Ah. So it wasn't just me this happened to... To think he'd forget who you were, as well."
"I'll remain with Hewg. He made me who I am today."
"So slay her, with the weapons he smithed. Slay the god, Marika, who cursed us all."
I'm trapped by the Hold, undying, smithing for you fools. That's all there is to it.
- Smithing Master Hewg
Smithing Master Hewg is a character in ELDEN RING (2022) . He is found at the Roundtable Hold and will bolster armaments in exchange for Runes .
4 Questline Progression
Hewg is a blacksmith with the appearance of a Misbegotten .
Hewg is a prisoner of Roundtable Hold , tasked with smithing weapons for the Tarnished . [ 1 ] His duty, given by Queen Marika the Eternal , is to smith a weapon to kill a god. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] In the past, Hewg was indebted to a spirit tuner, [ 4 ] and when he meets Roderika he recognises that her eyes share the same hue as those of his old acquaintance. [ 5 ] Although reluctant at first, Hewg agrees to take Roderika under his wing at the insistence of the Tarnished .
After the Tarnished sets fire to the Erdtree , the Roundtable Hold also begins to burn. Hewg's chains are broken, yet he insists on remaining in the hold, where he can continue to smith weapons to slay a god, despite Roderika's pleas for him to leave. [ 6 ] As the Roundtable burns, Hewg's mental state appears to deteriorate, causing him to slur his words and, eventually, to forget both Roderika and the Tarnished. [ 7 ] Roderika decides to respect Hewg's decision to stay at the Roundtable, and resolves to remain with him. [ 8 ] Her last request is for the Tarnished to use the weapons Hewg smithed to slay Queen Marika. [ 9 ]
Ash of War Duplication
Once Roderika arrives in Roundtable Hold, speak to Hewg and select the "About Roderika" dialogue option.
Return to Roderika and speak to her to tell her what Hewg said.
Return to Hewg and select "About Roderika" again, followed by "Would you watch over Roderika?" and finally "It's what she wants".
Reload the area and Roderika will be sitting opposite Hewg. She may now be used to perform spirit tuning services.
After burning the Erdtree, return to the Roundtable Hold and select "Let's talk a while". You may then select the dialogue option "Why are you still making weapons?" After talking to Roderika and selecting "About Hewg", return to Hewg and select "What happened to her?"
After defeating the Godskin Duo , return to Roundtable Hold and speak to Hewg and select "About the god-slaying weapon".
After defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade , return to Hewg and select "About yourself".

area: Liurnia of the Lakes
chapter: 4
name: Cursemark of Death
Cursemark of Death is a Key Item in Elden Ring . Key Items in Elden Ring include a wide variety of items found in specific locations or are given by a related NPC which are used to unlock areas, quests, and to further progress the game's story.
Cursemark carved into the discarded flesh of Ranni the Witch. Also known as the half-wheel wound of the centipede.
This cursemark was carved at the moment of Death of the first demigod, and should have taken the shape of a circle.
However, two demigods perished at the same time, breaking the cursemark into two half-wheels.
Ranni was the first of the demigods whose flesh perished, while the Prince of Death perished in soul alone.
Found on Ranni's discarded corpse lying at the top of the Divine Tower of Liurnia . [ Map Link ]
The Cursemark of Death must be given to Fia at Deeproot Depths after progressing her questline to the point she departs from the Roundtable Hold , and it is required to fight the Lichdragon Fortissax and obtain the Mending Rune of the Death-Prince .
This mark is similar to the wounds inflicted on the Fingers at the end of Ranni's questline.
Cursemark of Death is a Key Item in Elden Ring .
This item is part of Fia, Deathbed Companion 's questline.
Cursemark carved into the discarded flesh of Ranni the Witch.Also known as the half-wheel wound of the centipede.
Liurnia of the Lakes (Divine Tower of Liurnia)
Area : on the remains of Ranni the Witch 's original body atop of the Tower, alongside the Stargazer Heirloom . This area can only be reached from the Carian Study Hall , after placing the Carian Inverted Statue, received from Ranni in exchange for the Fingerslayer Blade of Nokron, Eternal City , on the altar at the entrance.
Give it to Fia and, after reloading the area, interact with her to enter the Deathbed Dream, where Lichdragon Fortissax can be challenged. After defeating this boss , retrieve the Mending Rune of the Death-Prince from Fia's corpse: you will now be able to attain the Age of the Duskborn ending after defeating the final boss .
It is also called "the stolen hallowbrand, of the exalted noble (貴い方が、奪われた聖痕, tōtoi kata ga, ubawareta seikon ; lit. "the stolen stigma(ta), of the noble one") by Fia.

area: Nokron Eternal City
chapter: 8
name: Fingerslayer Blade
Fingerslayer Blade is a Key Item in Elden Ring . The Fingerslayer Blade is required to progress Ranni's questline. Key Items in Elden Ring include a wide variety of items found in specific locations or are given by a related NPCs which are used to unlock areas, quests, and to further progress the game's story.
The hidden treasure of the Eternal City of Nokron; a blade said to have been born of a corpse.
This blood-drenched fetish is proof of the high treason committed by the Eternal City and symbolizes its downfall.
Cannot be wielded by those without a fate, but is said to be able to harm the Greater Will and its vassals.
Found in a chest under the massive skeleton statue in Night's Sacred Ground . [ Map Link ] Video Location
Fingerslayer Blade is used to progress in Ranni's questline. This is the 'Hidden treasure of Nokron' she asks you to find. After giving this item to her, she gives you the Carian Inverted Statue as a reward.
The chest cannot be opened unless you have talked to Ranni and entered her service.
Handing this item to Ranni results in the end of Seluvis's questline. Be sure to obtain the Magic Scorpion Charm from Seluvis before handing this item to Ranni, otherwise the talisman would become unobtainable.
This item bears similarities to two weapons in Elden Ring: It's shape is similar to that of the Black Knife , being that of a curved dagger . The cross guard of the Fingerslayer Blade lines up with two of the three spikes of the Black Knife, but it is missing the Black Knife's additional spike. It shares its double helix blade with the Sacred Relic Sword , which, similarly to this item's description, was fashioned from a corpse. The description of the Sacred Relic Sword states it is considered to be an omen of the end of era and a beginning that might bring ruin or prosperity. Similarly, this item brought about the doom of the society inhabiting the Eternal Cities, as it caused the Greater Will to banish them underground, which made way for the Age of the Golden Order.
It's shape is similar to that of the Black Knife , being that of a curved dagger . The cross guard of the Fingerslayer Blade lines up with two of the three spikes of the Black Knife, but it is missing the Black Knife's additional spike.
It shares its double helix blade with the Sacred Relic Sword , which, similarly to this item's description, was fashioned from a corpse. The description of the Sacred Relic Sword states it is considered to be an omen of the end of era and a beginning that might bring ruin or prosperity. Similarly, this item brought about the doom of the society inhabiting the Eternal Cities, as it caused the Greater Will to banish them underground, which made way for the Age of the Golden Order.
Fingerslayer Blade is a Key Item in Elden Ring .
This item is part of Ranni the Witch 's questline.
The hidden treasure of the Eternal City of Nokron ; a blade said to have been born of a corpse .
This blood-drenched fetish is proof of the high treason committed by the Eternal City and symbolizes its downfall .
Cannot be wielded by those without a fate, but is said to be able to harm the Greater Will and its vassals .
Nokron, Eternal City (Night's Sacred Ground)
Area : inside the treasure chest at the end of the Silver Tear-infested path.
Give it to Ranni to receive the Carian Inverted Statue that opens the path from the Carian Study Hall to the Divine Tower of Liurnia , where Ranni's corpse holding both the Cursemark of Death sought by Fia, Deathbed Companion and the Stargazer Heirloom is found.
The upper section of Nokron can only be reached from a crater near Fort Haight formed after the defeat of Starscourge Radahn .
As the chest can only be opened after selecting Serve Ranni the Witch after talking to her at Ranni's Rise , attempting to interact with it prior to this will prompt the message You are not destined to open this instead.

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Coded Sword
Coded Sword is a Straight Sword in Elden Ring . The Coded Sword scales exclusively with Faith and is a potent Weapon for dealing holy damage. It deals pure Holy Damage like the Cipher Pata .
Hidden sword once granted to the Tarnished of the Roundtable by the Two Fingers.
A formless cipher comprises its blade, which deals holy damage no shield can repel.
Champions would gather at the Roundtable Hold in days long past, when the Two Fingers were masters of oration, their flesh yet full of vigor.
The Coded Sword weapon can be found at the following location:
Found in upstairs throne room of Fortified Manor, Leyndell Royal Capital . From Fortified Manor, 1st Floor site of grace, head outside the building, turn right, climb the stable roofs to the right and re-enter the second floor of the Fortified Manor. Head towards the Round Table room, and the Coded Sword is on the throne of the Throne Room southwest of the Round Table. [ Elden Ring Map Link ] Video Location This weapon must be acquired before defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade , as it is not accessible in Leyndell, Ashen Capital .
This weapon must be acquired before defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade , as it is not accessible in Leyndell, Ashen Capital .
Weapon Skill : Unblockable Blade .
Cannot be infused with Ashes of War
Cannot be enchanted with Magic nor boosted with Consumables .
The Coded Sword is resilient to "weapon bounce" commonly experienced by other small armaments when attacking into shields, or certain enemies (eg. Miners ). This allows the weapon to excel with one-handed use in situations where other armaments would demand a two-handed approach. The ability to attack uninterrupted into an enemy's block plays into the weapon's overall "unblockable" theme, with its unblockable weapon skill, and pure Holy damage.
Due to its damage being purely Holy, the Coded Sword benefits greatly from the Sacred Scorpion Charm . It does not deal Standard or Pierce damage in any way despite its ingame description.
The sword's shape, appearance, and purely Holy damage, as well as the effect that it "turns off" when sheathed, might be a reference to the Sunblade, a magical weapon from the Dungeons & Dragons tabletop RPG.
NOTE: As of Patch 1.07 , FP consumption has been reduced from 25 to 18.
NOTE: As of Patch 1.08 , Straight Swords poise damage has been reduced only on PVP .
NOTE: As of Patch 1.09 , the scaling values of this weapon were changed. Please refer to the Upgrades Table of this weapon below. Additionally, the running attack speed, first attack speed and speed of guard counter of straight sword has increased. The attack recovery time has also decreased.
NOTE: Patch 1.13 Increased poise damage of dual wield attacks of all Straight Swords (in PVP)

Coded Sword is a straight sword in ELDEN RING (2022) .
4 Upgrade Information
Hidden sword once granted to the Tarnished of the Roundtable by the Two Fingers . A formless cipher comprises its blade, which deals holy damage no shield can repel.
Champions would gather at the Roundtable Hold in days long past, when the Two Fingers were masters of oration, their flesh yet full of vigor .
かつて 二本指 が、 円卓 の 褪せ人 に贈った秘剣 実体のない秘文字の連なりを刃とし 聖属性の攻撃力を持ち、盾に弾かれない
The Coded Sword can be found on the upper floor of the Fortified Manor in Leyndell, Royal Capital , in the room that mirrors the one that the Two Fingers of the Roundtable Hold can be found it. This weapon cannot be acquired once the Tarnished has defeated Maliketh .
As the name suggest, the skill, Unblockable Blade , will pierce through all the damage negation from a blocking shield or weapon. It also has a very wide reach since it's a sweeping attack and deals Stance damage on par with a Straight Sword's charged heavy attack, meaning it can break the Stance of most enemies within 2 hits and most bosses within 3 or 4 hits.
Unlike all other weapons, this weapon doesn't bounce away from any shield when wielded one-handed, not even when hitting the Ant's Skull Plate or sturdy enemies such as Miners . Since this weapon has no Physical damage and instead is fully elemental, and its skill fully ignores all the damage negation of a block, it's not very wise to try to block it.
Since this weapon has no Physical damage and instead is fully elemental, and its skill fully ignores all the damage negation of a block, it's not very wise to try to block it.
Since this weapon deals pure Holy damage it benefits fully from Holy-Shrouding Cracked Tear but is also heavily weakened if the fought enemy has high Holy Damage Negation, something that becomes increasingly common among Bosses after entering Leyndell, Royal Capital , with most of them having 40% Holy damage negation and 3 (technically 2) late-game bosses having 80% Holy damage negation.
Upgraded with Somber Smithing Stones .
Cannot have its Affinity and Skill changed.
The upper number in the Atk column denotes Physical base damage while the lower, colored number in the Atk column denotes elemental base damage: red for Fire damage, yellow for Lightning damage, orange for Holy damage, and cyan for Magic damage.
See the Weapon Scaling page for what the scaling values mean.
This weapon makes a unique, high-pitched swishing sound when swing. This trait is shared with the Cipher Pata , another cipher-like weapon associated with the Two Fingers .
Patch 1.07 reduced FP consumption, increased motion speed. Applies to the skill. FP cost was reduced from 25 to 18.
Applies to the skill. FP cost was reduced from 25 to 18.
Patch 1.04 increased cast speed with the weapon Coded Sword. Applies to the skill.
Applies to the skill.
Sword of Night , a weapon made from darkness

---

area: Liurnia of the Lakes
chapter: 4
name: St. Trina
Miquella, also known as Miquella the Kind or Kindly Miquella , is a background character in Elden Ring and a central character in Shadow of the Erdtree . One of the Empyrean children born from the union of Queen Marika and Radagon , he is the twin brother of Malenia , together known as the Twin Prodigies. It is revealed that Miquella has stripped himself of his flesh and Empyrean lineage in the Land of Shadow , a place obscured by the Erdtree and purged in an unsung war.
My brother will keep his promise. He possesses the wisdom, the allure, of a god - he is the most fearsome Empyrean of all." - Malenia
Miquella the Kind...is a monster. Pure and radiant, he wields love to shrive clean the hearts of men. There is nothing more terrifying." - Sir Ansbach
Miquella is cursed to be perpetually young. He is said to be well loved as a demigod and has the ability to compel affection from others (see Bewitching Branch ). This is why Malenia considers him the most fearsome Empyrean.
Miquella and Malenia were very close, with her becoming his sworn blade and protector. Like his half-sister Ranni , Miquella would be chosen by the Two Fingers as a potential successor to Queen Marika. Originally a fundamentalist, as well as possessing genius intellect, he would develop incantations for his father (see Discus of Light and Triple Rings of Light ) and peculiarities such as the Pulley Bow . However, he would become disillusioned with the Golden Order after it was unable to cure Malenia from her Scarlet Rot (see Radagon's Rings of Light ), nor grant his half-brother Godwyn a true death (see Golden Epitaph ). This compelled him towards a different path, one of Unalloyed Gold (see Miquella's Needle ) that would accept all who had been discarded by the Golden Order.
Miquella is described as benevolent, kind of heart, and envisioned a world where all would be accepted (see Light of Miquella ). However, in the base game it is heavily hinted that Miquella used others for his own ends, which is later confirmed during the events in the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion.
Miquella created the Haligtree to allow himself to grow into a god and to provide shelter to those without grace, such as the Albinaurics , an oppressed race of artificial beings who sees the Haligtree as their chosen land. Before he could grow the Haligtree to its full potential, Miquella was stolen by his half-brother, Mohg, Lord of Blood , in his cocoon form, and taken to Mohgwyn Palace . Mohg wished to have Miquella elevated to godhood with himself as Miquella's consort through the Formless Mother, however, Mohg's advances were not reciprocated (see Remembrance of the Blood Lord ).
(SHADOW OF THE ERDTREE SPOILERS BELOW)
It is later revealed in Shadow of the Erdtree by Sir Ansbach , one of Mohg's former disciples, that Mohg was enchanted by Miquella, and that Mohg was used by Miquella merely as a means to gain entry to the Land of Shadow. Mohg's corpse would later be defiled and repurposed as a vessel for the soul of Miquella's promised consort.
As if using Lord Mohg to gain entrance to the land of shadow were not enough, he plans to use his corpse as the vessel of his king consort. He has forsaken Lord Mohg's soul. He desires only his empty shell. It beggars belief, but… I'm afraid Tender Miquella fails to grasp the humiliation implied by this act. One thing is certain. My dear lord deserved better." - Sir Ansbach
Miquella is said to be waiting for his promised lord in the Land of Shadow. There, he has abandoned his flesh (see Miquella's Cross ), his love (see St. Trina ), as well as his own Empyrean lineage - a lineage secured by Marika through what appears to be a great war and a massacre carried out by Messmer the Impaler .
No matter our efforts if the roots are rotten, then we have little recourse. Ever-young Miquella saw things for what they were. He knew that his bloodline was tainted. His roots mired in madness. A tragedy if ever there was one. That he would feel compelled to renounce everything. When the blame lay squarely with the mother." - Count Ymir
It is revealed in Shadow of the Erdtree that Miquella seeks entry to the Tower of Shadow (the true form is Enir-Ilim ). There he wishes to ascend to godhood through the Gate of Divinity, which Queen Marika once used to ascend, and bring forth the Age of Compassion (see Circlet of Light and Miquella's Great Rune ). He dispossesses himself of his flesh to prepare for his ascension, but in doing so, loses himself and what he once was. Mohg's corpse is then used as a vessel for his half-brother Radahn . Miquella admired Radahn's qualities, which contrast that of his afflicted self (see Remembrance of a God and a Lord ).
In the endgame flashback cutscene, it is revealed that Miquella had long planned his ascension and made a promise with Radahn to be his consort. It is also revealed that Malenia knew of their promise (see Young Lion's Armor ) and reminded Radahn of it during their battle in Caelid . Though following this revelation, it is unclear why Malenia went into conflict with Radahn and unleashed her scarlet rot in the first place. One possible explanation is that the promise was not mutual, and Malenia sought to coerce Radahn into agreement. This is possibly supported by the fact that those with strength, such as the Tarnished, seem particularly resistant to Miquella's charm ( Leda comments on this after aiding her twice, mentioning that the Tarnished is seemingly identical before and after Miquella's rune was broken). However, Miquella mentions a vow independent of his promise with Radahn, suggesting that the two may have entered a mutual contract of some kind before the promise was made that Radahn was expected to uphold.
Lord brother. I'm going to be a god. If we honour our part of the vow, promise me you'll be my consort. I'll make the world a gentler place." - Miquella
Miquella's Needle can be used to purge the influences of an outer god, particularly the influence of the Frenzied Flame in Placidusax's arena.
The promotional material for Shadow of the Erdtree shows Miquella riding the spectral steed Torrent in the Realm of Shadow, though this isn't elaborated on nor shown ingame.
There are some incantations attributed to Miquella and his followers.
The state of Miquella's physical body in the Lands Between can be seen in the cocoon in Mohgwyn Palace. The physical body looks thin with misshapen skin. This, however, is not reflective of Miquella's true state, which is revealed in the DLC's endgame boss . A ghost in the Consecrated Snowfield also says that Mohg stole the precious "flesh" (but not the soul) of Miquella.
The dialogue by one of the phantoms in Stone Coffin Fissure hints that Miquella would've been an imperfect god and that his Age of Compassion wasn't what he envisioned: "Kindly Miquella... I see you've thrown away... Something you should not have. Under any circumstances. How will you salvation offer...to those who cannot be saved? When you could not even save your other self?"
Miquella's voice actor, Naomi Mc Donald, also provides voices for Dyna the Crow , Stone Trader Chloanne , and the Milfanito from Dark Souls II .
There is a cut ending found in the SOTE game files associated with Miquella's Age of Compassion, where Miquella says at some point during the finale, "I hereby swear... To every living being, and every living soul... Now comes the age of our eden... A thousand year voyage guided by compassion... Beginning here, love encompasses all... No living thing will be denied, no deed censured... If you have known sin, if you grieve for this world, I am ready. To embrace the whole of it..." (reminiscent of Ranni's speech) . This cut ending contains fully voice acted dialogue, as well as various unused animations. Additionally, Miquella says to the Tarnished, “ If thou covetest the throne, impress my vision upon thine heart. In the new world of thy making, all things will flourish, whether graceful, or malign ”. Based on the placement of the dialogue in the text data dump, this was originally going to be Elden Ring's 7th ending , available after beating the base game final boss.
Cut NPC dialogue confirmed that Miquella is St. Trina before the dlc. "Finally, I have found it.... St. Trina's, no, Lord Miquella's cadaver... I have partaken of untold secrets... Such that I may aid you, O Lord... So please, I hope you welcome your humble servant Rhico... Into your dream, the world of your heart.... Indeed, I beg you grant my wish, that when you transcend from empyrean to God, allow me a place by your side..." - Rhico, cut NPC.
Miquella has a cut monologue relating to the Haligtree, “Young seedling, young seedling. Return to the bosom of earth. But remember well, Thou’rt mine. So shall I give of myself. This is for thee. Mine abundance, my drop of dew. Quench thy thirst, throughout thy frame. Blossom and burgeon, time and again. Grow larger, stronger. Until the day cometh. When thou canst share in my dream.”
Cut armament shows that Miquella's Great Rune was originally supposed to be the Rune of Abundance. "Twinblade that contains the power of the Rune of Abundance. The holy power within the blade can call upon part of Miquella's own strength" - Abundance Twinblade
A cut quest shows that Miquella, under the guise of St. Trina, used to sing a lullaby to soothe the Frenzied Flame affliction of the Red Eyed Merchants, helping them to retain their personhood and prevent them from falling into madness .
The full text datadump for both the base game and DLC can be found on Git Hub - "The Carian Archive". Translator's note : the Japanese language has several first-person pronouns depending on the speaker's age, status, and gender, unlike the English "I" which is multipurpose. This is useful to determine the speaker, as the Japanese 僕 ("boku") is predominantly used by young males/boys and is only found in dialogue spoken by Miquella.

Miquella and Malenia are both the children of a single god. As such they are both Empyreans, but suffered afflictions from birth. One was cursed with eternal childhood, and the other harbored rot within.

- Sword of St. Trina
St. Trina is an NPC in Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree .
3 Notes 3.1 Cut Content
St. Trina is an enigmatic and fleeting individual who is said to appear in dreams. [ 1 ] While they are generally thought to be a young girl or boy, St. Trina is sometimes depicted in an adult form that is said to be unnerving to look at. [ 2 ]
Trina has attracted a following of priests rumored to use sleep-inducing arrows for ritual purposes. [ 3 ] A man was once so utterly captivated by St. Trina that he was said to have searched for her in his sleep. This obsession led to the creation of numerous remedies for invoking dreams. [ 4 ]
Trina's Lily is seen as a symbol of faith in the saint, and it is used to dull the senses and prevent agitation. [ 5 ]
St. Trina is the other half of Miquella discarded along his path through the Land of Shadow . [ 6 ] She lies at the bottom of the Stone Coffin Fissure , within the Garden of Deep Purple . Despite her abandonment, Trina's love for Miquella remained boundless. [ 7 ] For this reason, she wished for Miquella to be killed [ 8 ] , for she knew that his godhood would be his prison. [ 9 ]
St. Trina appears as a slumbering child with flowing violet hair swaddled within the nurturing petals of a motherly purple flower. Drinking its nectar will surely bring eternal sleep.
Dolores the Sleeping Arrow utilises St. Trina's Arrows in combat, possibly suggesting that she is one of his followers.
The Putrescent Knight became devoted to St. Trina after imbibing her nectar. [ 10 ]
Prior to the release of Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree there were several hints that Miquella and St. Trina were closely related: Both St. Trina and Miquella are typically characterised as children and are both associated with water lilies. Fevor's cookbooks were made by a devout of St. Trina, containing crafting recipes related to her powers of slumber. One of them contains the recipe for the Bewitching Branch , an item originating from Miquella, blessed with an incantation of Unalloyed Gold. The area in Altus Plateau where the Amber Starlight is found is internally referred to as "St. Trina's Hideaway", and it has a statue of Miquella and his twin, Malenia. The Albinauric archers in the Consecrated Snowfield, who guard the approach to Miquella's Haligtree , drop St. Trina's Arrows, and have lily motifs on their armor. The Cleanrot Knights, who serve Malenia, Blade of Miquella , have a chance to drop both Miquella's Lily and Trina's Lily .
Both St. Trina and Miquella are typically characterised as children and are both associated with water lilies.
Fevor's cookbooks were made by a devout of St. Trina, containing crafting recipes related to her powers of slumber. One of them contains the recipe for the Bewitching Branch , an item originating from Miquella, blessed with an incantation of Unalloyed Gold.
The area in Altus Plateau where the Amber Starlight is found is internally referred to as "St. Trina's Hideaway", and it has a statue of Miquella and his twin, Malenia.
The Albinauric archers in the Consecrated Snowfield, who guard the approach to Miquella's Haligtree , drop St. Trina's Arrows, and have lily motifs on their armor.
The Cleanrot Knights, who serve Malenia, Blade of Miquella , have a chance to drop both Miquella's Lily and Trina's Lily .
Several forms of cut content for St. Trina exist revolving around the removed dreambrew mechanic and collection of slumber fog with a tool referred to as St Trina's Crystal Ball . In one version, Trina is referred to as St. Trina of the Cradlesong and is implied to have granted succour to frenzied merchants by singing a lullaby to help them sleep. [ 11 ] In another version, there is a cut NPC called Rhico gives a quest to create Dreambrew, and after finding his way to Mohgwyn Palace and Miquella's cocoon, his dialogue explicitly reveals St. Trina was to be the same as Miquella .
In one version, Trina is referred to as St. Trina of the Cradlesong and is implied to have granted succour to frenzied merchants by singing a lullaby to help them sleep. [ 11 ]
In another version, there is a cut NPC called Rhico gives a quest to create Dreambrew, and after finding his way to Mohgwyn Palace and Miquella's cocoon, his dialogue explicitly reveals St. Trina was to be the same as Miquella .

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Crucible
The Crucible is an object of worship that existed long ago in Elden Ring . Described as the nature of life itself, there were many in Lands Between that were impacted by the Crucible's influence, including the Crucible Knights and lowly Misbegotten . Eventually faith faded for the Crucible with the rise of the Erdtree and the Golden Order. For Aspect of the Crucible spells, see Erdtree Incantations .
The primordial crucible, where all life was once blended together."
The Crucible was the primordial form of the Erdtree, existing long ago and fervently worshiped by the hornsent of Belurat Tower . Its hue resembled gold with a red tinge, and was said to be the color of homeward yearning. The Crucible, from which life took many shapes, was seen as sacred to the hornsent and the source of the horns that made them a distinct culture. The Crucible was also responsible for a number of other evolutionary traits, including feathers, boney knots, and scales. The hornsent believed that the evolutionary spiral was a normalized Crucible current that, one day, would form a column that stretches to the gods . Tangled horns are a symbol of spirituality, but most young hornsent born bearing the oversized horns meet a frightfully early demise. Because of the hornsent, the vitality of the Crucible flourished in the land that would come to be shrouded in shadow. Remains of the Crucible's influence can be seen in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh . Traits of the Crucible were considered a signifier of the divine in these ancient times, but would become increasingly disdained as an impurity as civilization has advanced. The Golden Order, helmed by Queen Marika , would scatter all trace of the Crucible's existence after coming into power.
Queen Marika, who harbored no love for the hornsent, ordered a vengeful crusade against them led by her son Messmer . At an indeterminate point, she likely utilized the Crucible that they worshiped in the creation of the Erdtree , which would become the object of the dominant faith in the Lands Between. After establishing her rule, it was clear that those affiliated with the Crucible had no place in the Golden Order. The hornsent were sealed away by Marika along with her son's forces, and the order of Crucible Knights that served Lord Godfrey were seemingly discharged and went their separate ways. Devonia , the longest serving Crucible Knight, quested in search of the Crucible's origin, and departed from the lands of the Erdtree alone. Others seemingly touched by the Crucible, such as Omen or the Misbegotten , are held to be a punishment for making contact with it, and from birth they are treated as slaves, or worse.
The affliction that the omen suffer from extends beyond the influence of the Crucible. The blood of omens is said to be cursed and they are born without the gift of grace. Their most distinctive feature are the horns growing all over their bodies, though a select few have a tail or wings, also traits of the Crucible. Some omen babies have all their horns excised after birth, causing most to perish from the procedure. Special bairns are made to memorialize them. Omen babies that are born of royalty do not have their horns excised, but instead are kept underground, unbeknownst to anyone, imprisoned for eternity. This would be the Subterranean Shunning-Grounds beneath Leyndell, Royal Capital , where they would never again see the light of day. Though they were Queen Marika's children, the omen twins Margit and Mohg were imprisoned here, shackled under the strictest confinement. Unique to the omen are nightmares featuring evil spirits that smirk at them in gleeful anticipation. Omenkillers, butchers of twisted conscience, wear a mask with this depiction, and hunt down the omen to forge weapons from their amputated horns. The first of the omenkillers was Rollo , a perfumer of antiquity. The origin of the omen curse is unknown, but it has become the mad obsession of the loathesome Dung Eater . Through sadistic ritual, the Dung Eater defiles the corpses of his victims and curses them forever, preventing them from returning to the Ertree. He desperately hopes to create a new order through a twisted malison, and to inflict the Lands Between with a Fell Curse .
Metal greatshield coated with gold. Carried by knights loyal to Godrick.
The red tinge in the gold coat mirrors the primordial matter that became the Erdtree. The color of homeward yearning.

A vestige of the crucible of primordial life. Born partially of devolution, it was considered a signifier of the divine in ancient times, but is now increasingly disdained as an impurity as civilization has advanced.

Found by hunting carnivorous beasts. These multiple, overlapping fangs grow from a single root. Perhaps they're a vestige of the primordial crucible.

This is a manifestation of the Erdtree's primal vital energies - an aspect of the primordial crucible, where all life was once blended together.

In an age long past, before this land was enshrouded in shadow, the vitality of the Crucible flourished. Remains of this can be seen in the ancient ruins.

The spiral is a normalized Crucible current that, one day, will form a column that stretches to the gods.

The divine bird warriors, and indeed this technique, are said to bear resemblance to the golden Crucible.
The primordial form of the Erdtree is close in nature to life itself, and this spear , modeled on its crucible, is imbued with ancient holy essence.
The Crucible , [ 5 ] [ 6 ] [ 7 ] also known as the golden Crucible , [ 3 ] the primordial crucible , [ 4 ] or the crucible of life , [ 2 ] was a primordial matter that formed into the Erdtree . [ 2 ] All life was once held together within the Crucible. [ 4 ]
1 Overview 1.1 Aspects of the Crucible 1.1.1 List of aspects
1.1 Aspects of the Crucible 1.1.1 List of aspects
1.1.1 List of aspects
The primordial Crucible was the divine essence that became the Erdtree and the place where all life energy was once blended together. [ 2 ] [ 4 ] [ 8 ] It was said to be close in nature to life itself, [ 9 ] [ 10 ] and was represented by gold with a reddish tint— primordial gold , the color of homeward yearning. [ 11 ] The power of the Crucible was also held in the armor of the Crucible Knights , [ 12 ] and channeled by their weapons. [ 9 ]
The aspects of various creatures grew on the bodies of humans and giants long ago, such as feathers, scales, and knots. Now considered vestiges of the Crucible, their presence was considered the mark of divinity in ancient times. [ 13 ] Of the Crucible's gifts, [ 5 ] the hornsent considered the tangled horn to be the most significant sign of primacy. [ 14 ] As society advanced, they were increasingly disdained as an impurity. [ 15 ] Budding horns and overlapping fangs that grew on herbivorous and carnivorous beasts were held in the same regard. [ 16 ] [ 17 ]
Ash of War: Aspects of the Crucible: Wings
Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom
Aspects of the Crucible: Breath
Aspects of the Crucible: Horns
Aspects of the Crucible: Tail
Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns
Divine Bird Feathers [ 3 ]
Ancient legends from the tower of Enir-Ilim claim that the "mother of all Crucibles" first appeared among the giants . [ 6 ]
The chimeric Misbegotten were believed to be a punishment for making contact with the Crucible. [ 7 ]
Crucible Knight Devonia uncovered lost Aspects of the Crucible in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh . [ 18 ] They journeyed to the realm of shadow in search of the Crucible's origin. [ 19 ]
Devonia calls upon an unnamed Aspect of the Crucible that transforms their lower body into a pachyderm centaur with six limbs. Its four back legs resemble those of even-toed ungulates , while the front legs terminate in large hands with opposable thumbs.
The Fine Crucible Feather Talisman depicts the membraned wings of butterflies and dragonflies .
The inhuman physiology of the Godskin Apostles may not have been derived from the Crucible. [ 20 ]

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Greater Will
Long ago, we began as stardust , born of a great rupture far across the skies. We, too, are children of the Greater Will. Is that not divine? Is that not sublime?
The Greater Will is an unseen character in ELDEN RING (2022) .
1 Overview 1.1 Eternal Cities 1.2 Age of the Erdtree
1.2 Age of the Erdtree
The Greater Will is a mysterious and supremely powerful entity or force from beyond that has great influence over the Lands Between . The vassals and envoys of the Greater Will are central to the Golden Order . It inhabits a lightless void outside the Lands Between. [ 1 ]
Long ago, the Greater Will fractured the One Great , creating births and souls. [ 2 ] The adherents of the Three Fingers consider this action to be a mistake, because it resulted in torment, despair, affliction, sins, and curses.
Together with a golden star, the Greater Will once sent an Elden Beast to the Lands Between that would later become the Elden Ring . [ 3 ] This beast was its vassal, and the living incarnation of the concept of Order. [ 4 ] The Greater Will has other vassals and envoys known as the Two Fingers , [ 5 ] who speak in a sign language of light. [ 6 ] The mother of the Two Fingers is Metyr , who in turn is a daughter of the Greater Will and the first shooting star to fall upon the Lands Between. [ 7 ]
Before the age of the Erdtree , [ 8 ] the denizens of the Eternal Cities rebelled against the Greater Will. To shield themselves from its influence, the Nox donned ritual mirrorhelms to block its intervention and that of its vassals. [ 9 ] In an act of blasphemy, they drew the ire of the Greater Will by wielding the Fingerslayer Blade against one of its vassals. [ 10 ] In response to this transgression, the Greater Will unleashed Astel , whose destruction of the Eternal Cities condemned them to an existence underground. [ 11 ]
During the age of the Erdtree begun by Queen Marika the Eternal [ 12 ] and at least the era immediately preceding it, the Greater Will's envoy Two Fingers selected Empyreans as candidates to become the God of the coming age. [ 13 ] Queen Marika was one such candidate, and created the Golden Order by confining the Rune of Death . [ 14 ] However, Marika would eventually shatter the Elden Ring, [ 15 ] leading to her demigod offspring inheriting the Great Runes which in turn would trigger a war known as the Shattering . [ 16 ] The Greater Will abandoned the demigods following the Shattering, as none managed to claim title of Elden Lord. [ 17 ]
According to the Two Fingers, the Greater Will has not abandoned the realm, nor the life that inhabits it, proclaiming this is why the Tarnished have been extended the Guidance of Grace . [ 18 ] After the Tarnished returned to the Lands Between following the Shattering, the Two Fingers, at the behest of the Greater Will, bade them kill the demigods in order to claim their Great Runes to become Elden Lord by becoming Marika's consort, [ 19 ] as the Greater Will had long since renounced the demigods. [ 20 ]
In the time of the Tarnished , it was discovered that the entrance to the Erdtree was blocked by impenetrable thorns, preventing anyone from becoming Elden Lord. This caused the Two Fingers to commune with the Greater Will in search of answers, which may take thousands or tens of thousands of moons. [ 21 ] [ 22 ] When the Tarnished suggested burning the Erdtree, which would unbind Destined Death and slay the world itself, [ 23 ] Finger Reader Enia exclaimed that the Fingers and the Greater Will would never allow it. [ 24 ]
After the Tarnished caused the Volcano Manor's recusants to disband following the defeat of Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy , the recusant known as Knight Bernahl declared himself the inheritor of his comrade recusant's will. He declared their refusal to become the Greater Will's pawns and swore it would fall by his blade. [ 25 ]
The Nox Mirrorhelms presumably worked by reflecting the Greater Will's language of light. [ 26 ]
Queen Marika the Eternal

---

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Outer Gods
The Outer Gods are mysterious beings mentioned throughout the lore of Elden Ring . They do not appear to manifest physical forms within the Lands Between and instead influence the events of the Lands Between in other ways, including through worship by denizens of the Lands Between and by powering incantations .
The Outer Gods are responsible for many of the events that happen both before and during Elden Ring's story, and have acted in both benevolent and malicious ways.
Gods & Deities ♦ Demigods
This is a list of Outer Gods whose status is confirmed explicitly by in-game descriptions.
An Outer God also known as the "Mother of Truth" who craves wounds. The Formless Mother is associated most closely with Mohg, Lord of Blood , giving him and his followers access to Blood Oath Incantations . When Mohg stood before her, deep underground, his accursed blood erupted with fire. Mohg's attempts to elevate his brother Miquella to Godhood imply that the Formless Mother possesses the ability to uplift someone to the status of a god. In the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC , the mother of truth is described as the savior of the Bloodfiends who engage in similar rituals to the Mohgwyn Dynasty .
Reference to the Formless Mother being an Outer God can be found in the description of Mohgwyn's Sacred Spear .
An Outer God associated with the corrupting Scarlet Rot . Sage Gowry , who proclaims himself dedicated to the "Order of Rot" likens said order to "the cycle of decay and rebirth". The Kindred of Rot are the servants of the Goddess of Rot, and are servants that have been forsaken. Other pests such as ants and scorpions have a connection to rot.
According to the Blue Dancer Charm , this Outer God was reportedly sealed away by a blind swordsman with a flowing sword , and the Lake of Rot Map confirms that the lake of rot is the location of this sealing. The Prosthesis-Wearer Heirloom implies that this same swordsman goes on to mentor Malenia , who is similarly afflicted with rot. Malenia's elevation to the Goddess of Rot maintains a pattern of people being uplifted to the status of God while also being associated with a higher being or Outer God. In the land of shadow, Romina, Saint of the Bud discovered a twisted divine element, which she weaved into the baleful scarlet rot . According to the Hefty Rot Pot , rot is one of the divine elements of the Outer Gods, and eats away at life like a vicious plague. This is reflected in Millicent's questline and Malenia's ultimate fate.
Reference to the God of Rot being an Outer God can be found in the descriptions of the Lake of Rot Map and the Scorpion's Stinger .
An Outer God related in some fashion to death and the undead. All that is stated about this Outer God is that the Twinbird is its vassal, and that the Twinbird is in turn the mother of Deathbirds . In the time when there was no Erdtree, death was burned in ghostflame. Deathbirds were the keepers of that fire.
In times of old, the dead were burned with ghostflame, and from those cinders arose vengeful spirits. Ghostflame sorceries such as Ancient Death Rancor and Explosive Ghostflame are explicitly related to the Deathbirds. As these are death sorceries which are boosted by the Prince of Death's Staff , it may be reasonable to infer that the Deathbirds, and therefore their god, are similar in nature to Those Who Live in Death and are aligned with them in some way. Deathbirds also count as Those Who Live in Death as they possess the same weakness . This idea is strengthened by a ghostflame weapon, the Helphen's Steeple , which is dropped by a Tibia Mariner . According to the Steeple, there is a place known as the Helphen which may be associated with this outer god. Priests would become guardians of the birds through the rite of Death, which also serves as an oath sworn to their distant resurrection. Mausoleum Knights evoke the Deathbirds with their armor and maintain a vigil after death over the Walking Mausoleums and the deceased demigod Godwyn the Golden in Deeproot Depths . Godwyn is the Prince of Death, scion of the golden bough and First of the Dead among the demigods.
Reference to the God of the Twinbird being an Outer God can be found in the Twinbird Kite Shield's description.
An Outer God with an association with the Fire Giants , Fire Prelates , Fire Monks , and the flame of ruin . In the land of shadow, there are verdigris items such as an armor set , a talisman , and a greatshield . Verdigris metal is said to be the gift of an Outer God. It is similar in appearance and hue to the large plate and adornments carried by the Fire Giant on the mountaintops. The giants were gifted smiths, and in ancient times, smithing was considered divine. Furthermore, like other Outer Gods, the Fell God let the Fire Giants borrow its power, but they were in turn given a curse. The Fell God, according to the One-Eyed Shield , was rumored to have been slain by Queen Marika but still lurks within the Fire Giants themselves according to the Flame of the Fell God spell description. Upon realizing the flames of their forge would never die, Queen Marika kept one of the Fire Giants alive. According to the Furnace Visage , the Furnace Golems have a stone mask surrounded by curled horns, depicting the fell god of fire that haunts the sagas of the hornsent.
Reference to the Fell God of Fire being an Outer God can be found in the Verdigris Armor description.
An Outer God associated with Madness and total chaos. At the end of Hyetta's questline, she is able to speak to the player on behalf of the Frenzied Flame's Three Fingers . It outlines its beliefs that the Greater Will's act of "fracturing" the "One Great" into life as we know it was a mistake and is the source of all suffering. Its intentions are to burn the world, "until all is One again". This Outer God is associated with the various Frenzied Flame Incantations and can be sided with, culminating in the "Lord of Frenzied Flame" ending .
Refence to the Frenzied Flame being an Outer God can be found in the Miquella's Needle description, which states its specific function is to "ward away the meddling of Outer Gods".
Trident of Mohg, Lord of Blood. A sacred spear that will come to symbolize his dynasty. As well as serving as a weapon, it is an instrument of communion with an outer god who bestows power upon accursed blood.
The mother of truth desires a wound.
A talisman engraved with the lore of an outer god.
The clan, who lost everything in the great fires, peered upon the corpse of their ancestor, normally an act of sanctity, and saw in its shadow a twisted deity. The clan had suffered such torment that the horrible thing was taken as an object of worship.
Ashen remains in which spirits yet dwell. Use to summon the spirit of a bloodfiend hexer. Summoning consumes HP rather than FP.
This spirit conducts bloodboon rituals with a sacred spear, casts bloodflame hexes, and takes a singular pleasure in letting the blood of foes.
Long ago, a subjugated tribe discovered a twisted deity amongst the ravages of war, and they were transformed into bloodfiends. The mother of truth was their savior .
Map of Lake of Rot and environs.
A great lake of standing water downstream of the Ainsel River. It is said that the divine essence of an outer god is sealed away in this land.
Dagger fashioned from a great scorpion's tail, glistening with scarlet rot.
A ceremonial tool used by heretics, crafted from the relic of a sealed outer god.
Craftable item prepared using a capacious cracked pot. A cocktail of rotten materials is sealed inside.
Throw at enemies to cause a large buildup of scarlet rot.
Rot is one of the divine elements of the outer gods, and eats away at life like a vicious plague.
Shield featuring a vividly painted twinbird. The twinbird is said to be the envoy of an outer god, and mother of the Deathbirds.
At dwindling levels of HP, this shield slightly boosts both attack and defense.
Sorcery of the servants of Death.
Strike the ground with the staff, triggering an explosion of ghostflame that burns the surrounding area.
In the time when there was no Erdtree, death was burned in ghostflame. Deathbirds were the keepers of that fire.
A smaller imitation of the furnace golem's visage. Material used for crafting items.
Exceedingly rare to find.
A stone mask surrounded by curled horns, depicting the fell god of fire that haunts the sagas of the hornsent.
Chest armor made from an unusual metal known as verdigris. Verdigris is said to be the gift of an outer god.
Possessed of an enormously hefty yet supple strength thanks to its rusted nature.
A great stone shield with an intricately carved fingerprint design.
One of the heaviest of all greatshields.
Part of the tomb of an ancient god, the Readerless Fingers relayed their message through these imprints, said to be the very seeds from which frenzy first sprouted.
One of the unalloyed gold needles that Miquella crafted to ward away the meddling of outer gods.
Capable of subduing the flame of frenzy if inherited, allowing one to cheat fate and avoid becoming Lord of Frenzied Flame.
However, the needle is as yet unfinished and can only be used in the heart of the storm beyond time said to be found in Farum Azula.
The Outer Gods (外なる神々, Sotonaru Kamigami ) are unseen, enigmatic entities wielding immense power and influence over the world of ELDEN RING (2022) . Each outer god possesses unique qualities and divine elements. [ 1 ] They often act indirectly, shaping the Lands Between to fulfill their inscrutable designs.
1 List of Outer Gods 1.1 The Formless Mother 1.2 Outer God of Rot 1.3 Outer God of the Twinbird 1.4 The Frenzied Flame 1.5 Outer God of Night
1.1 The Formless Mother
1.2 Outer God of Rot
1.3 Outer God of the Twinbird
1.4 The Frenzied Flame
1.5 Outer God of Night
The Formless Mother , also known as the mother of truth, is an outer god associated with bloodflame and blood loss . [ 2 ] She craves wounds and is said to bestow power upon those born with accursed blood. [ 3 ] The Blood Oath incantations channel her divine element [ 4 ] [ 5 ] , accessed by puncturing her omnipresent body with ritual spears . [ 6 ] It is believed that the eternal blood of the Formless Mother's wound will never dry or turn to rot. [ 7 ] When Mohg stood before her, deep underground, his accursed blood erupted with fire, and he was besotted with the defilement that he was born into. She plays a central role in the Mohgwyn Dynasty . Long ago, a subjugated tribe discovered a twisted deity amongst the ravages of war, and they were transformed into the bloodfiends . The mother of truth was their savior. [ 8 ]
The outer god and incarnation of Rot whose divine essence was sealed away by a blind swordsman in the Lake of Rot . [ 9 ] [ 10 ] A great scorpion's tail, which was crafted into a ceremonial dagger , is said to be a relic of this outer god. [ 11 ] Those who dwell within poison know rot all too well. The death that begets life, that comes to all equally. That is to say: it is the cycle of rebirth put into practice. [ 12 ] Miquella's Needle was crafted and inserted into Malenia, Blade of Miquella to ward off the affliction of the Scarlet Rot. [ 13 ] Though born into the accursed rot, when the young girl encountered her mentor and his flowing blade, she gained wings of unparalleled strength. [ 14 ] The wings symbolize Malenia and her undefeated prowess. Though she never knew relief from the accursed rot she was born into, her blade was forever beautiful - and relentless. [ 15 ]
Each time the scarlet flower blooms, Malenia's rot advances. It has bloomed twice already. With the third bloom, she will become a true goddess. [ 16 ] Millicent's sisters were born in the swamp of Aeonia , and came to the Haligtree under the aegis of Sage Gowry . And yet, those buds were doomed to never blossom. [ 17 ] Malenia rejected the Servants of Rot including the Kindred of Rot and Aeonian Butterflies , bereft of a master they were soothed by Romina, Saint of the Bud who reached out to them. [ 18 ] [ 19 ] After the church was burned to the ground, Romina had discovered a twisted divine element, which she weaved into the baleful scarlet rot. [ 20 ]
The outer god of the Twinbird once held influence in the Lands Between. Its envoy is the Twinbird which is the mother of Deathbirds . [ 21 ] A Deathbird is depicted as a malevolent deity involved in ancient sacrifical rites. [ 22 ] In times of old when there was no Erdtree, the dead were burned in ghostflame , and from those cinders arose vengeful spirits. Deathbirds were the keepers of that fire. [ 23 ] [ 24 ] Priests of old who were permitted to come among the Deathbirds became guardians of the birds through the rite of Death, which also serves as an oath sworn to their distant resurrection. [ 25 ] The Deathbirds are also Those Who Live in Death and as such are enemies of the Golden Order , as the Golden Order has no mercy for those who trespass beyond life's bounds. [ 26 ] Likely a product of the ancient hexing arts of the tower [ 27 ] , Gravebirds are ancient golems and spiritgrave keepers which according to legend were crafted to be kindred to the Deathbirds. [ 28 ]
The outer god  and incarnation of chaos, represented by the maddening yellow Flame of Frenzy . [ 13 ] Lightseeker Hyetta describes it as a "distant light," visible only after amassing numerous Shabriri Grapes . [ 29 ] [ 30 ] The Flame of Frenzy initially entered the world through impressions left by the Readerless Fingers upon the tomb of an ancient god. [ 31 ] Nonetheless, it poses profound danger to all life, [ 32 ] possessing the ability to melt away even eternal spirits . [ 33 ] This god seeks to restore the unity of the One Great by destroying the Lands Between and plunging the world into chaos. [ 34 ] Queen Marika , Melina , and the Hornsent vehemently suppress its worship and practice. [ 35 ] The god is associated with the Eye of Yelough , and its divine element manifests as contagious yellow embers in the eyes of its followers. [ 36 ] The flame of frenzy burns the eyes, but tranquility follows in its wake. [ 37 ] No other Tarnished was closer to the throne of the Elden Lord than Vyke . But without announcement, Vyke traveled far below the capital, and was scorched by the flame of frenzy. Did he make his choice for his maiden, or did some other force lure him with suggestion? [ 38 ] It is said that the man, named Shabriri , had his eyes gouged out as punishment for the crime of slander, and, with time, the blight of the flame of frenzy came to dwell in the empty sockets. [ 39 ] It is said that the sickness of the flame of frenzy first began to spread with him, the most reviled man in all history. [ 40 ] Nomadic merchants once thrived as the Great Caravan, but after being accused of heretical beliefs, their entire clan was rounded up and buried alive far underground. Then, they chanted a curse of despair, and summoned the flame of frenzy. [ 41 ] The Lord of Frenzied Flame shall take their torment, despair. Their affliction. Every sin, every curse. All melted away. Yet Midra at the encouragement of Nanaya , like others before him, was too weak to become a Lord. [ 42 ]
Pertinent only to the events of Elden Ring: Nightrei

---

area: Mountaintops of the Giants
chapter: 21
name: Haligtree Medallion
Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) is a Key Item in Elden Ring . Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) is used to operate Grand Lift of Rold , granting access to a hidden path. Key Items in Elden Ring include a wide variety of items found in specific locations or are given by a related NPCs which are used to unlock areas, quests, and to further progress the game's story.
Left half of a split medallion depicting the Haligtree.
Brandishing the medallion with both halves conjoined will activate the Grand Lift of Rold, granting access to a hidden path.
In other words, this secret medallion will guide the bearer to the land of Miquella's Haligtree.
The Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) can be found in the following location:
Castle Sol : Found on the roof of the tower in the northeast, accessible after defeating Commander Niall . [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) can be used to operate Grand Lift of Rold to access the secret location Consecrated Snowfield . See Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right) for the other half. In order to use it you must be at the bottom of the Grand Lift of Rold and switch actions from "Hoist Medallion" to "Hoist Secret Medallion". This will not work if you are at the top of the Lift.
Latenna hints at the location of this medallion if you choose to hear her request to take her to Haligttree.

---

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Age of Fracture
The fallen leaves tell a story. Of how a Tarnished became Elden Lord.
ELDEN RING (2022) features six total endings: Age of Fracture, Age of Order, Age of the Duskborn, Blessing of Despair, Age of the Stars and the Lord of Frenzied Flame.
The Age of Fracture is the simplest to obtain, only requiring The Tarnished to finish the game, while all others demand completion their respective side quests beforehand. The Age of Fracture, Age of Order, Age of the Duskborn and Blessing of Despair endings see The Tarnished rise as Marika's new Elden Lord of the Golden Order , mending the Elden Ring, and ushering in their new Age of choice to rule over. The Age of the Stars ending sees The Tarnished aid Ranni the Witch in ousting Marika and remaining at her side as Ranni's first Elden Lord. The Lord of Frenzied Flame ending, caused by the possession of the Frenzied Flame, overrides the possibility of any other ending. The Tarnished's disregards the notion of becoming Elden Lord becoming the Lord of Frenzied Flame instead and incinerating everything that came before.
For The Tarnished to separate themselves from the Frenzied Flame's influence after being inherited, they must aquire Miquella's Needle and follow its specific conditions of use. Once the Frenzied Flame is ridded, it can not be reclaimed in any way, preventing the Lord of Frenzied Flame ending.
2 Ending Explanations 2.1 Age of Fracture 2.2 Age of Order 2.3 Age of the Duskborn 2.4 Blessing of Despair 2.5 Age of the Stars 2.6 Lord of Frenzied Flame
2.3 Age of the Duskborn
2.4 Blessing of Despair
2.5 Age of the Stars
2.6 Lord of Frenzied Flame
3 Choosing an Ending
The Tarnished becomes Elden Lord, fulfilling the Two Fingers's plans of using the Tarnished to restore the sense of stability not felt since the Elden Ring was shattered. The Golden Order remains in the truest state possible to what it once was and the status quo is restored.
The Tarnished becomes Elden Lord, fulfilling Goldmask's idea of a "Perfect" Golden Order, based upon his research into how it had fallen to its current state. Having realized that the imperfections of the Golden Order stemmed from the fickleness of the gods, who in fact were no better than men, Goldmask's new transcendental ideology attempts to bring true order to the Lands Between .
The Tarnished becomes Elden Lord, wielding the rune born from Fia and the Prince of Death's union. Just as the Golden Order had been created by confining Destined Death , the Tarnished creates a new order of death restored. The principle of life in death is embeded within this order, and Those Who Live in Death are allowed to live in peace.
The Tarnished becomes Elden Lord, fulfilling the Dung Eater's twisted vision of a cursed world. All will become cursed, thus none can be scorned, which despite its radicalism, does bring order back to the Lands Between .
The Tarnished helps Ranni fulfil her vision of a Lands Between without the Golden Order by allowing her to replace Marika in godhood and taking the role of her consort. Ranni ushers in a new order not of gold, but of the stars and moon of the chill night. Rather than make herself and her order objects of worship, she chooses instead to keep her order and the Lands Between at a great divide, removing the certainties that had existed during Marika's rule.
The Tarnished and Ranni leave the Lands Between, embarking on a thousand year voyage into darkness under the wisdom of the Moon.
Rather than become Elden Lord, The Tarnished becomes the Lord of Frenzied Flame, fulfilling the machinations of Shabriri , and the Three Fingers's will. The Tarnished is left almost completely alone, the Frenzied Flame rapidly consuming everything in existence.
If Melina was not sacrificed, she is seen vowing to hunt down and kill the Tarnished for not heeding her earlier warnings.
After the final boss is slain, they will return alone to a room with Marika's statue and a Grace. Ranni's summon sign will also appear on the ground close by to the Marika's statue, if Ranni's questline has been completed. The Tarnished will now have the option to choose their ending by interacting with Marika's statue, or, if present, Ranni's summon sign. If The Tarnished possesses the Frenzied Flame, Ranni's summon sign will not appear unless the Frenzied Flame is ridden by Miquella's Needle . In this case, the only option will be to interact with Marika's statue, and the Lord of Frenzied Flame ending will automatically begin, offering no alternative choice at all.
Interacting with Marika's statue without the Frenzied Flame will allow The Tarnished to choose from one of three possible mending runes to repair the Elden Ring that can obtained during their adventures. Once the respective rune has been chosen, the Age of Order, Age of the Duskborn, or Blessing of Despair ending will begin. A fourth option to "Mend the Elden Ring" without a rune is always available, and if chosen, results in the Age of Fracture ending.
If present, interacting with the Ranni's summon sign, instead of Marika's statue, will begin the Age of the Stars ending.
In all cases, the nearby Grace can be used to teleport anywhere else and return later, allowing the ending to be postponed and play to continue.
To begin a New Game+, an ending must be chosen. To then do so, after the end credits, select "Begin Journey X" or later at the Roundtable Hold Grace if New Game+ is not originally selected.
There is an unnamed cut ending associated with Miquella, where at some point Miquella was supposed to say to the Tarnished, “ If thou covetest the throne, Impress my vision upon thine heart. In the new world of thy making, all things will flourish, whether graceful, or malign.” The word “malign” is not an accurate translation of the Japanese text, which literally says “not-so-graceful”. Based on the original Japanese text, Miquella may be envisioning a new world where both the graceful and the not-so-graceful (like the unwanted Albinaurics and Misbegotten) would flour

---

A fourth option to "Mend the Elden Ring" without a rune is always available, and if chosen, results in the Age of Fracture ending.
If present, interacting with the Ranni's summon sign, instead of Marika's statue, will begin the Age of the Stars ending.
In all cases, the nearby Grace can be used to teleport anywhere else and return later, allowing the ending to be postponed and play to continue.
To begin a New Game+, an ending must be chosen. To then do so, after the end credits, select "Begin Journey X" or later at the Roundtable Hold Grace if New Game+ is not originally selected.
There is an unnamed cut ending associated with Miquella, where at some point Miquella was supposed to say to the Tarnished, “ If thou covetest the throne, Impress my vision upon thine heart. In the new world of thy making, all things will flourish, whether graceful, or malign.” The word “malign” is not an accurate translation of the Japanese text, which literally says “not-so-graceful”. Based on the original Japanese text, Miquella may be envisioning a new world where both the graceful and the not-so-graceful (like the unwanted Albinaurics and Misbegotten) would flour

---

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Lord of the Frenzied Flame
The Lord of Frenzied Flame is no lord at all. When the land they preside over is lifeless.
- Melina — warning the Tarnished over the wickedness of becoming a Lord of Chaos.
The Lord of Frenzied Flame (狂い火の王, Kurui Hi no Ō ), also known as the Lord of Chaos (混沌の王, Konton no Ō ), [ 1 ] is a status/title in the world of ELDEN RING (2022) . It refers to the prophesied, messiah-like figure of the Frenzied Flame and its ailing followers . Promising them salvation by taking their torment, despair, their affliction, every sin, every curse, and melting it all away. [ 2 ]
1 Known Lords 1.1 Contenders
A chosen few of those afflicted with the flame of frenzy are capable of transforming into the Lord after death. The strength of its form can seemingly vary depending on who its original host is, with several having proven to be capable of becoming one, however many of them were either too weak-willed or physically feeble to accomplish anything, causing them to be considered frauds and failures in the eyes of their followers.
Similar to the power of its namesake, it has an uncanny ability to erase the original identity of its vessel. It can emerge from its host even if they don't want it to [ 3 ] and can even prompt them to abruptly die at a moments notice to force their transformation into itself. [ 4 ]
Once a Lord of Frenzied Flame manifests, the head of the original host is replaced by a black hole with yellow flames spewing out of its core, erasing any visible trace of the original host's identity. It moves their bodies around with mannerisms completely alien to not only the original host but even other chaos followers as well. Making gestures that suggest complete delight and overwhelming euphoria at the sight of its flames consuming everything in its path. An ironic contrast for something supposedly being born from unspeakable torment and despair.
While it is seen as a savior from the perspective of those stricken by madness, to everyone else it is a harbinger of insanity and death. A menace to all life and the joys of living.
The Tarnished (if they have made contact with the Three Fingers and interacted with the fractured Marika )
Roundtable Knight Vyke
The lord of Nanaya's Torch

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Destined Death
Destined Death is a Skill in Elden Ring . Destined Death allows the user to create a myriad of ethereal blade attacks to deal damage and inflicts a penalizing debuff onto enemies. Updated to Patch 1.07 .
Unique skill for Maliketh's Black Blade .
This is a Unique Skill
This Skill is not Chargeable
This Skill cannot be Parried This Skill can be Parried if performed with no Focus
This Skill can be Parried if performed with no Focus
When triggered, the weapon art causes the user to do a right-to-left horizontal slash (hitbox 1), after which the blade is stabbed into the ground (hitbox 2), which then releases the blade vortex after a brief delay (hitbox 3).
Reduces enemy max HP by 10% for 45 seconds.
Deals 17 stance damage.
Saps enemy HP by 2% + 20 HP.
Damage scales with weapon AR and deals split physical and holy damage. The amount of physical and holy damage depends on stat investment, with Faith increasing Holy damage and Strength and Dexterity increasing physical damage.
The debuff stacks with the Black Blade incantation but not the Blade of Death weapon skill. See Buffs and Debuffs for more information on stacking debuffs.
Destined Death does trigger Winged Sword Insignia , Rotten Winged Sword Insignia , Millicent's Prosthesis and Thorny Cracked Tear .
Note: This Skill was changed with Patch 1.07 as follows:
Motion speed has been increased.
Duration of the effect that reduces maximum HP has been extended.

Black Blade Written Guide
Updated to patch 1.07 . See Patch Notes for details.
Formerly called Destined Death Unbound in the 1.00 version.

---

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Empyrean
Empyrean is a term used to refer to powerful beings who could ascend to godhood and create a new era in the Lands Between. Empyreans typically descend from a royal line, and are selected by the Two Fingers , who are envoys of the Greater Will . Only a select few are given this title, and they have very important impacts on the story and lore . By the time of the Shattering , only three Empyreans would serve as possible successors to the current rule.
An Empyrean...is no mere demigod. In the age of the Elden Ring, and Queen Marika, the precious Empyrean was born. A new god to forge a new Order." - Gowry
Before her ascendence to godhood, Queen Marika was an Empyrean chosen by the Fingers. She was given a shadowbound beast to serve as her protector named Maliketh . Though the details on how she attained divinity are scarce, it is known that she was a Numen and originally lived in the Shaman Village . After her ascendency to godhood she served the Greater Will and used the Elden Ring to shape the Lands Between to its current condition. She did this through the creation of the Erdtree and the veiling of the land of shadow. She gained the title of Eternal after sealing away the Rune of Death , and had two children who were also Empyrean known as the Twin Prodigies. At an indeterminate point, she developed her Empyrean aspect Radagon , who was representative of her loyalty to her Order and served as the second Elden Lord and father to those two children.
The Gloam-Eyed Queen was chosen as an Empyrean long before the events of the game, and wielded the god-slaying black flame infused with Destined Death. She led the Godskin Apostles presumably against Marika in order to begin a new age. It is unknown if she was accompanied by a shadowbound beast or exactly why she was favored by the Fingers. She was defeated by Maliketh, and the Rune of Death was sealed away with him. Unlike the other Empyrean candidates, there is not a clear relation between Marika and the Gloam-Eyed Queen, but Maliketh's name once meant "Death of the Demigods " long ago.
Ranni is the demigod daughter of Radagon and Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon , and a member of the Carian Royal Family . When Godfrey was divested of grace, her father left to become the second Elden Lord. As an Empyrean chosen by the Fingers, she was given a shadowbound beast in the form of Blaidd , though he swore allegiance only to Ranni. She cares deeply for her mother, and learned powerful cold sorceries from a mysterious snow witch. Above all, she wished to be free from her current Empyrean path and from serving her Two Fingers. In an effort to achieve her goal, she orchestrated the Night of the Black Knives by empowering a group of assassins with the Rune of Death. They slew Godwyn the Golden in soul while she divested herself from her own body. She then carried on as a spirit possessing a doll in the form of her former mentor. She is still not entirely free, and her allies search for a way to enter Nokron, the Eternal City , which houses the Fingerslayer Blade . Due to the nature of her dream, they act in discretion. Assisting her can achieve a certain ending .
Miquella is the demigod son of Queen Marika and Radagon, considered to be one of the Twin Prodigies. Miquella was an Empyrean chosen by the Fingers, but was born with a curse to be forever young. It is unknown whether they received shadowbound beasts to protect them. Perhaps because their afflictions they were not granted ones from the Fingers, and so Malenia instead vowed to be his sworn blade. Miquella had an incredible intellect, and knew how to charm others in order to gain their allegiance. For a time he adhered to Golden Order Fundamentalism , and exchanged gifts with his father through various incantations . Miquella sought to grant his brother Godwyn a proper death and his sister Malenia a cure from her scarlet rot, but was unsuccessful. Planning to start a new Order more accepting of those turned away by the Golden Order, Miquella cocooned himself inside his Haligtree so that he might become a god, but was stolen away by Mohg, Lord of Blood . He then discarded his flesh including his Empyrean aspect St. Trina and from the land of shadow plans to ascend to godhood with the strength of a new Lord .
Malenia is the demigod daughter of Queen Marika and Radagon, considered to be one of the Twin Prodigies. Malenia was an Empyrean chosen by the Fingers, but was born with a curse of scarlet rot . It is unknown whether they received shadowbound beasts to protect them. Perhaps because of their afflictions they were not granted ones from the Fingers, and so Malenia instead vowed to be her brother's sworn blade. Her curse was the result of the meddling of an Outer God , one sealed away in a Lake of Rot . The toll the curse inflicted on her body required her to have a prosthesis for her right arm and both legs. Through training from the one who sealed away the God of Rot, she was able to withstand her curse and become a skilled fighter. She met General Radahn on the battlefield in Caelid and fought to a standstill before blooming into a Scarlet Aeonia . Each time the scarlet flower blooms, Malenia's rot advances. It has bloomed twice already. With the third bloom, she will become a true goddess, and bring true horror to the world.
Source from the Books of Knowledge, Volume II by Future Press.
Q) Ranni the Witch's dual face is one of her most striking features. Was this aspect of her design simply meant to convey that she occupies a body which is not her own, or is there a deeper symbolic meaning behind the left eye of her spirit form overlapping the right eye of the doll she inhabits?
A) There are several meanings behind Ranni's dual faces. As you pointed out, she intentionally killed her original body, transferring her soul to a doll, which itself signifies a warped state of existence. Similar to how Queen Marika and Radagon are one being, it touches on the nature of Empyreans and the multiple different aspects they can possess.
"Empyrean" comes from the cosmological theory of Aristotle and early/medieval Christianity, defined as "the highest heaven". It is used as both adjective and noun. In Dante's Paradiso , Dante meets God in the Empyrean.
In the Japanese version of the game 神人 (Shinjin) is used, which means "divine person".
As part of a scheme, Preceptor Seluvis attempts to use Amber Starlight to make a draught that can control Ranni. Amber Starlight portends to "command the fates of the gods" but likely has to do with Empyreans as it is found near a statue of Miquella and Malenia. For whatever reason, his plan fails, and Seluvis is killed whether the Tarnished goes through with his scheme or not.
Remembrance of Maliketh, the Black Blade, hewn into the Erdtree.
The power of its namesake can be unlocked by the Finger Reader. Alternatively, it can be used to gain a great bounty of runes.
Maliketh was a shadowbound beast given to his Empyrean. Marika's sole need of her shadow was a vessel to lock away Destined Death. Even then, she betrayed him.

Summons a circle of black flame pillars around the caster. Charging increases the size of the circle.
The Gloam-Eyed Queen led the apostles. It is said that she was an Empyrean chosen by the Fingers.

Ranni is an Empyrean, meaning her consort would by rights earn the title of lord.
A warning is engraved within; "Whoever thou mayest be, take not the ring from this place, the solitude beyond the night is better mine alone."
