Pierce a foe, using FP to turn them into a temporary ally.
The Empyrean Miquella is loved by many people. Indeed, he has learned very well how to compel such affection.
Remembrance of Malenia, Goddess of Rot, hewn into the Erdtree.
Miquella and Malenia are both the children of a single god. As such they are both Empyreans, but suffered afflictions from birth. One was cursed with eternal childhood, and the other harbored rot within.
Armor worn by Vargram the Raging Wolf, one of the first Tarnished to visit the Roundtable Hold.
According to the old legends, wolves are the shadows of the Empyrean.
Vargram aspired to such a state himself.
Greatsword made of light, modeled after the Elden Ring itself. Forged by King Consort Radagon to proudly symbolize the tenets of the Golden Order.
One of the legendary armaments.
Telltale signs betray that this was once the greatsword bequeathed to him by his first wife, Rennala.
Silver sword carried by clerics of St. Trina. Inflicts sleep ailment upon foes.
St. Trina is an enigmatic figure. Some say she is a comely young girl, others are sure he is a boy. The only certainty is that their appearance was as sudden as their disappearance.
A talisman depicting a raised prosthetic blade. An honor bestowed upon the valkyries who serve the goddess of rot.
Greatly raises attack power with successive attacks.
The Four sisters were born in the swamp of Aeonia, and came to the Haligtree under the aegis of Gowry. And yet, those buds were doomed to never blossom.
I was once an Empyrean. Of the demigods, only I, Miquella, and Malenia could claim that title.
Each of us was chosen by our own Two Fingers, as a candidate to succeed Queen Marika, to become the new god of the coming age.
Which is when I received Blaidd. In the form of a vassal tailored for an Empyrean.
Queen Marika and her King Consort Radagon were blessed with twin demigods, and Malenia was one of them.
She was born an Empyrean, carrying the scarlet rot. An Empyrean...is no mere demigod.
In the age of the Elden Ring, and Queen Marika, the precious Empyrean was born.
A new god to forge a new Order.
Well, I am much obliged. I can hardly believe it, he's divested himself of his very eye… Tender Miquella's eye is no mere morsel of flesh.
It is a vessel of soaring grace. Proof of his Empyrean lineage. I wonder, does Miquella the Kind intend to sever his very birthright? His fate as a child of the Erdtree?
I challenged Tender Miquella, only to have my own heart rather artfully stolen. I knew not how weak I was. I believed that with sufficient mastery, even an Empyrean would be within reach of my blade.
I could not have been more mistaken… Miquella the Kind... is a monster. Pure and radiant, he wields love to shrive clean the hearts of men. There is nothing more terrifying.
Empyreans (神人, Shinjin ) are special beings that are capable of becoming gods of a new age [ 1 ] and forging a new order that governs the world. [ 2 ] One such way in which they can achieve this is by becoming the vessel of the Elden Ring .
The most prominent Empyrean is Queen Marika, a Numen who became the vessel of the Elden Ring , using it to found the Golden Order .
Malenia and Miquella were born of a single god, which is indicated to be the reason that they were eligible to claim the title of Empyrean, and for the afflictions they were born with. [ 3 ] It is likely that this is due to their parents, Marika and Radagon , being two parts of one god. Malenia is an example of an Empyrean who can become a god without being vessel to the Elden Ring, as she will become a goddess upon blooming a third time. [ 4 ] It is unknown if other Empyreans possess their own means of godhood without the Elden Ring.
Lunar Princess Ranni is an Empyrean, and the daughter of Radagon and Queen Rennala of the Full Moon , suggesting that being born of a single god is not a strict requirement to be an Empyrean.
Malenia, Miquella, and Ranni, were selected during the Age of the Erdtree by their own Two Fingers to be candidates to succeed Marika. [ 1 ]
During the reign of Queen Marika, and at least the time immediately preceding it, Empyreans were chosen by the Two Fingers . [ 1 ] The Two Fingers grant Empyreans shadowbound beasts , also known as "shadows", to serve as loyal vassals. [ 5 ] Though incapable of treachery, shadows, against their own will, go mad if their masters resist the will of the Fingers, becoming a curse for their Empyrean. [ 6 ] The only two shadowbound beasts currently known are Maliketh, the Black Blade , who serves Marika, and Blaidd the Half-Wolf , who serves Ranni.
The consort of an Empyrean holds the title of lord. [ 7 ] Mohg wished to take the role of monarch by becoming the consort of Miquella, whom he wished to raise to full godhood, despite Miquella already having chosen Radahn as his consort. [ 8 ] The Tarnished may also gain the title of Lord by becoming the consort of Marika [ 9 ] or Ranni. [ 10 ]
The currently known Empyreans are:
Queen Marika the Eternal [ 11 ]
Ranni the Witch [ 1 ]
Malenia, Blade of Miquella [ 1 ]
Miquella of the Haligtree [ 1 ]
The Gloam-Eyed Queen [ 12 ]
Elden Ring (concept)
It is implied that Dragonlord Placidusax's unnamed god was also an Empyrean who was once vessel to the Elden Ring, apropos to his own title as Elden Lord. [ 13 ]
" Empyrean " refers to the highest heaven in ancient and medieval cosmology, usually consisting of fire or light. It can also refer to the firmament, or heavens, or an ideal place or state. [ 14 ]
In Japanese, Empyreans are referred to as 神人 (Shinjin), literally meaning "Divine Person", or "God-like Person." [ 15 ]

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Godwyn the Golden
Godwyn the Golden is a prominent background character in Elden Ring . He was a demigod and the son of Queen Marika the Eternal and Godfrey the First Elden Lord , making him a central member of the royal Golden Lineage. In the Night of the Black Knives , he was assassinated by Black Knife Assassins with the use of daggers imbued with the Rune of Death, stolen from Maliketh, the Black Blade . As the first of the demigods to fall, only his soul perished in the attack; his living corpse was entombed deep beneath the Erdtree, where it eventually twisted into the Prince of Death and began spreading Deathroot across the realm. His unprecedented death would precipitate the Shattering, a devastating war between the demigods which would be responsible for much of the current state of the Lands Between.
The rune of death was stolen. And the demigods began to fall, starting with Godwyn the Golden.
General Information of Godwyn the Golden
Godwyn the Golden Locations
Godwyn the Golden Notes & Trivia
Godwyn the Golden Lore
Lore Items Related to Godwyn the Golden
Dialogue Related to Godwyn the Golden
Godwyn the Golden Gallery
Godwyn the Golden was a scion of the so-called "Golden Line", of which Godrick the Grafted was the final descendant. Early in his mother's divine reign, the Erdtree came under assault by the Ancient Dragons , who had ruled the world before Marika's ascent. The forces of the Erdtree managed to triumph against the ancient dragons, with Godwyn prevailing against the dreaded Fortissax , earning the mighty dragon's friendship in the process and beginning the ancient dragon cult in Leyndell.
Seeking to be free from her fate as a pawn of the Greater Will, Godwyn's half-sister Lunar Princess Ranni stole a fragment of the rune of death, imbuing the weapons of Numen assassins with the rune. Ranni wished to shed her Empyrean body but preserve her spirit, which meant that someone else's spirit would need to die.  Nevertheless, when the assassins fell upon the Royal Capital and murdered Godwyn, his soul was destroyed and left only his soulless body behind. He was the first demigod in history to die since the ascension of Marika to the throne of the Lands Between.
Godwyn's death would be the catalyst for the Shattering of the Elden Ring, plunging the kingdom into chaos without the guidance of the Greater Will . Due to Ranni's ritual, Godwyn's flesh was branded with half of the Cursemark of Death and he became the first of Those Who Live in Death . His body was laid to rest deep in the roots of the Erdtree, but it would eventually corrupt it, leading Deathroot to sprout across the Lands Between, causing others to also rise in undeath. These undead worshipped Godwyn as the Prince of Death, believing he would eventually return and lead them. An aspect of Godwyn's corpse appeared in the depths of Stormveil Castle and is responsible for the loss of Rogier 's legs. Many figures, including his half-brother Miquella and his companion Fortissax , tried to cure him of his condition and allow him a proper death (see Golden Epitaph ), but to no avail. Fortissax would also become corrupted because of his failure to defeat the Death within Godwyn (see Remembrance of the Lichdragon ).
The deathbed companion Fia would become a leading figure among Those Who Live in Death, eventually making her way to Godwyn's corpse. Having received the other half of the cursemark from Ranni's discarded body with help of the Tarnished, Fia lay with Godwyn to grant him a second life as the Prince of Death. As a result of their union, Fia conceived a rune , which a Tarnished seeking to become Elden Lord might use to mend the Elden Ring, reimbuing the principle of death within the Golden Order.
Throughout the game, players can encounter Godwyn's corpse in multiple locations. His main body rests at the Prince of Death's Throne, located in a shallow pool of purple water at the base of a tainted root in the Deeproot Depths. Additionally, Godwyn's cadaver surrogates can be found at the Fog Rift Catacombs , Scorpion River Catacombs , and Stormveil Castle .
There is a statue in the Haligtree of an adult figure hugging the twin empyreans Malenia and Miquella . This is most likely Godwyn as the crown is too dissimilar to Marika's. Based on the available information, Godwyn was likely to have had a close relationship with the twins.
Godwyn's face appears on the back of some crabs.

The Erdtree's old sap becomes amber, treasured as the most precious of jewels in the age of Godfrey, the first Elden Lord.

The misshapen corpse under Stormveil? That is a sacred relic. Of the black knives plot. As that famed night of assassination is known. It happened during the Golden Age of the Erdtree, long before the shattering of the Elden Ring. Someone stole a fragment of the Rune of Death from Maliketh, the Black Blade. And on a bitter night, murdered Godwyn the Golden. That was the first recorded Death of a demigod in all history. And it became the catalyst. Soon, the Elden Ring was smashed, and thus sprang forth the war known as the Shattering.
Ohh... Oh, Lord Godwyn... Such cruelty, such humiliation... My poor, sweet lordling should have died a true death. As the first of the demigods to die. As a martyr to Destined Death. But why must it yet bring such disgrace? A scion of the golden bough, sentenced to live in Death...
When Godwyn died, a hallowbrand scored his flesh. But another exists. Another mark in the shape of the half-wheel wound of the centipede. And I must find it. Before the time comes we receive our Lord. My hands will be dirtied once more by the deed. Will you still let me hold you, even then?
This...is the other hallowbrand. How did you... Oh, my utmost thanks. With this, Godwyn can take his rightful place as First of the Dead. And claim a second, illustrious life. You are my-- our true champion. And though I can't be of any use to you... Can I hold you tight, if only for a moment?
I will soon lay with Godwyn. And it will surely stir within me. The new life of the golden prince, and first Dead of the demigods, as the rune of Those Who Live in Death. Please, do one thing for me. Brandish this child, my rune, and take for yourself the throne. Stay the persecution of Those Who Live in Death. By becoming our Elden Lord.
On a night of wint'ry fog, the rune of Death was stolen. And the demigods began to fall, starting with Godwyn the Golden.
- Elden Ring, Story Trailer
Godwyn the Golden was a demigod and son of Queen Marika the Eternal and Godfrey, First Elden Lord . On the Night of Black Knives , he was murdered by the Black Knife Assassins who used ritual daggers imbued with a fragment of the Rune of Death . [ 1 ]
1 Overview 1.1 Life 1.2 Death 1.3 Prince of Death
Godwyn was the son of Queen Marika the Eternal and her consort and first Elden Lord , Godfrey , making him a scion of the so-called "Golden Lineage", of which Godrick the Grafted was the final descendant. [ 2 ]
During his mother's reign, the Erdtree came under assault by the Dragons, [ 3 ] who had ruled the world before Marika's ascent. The forces of the Erdtree managed to triumph against the dragons, with Godwyn prevailing against the dreaded Fortissax , earning the mighty dragon's friendship in the process. [ 4 ]
This friendship between former foes led to the establishment of the capital ’s Ancient Dragon Cult, [ 5 ] in which Leyndell Knights could learn dragon incantations. Fortissax’s own sister, the dragon Lansseax , took the form of a human to commune with the knights as a priestess of the cult. [ 6 ] The cult was allowed within the Golden Order since its seal , and lightning itself, were both imbued with gold. [ 7 ] The red-hued incantations were also taught in secret. [ 8 ] [ 9 ]
Godwyn would eventually meet his end during the Night of Black Knives. [ 10 ] Seeking to be free from her fate as a pawn of the Greater Will , Godwyn's step-sister Lunar Princess Ranni stole a fragment of the Rune of Death from Maliketh , imbuing the weapons of Numen assassins with it through a fearsome rite. [ 11 ] His death was the first recorded death of a demigod, and served as the catalyst for the shattering of the Elden Ring and the subsequent war that broke out between his siblings and stepsiblings.
While Godwyn was believed to be the first dead among the demigods, he and Ranni both died at the same time. As a result, each was branded with one half of the Cursemark of Death . Ranni's flesh perished while Godwyn perished in soul alone. [ 12 ] He was buried at the Erdtree's roots below Leyndell , [ 13 ] but as he was lacking a soul, his body was unable to die a true death. [ 14 ] Some, including his younger half-brother Miquella [ 15 ] [ 16 ] and his companion Fortissax, tried to cure him of his condition and allow him a proper death, but to no avail. The ancient dragon Fortissax would unsuccessfully try to fight against the Death within Godwyn, but was ultimately corrupted by the blight, becoming a Lichdragon. [ 17 ] Godwyn's personal guard became known as the Death Knights , and quested for their master's cadaver surrogates [ 18 ] , which would lead them to the catacombs of the Realm of Shadow .
The living body, lacking a soul and unable to die a true death, transformed into an unrecognisable abberation, and became known as the Prince of Death. Deathroot sprouted from it, spreading across the Lands Between through the roots of the Erdtree, and giving rise to Those Who Live in Death . [ 19 ]
The deathbed companion Fia would become a leading figure among Those Who Live in Death, eventually making her way to the Prince's throne . With the help from a Tarnished champion , Fia gathers the other two halves of the cursemark, one from Ranni's discarded body and the other from Godwyn's body, and she then lays with Godwyn to grant him a second life as the Prince of Death. As a result of their union, Fia conceived a rune which a champion seeking to become Elden Lord might use to mend the Elden Ring , introducing the principle of life within death into the Golden Order . [ 20 ]
Items associated with:
The Prince of Death's Pustule and Prince of Death's Cyst feature his face multiplying.

---

area: Mohgwyn Palace
chapter: 23
name: Formless Mother
"The Mother of Truth desires a wound."
- Bloodfiend's Sacred Spear
The Formless Mother (姿なき母, Sugata-naki Haha ), also known as Mother of Truth (真実の母, Shinjitsu no Haha ), [ 1 ] is an unseen character in ELDEN RING (2022) .
The Formless Mother is an Outer God who was encountered by Mohg deep underground. She is known to crave wounds, and to bestow power upon accursed blood. [ 2 ] Her body can be pierced using Mohgwyn's Sacred Spear , causing the weapon to become coated in Bloodflame, or an arm can be thrust into her body in order to scatter Bloodflame on the ground, using the incantation Bloodboon .
Long ago, the Formless Mother was discovered by a subjugated tribe in the ravages of war, and were subsequently transformed into Bloodfiends . [ 3 ] [ 4 ] Much like Mohg, the Bloodfiends made use of a sacred spear to commune with the Formless Mother. [ 5 ] A delicacy of the bloodfiends, the blood from Sacred Bloody Flesh is said to have fallen from the Formless Mother's wound. Never will it dry, never will it rot. [ 6 ]
The Mother's designation as "Formless" resembles the title of a Great One (conceptually similar to an Outer God) that is indirectly featured in the From Software game Bloodborne , called "Formless Oedon" (姿なきオドン, Sugata-naki Odon ). In fact, both entities are strongly associated with blood.

---

area: Mountaintops of the Giants
chapter: 21
name: Fell God
The fell god still lurks within the Fire Giants.
- Flame of the Fell God
The fell god , [ 1 ] also known as the one-eyed god , [ 2 ] is an unseen character in ELDEN RING (2022) . It is the source of the Giants' Flame and Giants' Flame Incantations .
The fell god is an ancient deity worshiped by the Fire Giants . It is commonly referred to with a myriad of derogatory terms by the inhabitants of the Lands Between , such as the "corrupt", "grotesque", "malformed", "fell" or "evil" god of the Fire Giants. It is represented in the visage of a single eye with a fiery-orange iris and nine pupils within.
It physically manifests itself in the shape of a grotesque face in the torsos of its followers, channeling its power through them, its greatest flames emerging from its mouth (commonly located in the host giant's stomach) along with the undying flames found seeded within their giant black forge enabling its followers to develop innumerable fire incantations .
The fell god of fire has been around for a long time and predates the Erdtree . It was a dreaded figure in the old legends of the hornsent of Enir-Ilim . [ 3 ] It was worshiped by the Fire Giants, [ 2 ] who it cursed for eternity to tend the Flame within the Forge of the Giants . [ 1 ]
The powers of the Flame were once deemed as a threat to the Erdtree by Queen Marika and the Golden Order and thus they waged a bloody War Against the Giants . Once the Fire Giants were defeated, [ 1 ] Queen Marika realized the fires of their forge could not die and thus sealed it away, [ 4 ] reducing its once formidable flame into mere embers and cursing the last surviving Fire Giant to keep them so for all eternity. [ 5 ]
Even then, after countless years, innumerable Prophets would be haunted by visions of its flames consuming the Erdtree. [ 6 ] And the one-eyed god still lurks dormant within its subjects, [ 7 ] on the cusp of being awakened at any moment with a sufficient sacrificial offering.
The Furnace Golems , One-Eyed Shield , and Fire Monk Armor were created in the image of the fell god.
The fell god was said to possess curled horns. [ 3 ]
Eight small pupils encircle the central pupil of the fell god's eye. This distinctive pattern appears on the Divine Towers , with some pillar reliefs featuring central cavities filled with glowing meteorite ore. The Troll's Hammer also displays the nine-circle design, used by giants during an age when smithing held divine significance. [ 8 ] The Verdigris Discus shares this pattern with the fell god's eye. Verdigris, believed to be a gift from an outer god , [ 9 ] also appears on the talismans and great dish carried by the Fire Giant . This pattern is also seen on the One-Eyed Shield, Furnace Visage , Verdigris Armor , and Verdigris Greaves .

The fell god’s Flame was said to cleanse the body of poison and scarlet rot . [ 10 ]
The sun medallion on the Dung Eater 's Omen Armor resembles the Furnace Visage. Red hair appears to grow from the severed Omen horns.
Red hair grows on many of its followers.

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Two Fingers
The Two Fingers are a manifestation of the Greater Will . The Two Fingers act as a destiny to the demigods , choosing the path which they must take. They are the main manifestation of the Greater Will in the game, besides Elden Beast . The Two Fingers have responsibilities include selecting Empyrean candidates to inherit the Elden Ring, furnishing these candidates with shadowbound beasts, and offering wisdom to the Tarnished through Finger Readers . You can find a total of seven Two Fingers throughout the game, the only one found alive is in the Roundtable Hold , interpreted by Enia . There are Two Fingers for almost every demi-god in the game, except Morgott and Mohg , who share Two Fingers . There is also no Two Finger for Messmer , Melina , or Miquella . Miquella and Malenia might share a Two Finger like Morgott and Mohg but it is currently unknown.
After killing Morgott and inspecting the thorns, you discover that you must burn the Erdtree to enter. The Two Fingers will then point straight up in an attempt to contact the Greater Will. Once you kill a demigod and receive a Great Rune , it will be shown as powerless. To activate its power you must restore it at the demigod's respective Two Fingers . In Ranni's quest, you have to hunt down and obtain the Fingerslayer Blade . The Fingerslayer Blade allows for the Two Fingers to be killed. At the end of Ranni's quest, it is revealed that she used it to slay her Two Fingers , therefore changing her destiny. Ranni's Two Fingers are the only ones not to be found in a Divine Tower , besides the one at the Roundtable Hold.

Across the realm, life lies in ruin. Fallen to pieces. Foul curses and misery spread, unabating. But the Greater Will has not abandoned the realm, nor the life that inhabits it. So it is that the Tarnished are guided by grace. Called to act. Brave Tarnished, your Great Rune is a handsome shard of the Elden Ring. Seek another of its kind. Touching the Two Fingers for the first time gives you the Rapture gesture .
Some characters such as Varre and Count Ymir believe that when the Elden Ring was shattered, the Two Fingers lost their guidance.
The Two Fingers never truly speak, instead, they use Finger Reader Enia to communicate before eventually becoming still.
There are Two Fingers for every demigod with a few exceptions, such as Miquella or Messmer . It is possible that Malenia and Miquella share Two Fingers like Mohg and Morgott do.
As revealed in the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion, all Two Fingers and Fingercreepers originated from Metyr, Mother of Fingers , a gleaming entity that was the first shooting star to fall upon the Lands Between.

Fingers cannot speak, yet these were eloquent. Persistently did they wriggle, spelling out mysteries in the air. Thus did we gain the words. The words of our faith .
- Two Fingers Heirloom
The Two Fingers are NPC characters in ELDEN RING (2022) . They serve as vassals to the Greater Will in the Lands Between . [ 2 ]
1 Overview 1.1 Appearance 1.2 Background
Two Fingers have an appearance resembling part of a giant hand, with a slight indentation in the “palm”, and two finger-like appendages on the top, hence their name.
The Two Fingers are the children of Metyr, Mother of Fingers , [ 3 ] and serve as divine envoys of the Greater Will. [ 4 ] They are responsible for selecting Empyreans , [ 5 ] granting them shadowbound beasts , [ 6 ] and guiding their path toward inheriting the Elden Ring . The Fingers were present when the Empyrean Marika wove the Erdtree into existence from the primordial Crucible of life. [ 7 ] [ 1 ] [ 8 ]
In the past, the Fingers were masters of oration, their wisdom attracting champions to Roundtable Hold . [ 9 ] They were said to communicate using a language of light, [ 10 ] composed of intangible symbols that appeared to hang in the air. These inscriptions could be fashioned into weapons such as the Cipher Pata and the Coded Sword . The Two Fingers are also the source of Two Fingers Incantations and possess the ability to restore the blessings of the Great Runes atop the ancient Divine Towers .
The Fingers are attended by Finger Readers , who interpret their wisdom for the Tarnished, those to whom Grace has returned. [ 11 ] Churches dedicated to the Two Fingers exist beyond the Lands Between, served by confessors who hunt down their enemies. [ 12 ] While some Tarnished remain faithful, the Fingers are now believed to be corrupted and senile, [ 13 ] having been broken along with the Erdtree long ago. [ 14 ]
One living Two Fingers can be found in Roundtable Hold, behind a set of doors that remain closed until the Tarnished champion acquires their first Great Rune. They will be at the back of the chamber with Finger Reader Enia sitting close by. The Two Fingers here appears to be in a state of decay, missing patches of skin with exposed flesh on its body and “fingers”.
There are several Two Fingers that can be found throughout the Lands Between . They can be found at the top of the Divine Towers littered about the world, however, they appear to have long since died through unknown means.
The Two Fingers cannot be attacked or killed through any means during normal gameplay. They can only be harmed by the Fingerslayer Blade , wielded by someone with a destined fate.
They can only be harmed by the Fingerslayer Blade , wielded by someone with a destined fate.
The individuals known to have been named Empyrean by the Two Fingers include Malenia , Miquella , Ranni , the Gloam-Eyed Queen , and Queen Marika herself.
The Finger Mimic mushrooms were known as the "stillborn of the Two Fingers." [ 15 ] These fungi were harvested and consumed by the finger-weavers for their hallucinogenic properties.
Many characters distrust the Two Fingers: Count Ymir believes that the Fingers are themselves flawed, that the instructions they give do not come from the Greater Will but instead from the Finger's belief in what the Greater Will says. [ 16 ] [ 17 ] Patches ridicules the Fingers. [ 18 ] Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing believes the purpose for the Two Fingers was lost long ago. [ 19 ] White Mask Varré views the Two Fingers in a poor light, believing they had fallen into madness and lost their purpose after the Greater Will abandoned the Lands Between. [ 13 ]
Count Ymir believes that the Fingers are themselves flawed, that the instructions they give do not come from the Greater Will but instead from the Finger's belief in what the Greater Will says. [ 16 ] [ 17 ]
Patches ridicules the Fingers. [ 18 ]
Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing believes the purpose for the Two Fingers was lost long ago. [ 19 ]
White Mask Varré views the Two Fingers in a poor light, believing they had fallen into madness and lost their purpose after the Greater Will abandoned the Lands Between. [ 13 ]
Although the Two Fingers do not speak directly, their words are relayed by Finger Reader Enia at certain points in the game.

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Three Fingers
Three Fingers is an NPC in Elden Ring . Three Fingers is an integral part of The Lord of Frenzied Flame ending and can be found near the Cathedral of the Forsaken , below the Subterranean Shunning-Grounds . There they pass on the unspeakable message of the Frenzied Flame .
See Three Fingers Lore
Seek audience with the Three Fingers and the flame of frenzy. If you inherit the flame of frenzy, your flesh will serve as kindling and the girl can be spared ...setting you on the righteous path of lordship. — Shabriri
Three Fingers can be found near the Cathedral of the Forsaken , below the Subterranean Shunning-Grounds . You will need to remove your armor to open the door.
This NPC does not move and cannot be interacted with outside of the cutscene where it gives you the Flame of Frenzy .
Finding Three Fingers will imbue the character with the Flame of Frenzy which unlocks the Lord of the Frenzied Flame ending. See Endings page.  Interacting with the Three Fingers will also affect the character with large burn scars across their body as well as burning yellow eyes, these can be toggled on or off at Fia's cosmetic mirror in Roundtable Hold or by selecting cosmetics while speaking to Rennala in the Raya Lucaria Grand Library.
Note: Removing the cosmetics will not remove the Flame of Frenzy nor will it affect the ending.
Note: The player's burning yellow eyes are removed from their eye sockets when they are not the host.
Note: Receiving the Flame of Frenzy will prompt Melina to leave your party thinking that you are no longer fit. She will also threaten to kill the player should they become the Lord of Frenzy. She does not appear in the final cutscene if she sacrificed herself to the Flame of Ruin.
By completing Hyetta's questline you will be rewarded with the Frenzied Flame Seal when you speak with her after interacting with the Three Fingers and acquiring the Flame of Frenzy .  She will disappear, leaving the seal in her place as well as 5 Frenzyflame Stones .
after the interaction between the player and the three fingers, they will mark your character skin and eyes, it can be removed. Once removed you can't restore the yellow eyes.
Meeting with the Three Fingers will lock you out of all other ending choices, unless Miquella's Needle is used at Dragonlord Placidusax's arena at Crumbling Farum Azula. This removes the Frenzied Flame, and is a permanent reversal. Players don't need to kill Dragonlord Placidusax, just by entering the arena and using Miquella's Needle is enough. It can be done after fighting the dragon, since you can still access the area.
Incantation originating from the maddening Three Fingers.
Causes the yellow flame of frenzy to burst forth from the caster's eyes. Charging increases the range of the burst.
The flame of frenzy deals damage and causes buildup of madness. This incantation also causes buildup of madness in the caster, and is only effective against Tarnished.
A great stone shield with an intricately carved fingerprint design.
One of the heaviest of all greatshields.
Part of the tomb of an ancient god, the Readerless Fingers relayed their message through these imprints, said to be the very seeds from which frenzy first sprouted.
War spear singed and blistered by fingers, used by Vyke, Knight of the Roundtable Hold.
Like Vyke himself, it has been tormented by the yellow flame of frenzy from within.
Eyeball of the knight Vyke, inflamed yellow. Seared with a repulsive fingerprint burn akin to those that marred his entire body.
If offered to the blind maiden, it will lead her to the source of the distant light, allowing her to become a Finger Maiden.
Formless sacred seal bestowed by the maiden of the Three Fingers. Enhances incantations of the frenzied flame.
This seal is the mark of the Lord of Frenzied Flame. Scales incantations using strength, dexterity, intelligence, and faith all together.
One of the unalloyed gold needles that Miquella crafted to ward away the meddling of outer gods.
Capable of subduing the flame of frenzy if inherited, allowing one to cheat fate and avoid becoming Lord of Frenzied Flame.
However, the needle is as yet unfinished and can only be used in the heart of the storm beyond time said to be found in Farum Azula.
Craftable item prepared using a cracked pot. A cocktail of Frenzied Flame materials is sealed inside.
Throw at enemies to cause buildup of madness.
The flame of frenzy burns the eyes, but tranquility follows in its wake. May this pot bring salvation to those it strikes.
Remembrance of Metyr, the Mother of Fingers, hewn into the Scadutree. The power of its namesake can be unlocked by the Finger Reader. Alternatively, it can be used to gain a great bounty of runes.
The mother of all Two Fingers and Fingercreepers was in turn a magnificently gleaming daughter of the Greater Will, and the first shooting star to fall upon the Lands Between.
An eyeball of the frenzied flame that has swollen from within. Material used for crafting items.
Exceedingly rare to find. Marked with fingerprint burns, proof of having being touched directly by the Three Fingers' embrace.
The Three Fingers (三本指, Sanbonyubi ) are a mysterious entity that act as the vassal of the Frenzied Flame, the incarnation of chaos. They seek to imbue a host with the Frenzied Flame curse, that they might become the Lord of Frenzy and destroy the world, returning it to the primal state of chaos from before the Greater Will fractured the One Great .
As its name implies, the Three Fingers are an entity that appears as a vaguely humanoid hand with three finger-like appendages, similar to that of the Greater Will’s Two Fingers . However, unlike the former, the Three Fingers appear to be burned and smoldering, as they embody the curse of the Frenzied Flame.
Three Fingers can be located at the very bottom of the Subterranean Shunning Grounds, behind an Illusory Wall located at the back of the Cathedral of the Forsaken. There will be a door that can only be opened if the player removes all the clothes from their character. The Three Fingers will be behind the door, in a small chamber.

name: Deathroot
Deathroot is a Key Item in Elden Ring . Gurranq Beast Clergyman seeks and devours these. Key Items in Elden Ring include a wide variety of items found in specific locations or given by a related NPC , which are used to unlock areas, quests, and to further progress the game's story.
A source that gives rise to Those Who Live in Death.
The beast clergyman, found at Bestial Sanctum in the distant east, collects and devours these roots.
On the night of the dire plot the stolen Rune of Death enabled the first Death of a demigod. Later, the Rune of Death spread across the Lands Between through the underground roots of the Greattree, sprouting in the form of Deathroot.

Can be traded to Gurranq, Beast Clergyman for diverse items. See his page for a list of rewards.
The Tibia Mariner at Summonwater Village is the weakest one, so it is recommended that you fight this one first if you want a Deathroot early on. [ Map link ]
Deathroots are a key item in ELDEN RING (2022) . They can be given to Gurranq, Beast Clergyman , who will eat them and reward the player with a variety of items in return.
A source that gives rise to Those Who Live in Death .
The Beast Clergyman, found at Bestial Sanctum in the distant east , collects and devours these roots.
On the night of the dire plot the stolen Rune of Death enabled the first Death of a demigod . Later, the Rune of Death spread across the Lands Between through the underground roots of the Greattree , sprouting in the form of Deathroot.
Deathroot can be acquired in the following places:
Defeating the Tibia Mariner in Summonwater Village in Limgrave.
Deathtouched Catacombs at Stormhill in Limgrave , in a chest after defeating the Black Knife Assassin boss.
Defeating the Tibia Mariner in eastern Liurnia of the Lakes .
Black Knife Catacombs in eastern Liurnia of the Lakes, in a chest after defeating the Cemetery Shade boss.
Defeating the Tibia Mariner in western Altus Plateau near Wyndham Catacombs .
Gelmir Hero's Grave at Mt. Gelmir , in a chest after defeating the Red Wolf of the Champion boss.
Giants' Mountaintop Catacombs on Mountaintops of the Giants , in a chest after defeating the Ulcerated Tree Spirit boss.
Defeating the Tibia Mariner in Mountaintops of the Giants in the graveyard on the way to Castle Sol .
Hidden Path to the Haligtree in Forbidden Lands , in a chest after defeating the Stray Mimic Tear boss.
In the original Japanese text, the Greattree doesn't exist as a proper noun. Its roots are simply "great" or "large tree roots" (大樹根, taijune.) [ 1 ]

---

area: Enir-Ilim
chapter: 34
name: Miquella
Miquella, also known as Miquella the Kind or Kindly Miquella , is a background character in Elden Ring and a central character in Shadow of the Erdtree . One of the Empyrean children born from the union of Queen Marika and Radagon , he is the twin brother of Malenia , together known as the Twin Prodigies. It is revealed that Miquella has stripped himself of his flesh and Empyrean lineage in the Land of Shadow , a place obscured by the Erdtree and purged in an unsung war.
My brother will keep his promise. He possesses the wisdom, the allure, of a god - he is the most fearsome Empyrean of all." - Malenia
Miquella the Kind...is a monster. Pure and radiant, he wields love to shrive clean the hearts of men. There is nothing more terrifying." - Sir Ansbach
Miquella is cursed to be perpetually young. He is said to be well loved as a demigod and has the ability to compel affection from others (see Bewitching Branch ). This is why Malenia considers him the most fearsome Empyrean.
Miquella and Malenia were very close, with her becoming his sworn blade and protector. Like his half-sister Ranni , Miquella would be chosen by the Two Fingers as a potential successor to Queen Marika. Originally a fundamentalist, as well as possessing genius intellect, he would develop incantations for his father (see Discus of Light and Triple Rings of Light ) and peculiarities such as the Pulley Bow . However, he would become disillusioned with the Golden Order after it was unable to cure Malenia from her Scarlet Rot (see Radagon's Rings of Light ), nor grant his half-brother Godwyn a true death (see Golden Epitaph ). This compelled him towards a different path, one of Unalloyed Gold (see Miquella's Needle ) that would accept all who had been discarded by the Golden Order.
Miquella is described as benevolent, kind of heart, and envisioned a world where all would be accepted (see Light of Miquella ). However, in the base game it is heavily hinted that Miquella used others for his own ends, which is later confirmed during the events in the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion.
Miquella created the Haligtree to allow himself to grow into a god and to provide shelter to those without grace, such as the Albinaurics , an oppressed race of artificial beings who sees the Haligtree as their chosen land. Before he could grow the Haligtree to its full potential, Miquella was stolen by his half-brother, Mohg, Lord of Blood , in his cocoon form, and taken to Mohgwyn Palace . Mohg wished to have Miquella elevated to godhood with himself as Miquella's consort through the Formless Mother, however, Mohg's advances were not reciprocated (see Remembrance of the Blood Lord ).
(SHADOW OF THE ERDTREE SPOILERS BELOW)
It is later revealed in Shadow of the Erdtree by Sir Ansbach , one of Mohg's former disciples, that Mohg was enchanted by Miquella, and that Mohg was used by Miquella merely as a means to gain entry to the Land of Shadow. Mohg's corpse would later be defiled and repurposed as a vessel for the soul of Miquella's promised consort.
As if using Lord Mohg to gain entrance to the land of shadow were not enough, he plans to use his corpse as the vessel of his king consort. He has forsaken Lord Mohg's soul. He desires only his empty shell. It beggars belief, but… I'm afraid Tender Miquella fails to grasp the humiliation implied by this act. One thing is certain. My dear lord deserved better." - Sir Ansbach
Miquella is said to be waiting for his promised lord in the Land of Shadow. There, he has abandoned his flesh (see Miquella's Cross ), his love (see St. Trina ), as well as his own Empyrean lineage - a lineage secured by Marika through what appears to be a great war and a massacre carried out by Messmer the Impaler .
No matter our efforts if the roots are rotten, then we have little recourse. Ever-young Miquella saw things for what they were. He knew that his bloodline was tainted. His roots mired in madness. A tragedy if ever there was one. That he would feel compelled to renounce everything. When the blame lay squarely with the mother." - Count Ymir
It is revealed in Shadow of the Erdtree that Miquella seeks entry to the Tower of Shadow (the true form is Enir-Ilim ). There he wishes to ascend to godhood through the Gate of Divinity, which Queen Marika once used to ascend, and bring forth the Age of Compassion (see Circlet of Light and Miquella's Great Rune ). He dispossesses himself of his flesh to prepare for his ascension, but in doing so, loses himself and what he once was. Mohg's corpse is then used as a vessel for his half-brother Radahn . Miquella admired Radahn's qualities, which contrast that of his afflicted self (see Remembrance of a God and a Lord ).
In the endgame flashback cutscene, it is revealed that Miquella had long planned his ascension and made a promise with Radahn to be his consort. It is also revealed that Malenia knew of their promise (see Young Lion's Armor ) and reminded Radahn of it during their battle in Caelid . Though following this revelation, it is unclear why Malenia went into conflict with Radahn and unleashed her scarlet rot in the first place. One possible explanation is that the promise was not mutual, and Malenia sought to coerce Radahn into agreement. This is possibly supported by the fact that those with strength, such as the Tarnished, seem particularly resistant to Miquella's charm ( Leda comments on this after aiding her twice, mentioning that the Tarnished is seemingly identical before and after Miquella's rune was broken). However, Miquella mentions a vow independent of his promise with Radahn, suggesting that the two may have entered a mutual contract of some kind before the promise was made that Radahn was expected to uphold.
Lord brother. I'm going to be a god. If we honour our part of the vow, promise me you'll be my consort. I'll make the world a gentler place." - Miquella
Miquella's Needle can be used to purge the influences of an outer god, particularly the influence of the Frenzied Flame in Placidusax's arena.
The promotional material for Shadow of the Erdtree shows Miquella riding the spectral steed Torrent in the Realm of Shadow, though this isn't elaborated on nor shown ingame.
There are some incantations attributed to Miquella and his followers.
The state of Miquella's physical body in the Lands Between can be seen in the cocoon in Mohgwyn Palace. The physical body looks thin with misshapen skin. This, however, is not reflective of Miquella's true state, which is revealed in the DLC's endgame boss . A ghost in the Consecrated Snowfield also says that Mohg stole the precious "flesh" (but not the soul) of Miquella.
The dialogue by one of the phantoms in Stone Coffin Fissure hints that Miquella would've been an imperfect god and that his Age of Compassion wasn't what he envisioned: "Kindly Miquella... I see you've thrown away... Something you should not have. Under any circumstances. How will you salvation offer...to those who cannot be saved? When you could not even save your other self?"
Miquella's voice actor, Naomi Mc Donald, also provides voices for Dyna the Crow , Stone Trader Chloanne , and the Milfanito from Dark Souls II .
There is a cut ending found in the SOTE game files associated with Miquella's Age of Compassion, where Miquella says at some point during the finale, "I hereby swear... To every living being, and every living soul... Now comes the age of our eden... A thousand year voyage guided by compassion... Beginning here, love encompasses all... No living thing will be denied, no deed censured... If you have known sin, if you grieve for this world, I am ready. To embrace the whole of it..." (reminiscent of Ranni's speech) . This cut ending contains fully voice acted dialogue, as well as various unused animations. Additionally, Miquella says to the Tarnished, “ If thou covetest the throne, impress my vision upon thine heart. In the new world of thy making, all things will flourish, whether graceful, or malign ”. Based on the placement of the dialogue in the text data dump, this was originally going to be Elden Ring's 7th ending , available after beating the base game final boss.
Cut NPC dialogue confirmed that Miquella is St. Trina before the dlc. "Finally, I have found it.... St. Trina's, no, Lord Miquella's cadaver... I have partaken of untold secrets... Such that I may aid you, O Lord... So please, I hope you welcome your humble servant Rhico... Into your dream, the world of your heart.... Indeed, I beg you grant my wish, that when you transcend from empyrean to God, allow me a place by your side..." - Rhico, cut NPC.
Miquella has a cut monologue relating to the Haligtree, “Young seedling, young seedling. Return to the bosom of earth. But remember well, Thou’rt mine. So shall I give of myself. This is for thee. Mine abundance, my drop of dew. Quench thy thirst, throughout thy frame. Blossom and burgeon, time and again. Grow larger, stronger. Until the day cometh. When thou canst share in my dream.”
Cut armament shows that Miquella's Great Rune was originally supposed to be the Rune of Abundance. "Twinblade that contains the power of the Rune of Abundance. The holy power within the blade can call upon part of Miquella's own strength" - Abundance Twinblade
A cut quest shows that Miquella, under the guise of St. Trina, used to sing a lullaby to soothe the Frenzied Flame affliction of the Red Eyed Merchants, helping them to retain their personhood and prevent them from falling into madness .
The full text datadump for both the base game and DLC can be found on Git Hub - "The Carian Archive". Translator's note : the Japanese language has several first-person pronouns depending on the speaker's age, status, and gender, unlike the English "I" which is multipurpose. This is useful to determine the speaker, as the Japanese 僕 ("boku") is predominantly used by young males/boys and is only found in dialogue spoken by Miquella.

Miquella and Malenia are both the children of a single god. As such they are both Empyreans, but suffered afflictions from birth. One was cursed with eternal childhood, and the other harbored rot within.

Miquella the Kind , [ 3 ] also known as Miquella of the Haligtree , [ 8 ] is an Empyrean and son of Queen Marika the Eternal and King Consort Radagon . He is the elder twin brother of Malenia , and together they are known as the Twin Prodigies (天賦の双子, Tenpu no Futago ). He is a Remembrance Boss in Shadow of the Erdtree .
1 Overview 1.1 Early Life 1.2 The Haligtree 1.3 The Realm of Shadow
1.3 The Realm of Shadow
Miquella and his twin sister were born with incurable afflictions, with Miquella's curse being perpetual youth. [ 9 ] Miquella made a promise to Malenia and experimented tirelessly in an attempt to undo their curses. The twins were very close, and Malenia became Miquella's sworn blade and protector. [ 10 ] Miquella was loved by many people and had a remarkable ability to compel affection from others. [ 11 ] For this reason, many regarded him as the most fearsome Empyrean. [ 12 ] [ 13 ]
Miquella was chosen by his Two Fingers to become an Empyrean, destined to succeed Queen Marika . [ 14 ] He was an adherent of Golden Order Fundamentalism , and developed new incantations as gifts to his father Radagon. [ 15 ] [ 16 ] However, Miquella abandoned Fundamentalism when it failed to treat Malenia's rot. He then began creating Unalloyed Gold , [ 17 ] which he used it to craft needles that could ward away the meddling of outer gods and forestall Malenia's incurable rot. [ 18 ] These needles could even be used to subdue the power of the Frenzied Flame . [ 19 ] He also unsuccessfully sought a solution for his half-brother Godwyn 's state of incomplete death. [ 20 ] [ 21 ]
At some point, Miquella started watering a sapling with his own blood in the hope that it would become a new Erdtree . [ 22 ] The sapling was grown on top of the fortress of Elphael and became known as the Haligtree . Soldiers and Knights sworn to protect the Haligtree would use Unalloyed gold as a symbol of their loyalty and devotion to Miquella, wearing crowns made of the metal and creating shields embedded with it. [ 23 ] [ 24 ] [ 25 ] [ 26 ] Miquella was also said to bless the low and meek. [ 23 ] The Haligtree would became a hidden haven, [ 27 ] and was intended to be the eventual salvation for many of the persecuted and the unwanted in the Lands Between , such as the Albinaurics. [ 28 ]
As Miquella ascended closer to godhood as an Empyrean, he would soon desire to take his half-brother General Radahn as a consort. He made a vow with Radahn, where Radahn would become his consort, [ 29 ] [ 30 ] and in return, Miquella would have to uphold some unspecified part of the vow [ 30 ] . During the shattering, Malenia and Radahn fought, culminating in Malenia unleashing the Scarlet Aeonia. As the flower blossomed, Malenia whispered in Radahn's ear "Miquella awaits thee, O promised consort." [ 31 ] After Malenia's battle with General Radahn in Caelid , one of her knights carried her slumbering body back to the Haligtree. Radahn survived the battle, but his mind was destroyed, and he was fated to aimlessly wander the battlefield, devouring the corpses of friends and foes alike, [ 32 ] until a Tarnished champion eventually bested him in combat.
Miquella embedded himself into the Haligtree, but before he could finish this process, his tree was cut open and Miquella was kidnapped. [ 33 ] His captor was none other than his half-brother Mohg, Lord of Blood , who had gained power from an outer god known only as the Formless Mother . [ 34 ] Mohg took the slumbering Empyrean's infant form deep underground, where he worked to raise Miquella to full godhood, wishing to become his consort, create a new dynasty, and take the role of monarch. [ 35 ] He would have his followers render offerings of blood and drench Miquella's cocoon in order to facilitate his awakening. [ 36 ] Despite all of Mohg's efforts, no matter how much of his bloody bedchamber he tried to share, Miquella would remain completely unresponsive. [ 35 ] In truth, Miquella had placed an enchantment on Mohg, wishing to use the Omen to gain access to the Realm of Shadow and to use his body as a vessel for his king consort. [ 37 ] Sir Ansbach , a Pureblood Knight in service to Mohg, attempted to free his lord by challenging Miquella, only to succumb to an enchantment of his own. [ 38 ]
With Miquella's absence, his Haligtree became a misshapen husk, slowly succumbing to the Scarlet Rot . [ 26 ] [ 39 ] The Haligtree's soldiers and Malenia, presumably unaware of Miquella's abduction, desperately awaited Miquella's return to the rotten tree. [ 40 ] [ 41 ]
Making use of Mohg, Miquella entered the Realm of Shadow , the location of the Gate of Divinity . As Miquella travelled through the Realm of Shadow, he abandoned parts of himself, including his flesh, his eye, his doubt and vacillation, his love, and his fears. Golden Miquella's Crosses resembling a Great Rune appeared at each location where he abandoned something, akin to Miquella's footprints. [ 42 ] Miquella's followers, who pursued him into the Realm of Shadow, used these crosses to trace his path through the realm. [ 43 ] Miquella also discarded his other half, St. Trina , [ 44 ] in the Stone Coffin Fissure . St. Trina, whose love for Miquella was boundless in spite of her abandonment, [ 1 ] recognized that godhood would be a prison for the Empyrean, [ 45 ] and desired for him to be killed out of mercy. [ 46 ]
Eventually, a Tarnished champion broke into Mohg's palace and slew the Lord of Blood for his great rune. Sir Gideon Ofnir suggested it might be safer to destroy the cocoon that Miquella remains asleep in. [ 47 ] Instead, the Tarnished met Needle Knight Leda , a loyal follower of Miquella, who urged them to travel to the Realm of Shadow by touching Miquella's withered arm within the cocoon. [ 48 ]
Within the Realm of Shadow, the Tarnished encounters several more of Miquella's followers , learns of his mission to ascend to go

---

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Queen Marika the Eternal
Queen Marika the Eternal is a background character in Elden Ring and Shadow of the Erdtree . She is the current ruler of the Lands Between, and the one who shattered the Elden Ring. Queen Marika the Eternal is central to much of the game's narrative and lore . She has many powerful Demigod offspring, who took part in the Shattering and other Major Events .
Now, Queen Marika the Eternal is nowhere to be found, and in the Night of the Black Knives Godwyn the Golden was first to perish. Soon, Marika's offspring, demigods all, claimed the shards of the Elden Ring. The mad taint of their newfound strength triggered the Shattering.
Queen Marika's Origins and the Land of Shadow
Queen Marika and The Lands Between
Queen Marika and the Numen
Queen Marika and Her Duality with Radagon
Queen Marika and Her Children
Queen Marika and Spoken Echoes
Lore videos about Queen Marika
Lore items related to Queen Marika
Queen Marika's Dialogue
Queen Marika Images Gallery
Queen Marika is the vessel for the Elden Ring, and by extension the Greater Will . The runes that constitute the Elden Ring represent the laws that govern the world, currently the Golden Order, which was founded by Queen Marika. The Elden Ring was shattered into shards referred to as Great Runes , which are in the possession of Queen Marika's offspring.
Queen Marika is a Numen. They are a race of people that originate from outside the Lands Between.
During the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, it is revealed that the future Queen Marika and her kin settled in Shaman Village some time ago (hereinafter they are referred to as Shamans).
Phantom dialogue and item descriptions found in Bonny Village further reveal that the Shamans were cruelly oppressed by the Hornsent, the past rulers of the Land of Shadow, and were tortured with whips (see Tooth Whip ) and stuffed into jars (see Bonny Butchering Knife ) as part of ancient rituals.
Shaman flesh is said to have innate properties which allow it to easily merge with others, which explains Godrick the Grafted's affinity with grafting, and the spreading of Deathroot from Godwyn's corpse, both being descendants of Queen Marika.
These practices gave rise to the Jar Innards creatures found in gaols within the Land of Shadow. The goal of the hornsent's actions against the shaman seemed to be to turn them into saints. At some point she became an Empyrean and received the shadowbound beast Maliketh who was loyal only to her (see Remembrance of the Black Blade ).
After ascending to godhood at the Gate of Divinity, Queen Marika would wage a genocidal war against the Hornsent, led by Messmer, the Impaler . Queen Marika's hatred for the Hornsent and the Crucible explains her distain for her own omen sons Mohg and Morgott , both of them being shunned far below the capital of Leyndell .
For pity's sake, your place is in the jar. Nigh-sainthood itself awaits you within. For shamans like you, this is your lot. Life were you accorded for this alone." - Phantom in Bonny Village
Queen Marika envisioned a world without True Death, establishing the Golden Order after becoming the reigning monarch of the Lands Between. Queen Marika sealed the Rune of Death away from the Elden Ring, tasking her shadow Maliketh to guard it, which would later be stolen in the Night of the Black Knives . Under the Golden Order, there can never be True Death, and all living beings undergo a cycle of reincarnation back to the Erdtree .
Queen Marika would take Godfrey as her first consort, of who would become the first Elden Lord. Godfrey would then lead her armies and wage wars to quell opposition to the Erdtree (see Protection of the Erdtree ), such as the war against the Fire Giants (see Smithing Stone (8) ) which led to the slaying of the Fell God.
Once the last of Queen Marika's enemies fell, it was said that the hue from Godfrey's eyes faded (see Elden Lord Crown ), and he was banished from the Lands Between, along with his kin, and were divested of their grace. Godfrey would lead them on what is known as The Long March, to distant lands outside The Lands Between, thus creating the first Tarnished (see Remembrance of Hoarah Loux ).
Radagon would become Marika's second consort, and thus the second Elden Lord. After the Elden Ring was shattered, Queen Marika would be punished by the Greater Will and imprisoned within the Erdtree (the Elden Beast , which is the vassal of the Greater Will, is implied to have crucified Marika, as during the boss battle it can crucify the Tarnished in a similar pose to Marika).
Marika would then beckon the Tarnished to return to the Lands Between, extending her grace, along with the guidance of the Two Fingers, in hopes of ushering in the third Elden Lord.
In the endings where the Tarnished mends the Elden Ring and becomes the new Elden Lord, they are also said to become Marika's third consort.
By using the Law of Regression incantation on the statue of Radagon on Leyndell, Royal Capital, the Tarnished learns that Radagon and Queen Marika are both the same person. Marika's Hammer also reveals that Marika was the one that shattered the Elden Ring, while Radagon used the same hammer to try and repair it, suggesting that although they are the same being, they have fundamentally different ideals.
This is further supported in one of the spoken echoes, "O Radagon, leal hound of the Golden Order. Thou'rt yet to become me. Thou'rt yet to become a god. Let us be shattered, both. Mine other self” .
The Numen is the group of people Queen Marika descended from. According to Numen's Rune . This connection is made aware. When reading about the items of the Black Knife Set, this connection to Queen Marika herself is reiterated. This begs the question of why Marika's flesh and blood participated in the Night of the Black Knives with Ranni and ended the Golden Order. They have a history of being loyal to Queen Marika and her kin.
Ranni's personal motivations are explored in her quest, as she states her need for personal freedom from the Greater Will. They were her main conspirators as they performed the deeds of the Night of the Black Knives, brought death to the lands between, slew Godwyn the Golden, and destroyed Ranni's Body.
The collaboration between Ranni and the Numen assassins presents a conflict that has no clear answer. With the historical ties those same Numen held to the Queen Marika. Their active participation in the murder of her son, Godwyn the Golden, indicates a subversion of the Golden Order from within.
This event effectively utilized the Queen’s own kin to facilitate the destruction of the status quo she had established. Which begs the question, would these people. The same numen people that followed Marika from beyond the lands between and became part of the Golden Order participate in its destruction. What changed to make them willing to bring death itself to the lands between and slay the holy Godwyn?
Since Ranni provided the means through the ritualized Black Knives, the act itself is weird: Ranni’s pursuit of personal agency is clear, but the Numen’s historical proximity to Marika is juxtaposed by their eventual betrayal of the royal line.
Following this event, the alliance ruptured, resulting in the imprisonment of the assassin leader, Alecto, and the subsequent targeting of Ranni’s associates by the remaining Black Knife forces.
Godfrey and Marika together had Godwyn the Golden , as well as the twin omens Mohg and Morgott , the first of the demigods.
Marika's other half, Radagon, married the Carian Queen, Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon . They would have three children together: Radahn , Praetor Rykard , and the Lunar Princess Ranni . Ranni, unlike her siblings, would be chosen by the Two Fingers as a potential successor to Queen Marika and elevated to Empyrean status. Like Marika, she would be granted her own shadow, Blaidd .
The union of Marika and Radagon gave birth to the twin Empyreans Malenia and Miquella . Both are born with severe afflictions, dialogue from pastor Miriel hints to this being part of the consequences of Radagon's broken vows to Rennala; "Severing a vow, strongest of bonds, has consequences ever more dire" .
In the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion, it is revealed that Marika has a son that was hidden away in the Land of Shadow, Messmer the Impaler . Messmer's Kindling also hints that Melina is the younger sister of Messmer, validating that Marika is her mother, and so Queen Marika the Eternal, by extension, is a demigod as well.
At certain sites of grace, Melina can share Marika's spoken echoes with the Tarnished, listed below:

Malenia is daughter to Queen Marika and Radagon, and her Great Rune should have been the most sacred of all.

Marika bathed the village of her home in gold, knowing full well that there was no one to heal.

On his mother's wishes, Messmer made himself a symbol of fear, undertaking the cleansing crusade she desired.
"Direct thy maledictions, thine ire, and thy grief towards me alone."
A braid of golden hair, cut loose. Queen Marika's offering to the Grandmother. Boosts holy damage negation by the utmost. What was her prayer? Her wish, her confession? There is no one left to answer, and Marika never returned home again.
Queen Marika is the vessel of the Elden Ring, carrier of its vision.
A god, in truth. But after the Elden Ring's shattering, she was imprisoned in the Erdtree. A grim punishment for shattering the Order, despite her godhood. The Fingers speak... "Marika's trespass demanded a heavy sentence. But even in shackles, she remains a god, and the vision's vessel. Confer Great Runes to become Elden Lord, and join Queen Marika as her consort. The Fingers have willed it so".
Your divinity, have mercy, and grant me forgiveness. The road is yet long. A God is not easily felled. But one day, without fail, you will have your wish. So please, grant me forgiveness, Queen Marika...
Please, become Elden Lord. Hewg was always saying that you were a Lord, to him. So slay her, with the weapons he smithed. Slay the god, Marika, who cursed us all.
Ahh, I knew you'd come.
To stand before the Elden Ring. To become Elden Lord. What a sad state of affairs. I commend your spirit, but alas, none shall take the throne.
Queen Marika has high hopes for us.
That we continue to struggle. Unto eternity.
Our lands were by thy kind set aflame, our tower by thy kind veiled in shadow. Thou cam'st, robbing us of all, spoiling all. Have ye not basked in these deeds long enough? Listen, progeny of the wanton strumpet. For thy sins thou shalt have thy recompense
O Horn-deck'd beast, from higher sphere deliver'd. Take root inside the tower's sculpted keepers. And perch'd within, we beg of thee; rise.
Dance and cavort, cleanse all that thou wilt. Cruelty, woe, and those who plague the tower. Cleanse away the strumpet's vile progeny.
Mother, wouldst thou truly Lordship sanction, in one so bereft of light?
I will not suffer... a lord devoid of light.
O mother, forgive me. Grah!
A curse...upon thee...
Queen Marika during the breaking of the Elden Ring
Visage of Queen Marika
Let a new epoch begin. An epoch glistening with life. Brandish the Elden Ring, for the Age of the Erdtree!
- Queen Marika the Eternal — as quoted by Melina
Queen Marika the Eternal is the current goddess and  vessel of the Elden Ring in the Lands Between .
1 Overview 1.1 Early Life 1.2 Birth of the Golden Order
1.2 Birth of the Golden Order
2 Age of the Erdtree 2.1 Night of Black Knives 2.2 The Shattering 2.3 Endings
2.1 Night of Black Knives
Marika was a Numen [ 2 ] from the Shaman Village . [ 3 ]
Her people, the Shamans , were used by The Hornsent for the sake of Living Jars : a process in which a Greatjar is put on top of a Shaman. [ 4 ] Those condemned by the Hornsent would also be cut down and their flesh thrown into the jars [ 5 ] , so that they would meld with the shamans [ 6 ] who prayed inside so that the contents could become "righteous people" on their next life. [ 4 ] This created the tortuous existence of Jar Dwellers .
Marika, guided by the Two Fingers , managed to get to the Gate of Divinity , becoming Goddess and Vessel of the Elden Ring . [ 7 ] [ 8 ] This affair led to the birth of the Erdtree and Scadutree , [ 9 ] [ 10 ] and was considered a betrayal by the Hornsent. She then declared a holy war against them, with her son Messmer the Impaler leading the "cleansing" Crusade where the Hornsent would be burnt in body and Soul.
Eventually, Marika returned to the abandoned Shaman Village for the last time. After cutting off a braid of her hair and leaving it as an offering to the Grandmother , she bathed her home in the healing light of gold, knowing full well that there was no one to heal, and would never return. [ 3 ] [ 11 ]
At some point, Marika came into conflict with another Empyrean known as the Gloam-Eyed Queen , who perished at the hand of Maliketh . After emerging victorious, and desiring a world free of Destined Death , Marika removed the Rune of Death from the Elden Ring, [ 12 ] using Maliketh as a vessel to lock it away, [ 13 ] thus establishing the Golden Order . [ 14 ] [ 15 ]
Godfrey was a ferocious warrior who vowed to become a lord. He took the Beast Regent Serosh upon his back to suppress the ceaseless lust for battle that raged within and became King Consort of Marika and first Elden Lord. [ 16 ] [ 17 ] [ 18 ]
In the early days, everything was in opposition to the Erdtree . [ 19 ] With Godfrey’s aid, [ 20 ] Marika waged a War Against the Giants atop the frigid northern mountains , whose Flame of Ruin was capable of burning the Erdtree. [ 21 ] Ultimately victorious, Marika was believed to have slain the Fell God of the Fire Giants . [ 22 ] Upon learning that the Flame of Ruin would never die, however, she cursed the last of the giants to tend to it for eternity. [ 23 ] Marika declared the path to the mountains as Forbidden Lands and excised details of the war from history. [ 24 ]
Marika and Godfrey would rule together and have at least three children: Godwyn the Golden and the twins Morgott and Mohg . The twins were both born as accursed Omen and were hidden in the depths below the capital . Even so, Godfrey and his Golden Lineage would be counted as the first of the demigods. [ 25 ]
With her rule secure, Marika announced her intention to search the depths of the Golden Order, declaring the early days of blind belief a thing long past. [ 26 ] When Godfrey's last foe fell it was said the hue of his eyes had faded. [ 27 ] Marika banished him and his kinfolk from the Lands Between, robbing them of their grace , [ 28 ] thus creating the first Tarnished , all in the hope that they would grow stronger by waging war in lands outside her rule, dying, and then eventually being revived with their guidance of grace returned. [ 29 ]
Marika sent her son Messmer away on a crusade into the lands of the Hornsent. Despite her precautions, her fear of the Abyssal Serpent sealed within him led her to trap him within the Realm of Shadow , where he would wage war to exterminate the Hornsent and devastate their lands. No matter how many of her enemies burned in his flame , however, Messmer received no sign from Marika to end his crusade. To hide away these actions and the true nature of her godhood, Marika hid the Realm of Shadow behind a magical veil to obscure it from public knowledge.
After Godfrey's banishment from the Lands Between, Marika took Radagon as second husband and King Consort, elevating him to become the second Elden Lord. [ 30 ] Radagon already had three children with Queen Rennala of the Carian Royal Family , and after his union with Marika, she would raise her new step-children, Radahn , Rykard and Ranni , to demigod status. [ 31 ]
Marika and Radagon would go on to have two more children of their own, the twins Malenia and Miquella , [ 32 ] who like Marika herself and Radagon's daughter Ranni, were Empyreans, [ 33 ] and were thus potential successors to Marika. However, Radagon and Marika were in truth the same. Radagon was described as Marika's male "other self". [ 34 ] Thus the twins were the children of a single god, and they were both born afflicted: Malenia with rotting sickness and Miquella with eternal childhood. [ 35 ]
On the Night of Black Knives , a fragment of the Rune of Death was stolen from Maliketh. [ 36 ] For a long time it was not known who was behind the plot, [ 37 ] but it is finally revealed it was in fact Ranni who stole the fragment of the rune to forge god-slaying black knives through a fearsome rite. [ 38 ]
The Black Knife Assassins , Numen women purported to have close ties to Marika herself, [ 39 ] used these knives to slay Godwyn the Golden, the first recorded death of a demigod in all history. [ 40 ] Queen Marika is said to have been driven to the brink by this event, [ 41 ] and some time later she shattered the Elden Ring using her hammer . [ 42 ] She had already instructed her demigod children to fight for the positions of Elden Lord and that of godhood. [ 43 ]
Fragments of the ring splintered into Great Runes and spread across the

---

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Twinbird
The Twinbird is a mentioned entity in ELDEN RING (2022) . It is said to be the envoy of an Outer God , and the mother of the Deathbirds .
The Twinbird is scarcely mentioned in the Lands Between . It is portrayed as a two-headed bird with half-red and half-blue or black plumage.
The Twinbird is said to be the envoy of an Outer God, and the mother of the Deathbirds. [ 1 ] Its whereabouts are unknown, and only its offspring being seen throughout the Lands Between. The colour of its plumage may be alluded to by the red and blue feathers on the Red-Feathered Branchsword and Blue-Feathered Branchsword used in ancient Death rituals before the age of the Erdtree. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] [ 4 ]
It has been speculated that the relief portraying two birds in Crumbling Farum Azula may depict the Twinbird.
↑ Twinbird Kite Shield
↑ Red-Feathered Branchsword
↑ Blue-Feathered Branchsword
↑ Explosive Ghostflame

---

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Gloam-Eyed Queen
The Gloam-Eyed Queen cradles newborn apostles swaddled in this cloth. Soon they will grow to become the death of the gods.
- Godskin Swaddling Cloth
The Gloam-Eyed Queen was an Empyrean who was defeated long ago by Maliketh . She was the leader of the Godskin Apostles and wielded the god-slaying power of Destined Death . [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ]
1 Overview 1.1 The Golden Order
1.1 The Golden Order
The Gloam-Eyed Queen was an Empyrean chosen by her own Two Fingers . She led the apostles in their god hunt, [ 3 ] wielded the power of Destined Death and channeling its Black Flames through her Godslayer's Greatsword . [ 1 ] She cradled newborn Godskin Apostles in a swaddling cloth made from patches of supple skin sewn together. [ 4 ] Her apostles developed inhuman physiology and would grow to be the death of the gods. [ 5 ]
Queen Marika desired the removal of the Rune of Death from the Elden Ring to create the Golden Order . [ 6 ] After the Gloam-Eyed Queen and her apostles were defeated at the hands of Maliketh , the Rune of Death was sealed away within his Black Blade and the true power of the Godslayer incantations was lost. [ 7 ] [ 8 ] [ 9 ] The Gloam-Eyed Queen's sword was locked away at the bottom of the Divine Tower of Caelid , and her fate remains unknown.
The Gloam-Eyed Queen's Godskin Swaddling Cloth is guarded by the summoned spirits of Godskin Apostles in Spiritcaller's Cave on the Mountaintops of the Giants .
Maliketh defeated the Gloam-Eyed Queen
The apostles were swaddled in cloth made from skin
The Black Flame was once capable of killing the gods
The apostles could summon a raging vortex of Black Flame [ 10 ]
