area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Nepheli Loux
Nepheli Loux, Warrior is an NPC in Elden Ring . Nepheli Loux is a warrior and fellow Tarnished who was ordered by decree from her adoptive father to kill and dethrone Godrick. She wears clothing similar to the starting Hero class, and she can help you in the battle against Godrick the Grafted if summoned. Her summoning sign is golden and is just outside the boss fog. Other NPC Summon opportunities include boss fights against Godfrey, First Elden Lord and Omenkiller (in Village of the Albinaurics ). Progress in her questline to have her summon sign available for these encounters.
The puppet of Nepheli Loux can be summoned for aid in battle by using the Nepheli Loux Puppet Ashes .
See Nepheli Loux, Warrior Lore
Let the scars I carve remind them.
I am Nepheli Loux. Warrior.
Nepheli Loux can initially be found at Stormveil Castle , inside a room before reaching the Secluded Cell site of grace . [ Map link ]
While outside Roundtable Hold , she can be attacked and drops Stormhawk Axe when defeated.
Her adoptive father is Gideon Ofnir , leader of Roundtable Hold . Her real father might be Godfrey, First Elden Lord .
After defeating Godrick the Grafted and exhausting the initial dialog with Gideon Ofnir at Roundtable Hold, you can find her in the room outside the library.
After exhausting her dialogue at Roundtable Hold, you can find her under the stone bridge leading up to the Village Of The Albinaurics . [ Map link ]
After defeating Omenkiller , Nepheli will return to Roundtable Hold, crouching near the bottom of the stairs next to the blacksmith.
Nepheli questline point of failure: If the player speaks to Melina at the Forge of Giants site of grace (triggering the cutscene with the Roundtable Hold) before finishing Nepheli's dialogue and defeating Omenkiller at the Village of the Albinaurics, then she will disappear for good. Her Stormhawk Axes drop at the village and the questline ends. To avoid this failure, defeat Omenkiller and finish her dialogue before resting at the Forge of Giants.
Nepheli questline point of failure: if you do not give Nepheli the Stormhawk King key item at roundtable hold before you defeat Maliketh, she leaves and her questline cannot be completed. Once you unlock Ashen Capital she disappears from the roundtable hold, and her questline is locked out for the playthrough.
Find Nepheli in the room to the north of the heavily guarded Stormveil Castle courtyard. Exhaust her dialogue to receive her help in fighting Godrick the Grafted .
(Optional) Before entering the gold fog door, you can summon Nepheli using the sign next to it, available whether you’re online or offline.
After defeating Godrick, Nepheli will move to the room outside the library in the Roundtable Hold . Speak with her and exhaust her dialogue. If she does not appear, make sure that you have spoken to Gideon Ofnir and rested at the Godrick the Grafted Site of Grace. She will then reward you with the Arsenal Charm .
Complete Nepheli's previous quest or skip it; you must meet her at the Roundtable Hold at least once. NOTE : If you offer Seluvis's potion to Nepheli during your first meeting at the Roundtable Hold, she will refuse it. After she returns from the Village of the Albinaurics , you can offer it again by speaking to Gideon Ofnir about her despair.
Speak to Preceptor Seluvis to receive the quest. If Seluvis is dead, you can continue with the quest from the next step.
Find Nepheli at the Village of the Albinaurics , exhaust her dialogue, and defeat the elite enemy Omenkiller located across the bridge above her.
Return to the Roundtable Hold and speak to Nepheli downstairs, next to the first fog wall.
Speak to Gideon Ofnir about Nepheli. If you have the potion, he will offer you a choice: Give him the potion to dispose of it. Give the potion to Nepheli, which will make her unresponsive and she will leave. She will then be found in Seluvis's secret room, turned into a puppet. Alternatively, give the potion to Dung Eater in the Roundtable Hold after obtaining a Seedbed Curse . This will allow you to obtain the Dung Eater Puppet summon but will end Dung Eater's questline and prevent the Blessing of Despair ending. Return to Preceptor Seluvis to complete his portion of the quest.
Give him the potion to dispose of it.

If you do not have the potion, the quest progresses as follows: Nepheli does not take the potion. This can happen if Seluvis is dead and you’ve already found Nepheli at the Albinauric Village and defeated the Omenkiller , or if you never received the quest from Seluvis.

To progress the quest, you need to give Nepheli The Stormhawk King ashes found in the Chapel of Anticipation , provided she did not take Seluvis’s Potion . NOTE : Make sure that Roderika’s questline is advanced to the point where she can upgrade spirit ashes before giving Nepheli the Stormhawk King ashes.
(Optional) After completing the quest above by not giving Nepheli Seluvis's Potion , you may progress Kenneth Haight's quest to the point where he's in Fort Haight saying he has to look for a fitting lord to rule Limgrave.
After defeating Morgott and (optionally) talking to Gideon about helping Nepheli get back on her feet, you can find Nepheli, Kenneth, and Gostoc in the Stormveil Castle throne room near the Godrick the Grafted site of grace. If they do not appear, spawn from the Godrick the Grafted site of grace and rest at the Secluded Cell site of grace (do not merely teleport to it), which may fix the issue. (If this doesn't work, try the same in reverse: travel to Secluded Cell and rest there, then run to Godrick and rest again there. Also, you can try going back to Fort Haight, where Kenneth Haight usually stands, which might trigger him and Nepheli to move to the throne room.) The quest will still advance if either Kenneth or Gostoc are dead. They are not required for Nepheli to move to the throne room. Warning : Killing Gideon prevents this dialogue and results in Nepheli dying where she is at the time, leaving her items behind. If all requirements have been met and the NPCs have still not moved to Godrick's throne room, rest at the Godrick the Grafted site of grace. If none of the above works, you may still need to talk to Nepheli downstairs at Roundtable hold and give her the Stormhawk King . If resting at the Godrick the Grafted site of grace does not work, try repeatedly passing time. If you are still stuck, you might not have progressed far enough into game and need to kill Morgott The Omen King at Leyndell Royal Capital . If the issue persists, try to get Godrick's Great Rune , then proceed to remove and equip it. Rewards : Nepheli will give the player an Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone at the end of her questline. She can also be summoned for the fight with Godfrey, First Elden Lord in Leyndell, Ashen Capital . An additional Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone will be sold by Gatekeeper Gostoc for 20,000 Runes . Depending on quest progression, Nepheli can be summoned for the fights against Godrick the Grafted , Omenkiller , and Godfrey, First Elden Lord .
In combat, she wields a pair of Stormhawk Axes .
Headband reserved for the badlands' bravest. Proof that the wearer has slaughtered countless foes.
Following the example of their chieftain Hoarah Loux, the brave warriors of the badlands shun excess adornment.
Battle axe designed to resemble a hawk, with its wings comprising the blade. Signature weapon of warriors who strive to remain one with the storm, despite being so far from their place of birth.
Their hearts are proud, and thereby easily undone.
Ashes of a hawk revered by all others as sovereign back in the days when Stormveil's winds still raged like no other.
This ancient monarch is proud however, refusing to answer anyone's summons.
A talisman depicting an axe and a warrior. Enhances charge attacks.
The Lord who led the Long March bore an axe, and his loyal warriors honored him by wielding axes of their own, making them very effective at dealing decisive blows.
Ashen remains in which spirits yet dwell. Use to summon the spirit of a warhawk.
Spirit of a Stormveil warhawk, the talons of which have been sliced off so that razor-fine swords could be grafted in their place. With its lord vanquished, and its wings wounded, the hawk perished as it solemnly gazed at its former home. The storm is a warhawk's cradle.
One of Seluvis's favorite puppets. Use to summon the spirit of Nepheli Loux.
Puppet of a warrior who was betrayed by her pure heart. Wields a Stormhawk Axe in both hands and uses the Thunderstorm skill.
A diamond in the rough, deserving of special care lest its potential be squandered. Nepheli Loux truly was a warrior.

She's not herself right now, and though I have no need for her, she still has potential. Certainly more value than she'd have as a bloody puppet.

- Nepheli Loux, Warrior
Nepheli Loux, Warrior is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Nepheli Loux is a Tarnished warrior [ 1 ] from the Badlands . In her infancy, Nepheli lived through murder and pillage unchecked [ 2 ] At some point, she lost the guidance of grace, and was taken in by Gideon Ofnir , who became her foster father. [ 3 ] Gideon promised Nepheli that if he became Elden Lord he would never allow the downtrodden to be cheated again [ 4 ] , earning her loyalty and aid in his pursuit of the title of Elden Lord. [ 5 ]
At the behest of Gideon, Nepheli infiltrates Stormveil Castle [ 6 ] , the home of the demigod Godrick the Grafted . Here, she encounters a Tarnished , who aids her in defeating the decrepit demigod. With Godrick slain, she returns to Roundtable Hold , where she awards them with an Arsenal Charm she found in Stormveil, and encourages them to speak to her foster father.
Travelling to Liurnia of the Lakes , Nepheli comes upon the Village of the Albinaurics , where she witnesses the slaughter of the Albinauric inhabitants at the hands of her father's men. [ 7 ] Reminded of the brutality she witnessed as a child, Nepheli vows to deliver justice to the oppressors [ 8 ] , assisting the Tarnished in defeating the Omenkiller responsible for the slaughter.
For giving into her emotions and abandoning the mission, Nepheli is cast aside by Gideon. [ 9 ] Without her estranged father's guidance, she becomes crestfallen, resigning to the lower levels of Roundtable Hold where she questions the faith she had in Gideon. [ 10 ]
The Tarnished obtains the ashes of The Stormhawk King in the Chapel of Anticipation , which they may choose to give to Nepheli. She can smell the ancient storm within the ash [ 11 ] , and is reminded of her first hawk. [ 12 ] Despite her inability to sense spirits within ash [ 13 ] , she gladly receives them.
After claiming the Stormhawk King's ashes, Nepheli encounters Kenneth Haight , who recognises her as a worthy heir of Limgrave . [ 14 ] Nepheli accepts the role, having been reminded by Limgrave of her own home. [ 15 ] As the new lord of Limgrave, Nepheli pledges to aid the Tarnished should they become Elden Lord [ 16 ] , pledging to call upon the storm to drive away the foulness that has settled on the winds. [ 17 ]
NOTE : as Gatekeeper Gostoc 's questline is intertwined with Nepheli's, hers will fail is he's killed and if aggroed you'll have to cleanse your sin at the Church of Vows via Celestial Dew .

Otherwise, just entering Liurnia of the Lakes is enough to trigger this passage and, while the Spirit Jellyfish Ashes will be lost, the Golden Seed you'd have received at the Hold can instead be found in her spot at the shack.

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Kenneth Haight
Kenneth Haight is an NPC in Elden Ring . Kenneth Haight claims to be the next ruler of Limgrave, you will encounter him as you make your way down the ledges at Limgrave . Haight will ask for your help to clean and clear out Fort Haight that's currently being overrun by different enemies.
Hello? Is anybody there? Someone who might be interested in rescuing the great Kenneth Haight? Servant to the true Order, and celebrated repudiator of the false! O Erdtree! Grant me succour!
Kenneth Haight can be found in north-east Limgrave , atop the ruins acting as an improvised bridge covering the roadway between the Mistwood Outskirts and Third Church of Marika sites of grace. [ Map Link ]
Kenneth will move to Fort Haight upon clearing the fort and returning to speak with him atop the ruins in Limgrave. [ Map Link ]
Kenneth can be found atop the battlements of Fort Haight after it has been overrun by Demi-Humans , where he will lament the state of the fort and ask the player to find a lord to manage the fort for him.
Kenneth will move the Stormveil Castle Throne Room north-west of the Godrick the Grafted site of grace after the following has been completed: Clearing Fort Haight and speaking to Kenneth atop the battlements. Giving Nepheli Loux the Stormhawk King key item and not giving her Seluvis's Potion , Defeating Morgott the Omen King atop the Leyndell Royal Capital and then speaking to him after resting at the grace (he's behind the throne). Resting at Godrick the Grafted 's site of grace, then going to the throne room.

Kenneth Haight can be found in the north-east area of Limgrave atop the ruins covering the roadway between the Mistwood Outskirts and Third Church of Marika sites of grace.
Kenneth asks for your help to clear out Fort Haight , which has been overtaken by a Knight commander from Stormveil, promising a reward given that, according to him, he is "known for his considerable largesse" .
Defeat the Godrick Knight atop the battlements at Fort Haight to receive the Ash of War: Bloody Slash .
After clearing out the enemies at Fort Haight, return to Kenneth atop the ruins in north-east Limgrave to be rewarded with the Erdsteel Dagger . Speak to Kenneth again and he will ask you to to pledge your service to him. Should you accept, he exclaims that a knighting ceremony will be held for you at Fort Haight. Note: the player must rest at the Fort Haight West site of grace in order for him ask to ask the player to pledge their service. After receiving the Erdsteel Dagger from him, you can safely kill him for an early Golden Seed if you wish as he has no significant effect on any other quests and grants no further quest rewards.

Upon resting at a site of grace or fast traveling Kenneth will move to Fort Haight atop the battlements where the Godrick Knight was located.
Upon arriving at Fort Haight, which is now overrun by Demi-Humans , speak with Kenneth where he explains that he is not yet able to anoint you as a knight, and that he must find a true and stalwart Lord worthy of Limgrave.
After defeating Morgott the Omen King , giving Nepheli Loux the Stormhawk King key item (and not Seluvis's Potion ), and resting at the Godrick the Grafted site of grace, Kenneth, Gatekeeper Gostoc , and Nepheli Loux will move to the Stormveil Castle Throne Room, where he will tell you that he will eventually return to his fort to hail you as knight. He will never actually do this however, and his presence is not required for Nepheli to move to the throne room, it is possible for Nepheli to move there by herself.

"Right then. Time for me to head to the fort. I've much to do. First I'll have to reestablish communication with the demi-humans. What's that look? You don't believe me? Well, under the Erdtree, commingling with the demi-humans is made possible. Even the vulgar shall not be left behind, under the rule of true Order. Which is why I, Kenneth Haight, next in line as the rightful ruler of Limgrave, have sworn to uphold it. Just you watch, my friend. Just you watch."
"Ah, yes. I've been meaning to ask. Would you like to enter my service? I see bright things in your future, stout warrior. Enter into my service and learn the workings of the Erdtree's true Order. And who knows, perhaps some time down the line, the grace of gold will return to those Tarnished eyes of yours. What say you? A fine accord, is it not?"
"Very well. Very well, indeed. I knew I saw something in you. I shall await you at my fort. We shall hold the ceremony for your knighting there, I think. I see great things ahead of us. We are truly by the Erdtree blessed."
"Ah, you, is it? I suppose you must have seen by now, yes, the sorry state of my fort? Oh, indeed, it is a foul fate for a land, to be without a ruler. One must be found, with haste. And not that Awful Godrick. But a true and stalwart Lord of the proper lineage to take the reigns of Limgrave. I am sorry for have lifted your hopes... But I haven't the authority to raise you to the knighthood as things stand. The Great Kenneth Haight issues his sincerest apology. But now, I must begin my search post-haste. For a true and stalwart Lord of the proper lineage to take the reigns of Limgrave."
At Stormveil Castle throne room (after Nepheli Loux has been given Stormhawk King)
Ah, nice to see you after so long. Safe and sound, I take it, yes? Good. Ah, quite. I have indeed selected a new ruler. Lady Nepheli is strong and just. Worthy of the burden of Limgrave's lineage. Such is the sincere opinion of I, Kenneth Haight, no less. For the time being, I share command of the castle with my Lady, but I plan on returning to my fort afterwards. At which time I can fulfil my old promise. By raising you to the glory of knighthood. I hope it is just as wonderful as you imagine.
Drops a Golden Seed upon defeat.
Mentions re-establishing communication with the demi-humans. The ghost outside the fort also mentions a dead mother demi-human, which is present on the ground inside the fort.
In combat, Kenneth wields an Erdsteel Dagger and a sacred seal, which he uses to cast Wrath of Gold and Order's Blade .
Kenneth wears Ruler's Robes and Consort's Trousers .
Luxurious robe worn by lords in a smaller nation.
Its fine blue mantle serves to prove its wearer's status.
An erdsteel dagger with a grass crest engraved upon its blade. Carried by the Erdtree royalty for self-defense in times of peace.
Though forged to a high standard, the weapon is difficult to wield. Attack power also scales with faith.
Left half of a split medallion depicting the Erdtree.
Brandishing the medallion with both halves conjoined will activate the Grand Life of Dectus, connecting the Altus Plateau to Liurnia.
The right half is said to reside in Fort Faroth in the Dragonbarrow, far to the east.
Kenneth Haight is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) . He claims to be the next ruler of Limgrave but is in need of assistance as his fort has fallen.
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Kenneth Haight is a nobleman and claims to be next in line as the rightful ruler of Limgrave . He resided in a fort known as Fort Haight , and is known for his considerable largesse, at least by his own account. Godrick the Grafted assumed control of Limgrave following the Shattering , and at some point sent a knight commander to seize Fort Haight.
Driven out of his home, Kenneth began searching for adventurers who might help him restore control of his fort. He was stumbled upon by a Tarnished champion , and despite some reticence in accepting the help of a Tarnished, bid them to retake his fort in exchange for a handsome reward, while also sharing with them his exceedingly low opinion of Godrick.
Upon the Tarnished's success, a joyous Kenneth Haight grants them a reward and thanks them profusely. In addition, he offers them the opportunity to be knighted and enter into his service. He tells them to meet him as his fort, where the ceremony shall be held.
However, upon seeing the sorry state of his fort, Kenneth becomes maudlin. Believing that Limgrave cannot remain without a proper ruler, he dedicates himself to finding a true and stalwart Lord of the proper lineage as soon as possible. He apologizes to the Tarnished, telling them he now lacks the authority to lift them to knighthood.
After the death of Godrick, Kenneth eventually encounters a worthy lord; Nepheli Loux . Kenneth joins her in the throne room of Stormveil Castle to aid her in assuming the reins of the castle. When the Tarnished who had once aided him comes to visit, Kenneth tells him he intends to eventually return to his fort, where he can fulfill his old promise and raise them to knighthood at last.
Limgrave (near Third Church of Marika) Speak to Kenneth atop the ruin west of the Third Church of Marika and agree to clear his fort. Can also be found by heading east down the cliffs at the Artist's Shack in Limgrave, or north from the Mistwood Outskirts Site of Grace.
Speak to Kenneth atop the ruin west of the Third Church of Marika and agree to clear his fort. Can also be found by heading east down the cliffs at the Artist's Shack in Limgrave, or north from the Mistwood Outskirts Site of Grace.
Can also be found by heading east down the cliffs at the Artist's Shack in Limgrave, or north from the Mistwood Outskirts Site of Grace.

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Gostoc
Gatekeeper Gostoc is an NPC in Elden Ring . Gatekeeper Gostoc is an old tall fellow that may seem a bit suspicious, but he seems to want to help. It is your choice to trust him or not. If  you die within Stormveil Castle, he will steal 30% of any runes dropped when dying. However, the area outside of the castle where the Warhawks are at is safe from rune-theft if you die there. He also stops stealing your runes once he opens his shop.
Gatekeeper Gostoc can be found by the entrance of Stormveil Castle , in the room next to the main gate into the castle. [ Map Link ]
After defeating Godrick , Gostoc moves to the Godrick the Grafted Site of Grace, stomping on his remains.
If defeated, drops: Gostoc's Bell Bearing Silver Pickled Fowl Foot Glass Shard
Gostoc's Bell Bearing
Silver Pickled Fowl Foot
Gatekeeper Gostoc advises you to avoid entering the castle using the main gate because he says it's tightly guarded. He suggests you to try the opening through the other way, claiming that guards don't know about that entrance. You will be given the choice to accept or reject his suggestion. If you accept the offer, he will wish you luck and send you on your way to the side path. If you reject the offer, he will seemingly respect your decision and open the gate for you.
If you accept the offer, he will wish you luck and send you on your way to the side path.
If you reject the offer, he will seemingly respect your decision and open the gate for you.
Once you have obtained the Rusty Key or defeated Godrick , or catch him at one the 3 locations stalking you, he will start to provide merchant services.
Gostoc at first may seem to want to help the player, although in a suspicious manner. This is however just a facade. He will actually steal 30% of your runes every time you die in the castle, even if you enter it through the main gate.
Note : In the following locations listed for Gostoc, you cannot see him at all three . The first and last locations are mutually exclusive, so if you see him at the first position, he will not appear at the third, and vice versa.
After passing through specific trigger points, Gostoc will appear in certain locations behind the player. He will give you a Grace Mimic the first time you speak to him at one of these locations. He will not appear at them if you already opened the main gate of the castle. Otherwise, Gostoc can be found at these locations: Ruined Watchtower, behind the Stormveil Cliffside site of grace and must walk up the side path and into the room with 2 servants sitting on the ground for him to appear here. He appears on the cliff overlooking the Stormveil Cliffside site of grace. Inside the rampart tower. After you exit out to the ramparts with the stormhawks carrying explosive barrels, go to the balcony area along the ramparts and drop down to the ledge. Keep dropping down till you find a teardrop scarab and fight a crucible knight guarding a lift. When you take the lift back up, Gostoc will be near the lift examining a corpse. Alternatively, going out to the ramparts with the stormhawks and crossing the first ledge may cause him to spawn at the corpse near the lift. This is dependent on you killing the stormhawks, therfore, the reason he shows up is you make the path safe for him to go down. On the castle chapel roof, above Sorcerer Rogier . Go to the wooden balcony above the grafted scion and double back outside to the long path with the banished knight and torchpole soldier, Gostoc will be on the roof overlooking the path. He will not appear here if you have already spoken to him at any of the previous locations.

When you enter a dark room containing the Rusty Key , the door will close behind you and you can hear Gostoc's laugh. This will leave you alone with a dangerous Banished Knight . If you kill Gostoc beforehand, you won't be locked into the room containing the Rusty Key.

After defeating Godrick, Gostoc will move to the boss room and begin stomping on the corpse, cursing Godrick for treating him badly. As of 1.04 he continues to be a merchant.
Killing Gostoc will not lock you out of Nepheli Loux and Kenneth Haight 's quest as it can still be completed without him, but you will miss out on the Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone and won't be able to buy it from the Twin Maiden Husks even if you gave them his bell bearing.
Once Nepheli Loux has been given The Stormhawk King , Kenneth Haight has regained control of his fort and Morgott has been defeated then Gostoc and the other two NPCs will move the Godrick's Throne Room. At this point Gostoc re-opens his shop and has a new item: an Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone .
Gatekeeper Gostoc will open his store after you obtained the Rusty Key from Stormveil Castle .

Gostoc being found hiding at difficult to access locations around the castle suggests he is stalking you to collect your runes after you die. He also presumably collects his goods from corpses in the fortress.
Cut dialogue suggested that he is the son of Godrick, and that there was an option to crown him as Ruler of Stormveil, instead of Nepheli . Should he received the crown in his cut questline, he would follow Godrick's footsteps and begin grafting himself.
Killing the Banished Knight in the dark room before being locked in by Gostoc will result in him not closing the door behind you once you do enter, although you will still hear him laughing behind you.
Gatekeeper Gostoc is missing his left hand. Removal of one or both hands was a historical punishment for stealing. His arm may have also been taken for Grafting.
The graveyard after the fight with Godrick counts as castle grounds and he will steal your runes here as well.
One of few NPC's that is immune to grab from Golden Crux ( Greatsword of Damnation ).
Garb with a thick embroidered cape attached. Standard wear for commoners of the Lands Between.
The board hung from the neck depicts a sprawling tree, its roots and branches forming two holes. This is a self-imposed shackle, a voluntary display of allegiance to the Erdtree that increases faith.
A standard dagger with a straight blade.
Though modest in reach and capacity for harm, this weapon is light enough to jab in rapid succession and delivers devastating critical hits.
Four-toed foot of a fowl, pickled in a silvery medicinal solution. Craftable item.
Temporarily boosts item discovery.
Since old times, the needy would scrape the meat clean even from a fowl's claw.
Now, I suppose I'm free. I can do whatever takes my fancy... Can't I, mate?
Gatekeeper Gostoc is an NPC and merchant in ELDEN RING (2022) .
3 Character Information 3.1 Basic Info 3.2 Quest Items
4 Questline Progression
Gostoc is a servant working in Stormveil Castle , in the role of gatekeeper. Gostoc detests his master, Godrick the Grafted , who always looked down on and mistreated him. [ 1 ] Gostoc prowls the castle, looting from the corpses of the Tarnished who die in their hunt for Godrick. [ 2 ] When he encounters the Tarnished , he offers to signal the gates to open for them, but also suggests a hidden path around the outer wall of the castle which will allow the Tarnished to get in undetected.
However, Gostoc secretly planned for the Tarnished to die, so that he could loot their corpse. He stalks the Tarnished through the castle, stealing their left behind Runes any time they die. He also locks them in a storeroom with a Banished Knight , seemingly intending for them to perish.
After the Tarnished slays Godrick, Gostoc can be found stomping on his corpse, cursing him for the abuse levelled at him. Later, when Nepheli Loux becomes lord of Limgrave , Gostoc quickly becomes her attendant, giving him free reign to continue looting as he pleases. [ 3 ]
Stormveil Castle Gostoc can be found in the gatehouse to the left of the main gate. He will offer you the choice of taking the main gate or using the side entrance. Choosing "No, I'll use the main entrance" will cause the main gate to open when you approach it. This will not prevent you from taking the side entrance, however.
Gostoc can be found in the gatehouse to the left of the main gate. He will offer you the choice of taking the main gate or using the side entrance. Choosing "No, I'll use the main entrance" will cause the main gate to open when you approach it. This will not prevent you from taking the side entrance, however.
Stormveil Castle (Optional) Upon entering a dark room in the western section of the castle, Gostoc will close the door, locking you in with a Banished Knight enemy. To leave, grab the Rusty Key and return to the door to open it. If you die in this room, you can return to it and loot the item without being locked in again. (Optional) Gostoc will now appear in several locations around the dungeon. Speaking to him in one of these locations for the first time will reward you with a Grace Mimic. The locations are as follows: Stormveil Cliffside. He will appear on a ledge above the Site of Grace, only if you enter the castle via the wooden scaffold and head straight back. Western Wall ground floor. He will appear here after you have gone up the ladder past the door unlocked with the Rusty Key. Chapel roof. He will appear on the roof of the chapel where you meet Rogier, after visiting the roof with the Grafted Scion and returning. Rampart Tower. He will appear in the elevator tower, on a wooden platform that needs to be jumped to. While he is in any of these locations, he will take 30% of the Runes you drop if you die in the dungeon. In order to get him to stop, talk to him at any one of the first three listed locations, and then at the fourth location. Then, return to his location at the gatehouse and talk to him again. He will no longer steal any Runes. Additionally, he will become available as a merchant if you return to him after he begins stalking you.

To leave, grab the Rusty Key and return to the door to open it. If you die in this room, you can return to it and loot the item without being locked in again.

He will appear on the roof of the chapel where you meet Rogier, after visiting the roof with the Grafted Scion and returning. Rampart Tower. He will appear in the elevator tower, on a wooden platform that needs to be jumped to.

Stormveil Castle (Godrick's arena) After defeating Godrick the Grafted, Gostoc will appear in the room. After exhausting his dialogue his shop will be open once again. He will also stop stealing your Runes if you skipped the previous part.

---

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Sorceress Sellen
Sorceress Sellen is a Merchant and NPC in Elden Ring . She can be found inside the Waypoint Ruins , far east from where you first arrive in Limgrave . She can teach you Sorceries . She can also be made available as an NPC Summon for specific battles. As part of her quest she wishes to restore the primeval current of glintstone sorcery to Raya Lucaria .
See Sorceress Sellen Lore
Tarnished, are we? A wonder you should turn up here. I am Sellen, a sorcerer, quite plainly."
She is found standing near a treasure chest within the Waypoint Ruins in the central-east part of Limgrave . Players can reach her after defeating the Mad Pumpkin Head . After defeating the boss, the Site of Grace "Waypoint Ruins Cellar" appears in their boss room, allowing easy access to Sellen's services. Map Link for Waypoint Ruins.
Sorceress Sellen can also be found kept prisoner at Witchbane Ruins , however, other than speaking to her, nothing can be done with her here until progressing far enough into her quest line. Be warned, attacking Sellen here can cause issues with her availability as a merchant elsewhere and stall progression in her quest line. This can be resolved by seeking absolution at the Church of Vows . Map Link for Witchbane Ruins.
She can be summoned to aid in the Red Wolf of Radagon boss fight, after progressing her quest line to the point that she leaves for the Raya Lucaria Academy .
Once the player has progressed her quest line far enough, two new summon signs will appear at the entrance to the Raya Lucaria Grand Library, offering the player a choice between assisting her (gold sign) or challenging her (red sign).
You can choose among the following options while talking to Sorceress Sellen :
Study sorcery: Learn a sorcery from Sellen in exchange for Runes.
Give a scroll: Give Sellen a sorcery scroll key item to add the sorceries contained within it to Sellen's merchant inventory.
Give letter of introduction: After receiving the Letter of Introduction from Preceptor Seluvis , you can then speak with Sellen about it. She will inform you that General Radahn must be defeated in order to restore the movement of the stars, which will reveal a way to access the lost city of Nokron.
You first encounter Sellen at the Waypoint Ruins after defeating the Mad Pumpkin Head guarding her room. She introduces herself as the Graven Witch. If you accept her offer to study sorceries, you can begin her questline. Learning enough sorceries from her will unlock additional dialogue.
( Optional ) You can progress Ranni's questline until you are asked to find Blaidd in Siofra River . He will direct you to question Seluvis , who sends you to Sellen for information.
To obtain the Comet Azur sorcery, find Primeval Sorcerer Azur north of Hermit Village on Mt. Gelmir. You will need to circle around the volcano to reach him. [ Map Link ]
Go back and speak with Sellen. She will give you a special glintstone key and task you with finding the sorcerer Master Lusat .
(Note: the following step can be completed now or skipped and left until the very end of the questline). Travel to Caelid and find the secret cave Sellia Hideaway , hidden behind an invisible wall. [ Map Link ]. Deep inside the Hideaway, at the bottom of a chasm filled with crystal ledges, you will encounter a glintstone barrier blocking access to a very small cave. Interact with the barrier to open it using Sellen's key. Inside you will find Master Lusat. Interact with him to learn the spell Stars of Ruin . Return to Sellen and she will reward you with a Starlight Shard.
After defeating Starscourge Radahn , return to Sellen . She will ask you to meet her at the Witchbane Ruins , where she will give you Sellen's Primal Glintstone .
If you haven't done so yet, travel back to Redmane Castle and speak with Witch-Hunter Jerren in the throne room. Exhaust his dialogue, then reset the area by resting at any Site of Grace. This will restore the castle to its enemy-filled state, as it was before the Radahn Festival.
Next, return to Witchbane Ruins to find Jerren standing over Sellen's now-deceased body. Speak with him again and exhaust his dialogue.
Journey to the Three Sisters area and locate the ruins on the right side of the approach up to Ranni's Rise. You must locate an illusory floor that must be struck to reveal. The location of the illusory floor is shown below.
In the secret basement of dolls, look for an illusory wall to the left of the glowing message that says "Seluvis's puppet. Do not touch." Behind the wall is a room with a doll duplicate of Sellen. Interact with it to insert the Primal Glinstone. After reviving Sellen, she will resume selling her sorceries here until Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon , is defeated.
After completing the previous steps and defeating Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon, fast travel to the Raya Lucaria Grand Library Site of Grace. Right outside the entrance should be two summon signs. The gold summon sign offers the option to assist Sellen, where you will need to aid her in fighting Witch Hunter Jerren: Defeating Witch-Hunter Jerren will reward you with the Eccentric Set . You can then revisit the spots where you first encountered Master Azur and Master Lusat to find their armor sets ( Azur's Glintstone Set and Lusat's set , respectively) laying on the ground where they once were. It is important to note that Lusat's Set can only be acquired after Sellen transforms. After the fight, you can find Sellen standing inside the Grand Library where Renalla once was, with Renalla sadly sitting off to the side of the room. Speak with Sellen and exhaust her dialogue to receive the Glintstone Kris and have the sorcery Shard Spiral added to Sellen's merchant inventory. Sellen will remain where she stands in the Grand Library until you have completed the part of her quest line to find Master Lusat. Once that part of her quest line has been completed, reset the area. You will find that Sellen will have moved to the side of the room and transformed into a School of Graven Mages (beside of which can be found her Witch's Glintstone Crown ) and Rennala will have returned to her original position in the center of the room. Despite Sellen's "condition," you can still purchase sorceries from her at any time by speaking with her.
The gold summon sign offers the option to assist Sellen, where you will need to aid her in fighting Witch Hunter Jerren: Defeating Witch-Hunter Jerren will reward you with the Eccentric Set . You can then revisit the spots where you first encountered Master Azur and Master Lusat to find their armor sets ( Azur's Glintstone Set and Lusat's set , respectively) laying on the ground where they once were. It is important to note that Lusat's Set can only be acquired after Sellen transforms. Despite Sellen's "condition," you can still purchase sorceries from her at any time by speaking with her.

Despite Sellen's "condition," you can still purchase sorceries from her at any time by speaking with her.
The red summon sign offers the option to challenge Sellen, where you will need to aid Witch Hunter Jerren in defeating Sellen: Defeating Sellen will reward you with the Witch's Glintstone Crown and Sellen's Bell Bearing . Jerren can be found afterwards, standing just outside the entrance to the Grand Library. Speak to him and he will reward you with an Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone . At this point, Jerren can still be killed in order to obtain the his armor set, the Eccentric Set. If you choose this option and kill Sellen, you will not be able to get the Lusat Set and Azur Set until your next game cycle.
Defeating Sellen will reward you with the Witch's Glintstone Crown and Sellen's Bell Bearing .
Jerren can be found afterwards, standing just outside the entrance to the Grand Library. Speak to him and he will reward you with an Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone .
At this point, Jerren can still be killed in order to obtain the his armor set, the Eccentric Set.
If you choose this option and kill Sellen, you will not be able to get the Lusat Set and Azur Set until your next game cycle.
Tarnished, are we? A wonder you should turn up here. I am Sellen, a sorcerer, quite plainly. Why are you here?
What's the meaning of this? How unfortunate. <disappears>
You sadden me, my apprentice.
(On player kill) For a time, I believed us kindred spirits. My apprentice...
I want to learn sorcery (Intelligence >10)
Ahh, a yen for glintstone sorceries? Well, your aptitude does appear...passable. But one must choose one's masters wisely. I was exiled from the Academy of Raya Lucaria, as a reviled, apostate witch. Do you still wish to learn from me?
I want to learn sorcery (Intelligence <10)
Ahh, a yen for glintstone sorceries? I dare say your proclivities are far from ideal… Oh well, perhaps nurture will defy nature, with a bit of luck. But one must choose one's masters wisely. I was exiled from the Academy of Raya Lucaria, as a reviled, apostate witch. Do you still wish to learn from me?
I still want to learn
Well, you are a piece of work. Very well. You are now my protégé in glintstone sorcery. But I refuse to coddle. Or cast kind words. Never. Anticipate grievances, young apprentice. [Nod in Thought Gesture received]
Young apprentice, first things first. You are a tadpole when it comes to magic. Infantile. Without the legs to walk. So, to become a sorcerer, first, you must face your ignorance. Now, shall we start learning?
Giving her a sorcery Scroll
Oh, is that a scroll? Takes me back to my academy days. Very well. This is sorcery with which I'm not entirely unfamiliar. I'll be sure to incorporate it into one of your lessons. Never lose that inquisitive spirit, my apprentice.
About glintstone sorcery
Your appetite for learning is impressive. You make me proud. To have grown little legs so readily. You must have a fabulous teacher.
While imprisoned at Witchbane Ruins
It will do no good, I swear it...and yet you persist, you frothing degenerates...A...argh!
Seluvis' Letter of Introduction
Ah, then you have seen Master Azur. Master Azur was a founding glintstone sorcerer, and my first teacher. A stern judge of men, but he must have seen something in you. You make us both proud. We can speak more later, my apprentice.
§ Tell me your story
If you recall, I was exiled from the Academy of Raya Lucaria. It was for attempting to restore the primeval current of glintstone sorcery. The toothless pedantry peddled by the Carian royal family can rot for all I care. I want glintstone sorceries that open our minds, unbound by terrestrial taboos. No matter what we give in return. My apprentice. I presume nothing. Teacher and student are not bound to tread the same path. But hear me out, my apprentice. I need your help to restore the primeval current of glintstone sorcery. Perhaps this is a journey we could take together?
(Let's journey together) Splendid. I thank you. This pleases me. It's been far too long since I found a fellow kindred spirit. You must have a fabulous teacher.
I have a favour to ask
I need your help, my apprentice. Master Lusat is another founding glintstone sorcerer. And like Master Azur, he was banished from the academy. Now he languishes, imprisoned somewhere. My apprentice, can you find Master Lusat? With this glintstone key, you should be able to cross the boundary that encloses him.
I need him to restore the primeval current of glintstone sorcery. He's nigh a child of the stars, such is his body now...
After his expulsion from the academy, I heard that Master Lusat returned to his home. A place called Sellia, in the eastern Caelid Wilds. Thinking about it, I obtained the glintstone key I gave you from a Sellian sorcerer. It wouldn't be too much of a leap to suppose he's still cooped up nearby.
After meeting Master Lusat
My apprentice. Give me a moment later. I have a favour to ask.
Who could've guessed. What a place to find Master Lusat...You have my gratitude. This is all your work. I am truly pleased to have you as an apprentice, and a kindred spirit. This is a mere token of my thanks. Please, take it.
This brings us a full step closer. The bodies of Masters Azur and Lusat can be admitted to the academy. If only I could undo these wretched shackles...
The form you see before you is merely a projection. My body lies elsewhere. But the academy's shackles prevent it being moved. My body is on the Weeping Peninsula, at the southern tip of the Lands Between, imprisoned in the ruins just down from the Church of Marika, past the plain of the Wandering Mausoleum. Can you make the journey to my body? I have something that I can only trust with you.
At Witchbane Ruins (after hearing about her request)
My apprentice, thank you for coming. These shackles take a toll on us all. There is something I need you to look after. My primal glintstone. A star has fallen, and my fortunes waver. someone may come for my life. And so, I entrust it with you. My self.
Thank you...my apprentice... This...is my essence. Please, treat it with care.
Treat it with care, my apprentice... What you hold is my very being...
Using Sellen's Primal Glintstone in the secret room at the Three Sisters §
My apprentice, just how long has it been? Thank you. You've helped me fill a new body, once again. And it's truly a gem. Young and full of vigor, a snug fit for my Primal Glintstone. Better still, I've shed those awful shackles. Finally, I can return to the academy. To expel the Carian royal family, and restore the primeval current. My dear apprentice, I owe this all to you.
Jerren, bringer of my death. You have my gratitude. For freeing me from my shackles. But I am afraid your work is done. Join the school. To reflect on your mistake.
After defeating Jerren
Ahh, my apprentice. You've saved my skin once again. Do you see this? The Queen of Caria is no more. With the bodies of Masters Azur and Lusat returned, the academy can hone the primeval current. So that we, fallen children of the stars, shall beam with brilliance once again. My apprentice. Will you stay with us, here at the academy? Oh, I know it's not possible. You have your own calling. To be the next Elden Lord. But do think of me. Of your teacher. On the eve of your crowning. You will always be my darling pupil. Rest assured that I... No, the entire academy, will swear allegiance to the new monarch. My apprentice. Become Elden Lord.
Hmm. Perhaps I'm jumping ahead, but here is a symbol of my allegiance, and the academy's. (gives Glintstone Kris ) Do you recall what I once told you? That glintstone is the amber of the cosmos, and sorcery is the study of the stars, and the life therein. When you become Elden Lord, please illuminate me. Lay bare the secrets of life which course the Elden Ring. Next time, I will be your student.
Oh, one last thing. If you fail to claim your throne, you can always pay me a visit. Oh, don't fret. Even my dullest pupils will always have a place here.
About Lusat (if he is not found yet)
It warms my heart. That you still think of me. Yet I still yearn, for you to find Master Lusat. His body is needed. At the academy, to hone the primeval current...
Talking after reloading the area
U...urgh. M-my...apprentice...I-I'm...
(Subsequent Visits) There you are, my apprentice. Shall we commence the lesson? .
(Talking again without reloading) You wish to continue the lesson? Very well, I have time aplenty.
You can give Sellen the Royal House Scroll to add the sorceries Glintblade Phalanx and Carian Slicer to her merchant inventory.
You can give Sellen the Academy Scroll to add the sorceries Great Glintstone Shard and Swift Glintstone Shard to her merchant inventory.
You can give Sellen the Conspectus Scroll to add the sorceries Glintstone Cometshard and Star Shower to her merchant inventory.
In combat, Sellen wields a Carian Glintstone Staff and can cast Crystal Release , Shattering Crystal , Founding Rain of Stars , and Glintstone Cometshard.
Completing Sorceress Sellen's questline will affect the boss fight with Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing , preventing him from casting Comet Azur .
Despite being located in a no combat zone, the orb form Sellen transforms into at the end of her questline can be killed by the damaging roll of the Briar Set . With all 4 pieces equipped alongside the Ritual Sword Talisman at full health, each roll with deal 30 damage. She drops 1000 runes on death (base, NG) but can't be permanently killed and will respawn upon reloading the area.
The dialogue/lore in game that suggests that Sellen was the one whom created the Graven Mages is backed up by the fact that her mentor was Master Azur, the founder of the Karolos Conspectus, whose masks are notably spared from being incorporated into any of the Spheres. The Karolos Sorcerers are also the most plentiful type encountered throughout the academy, and world in general save for places still held by Carians, where Lazuli sorcerers still reside. Furthermore, paintings of the founding sorcerers found throughout the academy (except for the ones hanging in the debate parlor) are positioned such that Lusat's and the Lazuli founder's are either on their side or upside down, whereas Azur's are positioned properly. A notable few of these are on the secret path that's before the debate parlor, the one leading to the Graven School Talisman that rests upon an inert Graven Mage (part of the world geometry; not an enemy to fight). The implication here being that Sellen had a certain level of disdain for the other conspectuses, explaining her willingness to use their sorcerers in her experiments in creating the seeds of new stars, ultimately resulting in the making of Graven Mages.
Sellen's name in the Japanese release is "Seren", which could be translated as Sellen or Seren; given her voice actor has a Welsh accent and the ubiquitous use of the Welsh language in Elden Ring's world, it could be that her name is in some way linked to the Welsh word "Seren", meaning "star", which would thematically align with the Glintstone sorceries she practices that draw on the power of stars.
One of the glintstone crowns bestowed upon Raya Lucaria scholars whose pursuits were deemed worthy.
Increases intelligence to the detriment of stamina.
The Karolos Conspectus is the oldest of the academy's lineages of study, begat by the sorcerer Azur. Scholars who follow in his footsteps pursue the mysteries of comets.
An old glintstone blade that has been stained with blood.
Reduces FP consumption of sorceries and incantations at the cost of maximum HP.
The old sorcerers would slice open their hearts with these blades to imbue a primal glintstone with their soul, and thus did they die.
Increases intelligence and arcane to the detriment of stamina.
This gentle-looking crown was granted to a scholar who excelled in her studies, which also merited the title of "witch."
Note sold by a nomadic merchant imparting knowledge in brief.
"Preceptor Seluvis is hiding a dirty secret in a cellar among the ruins not far from the sisters three.
The vile Graven Witch seems to be a frequent visitor to that place."
Manchettes corroded by blue glintstones. Worn by Lusat, primeval current sorcerer.
Lusat had reached a near-inorganic state.
A talisman depicting the first school of graven mages — a nightmare that would continue to haunt the academy.
Greatly raises potency of sorceries.
The primeval current is a forbidden tradition of glintstone sorcery. To those who cleave to its teachings, the act of collecting sorcerers to fashion them into the seeds of stars is but another path of scientific inquiry.
Sellen was well known. The most promising sorceress in the history of the academy.
But Sellen was expelled from the academy. Accused of unthinkable treatment of certain sorcerers, under the name of the Graven Witch.
With the stars of fate set into motion, a certain sorceress is dispossessed of her immortality... Finally, we can be rid of a longstanding Carian weed...
There's a glintstone sorcerer by the name of Sellen in Limgrave. She owes me for the help I gave her when she was expelled from the academy. I asked her to look into the matter some time ago.
You didn't...know Sellen, did you? Well, whatever the case, she's dead now.
Tarnished, are we? A wonder you should turn up here. I am Sellen, a sorcerer, quite plainly.
Sorceress Sellen is a merchant NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) . She is initially found in Waypoint Ruins in Limgrave and is eager to share her glintstone sorceries .
1 Background 1.1 Freeing Sellen
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Summon sign 2.3 Shop inventory 2.4 Equipment
3 Quests 3.1 Quest items 3.2 Questline progression
3.2 Questline progression
Sellen was considered to be the most promising glintstone sorceress to ever attend the Academy of Raya Lucaria . [ 1 ] Her first teacher at the was the sorcerer Azur , one of the founding glintstone sorcerers and originator of the Karolos Conspectus . [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Azur, along with Lusat , was banished from the academy due to their study of the primeval current, [ 4 ] a study Sellen wished to pursue. However, following accusations of unspeakable treatment of her fellow scholars under the name of the Graven Witch, Sellen too was expelled from the academy. [ 5 ] Sellen had sought to restore the Primeval Current, desiring glintstone sorceries unbound by terrestrial taboos and the pedantry of the Carian Royal Family . [ 6 ]
Sellen was also an acquaintance of Preceptor Seluvis , and a frequent visitor to his underground cellar, [ 7 ] where he kept his collection of puppets. When Sellen was expelled, Seluvis helped her in some capacity, for which she owes him. [ 8 ]
At some point following her banishment, Sellen was imprisoned in Witchbane Ruins on the Weeping Peninsula , bound by the Academy's shackles. [ 9 ] She managed to manifest a projection of herself [ 10 ] in the Waypoint Ruins in Limgrave . It is here that she meets the Tarnished , who becomes her apprentice [ 11 ] and starts learning glintstone sorceries from her. When she learns that the Tarnished has found Master Azur on Mt. Gelmir , she requests that they seek out Master Lusat, speculating that he returned to his home of Sellia, Town of Sorcery . [ 12 ] The Tarnished discovers Lusat, in a near-inorganic state [ 13 ] much like Azur, in Sellia Hideaway , and reports back to Sellen.
Much like Ranni the Witch , Sellen's fate is tied to the stars, and with the defeat of Starscourge Radahn , Sellen becomes dispossessed of her immortality. [ 14 ] This makes her a target of Jerren , a friend of the Carian Royal Family who once made a promise to exterminate the witch. Sellen reveals to the Tarnished that the form they have been speaking to is a projection, [ 10 ] and that her true body resides in Witchbane Ruins. [ 15 ] The Tarnished meets with Sellen's true body, shackled in the ruin's cellar, and she asks them to remove her Primal Glintstone , beseeching them to look after it. [ 16 ] Jerren arrives to find Sellen's previous body already perished. [ 17 ] The Tarnished later places Sellen's Primal Glintstone into a puppet in Seluvis's basement, which bears a striking resemblance to Sellen's old body.
Sellen, now in her new, more youthful body, [ 18 ] returns to the Academy and displaces Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon . [ 19 ] She has the bodies of Azur and Lusat moved back to the Academy, so that she can finally hone the Primeval Current. [ 20 ] She offers her support to the Tarnished in their mission to become Elden Lord , and requests that when the time comes, they illuminate her about the secrets of life which course the Elden Ring . [ 21 ]
Before long, however, Jerren finally catches up to her. [ 22 ] Should the Tarnished aid her in beating back Jerren, Sellen will continue her research. However, the next time the Tarnished visits her, Sellen will have fallen to the fate of many glintstone sorcerers before her, and transformed into a Graven Mass .
Waypoint Ruins Sellen can be found in the cellar of Waypoint Ruins after defeating the Mad Pumpkin Head . Talk to Sellen and then select "I wish to learn glintstone sorceries" to receive the "Nod in Thought" Gesture, and to unlock her sorcery shop. (Optional) If the Radahn Festival has not commenced, Sellen will have dialogue about it if you have Seluvis's Introduction in your inventory. This is obtained by progressing Blaidd the Half-Wolf's questline and speaking to Seluvis about Nokron prior to initiating the Festival.
Sellen can be found in the cellar of Waypoint Ruins after defeating the Mad Pumpkin Head . Talk to Sellen and then select "I wish to learn glintstone sorceries" to receive the "Nod in Thought" Gesture, and to unlock her sorcery shop.
(Optional) If the Radahn Festival has not commenced, Sellen will have dialogue about it if you have Seluvis's Introduction in your inventory. This is obtained by progressing Blaidd the Half-Wolf's questline and speaking to Seluvis about Nokron prior to initiating the Festival.
Waypoint Ruins Find Primeval Sorcerer Azur, located beyond the Hermit Village on Mt. Gelmir , to obtain Comet Azur . Return to Sellen and select the "Show Azur's Sorcery" dialogue option. After listening to this dialogue, select the "Tell me your story" option, and then select "Let us journey together". Next, select "I have a favor to ask" to obtain the Sellian Sealbreaker. The Sellian Sealbreaker can be used to open a sealed tunnel in Sellia Hideaway , behind which is Primeval Sorcerer Lusat. Interact with him to receive Stars of Ruin . It is now possible to select the "Lusat's location" dialogue option when talking to Sellen, which will reward you with a Starlight Shards. This option can be avoided until later in the questline.
Find Primeval Sorcerer Azur, located beyond the Hermit Village on Mt. Gelmir , to obtain Comet Azur . Return to Sellen and select the "Show Azur's Sorcery" dialogue option. After listening to this dialogue, select the "Tell me your story" option, and then select "Let us journey together". Next, select "I have a favor to ask" to obtain the Sellian Sealbreaker.

---

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Blaidd the Half-Wolf
Let's give them a show to remember, eh. Just don't you go dying on me. For Ranni's sake too.
- Blaidd to the Tarnished, during the Festival of Radahn.
Blaidd the Half-Wolf is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) . He is a half-wolf warrior in service to Lunar Princess Ranni and serves as her faithful shadow .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Blaidd was created by Ranni's own Two Fingers as a vassal to Lunar Princess Ranni and to be incapable of treachery towards her, as she was one of the three Empyreans candidates chosen by the Greater Will, along with Miquella and Malenia , to eventually replace their mother Queen Marika the Eternal as the God of a new age. However, the Two Fingers also created a failsafe within Blaidd that was unknown to him; that if Ranni should ever deny the destiny set out for her by the Greater Will and rebel against it, the loyal shadow would slowly descend into madness, which would inevitably pose a threat to Ranni herself.
He was approved by Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon [ 1 ] and became raised as Ranni's stepbrother. He was willing to descend into madness as his ultimate end to support his stepsister Ranni's will from his heart.
Limgrave ( Mistwood Ruins ) Blaidd can be heard howling in Mistwood. After hearing the howl, speak to Kalé and select "About the howling in Mistwood." This will unlock the Finger Snap gesture. Use Finger Snap in Mistwood near a ruined tower containing a Runebear to cause Blaidd to jump down. Talk to him and exhaust his dialogue.
Blaidd can be heard howling in Mistwood. After hearing the howl, speak to Kalé and select "About the howling in Mistwood." This will unlock the Finger Snap gesture.
Use Finger Snap in Mistwood near a ruined tower containing a Runebear to cause Blaidd to jump down. Talk to him and exhaust his dialogue.
Limgrave ( Forlorn Hound Evergaol ) Enter the Forlorn Hound Evergaol in Limgrave and defeat Bloodhound Knight Darriwil . Blaidd will be available as a summon during this fight. Speak to Blaidd next to the Forlorn Hound Evergaol to earn a Somber Smithing Stone [2].
Enter the Forlorn Hound Evergaol in Limgrave and defeat Bloodhound Knight Darriwil . Blaidd will be available as a summon during this fight.
Speak to Blaidd next to the Forlorn Hound Evergaol to earn a Somber Smithing Stone [2].
Liurnia of the Lakes ( Ranni's Rise ) After entering Ranni's service, a projection of Blaidd will appear in Ranni's Rise. This step is skipped if Starscourge Radahn has already been defeated.
After entering Ranni's service, a projection of Blaidd will appear in Ranni's Rise. This step is skipped if Starscourge Radahn has already been defeated.
Siofra River Bank Blaidd will appear in Siofra River. He will suggest talking to Seluvis about how to get into Nokron. This step is skipped if Starscourge Radahn has already been defeated.
Blaidd will appear in Siofra River. He will suggest talking to Seluvis about how to get into Nokron. This step is skipped if Starscourge Radahn has already been defeated.
Siofra River Bank (Optional) After speaking to Seluvis about Nokron and then speaking to Sellen, return to Blaidd in Siofra River Bank and exhaust his dialogue. He will move to Redmane Castle afterwards.
(Optional) After speaking to Seluvis about Nokron and then speaking to Sellen, return to Blaidd in Siofra River Bank and exhaust his dialogue. He will move to Redmane Castle afterwards.
Caelid ( Redmane Castle ) Blaidd will appear in the plaza at Redmane Castle when the Radahn Festival begins. He also appears as an optional summon in the fight against Starscourge Radahn. (Optional) After defeating Radahn, speak to Blaidd next to the Starscourge Radahn Site of Grace. He will mark the location of the entrance to Nokron, Eternal City, on your map.

Liurnia of the Lakes ( Kingsrealm Ruins ) (Optional) Speak to War Counselor Iji to deliver the news of Blaidd's death.
(Optional) Speak to War Counselor Iji to deliver the news of Blaidd's death.
During Ranni's questline, the Tarnished will come across a Baleful Shadow, one of the assassins sent after Ranni by her Two Fingers. This Shadow takes the form of Blaidd, although it is not actually him.
Interestingly, near Ranni's Rise a corpse can be found which holds a mask that is nearly identical to Blaidd's head. Whether or not this mask belonged to the Baleful Shadow assassin encountered in Nokron by Ranni and the Tarnished, or if it belonged to another assassin who had tried to disguise themselves as Blaidd in order to get close to Ranni before the events of the game, is unknown.
The name "Blaidd" is Welsh for "Wolf."

area: Altus Plateau
chapter: 13
name: Millicent
Millicent is an NPC in Elden Ring . She is a young woman stricken with an incurable case of Scarlet Rot . Gowry will send players to look for her in an attempt to cure her of Scarlet Rot. Progress through her questline in order to unlock her as an NPC Summon Cooperator during specific boss battles.
Millicent can also be encountered as an NPC Invader . She will invade the player in the Swamp of Aeonia at all times of the day. She has light armor and appears to use a Shamshir , but with the Waterfowl Dance skill, the same as Malenia .
If I am to flower into something other than myself, I would rather rot into nothingness as I am.
Millicent can be found initially at Church of the Plague , northeast of Gowry's Shack .
After you give her the Unalloyed Gold Needle , she will move to Gowry's Shack. [ Map link ]
From there, she will head to the southern Altus Plateau. [ Map link ]
After defeating the Godskin Apostle and giving Millicent Valkyrie's Prosthesis , she will be at the Site of Grace in the northern part of Windmill Village . [ Map link ]
Millicent can be fought as an NPC . If defeated, she drops 6,000 Runes and Millicent's Prosthesis (after she moves to Windmill Village )
As an NPC Invader , Millicent appears southeast of the Swamp of Aeonia , near the geysers and the Commander O'Neil boss fight [ Map link ]. Millicent is immune to Scarlet Rot but vulnerable to Poison . If defeated as an invader, she drops x5 Sacramental Bud , Golden Rune [3] , Golden Rune [5] , Golden Rune [10] , and 316 Runes .
If defeated as an invader, she drops x5 Sacramental Bud , Golden Rune [3] , Golden Rune [5] , Golden Rune [10] , and 316 Runes .
Millicent can be summoned for the following bosses:
Magma Wyrm Makar : After giving Millicent the Unalloyed Gold Needle and exhausting all her dialogue at Gowry's Shack .
Godskin Apostle : After giving her the Valkyrie's Prosthesis .
Draconic Tree Sentinel ( Capital Outskirts ): After giving her the Valkyrie's Prosthesis and defeating the Godskin Apostle in Windmill Village .
Black Blade Kindred ( Forbidden Lands ): After advancing her questline to defeat the Godskin Apostle in Windmill Village . Her summon sign will be available north of the Statue of Marika, along the road to the boss.
Millicent has a questline related to Gowry , but also appears as an NPC Invader at the Aeonia Swamp . The quest can be completed after reaching the Haligtree Roots site of grace, and can still be completed after defeating Malenia . The quest can be done quickly if the player defeats the bosses or otherwise obtains the quest items before exhausting dialogue.
Questline Objectives:
Speak with Gowry in his shack and he will ask for a favor. He wants help to heal a young girl, Millicent, found beyond Sellia , who has been infected with Scarlet Rot . To do this, he will ask you to seek an Unalloyed Gold Needle , which is dropped by Commander O'Neil , located in the Swamp of Aeonia . In return, he will share the secrets of the town of Sellia. Speaking to Gowry and accepting his quest will unlock Polyanna, Adopted Daughter's summon sign, north of O'Neil's battle arena.
Speaking to Gowry and accepting his quest will unlock Polyanna, Adopted Daughter's summon sign, north of O'Neil's battle arena.
Give the Unalloyed Gold Needle to Gowry. He'll need some time to repair the needle and will give you Sellia's Secret . Warp to a nearby site of grace or quit the game to reload, and he'll give you the repaired needle.
Warp to a nearby site of grace or quit the game to reload, and he'll give you the repaired needle.
Bring the needle to Millicent at the Church of the Plague . You may need to complete the puzzle in Sellia, Town of Sorcery to open the path uphill. Or, coming from Dragonbarrow , jump down a Spirit Spring to the north of the Church.
After Millicent uses the needle, rest at the nearby site of grace, then exhaust her dialogue. She will gift you the Prosthesis-Wearer Heirloom . Upon reloading the area, she will have moved to Gowry's shack.
Return to Gowry's shack to find him gone, but Millicent there. Exhaust her dialogue. Reload the area, and Millicent will move, and Gowry will return. You can now purchase spells from him. Millicent can be summoned to fight Magma Wyrm Makar in the Ruin-Strewn Precipice after this point. This summon sign will disappear if she is given the Valkyrie's Prosthesis.
Millicent can be summoned to fight Magma Wyrm Makar in the Ruin-Strewn Precipice after this point. This summon sign will disappear if she is given the Valkyrie's Prosthesis.
Millicent will now be located just north-northeast of the Erdtree-Gazing Hill site of grace in Altus Plateau , standing near the edge of the cliff. [ Map Link ] The player will need to go north to The Shaded Castle and retrieve the Valkyrie's Prosthesis , which is found in a chest in a room guarded by a single Cleanrot Knight , on the northwestern edge of the castle. Return to her, and give her the Valkyrie's Prosthesis item to advance her quest.
The player will need to go north to The Shaded Castle and retrieve the Valkyrie's Prosthesis , which is found in a chest in a room guarded by a single Cleanrot Knight , on the northwestern edge of the castle. Return to her, and give her the Valkyrie's Prosthesis item to advance her quest.
Go to Dominula, Windmill Village , and a golden summon sign for Millicent will appear before the Godskin Apostle boss..
After defeating the Apostle, rest at the Windmill Heights site of grace, and she will appear next to it. Exhaust her dialogue to continue the quest. [ Map Link ]
Millicent can be summoned to fight the Black Blade Kindred boss in Forbidden Lands , even before defeating the Godskin Apostle.
Millicent will move to the Ancient Snow Valley Ruins site of grace in Mountaintops of the Giants . Exhaust her dialogue to continue. [ Map link ] If Millicent does not appear at the site of grace, try returning to the Windmill Heights again and see if she's there, and if so, exhaust her dialogue there.
If Millicent does not appear at the site of grace, try returning to the Windmill Heights again and see if she's there, and if so, exhaust her dialogue there.
Millicent will move to the Prayer Room site of grace [ Map Link ] in Elphael, Brace of the Haligtree . She will not move from this spot until the player has defeated the nearby Putrid Tree Spirit enemy. To reach this enemy, go to the Drainage Channel site of grace, go west through the doorway, climb up the ladder at the end of the corridor, and go out the doorway on your right at the top. The tree spirit is at the landmass across the gap to the left. To reach it, go up the slope on the right and walk across the tree branch, heading southwest across the gap. [ Map link ]
Once the player has defeated the mini-boss, there will be two summon signs near where the mini-boss used to be. Specifically, on the northwest hill around the lake. The player can either: Be summoned into her world and help fight four NPC Invaders called Millicent's Sisters, granting you the Rotten Winged Sword Insignia upon completion. Mary, Eldest Sister (wields a Halo Scythe ). Maureen, Second Sister (wields a Treespear ). Amy, Third Sister (wields two Flowing Curved Swords ). Polyanna, Youngest Sister (wields a Blade of Calling ). Be summoned as an invader in order to challenge her, granting you Millicent's Prosthesis upon completion, but not the Unalloyed Gold Needle .

If the player chose to help Millicent, they can find her near where her summoning sign was, sitting by the rotten pool. Exhaust all of her dialogue, then reload the area. The Unalloyed Gold Needle is on the ground. If you return to Gowry, he will talk about Malenia and Millicent's connection. You will no longer be able to purchase from the Sage. If you kill Gowry at this point, you will receive the Flock's Canvas Talisman and Gowry's Bell Bearing . If he is already dead, both items will still be obtainable from his corpse, found in Gowry's Shack .

Attacking her after giving her the Unalloyed Gold Needle
(Warning) Ugh. Is there something wrong? Please, cease this at once.
(Combat) Why are you doing this? Now you've forced my hand.
(On player kill) I will not forget what you did for me. But this day marks our farewell.

I did not expect it to end like this. But I will not forget what you have done.
After choosing to challenge Millicent
Ahh, how could you... Is this your true heart? Was I... was it all...for this?
In combat, Millicent wields a unique version of the Shamshir that has the Waterfowl Dance skill (which is normally only found on the Hand of Malenia ), and the R2 attack of the Flowing Curved Sword . Before she receives the prosthesis, she uses it in her left hand, never two-handing it or doing anything else that would involve her right arm, and she won't use its skill either.
When fighting her as an invader in the swamp, it is recommended to use Flame, Cleanse Me or Preserving Boluses to cleanse the Scarlet Rot .
The name "Millicent" comes from the old Germanic name Amalasuintha, composed of the words "Amal" which means "work" or "labor" and "Swinth", which means "strong". Alternatively, the French variation, Mélisande, means "brave strength". This fits directly back into Millicent's character, who despite being heavily afflicted with the Scarlet Rot and lacking an arm, is able to travel from Caelid all the way to the Haligtree almost on her own, requiring only some assistance from the player.
The items dropped by Invader Millicent in the Swamp of Aeonia may be a reference to other aspects of her lore: The 5 sacramental buds represent Millicent and her 4 sisters, while the rune items she drops are the same as the percentage damage increase of the base Winged Sword Insignia; 3, 5, and 10.
Curved sword with a thin blade of ample length. Light of weight despite its larger size, its slicing attacks come in rapid succession.
A devastatingly powerful weapon in the hands of a skilled swordsman.
An intricately crafted needle of unalloyed gold. Once snapped in half, it has been repaired by Sage Gowry.
A ritual implement crafted to ward away the meddling of outer gods, it is thought capable of forestalling the incurable rotting sickness.
"Now, all you need to do is deliver the needle to Millicent, convalescing in the church atop the cliff just beyond Sellia."
A talisman engraved with a scene from a heroic tale. Raises dexterity.
Though born into the accursed rot, when the young girl encountered her mentor and his flowing blade, she gained wings of unparalleled strength.
Golden prosthesis once used by the one-armed valkyrie. A masterwork of craftsmanship, with practice and skill it can be used as proficiently as a real arm.
When Maleigh Marais, Lord of the Shaded Castle, embraced this prosthesis, he claimed to feel the presence of his personal goddess.
A talisman depicting a raised prosthetic blade. An honor bestowed upon the valkyries who serve the goddess of rot.
Greatly raises attack power with successive attacks.
The Four sisters were born in the swamp of Aeonia, and came to the Haligtree under the aegis of Gowry. And yet, those buds were doomed to never blossom.
Part of the golden prosthesis used by Millicent. The hand is locked into a fist that once raised a sword aloft.
Boosts dexterity and raises attack power with successive attacks.
The despair of sweet betrayal transformed Millicent from a mere bud into a magnificent flower. And one day, she will be reborn - as a beautiful scarlet valkyrie.
"Well, I'm the one that found her. A mere babe, in the swamp of Aeonia. She is one of my dear daughters."
"The needle is repaired. Now it will forestall the rotting sickness, I'm sure."
"If you happen to be present for the girl's fight with her sisters, I ask that you side with the sisters and kill Millicent. It must be done by your hand; no other. Millicent trusts you, rather deeply in fact. Sever that trust. Nurtured by betrayal, her bud will flower most vividly. When Malenia ascends to godhood, Millicent too shall be reborn. As a scarlet valkyrie."
Tell whoever put you up to this. That if I am to flower into something other than myself, I would rather rot into nothingness as I am.
Millicent is an NPC cooperator in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Millicent is a red-haired woman afflicted by Scarlet Rot . She and her sisters were found in the Swamp of Aeonia in Caelid by Sage Gowry , who raised them as his daughters. [ 1 ] Her four sisters travelled to Miquella's Haligtree under Gowry's guidance [ 2 ] , however Millicent alone remained behind.
At Gowry's behest, [ 3 ] the Tarnished obtains an Unalloyed Gold Needle and brings it to the Church of the Plague , where Millicent is slowly succumbing to the Rot. The Tarnished uses the needle to stall the Rot, allowing Millicent to regain lucidity. [ 4 ] However, her memories appear to have been affected by her sickness, as when she struggles to remember Gowry upon returning to his shack to find it abandoned. [ 5 ]
Millicent sets off on a journey across the Lands Between , retracing the route taken by Malenia, Blade of Miquella after the battle of Aeonia. [ 6 ] She once again meets the Tarnished on the Altus Plateau , where they present her with a Valkyrie's Prosthesis to replace her missing sword arm. [ 7 ]
Millicent's journey takes her to the Mountaintops of the Giants , in search of a fort containing a medallion which will allow her to access the Haligtree. [ 8 ] After the Tarnished claims the medallion and travels to the Consecrated Snowfield , Millicent makes her way to the Haligtree, and to Elphael , the city at its base. Here, she reveals to the Tarnished that she is of Malenia's blood, although she is unsure whether she is a sister, daughter, or offshoot of the Demigod. [ 9 ] Unbeknownst to Millicent, however, the Tarnished has been advised by Gowry to assist his remaining four daughters in killing Millicent, so that she can be reborn as a scarlet valkyrie when Malenia ascends to godhood. [ 10 ]
The Tarnished may then make a choice: kill Millicent alongside her four sisters, or fight alongside her against the attackers. Should the Tarnished kill Millicent, she will become a scarlet aeonia flower , waiting for Malenia's ascension. If the player fights alongside her, she will remove the needle from her body, allowing her to succumb to the Rot.

Selecting the gold summon sign will send you into Millicent's world to help her fight off four NPCs: Mary, Eldest Sister; Maureen, Second Sister; Amy, Third Sister; Polyanna, Youngest Sister. Afterwards, after exhausting his dialogue, Gowry will drop the Flock's Canvas Talisman if killed.
