After leaving them (reloading in the same place won't make them disappear), Goldmask and Corhyn will move to the bridge south of Stargazers' Ruins right above the Ancient Snow Valley Ruins Site of Grace. [ Map Link ] A dialogue option also appears to give Brother Corhyn the Tonic of Forgetfulness upon advancing his questline to Mountaintops of the Giants , near the Stargazers' Ruins. Corhyn will refuse the potion and the item will remain in the player's inventory, but it will change the final step in this questline.
A dialogue option also appears to give Brother Corhyn the Tonic of Forgetfulness upon advancing his questline to Mountaintops of the Giants , near the Stargazers' Ruins. Corhyn will refuse the potion and the item will remain in the player's inventory, but it will change the final step in this questline.
After completing Crumbling Farum Azula , Goldmask can be found in Leyndell, Ashen Capital , he moves to the bottom of the colosseum cliffs facing the west side of the Erdtree Sanctuary Site of Grace.. He will give you the Mending Rune of Perfect Order . Reload the area to loot Goldmask's Set .
Corhyn can then be found at one of the possible two locations: Corhyn location 1: It is advisable to check for him here first, as the game loads you into Leyndell, Ashen Capital after you finish the main boss fight at Crumbling Farum Azula . He will be near the base of the giant spiral spear. Speak with him to exhaust all dialogue and then, after reloading the area, you can find a Flail , Corhyn's Bell Bearing , and Corhyn's Robe where he was sitting. [ Map Link ] Corhyn location 2: If you offered Brother Corhyn the Tonic of Forgetfulness , he will remain at the bridge south of the Stargazers' Ruins where you last saw him. Talk to him; he'll be unaware of Goldmask's absence. Reload the area and then collect all three of the above items where he was.
Corhyn location 1: It is advisable to check for him here first, as the game loads you into Leyndell, Ashen Capital after you finish the main boss fight at Crumbling Farum Azula . He will be near the base of the giant spiral spear. Speak with him to exhaust all dialogue and then, after reloading the area, you can find a Flail , Corhyn's Bell Bearing , and Corhyn's Robe where he was sitting. [ Map Link ]
Corhyn location 2: If you offered Brother Corhyn the Tonic of Forgetfulness , he will remain at the bridge south of the Stargazers' Ruins where you last saw him. Talk to him; he'll be unaware of Goldmask's absence. Reload the area and then collect all three of the above items where he was.
Note: If you don't complete the questline, you can find both Corhyn's Bell Bearing and Corhyn's Robe on a cliff east of the colosseum, in Leyndell, Ashen Capital .
Oh, I see you've just arrived. Welcome to the Roundtable Hold. I'm Corhyn, a man of the cloth. I teach incantations, the strength granted us by the Two Fingers, and explore the secrets of the Golden Order. So that one day, if a Tarnished of the Roundtable Hold should become Elden Lord, I might counsel them, ensuring order regains its proper form, righting rule over men. By the way, do you still see it? The guidance of grace.
( I don't see it ) Ah, well, that is unfortunate. But no cause for despair. It's likely a temporary condition, I'm told. For whatever reason, it happens from time to time. Just keep your chin up, and stay the path. I pray the great guidance of grace returns to you.
What is it? Has the guidance of grace returned to your vision?
( I see it ) You do! Wonderful news. Most Tarnished are blind to it these days. You are something of a rare breed. Well, what do you say? Care to learn an incantation of the Two Fingers?
Subsequent Visits § :
Ah, you appear to be doing well. Very good. Well then, would you like to learn an incantation?
Further study of incantations, is it? I can happily spare the time, and applaud your enthusiasm.
[Giving any other prayerbook] That... is a work of heresy. Its incantations bear no lineage from the Erdtree. (sigh) Very well... I'll take it off your hands.
[Giving the Golden Order Principia ] This...is a volume of incantation. It's good to see your enthusiasm. Indeed, I will happily take it off your hands.
[Leave] May the Golden Order shine through you.
Leaving the Roundtable Hold:
There's something I should mention to you as well. I'm thinking of leaving the Roundtable Hold. Do you know of the noble Goldmask ? Though he was a Tarnished, living outside the Lands Between, he was a great scholar, who foresaw the coming guidance of grace. And now, I hear he has come to the Lands between alone, to contemplate the Golden Order... I wish nothing more than to seek his instruction, and perhaps even help him in his research.
Ah, I've been waiting your return. I've decided to leave the Roundtable Hold after all. As I mentioned, I'm off in search of the noble Goldmask. We may not meet again for some time. If there's any incantations you wish to learn, now is the moment.
At the Altus Plateau:
Ah, look who we have here. How delightful, to meet a familiar face, even after departing the Roundtable Hold. I've been doing some learning of my own since then, actually. And will happily pass it along to you.
[About the noble Goldmask] I'm yet to find the noble Goldmask. I suppose he'll be closer to the Erdtree. The path ahead might be perilous, but tread it I shall. Since departing the Roundtable Hold, I've come to understand, in my solitude, how little it is I truly know...
[Tell the noble Goldmaks's whereabouts] Do...do you sport with me!? From your description, it can be no other than the Goldmask himself! Of course, of course, I knew he would be close by. Bless the Golden Order, and its benevolent rays. And to you too. My sincerest thanks.
Bridge above Road of Iniquity Side Path :
Ah, we meet yet again. Thanks to you, I have become acquainted with the noble Goldmask himself, and taken my place by his side as you can see. Have no fear, I will still teach you incantations, as before. Though we must do so quietly, such that we do not disturb the great master's cogitation.
[About the noble Goldmask] The master is always deep in contemplation. While I frantically attempt to record his wisdom; the movement of his finger. And though I am yet to comprehend the daintiest morsel of his wisdom... I know that this, this is my life's calling. The Golden Order has bestowed me, talentless as I am, the great duty of documentarian.
[Handing over a prayerbook after meeting Goldmask, except Golden Order Principia] That... is a work of heresy. Its incantations bear no lineage from the Erdtree. (sigh) Very well... I'll take it off your hands. I cannot afford to maintain my innocence any longer. I must dirty my hands to test my faith. Even if it offers only the most meagre of hints, I will do whatever I can to help my master.
§ At Leyndell, Royal Capital:
[To Goldmask] Master! What ever is the matter? Please, I implore you continue! Continue your reflections, your rhythms! I must be the one to record them! What matters this issue of Radagon, really? The Erdtree, heart of the Golden Order, lies before our very eyes! Why must these qualms come to you now? We were on the very cusp!
Oh, was that you? Sorry, I hardly noticed. I'm a little shaken since the master ceased his movements.
The master's reflections had heightened as we neared the Erdtree. While still a precise calculus, the rhythms grew increasingly wild. Until he simply ceased. Now the master is facing quite the puzzle. The Golden Order is founded on the principle that Marika is the one true god. However... The name of Marika's second husband, King Consort Radagon, also appeared... Who exactly was Radagon? The master is stumped. His finger has remained still, ever since Radagon's name was discovered. Curse my mediocre mind. The master only has me; and here I fail him.
[After telling Goldmask the statue's secret] What on Earth did you do to the master? Well, not that I'm complaining. Master's finger moves again, resuming his cogitation; more than good enough for me. I haven't the words to thank you. So I'd like to pass this on to you instead. A glimpse into the heart of the Golden Order, documented by yours truly.
[After telling Goldmask the statue's secret] To think, that Radagon was Marika herself. Or at least, such is all I can interpret from the rhythm and calculus of his finger. How would such a thing even have been possible, I wonder. Sadly, I cannot comprehend it myself. Do you have a fuller understanding of the matter? Well, either way, I can continue my documentation. In truth, it matters very little whether I understand the master's thoughts or not. I am merely his scribe. It is my sole and unwavering purpose.
At Stargazers' Ruins Bridge:
Ah, nice to see you. I can't believe you've come all this way.
I've been gripped by a terrifying thought. The rhythms and calculus of the master's finger... betray a suspicion of the holism of the Golden Order. A conceit, I am afraid, that cannot be overlooked. Oh, but how could this be? I dread to even entertain the possibility... but somehow, I cannot cast aside my doubts about the master. Tell me, have I simply lost my head? Only, if the master were true to the Golden Order, why would he think to breach this forbidden mount of fire?
Oh master, put my mind at ease. Dispel these fearsome thoughts. I want to place my trust in you, to be your scribe.
[Offering the Tonic of Forgetfulness ] It will make me forget, you say? So that I can push aside these frightful thoughts... Ha... ha ha... Would that I could... Would that I could...
[After completing Crumbling Farum Azula, if the Tonic was offered] Master... where did you go? I'm right here. Please, show me. Your finger. Your rhythms, and calculus... Let me document the motions... Master... Master... Where did you go?
At Leyndell, Ashen Capital (if the Tonic was NOT offered)
Oh, it's you. I've finally come to understand. The master was nothing more than a madman. Enchanted by a vain and ruinous delusion, he rejected the perfection of the Golden Order, seeking to supplant our glorious faith with his own! Could there be a more pitiable comedy? Look at it. The culmination of perfection, burning, before our very eyes! Ha... ha ha ha...
(Warning) Ngh! What are you trying to achieve? Cease that!
(Combat) You've done it now. Faith grants me strength, that I might endure. O Golden Order, shine through me.
( On player kill ) I pray... That the guidance of grace abandons you.
(On player kill with goldmask) Please don't trouble us again.
O guidance...of grace... Why...can't I
(With Goldmask) O master... To be ever at your side...
Corhyn can be accidentally killed or found dead in some cases if players progress too far without interacting with him. Some players have even mistaken him for an enemy soldier due to his appearance in the environments he is found in. Attack him and he will use his Flail and Sacred Seal to Heal himself during combat. If killed he drops Corhyn's Bell Bearing . Flail . Corhyn's Robe . Players have attempted to undo this action by going to the Church of Vows and seeking absolution, however this action does not resurrect fallen NPCs and can only absolve any hostile relationships. Players have also reported finding him dead at his second location: north of Altus Highway Junction after meeting him at the Roundtable Hold .
With Corhyn dead, you may miss out on his merchant stock: a few Incantations and items related to the parts of his questline that involve Goldmask . For example, progressing through his questline will enable him to sell you the incantation Immutable Shield .
It is possible to progress through the Goldmask questline without Corhyn, so long as you follow the questline steps and do not require the locational hints Corhyn provides. After casting the Law of Regression at the relevant location in Leyndell, Royal Capital , look for Goldmask on the cliff above and tell him the secret as usual. The next steps will occur after the Capital of Ash event is triggered and you find Goldmask at his final location. He will be in Leyndell, Ashen Capital at the level below the colosseum cliffs where he stood previously (facing the west side of the Erdtree Sanctuary Site of Grace). He will give you the Mending Rune of Perfect Order . Reload the area to loot Goldmask's Set .
After meeting Brother Corhyn for the first time, you'll receive the Prayer Gesture .
If provoked, Corhyn wields a Flail and a sacred seal, which he uses to cast Heal .
If you manage to burn the Erdtree before ever reaching the Altus Plateau, meaning Brother Corhyn never leaves the Roundtable Hold, he will die when the Roundtable erupts into flame. You can accomplish this by defeating Fia's Chosen within the Deeproot Depths, which unlocks a teleporter directly to the Capital. From there, you can access the Mountaintops of the Giants without passing through the Plateau proper.
You can accomplish this by defeating Fia's Chosen within the Deeproot Depths, which unlocks a teleporter directly to the Capital. From there, you can access the Mountaintops of the Giants without passing through the Plateau proper.
Robe of Corhyn the cleric.
Even after exile, Corhyn refused to recant his prophecies. And for this, he was blessed with the guidance of grace.
Since then, the cart wheel draped on his neck has served as a reminder. That true guidance awaits those with iron wills. Those with unwavering faith.
Blindfold of exiled prophets who foretold misfortune — and were persecuted and driven from their homes as a result.
Why hesitate, if the path leading to the future is clear? Just close your eyes, and walk.
One of the incantations of the Golden Order fundamentalists.
Enchants shield held in the left hand with the light of regression, increasing all forms of non-physical damage negation and ailment resistance.
Two concepts form the basis for Golden Order fundamentalism. One is the law of regression, to which this incantation relates.
Prayerbook of the Golden Order fundamentalists. A dense and complex academic treatise that contains the Order's fundamental principles.
Can be given to a learned cleric to gain access to the following incantations:
Radagon's Rings of Light
A mask designed to resemble a blazing golden halo. Created and left behind by Lord Goldmask, a staunch pursuer of Golden Order fundamentalism. Strengthens Golden Order incantations. Its striking design represents both the brilliant inspiration that once shone upon him, and the vision of a ring that he will surely find at the end of his pursuit.
"To you who seek to shine as I do, wear it well!"
Rune discovered by the noble Goldmask. Used to restore the fractured Elden Ring when brandished by the Elden Lord.
A rune of transcendental ideology which will attempt to perfect the Golden Order.
The current imperfection of the Golden Order, or instability of ideology, can be blamed upon the fickleness of the gods no better than men. That is the fly in the ointment.
Brother Corhyn at Leyndell, Ashen Capital, at the end of his questline.
I teach incantations, the strength granted us by the Two Fingers, and explore the secrets of the Golden Order.
Brother Corhyn is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) . He can be given prayerbooks in exchange for access to an array of Incantations.
3 Character Information 3.1 Basic Info 3.2 Equipment 3.3 Quest Items
4 Questline Progression
Brother Corhyn is a cleric of the Golden Order , who was granted the guidance of Grace due to his iron will and unwavering faith in the face of his exile [ 1 ] . At some point, Corhyn made his way to Roundtable Hold , where he teaches the incantations of the Two Fingers to Tarnished of the Roundtable, hoping that one of them might one day claim the title of Elden Lord , so that Corhyn might council them. [ 2 ] When a Tarnished champion makes their way to the Roundtable, Corhyn introduces himself to them, and offers his tutelage. Corhyn will even teach the champion heretical incantations, from outside the line of the Erdtree , though he does so reluctantly.
After the Tarnished champion opens the way to the Altus Plateau , Corhyn announces that he is departing the Roundtable to search for the legendary Goldmask , an eminent scholar of the Golden Order . While searching for Goldmask, Corhyn continues his own investigations into the nature of the Golden Order, and while he makes some advancements, he also becomes aware of how little he truly knows. [ 3 ] With guidance from the Tarnished champion, Corhyn is united with Goldmask.
Corhyn takes it upon himself to become Goldmask's disciple and to chronicle his wisdom, deciphering the meaning behind every minute movement made by the mute Goldmask. The process goes well, until Goldmask's movements suddenly cease, as he is confounded by the nature of Radagon . The Tarnished champion may solve the riddle, revealing to Goldmask that Radagon and Queen Marika the Eternal are in fact one and the same, allowing Corhyn to continue his work. [ 4 ]
Goldmask leads his disciple to the Forbidden Lands and the Mountaintops of the Giants , where none may tread. Corhyn continues his duty, but begins to doubt his master. He detects in his movements that Goldmask has begun to suspect the holism of the Golden Order , an unacceptable heresy in Corhyn's mind. [ 5 ] Corhyn continues to serve his master, but is tormented by doubt.
After the Tarnished champion unleashes the flame of the giants to burn the Erdtree , Corhyn is found in the ashen ruins of Leyndell, Royal Capital . Corhyn denounces his master as nothing more than a madman, who rejected the perfection of the Golden Order and sought to replace it with his own "perfected" order. [ 6 ] As the Erdtree burns above him, Corhyn loses his mind. Alternatively, Corhyn may be offered the Tonic of Forgetfulness, causing him to remain in the Mountaintops, seemingly unaware that Goldmask has returned to Leyndell. [ 7 ]
Roundtable Hold Corhyn can be found in the central chamber of Roundtable Hold, where he will offer to teach you several Incantations. His shop inventory can be increased by giving him prayerbooks.

Talking to him here after finding Goldmask will allow you to reveal Goldmask's location to him.
Altus Plateau (Forest-Spanning Greatbridge) Corhyn will now appear next to Goldmask. The pair will now travel together.
Corhyn will now appear next to Goldmask. The pair will now travel together.
Leyndell, Royal Capital Corhyn and Goldmask can be found near the colosseum. Corhyn will express frustration at Goldmask's current obsession with Radagon. After using Law of Regression in front of the Radagon statue past the lift near Erdtree Sanctuary, talk to Goldmask to reveal Radagon's secret and then talk to Corhyn.

Mountaintops of the Giants Corhyn and Goldmask will move to the bridge south of Stargazer's Ruins . Corhyn can be offered the Tonic of Forgetfulness, which will affect the end of his questline. If offered the Tonic of Forgetfulness, Corhyn will remain in this location after defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade after Goldmask leaves. After reloading the area, he will have disappeared, leaving behind his flail, robe, and bell bearing.

Leyndell, Ashen Capital If Corhyn was not offered the Tonic of Forgetfulness, he will appear in Leyndell, Ashen Capital, close to the giant Bolt of Gransax after defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade. After exhausting his dialogue and resting at a Site of Grace, he will be gone.

Corhyn will refuse to take the Tonic of Forgetfuln

area: Altus Plateau
chapter: 13
name: Goldmask
Goldmask is a Tarnished NPC in Elden Ring . Goldmask can be found at various locations starting in Altus Plateau . He is also part of Brother Corhyn's questline, and is a staunch pursuer of Golden Order Fundamentalism . At each location, he does not move.
Do you know of the Noble Goldmask? Though he was but a Tarnished, living outside the Lands Between, he was a great scholar, who foresaw the coming guidance of Grace. - Brother Corhyn
If attacked, they will simply disappear.
Attacking this NPC does not mark you as a sinner
Goldmask can be found at in multiple locations:
First found North in Altus Plateau region at the forest spanning greatbridge. [ Map Link ]
After gaining access to Leyndell, Royal Capital , he relocates to the colosseum in Leyndell, Royal Capital . [ Map Link ]
He then relocates to the Mountaintops of The Giants .
His corpse can then be found near his original location at the bottom of the colosseum in the Ashen Capital .
Goldmask is part of Brother Corhyn's Questline.
This NPC has no dialogue other than "..."
Gives Mending Rune of Perfect Order in Leyndell, Ashen Capital at the end of Brother Corhyn's Questline. Head south-southwest from the Ashen Capital Site of Grace past the round staircase on your left and cross the tree log bridge, heading up the stairs to the south opposite the Giant Miranda Sprout . Goldmask's corpse will be uphill just around the bend, lying in light along the right wall.
If you go back to his corpse after you get the Mending Rune of Perfect Order , he will drop Goldmask's Set (excluding the Radiant Gold Mask , which can be picked up in the Altus Plateau ). [ Map Link ]
His voice actor, Ryan Morris, also voices Horace the Hushed from Dark Souls III and the Messengers from Bloodborne.
Worn rags that just barely cover the body.
Lord Goldmask felt no draw to the vain excess of clothing; what use is finery to one who seeks greater brilliance?

Alleviates death blight buildup.
The noble Goldmask lamented what had become of the hunters. How easy it is for learning and learnedness to be reduced to the ravings of fanatics; all the good and the great wanted, in their foolishness, was an absolute evil to contend with.
Does such a notion exist in the fundamentals of Order?
Incantation of the Golden Order fundamentalists. One of the key fundamentals.
Heals all negative statuses, dispels special effects, and reveals mimicry in all its forms.
The fundamentalists describe the Golden Order through the powers of regression and causality. Regression is the pull of meaning; that all things yearn eternally to converge.
One of the incantations of the Golden Order fundamentalists. One of the key fundamentals.
Manifests a small ring of causality within that allows the caster to automatically retaliate upon receiving a certain number of blows. The fundamentalists describe the Golden Order through the powers of regression and causality. Causality is the pull between meanings; that which links all things in a chain of relation .
Rune discovered by the noble Goldmask. Used to restore the fractured Elden Ring when brandished by the Elden Lord.

"Do you know of the Noble Goldmask? Though he was but a Tarnished, living outside the Lands Between, he was a great scholar, who foresaw the coming guidance of Grace."
Goldmask is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Goldmask is a Tarnished scholar and staunch Golden Order fundamentalist. [ 1 ] He predicted the guidance of Grace, and upon being beckoned by it he came to the Lands Between to contemplate the Golden Order. [ 2 ]
Although a Golden Order fundamentalist himself, Goldmask was critical of the hunters of Those Who Live in Death, lamenting that their learnedness had been reduced to fanatical raving and an obsession with finding a great evil to contend with, a concept which he did not believe existed within the Golden Order. [ 3 ]
While contemplating the Erdtree in the Altus Plateau Goldmask is approached by Brother Corhyn , a man of the cloth who wished to travel alongside him as a scholar. In Leyndell , Goldmask becomes perplexed by the identity of Radagon , whose existence calls into question the principle that Queen Marika the Eternal is the one true god. [ 4 ] With the help of the The Tarnished , Goldmask learns that Radagon is Marika.
Goldmask and Corhyn then travel to the Mountaintops of the Giants , where Corhyn becomes aware of Goldmask's suspicions regarding the holism of the Golden Order. [ 5 ] Corhyn begins to doubt Goldmask's faith, questioning his decision to travel to the Mountaintops. [ 6 ]
Goldmask had come to the revelation that the imperfection of the Golden Order and the instability of ideology was due to the fickleness of the gods. [ 7 ] After the Erdtree burns, Goldmask returns to Leyndell and dies on the hill near the Erdtree Sanctuary, leaving behind the Mending Rune of Perfect Order , which would allow the Golden Order to be perfected.
Goldmask cannot be made hostile, and will disappear if attacked, giving no Runes or item drops. He will reappear in his last location after resting at a Site of Grace.
Altus Plateau (Forest-Spanning Greatbridge) Goldmask can be found at the end of the broken bridge, facing the Erdtree. Interact with him to progress.

Speak to Corhyn to reveal Goldmask's location.

Leyndell, Royal Capital Goldmask and Corhyn can be found near the colosseum. Corhyn will express frustration at Goldmask's current obsession with Radagon. After using Law of Regression in front of the Radagon statue past the lift near Erdtree Sanctuary, talk to Goldmask to reveal Radagon's secret and then talk to Corhyn.

Leyndell, Ashen Capital After defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade , Goldmask's corpse can be found on a slope near the colosseum. Loot his corpse to receive the Mending Rune of Perfect Order, which will unlock the option to complete the Age of Order ending. After reloading the area, return to Goldmask's corpse to retrieve Goldmask's Rags, Gold Bracelets, and Gold Waistwrap.
After defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade , Goldmask's corpse can be found on a slope near the colosseum. Loot his corpse to receive the Mending Rune of Perfect Order, which will unlock the option to complete the Age of Order ending.
After reloading the area, return to Goldmask's corpse to retrieve Goldmask's Rags, Gold Bracelets, and Gold Waistwrap.
Goldmask is among the Tarnished listed in the game's intro. He is depicted splayed on the ground across a carving of the Erdtree Incantation sigil, alongside a book and a container leaking a golden fluid. Grace can be seen gleaming in front of him.
Goldmask is voiced by Ryan Morris, a translator and localizer at Frognation. He's also a voice director who has a history of voicing mute/non-talkative characters in FROMSOFT games, such as Horace the Horace the Hushed in Dark Souls III and the Messengers in Bloodborne .
While Goldmask is a silent NPC, he technically has one line of dialogue: a simple gasp when you reveal them him that Radagon is Marika .

---

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Enia the Finger Reader
Enia or Finger Reader Enia is an NPC in Elden Ring . Enia is an ancient woman, wielding a massive club. She is known as the Finger Reader . Enia can help the player draw out the power that lies within a remembrance of a powerful deity. Enia is different from the nameless Finger Readers that you meet in different areas, Enia seems to know you, but for the Finger Readers, their interests pique as they try to read what is written on your fingers. Enia also serves as a direct interpreter of the Two Fingers , envoys to the Greater Will .
Champion and Boss Equipment
Remembrance Transformation
The Fingers guide us all. And you Tarnished, you are here to take, are you not?
Enia can be found at Roundtable Hold as a merchant. She will be in the room with the Two Fingers, which can be accessed after defeating a Shardbearer.
This NPC does not move.
This merchant can't be fought.
You can draw out heroic powers from memories, known as Remembrances . Note that each memory can provide one power. Once that power is claimed, the memory is lost. Note that Remembrances can be duplicated at a Walking Mausoleum . Only one remembrance may be duplicated per mausoleum, and only one time. Using this method makes it possible to obtain both powers from the same remembrance, in the same playthrough. You cannot obtain two copies of a power in the same playthrough.
Shadow of the Erdtree added Remembrance Duplication Coffins which function the same as the Mausoleums, they fulfill the same role, but unlike their base game counterpart, they are not guarded nor do they move in place.
Note : Purchasing all remembrance items costs 72,000.
Are you the new Tarnished? You've done well. I am Enia, the Finger Reader.
I interpret the words of the Fingers, envoys to the Greater Will.
Look there. The Fingers tremble. To welcome you, shardbearer. Let their wisdom wash over you.
Great Elden Ring, root of the Golden Order. Anchor of all lands, giver of grace, wellspring of all joy. Until it was shattered.
The tragic corruption of the Order has taken its toll. Across the realm, life lies in ruin. Fallen to pieces. Foul curses and misery spread, unabating.
But the Greater Will has not abandoned the realm, nor the life that inhabits it. So it is that the Tarnished are guided by grace. Called to act. Brave Tarnished, your Great Rune is a handsome shard of the Elden Ring. Seek another of its kind. To become Elden Lord, and restore the Golden Order.
Let the words of the Fingers guide you.
Well, well...I see... A remembrance of gold has found its way into your possession.
Demigods, and even the greater of the champions, are hewn by the Erdtree upon their end, into remembrances.
They are...valuable indeed.
These remembrances yet house the power of their former masters. And should you wish to wield that same power, well... I will lend you the strength of the Fingers.
Heh... Do not recoil from my offer. The Fingers guide us all.
And you Tarnished, you are here to take, are you not?
Ahh, Great Runes are the stuff of demigods: the children of the goddess, Queen Marika. She who is vessel of the Elden Ring. Tainted by the strength of their runes, her children warred, but none could become Elden Lord. And so grace was extended, to your kind, the Tarnished Listen, the Fingers speak.
The Greater Will has long renounced the demigods. Tarnished, show no mercy. Have their heads. Take all they have left.
...Indeed. But remember one thing. The demigods are each and all the direct offspring of Queen Marika. Godrick the Grafted was but a distant relation... The runt of the litter, his divine blood sorely diluted.
Queen Marika is the vessel of the Elden Ring, carrier of its vision. A god, in truth.
But after the Elden Ring's shattering, she was imprisoned in the Erdtree.
A grim punishment for shattering the Order, despite her godhood.
The Fingers speak...
Marika's trespass demanded a heavy sentence. But even in shackles, she remains a god, and the vision's vessel. Confer Great Runes to become Elden Lord, and join Queen Marika as her consort. The Fingers have willed it so.
I had my doubts, but...my, look at you. Only once before have I seen two Great Runes together. Look there. The Fingers shudder with exuberance.
Fine work, brave Tarnished. The Greater Will is pleased. You have earned the right to become Elden Lord. Now, seek the Erdtree, and an audience with Queen Marika. To become Elden Lord, and restore the Golden Order.
The Fingers expect... As much from you as they do young Gideon. Take this. A token of farewell.
Well, you managed to return. You know what this means. The Erdtree has spurned you. The Fingers remain still. Shaken by this turn of events, they are busy consulting the Greater Will. When they are finished, the Fingers will once again offer their guidance.
But thousands, if not tens of thousands, of moons must first pass. No matter for me. But you? How will you ever manage the wait... My, oh, my...
Heavens forbid... That is not the domain of mere men. The burning of the Erdtree is the first cardinal sin. And you say you seek the power of the Rune of Death, too?
The Rune of Death goes by two names; the other is Destined Death. The forbidden shadow, plucked from the Golden Order upon its creation... Unleashing the rune now would be unthinkable... The Fingers would never permit it. Nor would the Greater Will.
... But here we are... The Fingers, dormant, severing our link to the Greater Will. The realm, and all life, in ruins. Impossible events transpire, beyond the ken of the Fingers. Who is to say that the cardinal sin must be cardinal forever? Go on. Finish the job. Take the course you deem most worthy.
You must find kindling. Only the smoldering flame in the Great Forge of the Giants, on the highest peak in the Lands Between, can burn the Erdtree.
But special kindling is required to reignite the flame. For the flame to burn the Erdtree, a sacrifice is needed. Of one who envisions the flame. And can lead you to the Rune of Death.
You've done it, I see. What use do you have for me, now? I am a Finger Reader. I will remain here, until their dialogue ends, and the Fingers speak again. Do as you please. Take whatever course seems most worthy to you. Now, you may go.

Heh, heh, heh. You'd force this tired old crone to work, even now? A harder taskmaster I have never met.

The Rune of Death is unbound, and the Lands Between are shrouded by Death's dark fate. But the flames will also burn the impenetrable thorns. Farewell it is, then. You'll be Elden Lord yet.
Past this point, Enia will no longer have any further dialogue at the Roundtable Hold ; nor elsewhere.
However, she will continue to transform remembrances and sell boss armors .

Increases talisman equip slots.
As the voices of the Two Fingers, Finger Readers are said to live lives eternal, and one is even supposed to have served as a wetnurse to royalty.
One of the weapons originating from the Two Fingers.
A formless sequence of ciphers comprise its blade, and as such no shield can repel it. Deals holy damage.
The furtive inscription appears to hang in the air; the language of light spoken by the Two Fingers.
Hidden sword once granted to the Tarnished of the Roundtable by the Two Fingers. A formless cipher comprises its blade, which deals holy damage no shield can repel.
Champions would gather at the Roundtable Hold in days long past, when the Two Fingers were masters of oration, their flesh yet full of vigor.
Are you that new Tarnished? You've done well. I am Enia, the Finger Reader. I interpret the words of the Fingers, envoys to the Greater Will.
- Finger Reader Enia
Finger Reader Enia is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Shop inventory 2.1 Power from Remembrance 2.2 Equipment of Champions
2.1 Power from Remembrance
2.2 Equipment of Champions
3 Character Information 3.1 Quest Items
4 Questline Progression
Enia is a Finger Reader, much like the Finger Reader Crones scattered across the Lands Between . She interprets the words of the Two Fingers , envoys of the Greater Will . [ 1 ] The Tarnished meets her in the Two Fingers' audience chamber in Roundtable Hold after claiming a Great Rune , where she imparts the wisdom of the Two Fingers upon them, revealing that they must gather more Great Runes to repair the shattered Elden Ring and become Elden Lord . [ 2 ] In addition to her guidance, Enia offers to grant the Tarnished the power housed within the Remembrances they gain from killing powerful foes. [ 3 ] Enia remains in Roundtable Hold alongside the Two Fingers, offering words of wisdom throughout the Tarnished's journey. After the Tarnished defeats Morgott, the Omen King and finds the way inside the Erdtree blocked by impenetrable thorns, the Two Fingers begin their long communication with the Greater Will. [ 4 ] Enia, discovering that the Tarnished intends to burn the Erdtree and unleash the Rune of Death , Enia forewarns them of the dangers, telling them that the Two Fingers and Greater Will would not approve of this course of action. [ 5 ] With the Fingers dormant, Enia ultimately advises the Tarnished to do what they think is right. [ 6 ]
After the Rune of Death is unleashed, Enia dies along with the Two Fingers.
The following are items that can be purchased after defeating certain bosses.
Enia can first be spoken to in Roundtable Hold, in the door west of the Table of Lost Grace, after defeating a Shardbearer. Exhaust her dialogue to unlock her two shop inventories.
After defeating two Shardbearers, speak to Enia again to receive a Talisman Pouch. If you defeat Morgott, Omen King before speaking to Enia during this step, the Talisman Pouch will be available to purchase from the Twin Maiden Husks .
If you defeat Morgott, Omen King before speaking to Enia during this step, the Talisman Pouch will be available to purchase from the Twin Maiden Husks .
After defeating Morgott, Omen King and attempting to access the Erdtree, speak to Enia again for some additional dialogue.
After gaining access to Crumbling Farum Azula , speak to Enia again for some additional dialogue.
Enia will narrate the cutscene that plays after defeating Maliketh, the Black Blade. She will now be dead upon returning to Roundtable Hold, however her shop inventories can still be accessed by interacting with her corpse.

area: Leyndell Royal Capital

name: Twin Maiden Husks
Twin Maiden Husks is an NPC and Merchant in Elden Ring . Twin Maiden Husks look like two Finger Readers , holding a staff, and will take Bell Bearing objects to expand their inventory, giving players an option to purchase many Upgrade Materials and the stores of any dead merchant.
Twin Maiden Husks can be found at Roundtable Hold
This NPC does not move
This merchant can't be fought
This shop is expanded by bringing "Bell Bearing" Key Items.
Quick Search of All Skills
This table is also sortable! Click the table header to sort
*Original merchant requires a Scroll , a Prayerbook , or quest progression to unlock this item
Some items in the Twin Maiden Husks' shop can only be unlocked by offering bell bearings, which can be found across the world or looted from killed merchants. Note that, in the case of killed merchants, only unlocked items will appear in the Twin Maiden Husks' shop upon offering the bell bearing (e.g. giving Sorceress Sellen the Royal House Scroll is required for her bell bearing to add Glintblade Phalanx and Carian Slicer to the Twin Maiden Husks' shop). Likewise, items already purchased from a merchant will not be restocked if they are killed and their bell bearing offered.
As of Patch 1.05, all  items unlocked by offering the following Bell Bearings to the Twin Maiden Husks will carry over to NG+:
Bone Peddler's Bell Bearing
Meat Peddler's Bell Bearing
Medicine Peddler's Bell Bearing
Gravity Stone Peddler's Bell Bearing
Smithing-Stone Miner's Bell Bearing (1)

Smithing-Stone Miner's Bell Bearing (3)
Smithing-Stone Miner's Bell Bearing (4)
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing (1)
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing (2)
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing (3)
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing (4)
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing (5)
Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing (1)
Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing (2)
Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing (3)
Ghost-Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing (1)
Ghost-Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing (2)
Ghost-Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing (3)
See the Bell Bearings article for a complete list of bell bearings and where to find them.
The Twin Maiden Husks are silent and have no dialogue.

Important Note: If any other Merchants in the world are killed whether accidentally or by a hostile's attacks, their inventory is added to the Twin Maiden Husks' so you can still purchase said merchants' items in the event of their demise.
If you miss interacting with Ranni the Witch at Church of Elleh , and meet her at Ranni's Rise , Twin Maiden Husks will sell Spirit Calling Bell and Lone Wolf Ashes , which would otherwise be given to you by Ranni at Church of Elleh.
All non-boss items in Enia 's shop were relocated to the Twin Maiden Husks' shop
The Twin Maiden Husks are an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
The Twin Maiden Husks are a pair of merchants in the Roundtable Hold . They are corpses, and cannot speak, nor move. Their shop is expanded by giving them Bell Bearings that can be acquired throughout the game.
The Twin Maiden Husks are found on the upper level of the Roundtable Hold.

area: Mt. Gelmir
chapter: 14
name: Tanith
Tanith, Volcano Manor Proprietress is an NPC in Elden Ring . Tanith is the head of the Volcano Manor, and asks the players about joining forces against the Fingers. If you decide to join her quest, you will receive particular missions as well as access to some new Npcs .
I am Tanith, the proprietress of this house. An honour to have you.
Tanith can be found at Volcano Manor . [ Elden Ring Map here ]
This merchant can/can't be fought
If defeated, drops: Consort's Set
Can be given Dancer's Castanets .
When asked if you would like to join the Volcano Manor family, say yes and she will give you the Drawing-Room Key . This will allow you to access the other dorms and meet the other NPCs in the area.
Once you enter the room with Rya , there will be a Letter from Volcano Manor left on the table for you along with a Recusant Finger . Read the note and return to Tanith where she will tell you to assassinate Old Knight Istvan . In order to assassinate Istvan, you will need to invade him back in Stormhill, next to the Divine Bridge. [ Map Link ]
In order to assassinate Istvan, you will need to invade him back in Stormhill, next to the Divine Bridge. [ Map Link ]
Return to Tanith and you'll be rewarded with Magma Shot .
Reload. In the room with Knight Bernahl (second/third left door in hallway) will be a Letter from Volcano Manor on the table for you. Read the note and then assassinate Rileigh the Idle in Altus Plateau .
Return to Tanith and you'll be rewarded with the Serpentbone Blade .
Reload. In the room with Knight Bernahl will be a Red Letter on the table for you. It tells you to assassinate Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood . His invasion mark is located in Mountaintops of the Giants . [ Elden Ring Map here ]
Return to Tanith and you'll be rewarded with Taker's Cameo . Note: If you have defeated Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy before returning, the reward will appear on the table on the same spot as the letters.
Note: If you have defeated Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy before returning, the reward will appear on the table on the same spot as the letters.
Once you have completed her assassination quests, she will ask if you would like to meet their lord. Accepting her request takes you directly to a site of grace immediately before the boss Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy .
Once you have defeated Rykard , return to Tanith and speak to her once more.
Once Tanith has disappeared from Volcano Manor, you will find her back in Rykard's boss area feasting on his corpse.
At this stage you can give her Dancer's Castanets that you receive from Patches at the Shaded Castle , although this doesn't seem to any known effect.
She can be killed. Doing so causes Tanith's Knight to invade you. Upon defeat he will drop Aspect of the Crucible: Breath and Tanith's attire, Consort's Set , will be found where she left; Note: It is unconfirmed whether this is her final destination. Reports have shown she will still be eating Rykard's corpse at the end of the game. Receiving the Dancer's Castanets from Patches will not continue her quest after Rykard is defeated.
Note: It is unconfirmed whether this is her final destination. Reports have shown she will still be eating Rykard's corpse at the end of the game. Receiving the Dancer's Castanets from Patches will not continue her quest after Rykard is defeated.
Aren't you precocious. We rarely receive visitors to the Volcano Manor without invitation. Fascinating. And not unlike another guest we had long ago...So, Tarnished, have you ever harboured doubts? About the burden of grace, and the dogmatic ramblings of the Fingers? If you have,  why not join the Volcano Manor, and fight with us? Rise with us, against the Erdtree.
Introduction after journeying with Rya
Brave Tarnished, welcome to the Volcano Manor. I am Tanith, the proprietress of this house. Rya spoke well of you. "A warrior of promise", I believe she said. Perhaps you are prepared to make a commitment. Will you join us here at the Volcano Manor, and resist the tide? Why accept the burden of their grace, or be fooled by the dogmatic ramblings of the Fingers? Rise with us, against the Erdtree.
Your decision is most welcome. Now you belong to the Volcano Manor family. The drawing room lies down the hall. Make yourself comfortable. I am Tanith, the proprietress of this house. An honour to have you. (Gives the Drawing-Room Key )
After you read the letter
Did you read the letter left for you? That is the task the Volcano Manor desires you enact.You will be compensated once the deed is done. Good luck. If you are loath to hunt your own kin, so be it. But you must leave this house at once. This is a war against the Erdtree. We have no place for the meek, nor the luxury of keeping clean hands.
After completing the first quest
You've completed your task. I am pleased. Now you are a recusant true, and a full-fledged member of the Volcano Manor. This is your reward as promised. (Gives Magma Shot ). Never forget. That the recusant fights to tread the path of the champion. The way is tainted, but for this very reason, it is the true path to valour.
After completing the second quest
I see you've performed another task for us. Here is your usual reward. Please, take it. (Gives Serpentbone Blade ). Now, perhaps the time has come to tell you. Of the true ruler of this manor, Lord Rykard. The Erdtree blessed the Tarnished with grace. But it was all too meagre, in the fate of the enormity of their task. The Tarnished were forced to scavenge, squabbling for crumbs. Like the shardbearers, vying for power in the wake of the Shattering. Our Lord, indignant, had refused. To scurry about, fighting over what miserly scraps they allow us. If the Erdtree, and indeed the very gods, would debase us so, then we are willing to raise the banner of resistance, even if it means heresy. We at the Volcano Manor, under Lord Rykard, have sworn no rest until it is done. If you follow this heroic path, one day the Lord will see you. The Lord's visits with our champions are always a spectacle. It cannot come a day too soon!
After completing the third quest
I see that you've stayed the path of champions. This is your usual reward. Please, take it. (Gives Taker's Cameo ). Perhaps you are ready. Might you see our Lord? Our Lord will no doubt welcome you. Another kindred spirit, treading the path of champions.
(See the Lord) Now, close your eyes for a moment. I will transport you to the Lord's chamber.
Farewell, then. May your visit be fruitful.
After defeating Rykard
You... It's true then. You've defeated out Lord. No, I must thank you. Our Lord was yet weak. You have taught us that. Defeat is not the end. Our Lord is immortal, and will one day rise again, stronger. Until then, I must stay the path, and do my part.
I will leave the Volcano Manor before long. I suggest you do the same. I will miss these encounters. The champion who walks the tainted path shines all the more. I always was an admirer.
This is farewell. Perhaps we will meet again, along the path.
Did you see her? The girl, Rya... with her true face... Well, if she confided in you the name Zorayas, then perhaps it is not my place to speak. But as her adoptive mother, I ask of you. Please, be kind to her. Look after young Zorayas. Her true visage belies the purity of her heart... Honestly, I hardly deserve the sweet child.
I should have known something was wrong. The signs were clear enough. Well, Zorayas has placed her trust in you. All the more reason I must tell you. That some things are better left unknown. Besides, no-one should be blamed for their heritage. Think about it. We are resisting the ways of the Erdtree itself. What matters one's lineage in such a crisis.
And she has no scouting duties now. Has she gone off in search of answers? Why won't she just listen...
May I ask your aid? Not as the Manor's proprietress, but as Zorayas' mother. If she discovers the answer to her question, and it causes distress, have her drink this potion. To purge that which would cause her pain. (Gives Tonic of Forgetfulness ). Yes, I know. My wish is a grave disrespect to her. No different than the Erdtree's impostion. But I've no choice... It must be done.
After giving Rya the tonic
I will never be a good mother. My heart is too frail. Our Lord must have known this all along. My meekness is all too clear...
Sweet Zorayas, have I earned your scorn?
My gratitude for your forthrightness. If you would...allow me some time alone.
When you find her eating off Rykard's corpse
Oh, you... Allow me some time. Our lord's carcass is vast and not easily consumed. Dear Rykard, please find purchase within me, I wish to be your serpent; your family. One day, let us devour the gods together.
(Gives the Dancer's Castanets ) What is it? I have no need of that. I must continue devouring my beloved lord.
Before Tanith came to Volcano Manor, she was working as a dancer. Rykard came upon her and made her his consort, making her the only human to remain on his side when he became the serpent of blasphemy. Tanith has shown to have a strong obsession for Rykard.
The Consort's Mask dropped by Tanith raises your dexterity by 1.

Tanith, Volcano Manor Proprietress is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Tanith is the consort of Praetor Rykard and the leader of the Recusants, a band of Tarnished who have taken up arms against the Erdtree and vowed to hunt down their own kind.
When Tanith first met Rykard, she was working as a dancer in a foreign land, but soon she became his consort. [ 1 ] She became truly charmed by Rykard when he swore himself to blasphemy, being the only human to remain by his side when he was devoured by the great serpent [ 2 ] , even refusing to imbibe a Tonic of Forgetfulness offered to her by Rykard, believing there to be no greater distress than to forget him. [ 3 ]
Tanith is the adoptive mother of Rya [ 4 ] , although Rya believes her to be her true mother, having been told that she was born of the grace of a glorious king. [ 5 ] Although they are not biologically related, Tanith professes to care for Rya, who's true name is Zorayas. [ 6 ]
The Tarnished first meets Tanith in the entrance chamber of Volcano Manor , accompanied by her Crucible Knight bodyguard, where she offers them the opportunity to join the Recusants. Should the Tarnished accept, they are tasked with hunting down several other Tarnished. After tracking down and killing two Tarnished, Tanith divulges the truth about Volcano Manor, and about Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy. [ 7 ]
After the Tarnished slays Rykard, Tanith thanks them, as their victory proved Rykard to yet be weak. [ 8 ] She announces that she will leave Volcano Manor [ 9 ] , however she instead moves to Rykard's audience chamber, where she can be found devouring the corpse of her lord, wishing to become his serpent so that they can devour the gods together. [ 10 ]
Defeating Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy at any point during Tanith's questline will cause all subsequent steps in the questline to become locked.#Upon entering Volcano Manor, Tanith can be found to the right of the Site of Grace. Speak to her and agree to join the family. Tanith will give you the Drawing Room Key.
Go into the corridor to the right of Tanith and open the first door on the left. Pick up the Letter from Volcano Manor on the table as well as the Recusant Finger .
Travel to the entrance to the colosseum in Stormhill and interact with the invasion sign to fight Old Knight Istvan. After defeating him, report back to Tanith to obtain Magma Shot.
Go back to the drawing room and pick up the Letter from Volcano Manor. Travel to Altus Plateau and find an invasion sign at the bottom of the gorge leading to Shaded Castle. Interact with the sign to fight Rileigh the Idle. After defeating him, talk to Tanith to obtain the Serpentbone Blade.
(Optional) If you have advanced Rya's questline and met her in serpent form, and rested at a Site of Grace in the main dungeon area of Volcano Manor, speak to Tanith to obtain the Tonic of Forgetfulness. This step will be locked if you kill Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood, before giving Rya the Serpent's Amnion .
Pick up the Red Letter from the drawing room, and travel to the Shack of the Lofty in the Mountaintops of the Giants . Interact with the invasion sign to fight Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood . Report back to Tanith to obtain the Taker's Cameo.
Tanith will now offer to send you directly to Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy, bypassing the rest of the Volcano Manor. Defeat Rykard and return to Tanith and exhaust her dialogue.
Travel to the Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy Site of Grace to find Tanith eating the remains of Rykard.
(Optional) After obtaining the Dancer's Castanets from Patches in Shaded Castle, offer them to Tanith. There is no reward for doing so, however the Dancer's Castanets will be removed from your inventory.
(Optional) Attacking Tanith will cause her to die in a single hit. You will then be invaded by Tanith's Knight . Defeating the knight will reward you with Aspects of the Crucible: Breath and the Consort's Set.
Her name is a tribute to British author Tanith Lee , a friend and colleague of George R.R. Martin's. In her 1978 novel, Night's Master , one segment focuses on a girl named Zorayas, who was the child of a dictator, raised unaware of her origins. [1]

---

area: Mountaintops of the Giants
chapter: 21
name: Juno Hoslow
Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood is a Humanoid Enemy in Elden Ring . Juno Hoslow is a Hostile NPC that you can invade as part of the final contract of the Volcano Manor questline. A Tarnished knight equipped with the signature whips and silver armor of his house, he is also the older brother of Diallos . Other Invadable NPCs can be found here .
See Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood Lore
Juno Hoslow had a younger brother who was all talk and no trousers. His inability to commit to action was such that Juno inherited the Hoslow legacy without resistance, granting him the freedom to shower his little brother with adoration.
Where to Find Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood :
Mountaintops of the Giants : East of Shack of the Lofty . Touch the red Summon Sign on the ground to invade his world. Only available after progressing the Volcano Manor questline. [ Elden Ring Map here ]
Furlcalling Finger Remedy
Always performs the Hoslow's Oath gesture, giving it to the player once completed.
Dual wields Hoslow's Petal Whips, dealing high Strike Damage and inflicting Blood Loss build-up. He also uses Bloodhound's Step to weave in and out of attacks.
Assuming correct timing, all of Juno's right hand whip attacks - including his double whip attack - can be dodged by rolling towards him. Ensure the Tarnished remains just about striking distance away from Juno to bait out an attack.
If rolling towards Juno and immediately attacking, he will either take the hit or use Bloodhound's Step to dodge away. It is best to simply commit to one attack lest he immediately counters with a whip attack of his own.
If Juno uses Bloodhound's Step to dodge away from the follow up attack, he himself will either follow up with another attack or do nothing. However, Juno's idle is often short enough for him to use another attack. Thus, it is simply best to bait out another attack while he is idle.
While it is possible to dodge Juno's left hand whip attack, this is almost impossible as the whip hitbox is confusingly large. It is simply best to back away from Juno if he uses his left hand whip attacks.
Like any human encounter, status effects are very effective.
It is possible to use Inescapable Frenzy to grab him while is busy performing his gesture. (confirmed as of February 3, 2024)
Replica of a twin-tailed silver helm with flowery adornments. Only, without the twin tails.
Diallos Hoslow had an older brother who was a stern, self-possessed man of a few words. His achievements made him seem out of reach.
And so the younger aspired to be like the older. Yearning for the day that he would tell the tale of House Hoslow, in blood. Knowing full well that it would break his brother's heart .
Garish silver armor decorated with red embellishments. Passed down through the Hoslow family. When Juno Hoslow received an invitation to the Volcano Manor, he discreetly refused. "I have already walked many a road drenched in blood, yet never would I consider myself a champion."
A twin-tailed silver helm decorated with flowery adornments. Symbol of the head of the revered House of Hoslow.
Juno Hoslow had a younger brother who was all talk and no trousers.
His inability to commit to action was such that Juno inherited the Hoslow legacy without resistance, granting him the freedom to shower his little brother with adoration .
Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood is a hostile NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Character Information 2.1 Equipment 2.2 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Juno Hoslow is Diallos ' older brother [ 1 ] and the head of their illustrious house . [ 2 ]
A successful, stern and self-possessed man of a few words [ 1 ] , Juno inherited the legacy of the Hoslow family to protect his inept brother, whom he loved dearly. [ 2 ]
Despite dreching himself in blood time and time again, he refused the invitation to the Volcano Manor. [ 3 ] Later, he became their target.
His sign can be found in the Mountaintops of the Giants , outside the Shack of the Lofty .
Mt. Gelmir ( Volcano Manor ) After joining the Volcano Manor and eliminating Old Knight Istvan and Rileigh the Idle under Tanith 's request, retrieve the Red Letter to unlock Juno Hoslow's invasion sign in the Mountaintops of the Giants. With Rileigh dead, you can also talk to Recusant Bernahl to receive the Letter to Bernahl , offering you to join him in his hunt against Vargram the Raging Wolf and Errant Sorcerer Wilhelm in Leyndell, Royal Capital .
After joining the Volcano Manor and eliminating Old Knight Istvan and Rileigh the Idle under Tanith 's request, retrieve the Red Letter to unlock Juno Hoslow's invasion sign in the Mountaintops of the Giants.
With Rileigh dead, you can also talk to Recusant Bernahl to receive the Letter to Bernahl , offering you to join him in his hunt against Vargram the Raging Wolf and Errant Sorcerer Wilhelm in Leyndell, Royal Capital .
Mt. Gelmir (Volcano Manor) Once Juno is disposed of, return to Tanith to receive the Taker's Cameo : she'll now offer to transport you to the Audience Pathway Site of Grace by selecting See the Lord .
Once Juno is disposed of, return to Tanith to receive the Taker's Cameo : she'll now offer to transport you to the Audience Pathway Site of Grace by selecting See the Lord .
With the Red Letter in inventory, even If Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy is defeated prior to the invasion, abruptly concluding Tanith's questline, not only the invasion sign will still be available, but the reward will be relocated in the spot where the Red Letter was retrieved.

area: Mt. Gelmir
chapter: 14
name: Volcano Manor
Volcano Manor is a Legacy Dungeon in Elden Ring . Volcano Manor is unique in that much of its progression, if you so choose, can be done outside of the manor itself. You will be tasked with assassinating Tarnished from other worlds, rebelling against the Fingers and the Erdtree itself. There is another way to beat the dungeon, however, if this band of dissenters and their tasks do not appeal to you. You'll find guides for both the assassination questline and the dungeon area here for a complete run of this Legacy Dungeon.
Previous: Raya Lucaria Academy
Next: Leyndell, Royal Capital
Recommended Levels: 80+
Bosses: Abductor Virgins , Godskin Noble , God-Devouring Serpent , Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy
