Players have considered this the second snow region in Elden Ring since it is a secret area on the lower plains of the Mountaintops of the Giants . As shown through the instructions above, you will need to take the Grand Lift of Rold to this location. However you will be required to have both parts of the Haligtree Secret Medallion. Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right) is found in Liurnia of the Lakes. Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) is found in Mountaintops of the Giants. Once you have both pieces of the medallion, go to the Grand Lift of Rold and select "Hoist secret medallion" (make sure your action is "Hoist secret medallion" and not "Hoist medallion") to brandish your secret one.
After you unlock the Secret Path to the Haligtree site of grace, which is located in the Forbidden Lands fast travel menu tab, you can fast travel to it then go up the stairs to exit to Consecrated Snowfield.
Consecrated Snowfield Notes and tips go here.
- Map Consecrated Snowfield — ELDEN RING (2022)
Consecrated Snowfield is a sub-region located in Mountaintops of the Giants .
A snow-swept, misty area to the west of the Mountaintops of the Giants . The Consecrated Snowfield is final trial for those who seek Miquella's Haligtree , and can only be accessed by uniting the two halves of the Haligtree Secret Medallion, found in the Village of the Albinaurics and Castle Sol , and bringing them to the Grand Lift of Rold .
Visibility is limited in many parts of the snowfield. Small, golden lights illuminate the path from the upper section to the broad, snowy plane below, through which a frozen river runs. In the south-west are the Yelough Anix Ruins , consumed by the Flame of Frenzy. Far to the north lies the Apostate Derelict , where the gentle yet towering sister of Latenna the Albinauric resides. South-east of there is Ordina, Liturgical Town , which hides the entrance to the Haligtree. There is also a waygate, tucked away on the western cliffs, which leads to Mohgwyn Palace .

area: Mohgwyn Palace
chapter: 23
name: Mohgwyn Palace
Mohgwyn Palace is a Location in Elden Ring . The Mohgwyn Palace region is found beneath Caelid , but is only accessible from a gateway teleporter in the frozen west of Consecrated Snowfield (northwest of the Yelough Anix Ruins and west of where the Sanguine Noble invades you) or by using the Pureblood Knight's Medal given by White Mask Varré at the end of his questline.
In the lightless depths lies the grave of an ancient civilization.
It is here Mohg, the Lord of Blood, is building his palace, to be the seat of his coming dynasty named Mohgwyn. And whatever nightmares that may bring.
Map Mohgwyn Palace : Found on a corpse on the stairway leading to the palace, past some Albinaurics that summon giant skeletons. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
The Waygate Teleporter to Mohgwyn Palace is found northwest of the Yelough Anix Ruins in the Consecrated Snowfield . The Waygate is inactive until after you kill the Sanguine Noble NPC, who invades nearby. [ Elden Ring Map Link ] Northwest of Yelough ruins is a circular formation visible on the map, which is a rocky trunk-like spire.  Directly west of that circular spire on the map is the teleporter, but you just drop down a couple ledges near some white logs that form a ramp.
[overall map goes here]
Palace Approach Ledge-Road. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Dynasty Mausoleum Entrance. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Dynasty Mausoleum Midpoint. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Cocoon of the Empyrean [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Mohgwyn Dynasty Mausoleum
Imprisoned Merchant [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Needle Knight Leda (with SOTE DLC)

Smithing Stone (7) x 2
Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone
Somber Smithing Stone (9)
Ash of War: Blood Tax
Haligdrake Talisman +2
Nomadic Warrior's Cookbook (24)
When entering from the waygate in Consecrated Snowfield , you get teleported to a set of caves southeast of Mohgwyn Palace. The caves here are linear, so simply follow the path out and down and you'll be able to grab a Golden Rune (11) and get to the Palace Approach Ledge-Road Site of Grace. Make your way northwest where the slope is littered with Albinaurics and collect a Golden Rune (11) , a Smithing Stone (6) , and five Bloodroses on the way down. At the bottom, you'll emerge into the main blood-filled lake of Mohgwyn Palace.
If you'd like to grab the map fragment for this area to make navigation easier, keep heading north-northwest until you get to a section with three hunting horn-wielding Albinaurics who can summon large spectral skeletons. Once you get past them, head west to find a ruin, and the single corpse sitting in the middle here has the Map (Mohgwyn Palace) . Head up the stairs to quickly grab the Dynasty Mausoleum Entrance Site of Grace.
With our newfound map, it's time to get looting. Back from the slope, you descended earlier from the Palace Approach Ledge-Road, head east to reach the southern section of the lake. On your way, you're likely going to get invaded by a Nameless White Mask . Three of these NPC invaders will attack you at different areas in the blood lake. They have quick attacks and have access to a strong bleed spell that you should try to avoid as best as you can. On the southwestern side of the lake, you'll be able to find the Haligdrake Talisman +2 and a Ghost Glovewort (9) among some graves by the cliff. In the southeast, on the other hand, you can find a Golden Seed and the Nomadic Warrior's Cookbook (24) .
Head back to the northern part of the lake, where you'll find a Smithing Stone (7) , Smithing Stone (8) , and Golden Rune (12) on the western side. In the middle, in front of a large rock pillar is a Hero's Rune (4) . Along the eastern walls are small caves, each filled with loot. In the first cave (from the south) is a Somber Smithing Stone (9) and a Teardrop Scarab that drops the Ash of War: Blood Tax . The second cave is guarded by a few Albinaurics and has a Ghost Glovewort (8) and the Swarm of Flies incantation. The third cave, also guarded by some enemies, houses a Smithing Stone (6) and Golden Rune (13) . Hug the eastern wall north and you'll eventually find a Rune Arc .
Make your way back to the Dynasty Mausoleum Site of Grace and head up the stairs in the southwest. In this area, you'll be encountering a bunch of Putrid Corpses . Many of them are fairly docile, not minding even a shoulder bump, while some will run at you and attempt to explode in your face. Whether you decide to take them out or leave them alone, follow the linear path here, grabbing a Smithing Stone (7) , Numen's Rune , Stanching Boluses , and eight Bloodroses on your way.
You'll eventually get to a group of Putrid Corpses praising the sky in front of a dark doorway. This doorway leads into a very dark cave, so get your lantern or torch ready. At various points, while exploring the cave, you'll get ambushed by Sanguine Nobles . If you die by their hand, you can respawn at the Stake of Marika right outside the cave's doorway. At the first cave split that you encounter, a Sanguine Noble will appear. Head down the south split before heading north to find the Imprisoned Merchant .
When you head north, you'll immediately find a Lord's Rune in front of you. Continue northeast to get to a ledge overlooking a Great Ghost Glovewort . If you shine your torch down, you'll see a hoard of Putrid Corpses gathering around it. None of them, in particular, are the 'explode in your face' kind, so you can simply run past them to grab the glovewort. Next, head west, where you'll encounter another Sanguine Noble and a Smithing Stone (8) among some gravestones. Continue following the path, picking up the Ghost Glovewort (9) on the way, and you'll eventually emerge outside to the Dynasty Mausoleum Midpoint Site of Grace.
Right ahead are a group of Albinaurics and a Sanguine Noble facing up towards a large statue. You can take them out one by one since they don't seem to have situational awareness Attacking one Albirnauric triggers all of them to be hostile. Right in front of the statue is a Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone and if you take a peek on the lower ledge behind the statue, you'll find a Golden Rune (13) . From here, look down over the cliff to see a corpse holding a Golden Seed that you can jump to. Watch the pit between yourself and the rocky platform. From here, you can either go back the long way or fast travel. As of July 2022 the corpse described above is visible but does not hold any item. It is unknown if this true at all times, or if there is a trigger variable in the individual playthrough (total seeds already collected, etc.) If the corpse has no item feel free to forego the jump & skip to the paragraph below.
Turn around, facing northwest, and take the lift ahead up to a large, ruined temple. When you walk in a cutscene plays and the fight against Mohg, Lord of Blood commences. Defeat him and you'll be rewarded with the Remembrance of the Blood Lord and Mohg's Great Rune . The Cocoon of the Empyrean Site of Grace spawns here after his defeat as well.
The Temple of Blood is also known as the Dynasty Mausoleum in Elden Ring. This is where Mohg, Lord of Blood will be. In order to reach this location, ensure you made your way to the underground first and reach Mogwyn Palace which is accessed from a gateway teleporter [ Elden Ring Map Link ]  in the frozen west of Consecrated Snowfield (northwest of the Yelough Anix Ruins and west of where the Sanguine Noble invades you) or by using the Pureblood Knight's Medal given by White Mask Varré at the end of his questline. White Mask Varre will give you this item once you have completed the 3 invasions required for his questline to advance. He will give you the Medal after the reusable Bloody Finger , exhausting his dialogue is necessary. [ Elden Ring Map Here ]
Once you reach the underground, you will find yourself in a cave. Head out of the cave and this is Mohgwyn Palace. Make your way down and you will be heading directly west through the Bloody water until you see the arches that will signify that you have reached the Dynasty Mausoleum. To reach Mohg, Lord of Blood head up through the stairs until you reach the temple structure and head in. There will be a number of enemies along the way. Continue to head west and you will eventually find the doorway leading out. In this next area, there will be a lever where you can activate the lift to reach the Boss arena.
In the lightless depths lies the grave of an ancient civilization. It is here Mohg, the Lord of Blood, is building his palace, to be the seat of his coming dynasty named Mohgwyn. And whatever nightmares that may bring.
- Map Mohgwyn Palace
Mohgwyn Palace is a sub-region located in Siofra River in ELDEN RING (2022) .
7 Crafting Materials
An island in the underground Siofra River, far to the east of Nokron, Eternal City . The grave of an ancient dynasty civilization can be found here, and it is in these ruins that Mohg, Lord of Blood , has chosen to be the seat of his coming Mohgwyn Dynasty . [ 1 ]
A large part of the area is taken up by a blood-red lake, which is the habitat of many abominations. The Mohgwyn Dynasty Mausoleum juts out of the west side of the island, housing the Lord of Blood and his vassals.
There are two possible entrances to this area. The conventional route is via a sending gate on the western edge of Consecrated Snowfield , which leads to a cliff on the southeastern side of the palace. Alternately, by becoming a Pureblood Knight of the Mohgwyn Dynasty, one can obtain a Pureblood Knight's Medal from White Mask Varré , which will teleport the player to the mausoleum entrance at will.

area: Siofra River
chapter: 7
name: Siofra River
Siofra River is a Location in Elden Ring . This underground region can be reached by taking the lift at either the Siofra River Well in Limgrave or Deep Siofra Well in Caelid .
Two great rivers flow beneath the Lands Between, the Siofra and the Ainsel. This vast region is said to be the grave of civilizations that flourished before the Erdtree.
Fragment Siofra River : Map (Siofra River) is found next to the staircase leading up to Hallowhorn Grounds . Elden Ring Map Link .
Ancestral Woods. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Aqueduct-facing Cliffs. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]

Great Waterfall Basin. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]

Night's Sacred Ground. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Nokron, Eternal City. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Siofra River Bank. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Siofra River Well Depths. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Worshippers' Woods. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Night's Sacred Ground
Nokron, Eternal City
Siofra River East Waygate (gives access to north-western section of Siofra River). [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Siofra River South Waygate. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Blaidd - Standing by a ledge, near Hallowhorn Grounds (Siofra River). [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
D's Twin Brother - Found in Nokron, Eternal City , east of the Great Waterfall Basin site of grace. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Nomadic Merchant Siofra River - Found playing music by an unlit campfire in the ruins of Central Siofra River. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Somber Smithing Stone (2)

Ashes of War Ash of War: Square Off
Ash of War: Square Off
Cookbooks Armorer's Cookbook [6]
Armorer's Cookbook [6]
The first time you enter Siofra River is likely by taking the lift down the Siofra River Well in Mistwood . This area is intended for higher levels (50 - 60), with upgrades of +8 and above.
Right at the bottom, you'll find the Siofra River Well Depths Site of Grace and the area here can be looted for two Ghost Glovewort (1) , a Smithing Stone (4) , and a Furlcalling Finger Remedy . The ruin ahead and the cavern areas here are infested with Claymen . They move slowly but be careful when dealing with them in large numbers as they can easily swarm you. In the building and waterfall area here, you can find a Smithing Stone (2) , two Smithing Stone (4) , and a Ghost Glovewort (1) . You can also scale the ruins to get to a Teardrop Scarab that drops Oracle Bubbles . Head into the intact building next to the waterfall area and you'll get taken up to the main section of Siofra River.
Walking forward, you'll eventually get to the Siofra River Bank Site of Grace. Directly east of this Site of Grace, you'll see a pillar with a brazier you can light up. This pillar is one of eight, and all of them are required to be lit to access the Ancestor Spirit boss fight. You can find the coordinates of all 8 pillars on our interactive map in the notes section below. Slightly south from this first pillar, you'll see a large temple ruin. That's the Hallowhorn Grounds and after you've lit all eight pillars, you can interact with the deer carcass inside to take you to the Ancestor Spirit. Make your way to the temple ruin to grab the Map (Siofra River) from the bottom of the staircase. You'll also find a Ghost Glovewort (2) here and the Horn Bow on the supporting structure underneath the Hallowhorn Grounds staircase.
With the map-making navigation easier, backtrack back to the Siofra River Bank Site of Grace and scale the cliff to the southwest to find the Inverted Hawk Heater Shield .  The Armorer's Cookbook (6) can be found on a corpse west of the Siofra River Bank Site of Grace. The waygate here in the south [ Elden Ring Map Link ] just makes traveling to the northeastern side of Siofra River quicker but doesn't take you to any place inaccessible by foot. In the water-filled area to the northeast of this Site of Grace, you can find many upgrade materials, including a Somber Smithing Stone (2) , Smithing Stone (2) , and Ghost Glovewort (2) . Besides these, you can also find a Furlcalling Finger Remedy within a group of Land Octopuses and a Smithing Stone (4) in a room occupied by a Giant Poison Flower . You'll find a Nomadic Merchant on the level above this room and can get to him by scaling the scaffolding just to the east.
Backtrack back to the cliffs east of the Hallowhorn Grounds and if you've started Blaidd or Ranni's quest, you'll be able to find and talk to Blaidd by the cliff's edge [ Elden Ring Map Link ]. East of him, you'll find a pillar of wind that Torrent can use to boost him up onto the ruins nearby. Parkour your way to the end and you'll find a Ghost Glovewort (3) and a Stonesword Key . In the distance, you may be able to see another unreachable ruin to the southeast. You can get there by taking one of the waygates at The Four Belfries . In that ruin, you'll be able to find a Ghost Glovewort (2) and Mottled Necklace .
Head back down from the ruin and continue scaling the cliff northeast. The ruins here will have a Smithing Stone (3) and a Somber Smithing Stone (2) . There's another waygate here, we'll call it the eastern waygate, that you should come back to later. Up ahead, within some ruins is the Worshipper's Woods Site of Grace. Continue scaling up the cliff to find a Smithing Stone (2) and a Teardrop Scarab that drops the Ash of War: Square Off .
From here, scaling the cliffs get scarier as Ancestral Followers with bows and arrows can and will snipe you. Always try to break line of sight of them whenever possible and use the terrain to your advantage to block incoming projectiles. While heading north, there will be a detour you can take to the west where you can find a Golden Seed and a wall ruin you can scale for a Stonesword Key . Follow along the cliff to the north, and you'll come to a small cave behind waterfall. Inside you'll find Rune Arc . Head back off of the detour and continue heading north, where you'll eventually get to the Below the Well Site of Grace. Slightly north will be a Ghost Glovewort (3) and to the east is a staircase with the Clarifying Horn Charm . The staircase leads you to a lift that takes you back up to the surface at Deep Siofra Well in Caelid .
Head back to that eastern waygate we took note of earlier [ Elden Ring Map Link ], and it'll teleport you to the northwestern section of the Siofra River, on a cliff above the waterfall here. Immediately to the north is the Dragonkin Soldier , who you can defeat for the Dragon Halberd , and next to him, under a waterfall is the Marika's Scarseal .
South of the Dragonkin Soldier, in a little ruin, is a bunch of Claymen , a Somber Smithing Stone (5) , and a Teardrop Scarab that drops the Great Oracular Bubble . Outside the ruin (southwest of the Dragonkin Soldier's original position), tucked in a little corner, you can find a Ghost Glovewort (5) . Head back to the spot you teleported to originally, and jump down the ledge here to get to the top of a waterfall. Here you'll find a Ghost Glovewort (4) and the path to get back down safely.
By exploring (and taking a peek at the interactive map using the coordinates below), you should have found all eight pillars that you needed to light up for a boss fight. Head back to the Hallowhorn Grounds and touch the now glowing deer carcass at the very end to get teleported to the Ancestor Spirit fog door. After defeating it, you'll be rewarded with the Ancestral Follower Ashes .
You may have seen buildings and ruins in the sky here and wondered if those areas can be explored. The answer to that is yes! Those buildings are part of Nokron, Eternal City but can only be reached after defeating Starscourge Radahn .
The Gaelic word "Síofra" is pronounced "SHEE-fra" (/ʃifɹə/ or /ʃifrə/), and means "elf", "sprite", or "spirit".
The Ancestor Spirit boss can only be fought if all pillars in the area are interacted with. They are:
By the Siofra River Bank site of grace. [ Map Link ]
In a corner to the west of the first pillar. [ Map Link ]
On a small island in the watery area with the wisps. [ Map Link ]
By a cliff in the eastern part of Siofra River. [ Map Link ]
Just south of the Worshipper's Woods Site of Grace. [ Map Link ]
West of the Worshipper's Woods Site of Grace. [ Map Link ]
On the east side of the path to the Below the Well Site of Grace. [ Map Link ]
On the west side on the path to the Below the Well Site of Grace. [ Map Link ]
After lighting all 8 pillars, you'll see a popup that says "Powers gathers somewhere in horned remains".
Siofra River is an underground region in ELDEN RING (2022) .
One of the two great rivers that flow beneath the Lands Between . Siofra is said to be the grave of civilisations that flourished before the Erdtree [ 1 ] , and evidence of this can be found in the many ancient structures that litter the vast, subterranean space. The towering remains of an ancient dynasty [ 2 ] are prominent throughout the area, inhabited by the Claymen who once served as the dynasty's priests. [ 3 ]
The lower portion of Siofra River can be accessed via the Siofra River Well in Limgrave 's Mistwood. This wooded area is home to spectral Ancestral Followers , who live alongside a diverse assortment of wildlife. The remains of a great, horned beast can be found in the Hallowhorn Grounds. Interacting with the remains after lightning the flames scattered throughout the area will facilitate an encounter with an Ancestor Spirit . This area also connects to Caelid via the Deep Siofra Well .
Towering over the woodland is Nokron, one of the Eternal Cities inhabited by the Nox . Nokron is initially inaccessible, however a route is opened when a star falls upon Limgrave, following the defeat of Starscourge Radahn . The Fingerslayer Blade , a treasure of Nokron said to be able to harm the Greater Will and its vassal Fingers [ 4 ] , is found within Night's Sacred Ground, beneath a chair-crypt that holds a vast corpse. This upper region also houses another Hallowhorn Grounds, from which the Regal Ancestor Spirit can be encountered. Nearby, the aqueduct hides a coffin which can be used to access Deeproot Depths , guarded by a pair of Valiant Gargoyles .
To the east, on a separate island within the vast cavern, are the ruins of an ancient palace. It is here that Mohg, Lord of Blood has established the seat of his coming Mohgwyn Dynasty, and all the nightmares it may bring. [ 5 ]
The name Síofra is pronounced "SHEE-fra". It is a girl's name in the Irish Gaelic language, meaning "sprite, fairy, elf, changeling". [ 6 ] The síobhraí were believed to be fairies that had been left in place of a human (typically a child) stolen by other fairies. The name Ainsel has similar origins. The word Síofra could also refer to "weakling" in some contexts. [ 6 ]
The word Síofra could also refer to "weakling" in some contexts. [ 6 ]

area: Shadow Keep
chapter: 35
name: Messmer the Impaler
Messmer the Impaler is a Demigod Boss in Shadow of the Erdtree . He is the leader of the crusade against the Hornsent people, killing their Divine Beasts and burning the Tower Settlement . He is revealed to be Queen Marika 's son, secreted away and abandoned in the Land of Shadow. In combat, he utilizes Fire-based Spells and a Greatspear . This is not an optional boss as players need to defeat him to advance in Shadow of the Erdtree .
See Messmer the Impaler Lore
Those stripped of the Grace of Gold shall all meet death. In the embrace of Messmer's flame.
This is not an optional boss
The second phase of this fight is called "Base Serpent Messmer"
Closest Site of Grace: Dark Chamber Entrance
Multiplayer is allowed for this boss
You can summon Spirit Ashes for this boss
NPC Summons: Hornsent and Swordhand of Night Jolan . Both summon signs are within the arena and do not affect Messmer's HP, but choosing one will prevent you from accessing the other one for the fight. Hornsent's sign is inside the arena to the right. It only appears if you've spoken to him to get him to advance into the Shadow Keep after Miquella's charm is broken. It disappears if you cooperate with Leda to kill him. Jolan's sign is inside to the left. It only appears after you've sounded one of the bells at the Finger Ruins to advance Ymir's questline, and it disappears after you've defeated Metyr, Mother of Fingers .
Hornsent's sign is inside the arena to the right. It only appears if you've spoken to him to get him to advance into the Shadow Keep after Miquella's charm is broken. It disappears if you cooperate with Leda to kill him.
Jolan's sign is inside to the left. It only appears after you've sounded one of the bells at the Finger Ruins to advance Ymir's questline, and it disappears after you've defeated Metyr, Mother of Fingers .
Messmer the Impaler can be found in the Shadow Keep Specimen Storehouse, near the Dark Chamber Entrance Site of Grace. [ Map Link ]
Parryable : Yes, but 3 parries are required per stance break
Is vulnerable to a critical hit after being stance broken or parried
Damage: Standard , Slash , Strike , Pierce , Fire
Drops 400,000, Remembrance of the Impaler , Messmer's Kindling
The negation numbers are the % of your damage that gets blocked. For example, if a negation is 60, 40% of that damage by that type will go through and 60% will be negated. Bigger number = less damage. A negation of 100 means no damage goes through, and a negation of -100 mean the enemy takes 2x damage from that source. 0 means damage goes pretty much as is.
Poison : 435 / 533 / 823 / 1280
Scarlet Rot : 435 / 533 / 823 / 1280
Hemorrhage : 435 / 533 / 823 / 1280
Frostbite : 316 / 358 / 456 / 746 / 1203
The resistance numbers are the buildup amount to trigger it. For example, if a resistance is 100 you must deal 100 points of the given buildup to trigger it. Note that these go down over time, and increase each time the effect procs. The values after the "/"s indicate the increased resistances after each successive proc.
The Best Tips for Messmer the Impaler:
Recommended summons: Mimic Tear
Recommended armaments: Staff of the Guilty , Bloodfiend's Arm , Great Stars , Reduvia , Rivers of Blood , Heater Shield , Fingerprint Stone Shield (Fire affinity)
Recommended ashes of war: Lion's Claw , Giant Hunt , Cragblade , Golden Vow Any skill, that lowers the player's position is good to both attack and avoid damage (Messmer mostly go for spear in forward, not down).
Any skill, that lowers the player's position is good to both attack and avoid damage (Messmer mostly go for spear in forward, not down).
Recommended spells: Flame Protect Me , Impenetrable Thorns , Golden Vow , Swarm of Flies , Borealis's Mist
Recommended talismans: Flamedrake Talisman +3 , Great-Jar's Arsenal , Lord of Blood's Exultation
Other items: Albinauric Set , Briar Set , Greenspill Crystal Tear , Stonebarb Cracked Tear
Messmer, the Impaler, looks down over you as a towering humanoid boss with long limbs and a massive spear, accompanied by a red snake coiled around his body. His attacks are imbued with fire damage, making him a formidable opponent who relies heavily on his spear's reach and agility. To proceed in this boss fight, readiness for his swift movements is important. As a melee character, your strategy should begin by evading the fireball he summons upon leaping into battle. Pay attention while he's in mid-air and watch out for the white smoke that will appear around him. As soon as you see the white smoke, get ready to dodge to avoid the impact of his landing. Sometimes, the area around him will explode as soon as he lands, so you need to do a second roll going forward, and then get a free hit while he's recovering.
For the most part, you will want to stay close to him because of the long reach of his spear and flame spells. When you're fighting Messmer at a close range, you will have more chance of retaliating and chipping away at his health while he recovers. Engaging Messmer patiently is the best strategy to effectively take him down. When he finishes a string of attacks, focus on breaking his poise by pulling off heavy attacks for a chance to deal significant damage.
Due to Messmer's speed, you need to approach him with the right mix of patience and aggressiveness. This boss can quickly transition between offense and defense by quickly disengaging with a leap after he finishes a move. Be aware that Messmer counters swiftly after sustaining damage, too. His speed and precision in combat are his biggest asset, requiring observation of his movements to avoid being overwhelmed.
One of the basic moves that Messmer makes is the Flaming Spear Lunge , this is when you see him leap into the air as he thrusts his weapon and then crash on the ground with his spear engulfed in flames. This movement is dodgeable and will take some time to get used to but you can do this by rolling forward as he thrusts his weapon, waiting for the delay as he prepares to crash, and then rolling forward again to stay close to him. This allows you to attack him while he's recovering.
Next up is Messmer's Flaming Assault , which is a 3-string attack where he combines this move with his flame spells. While the spear is engulfed in flames, he will cast spells with his flaming right hand and he will do the following attacks:
When he swings his right arm, he will use a fire spell that affects the immediate area in front of him
Quick Thrust with the spear, followed by a quick jump to reposition himself and another spear thrust
Flame Spell where he twists in place to swing his spear and creates a flame surrounding him
To dodge this move, you need to learn when he will do this by paying attention to his arm. If you see him cross it over and it's engulfed in flames, he will do this attack. To dodge, roll to the right if you're too close, otherwise, rolling backward is safe enough. You need to dodge as soon as he swings his arm engulfed in flames. For the second part, roll forward as soon as he thrusts with his spear, wait for the delay as he jumps to reposition himself, then dodge as soon as he lands on his new spot. Finally, for the last step, you can dodge this either going to the right, backward, or forward. It is recommended to roll forward so you can get close to him and get a free attack while he's recovering.
Messmer's powerful Grab attack will kill most players instantly. It is important to dodge this attack to avoid an instant death. When Messmer's hand begins glowing, that is the sign he is about to grab you. Once he begins to descend his arm to grab you, roll either to the sides or forward. This will leave an opening to hit him. If you can break his stance, remember to go in front of him and just lightly press R1 (once) to do a light attack. This will do a critical hit and can deal a lot more damage than any attacks.
The next basic attack combo is a 4-String attack where he throws out a flame spell in close range, followed by 2 quick thrust, and a dash flame 360. To dodge this move, you will roll backward to dodge the flame spell, then wait out a bit, and roll toward him for the quick thrust, wait for the delay here, and roll toward him again for the second quick thrust, and then, roll forward towards him for the 360. This is the recommended move so you can get some free hits while he recovers. There's a variation of this move where he adds a Flame swipe and a backward 360 at the end. To dodge these additional moves, you would want to roll backward for the flame spell, hold out a bit, then roll forward again for the 360 Flame spell. This will also allow you to get some hits on him.
Finally, one more move that is worth mentioning is the Messmer's Assault which consists of multiple string attacks and usually ends with an explosion at the end. The move will begin with Messmer jumping up with an Airborne Sweep 360, followed by a quick spear swing, an upward thrust to launch himself upwards and end the move with a plunge to create an explosion. This move will create spears exploding from the ground. To dodge this move, you will do the following per string of attack:
Roll to your right back
Roll towards him to be point blank or back to mitigate damage
Again, roll towards than him and punish/heal, since recovery is long
There's also a variation of this move called Airborne Sweeps Combo , which is more manageable to dodge.
As the battle goes on and Messmer's health reaches the halfway mark, the fight stops. The fight pauses and a cutscene shows Messmer kneeling and performing a ritualistic act, removing one of his own eyes and shattering it, causing the area to glow red. This indicates the beginning of Phase 2.
Base Serpent Messmer steps up once you've depleted Messmer the Impaler's health to 50%. His health remains at 50% at the start of the fight. In this new phase, Base Serpent Messmer not only retains his old form's original attacks but also introduces a set of new, more lethal attacks with the help of the Abyssal Serpent revealed after he gouged out his golden eye.
The battle begins with the Serpent Explosive Bite which is where he turns into a big serpent with Scarlet Rot crawling on its skin. The Serpent bites down on the ground and as the fang hits, the ground beneath it will explode. Then, Messmer will revert to his humanoid form. While in this state, he executes his attacks with increased speed compared to his previous form, making him a more powerful boss.
Among the fresh additions to his move set is a serpent transformation that rises up to the ground to throw a fireball. However, one of his most powerful attacks is the Fire Grab . This technique has his right hand engulfed in flames. He'll unleash a torrent of fire from his grasp while impaling his spear to you with flames, almost guaranteeing instant death.
Proceeding in this battle requires a slow approach to Base Serpent Messmer's abilities and vulnerabilities. Capitalize on the small opening right after he uses Serpent Fireball to unleash a ton of heavy strikes, increasing your damage output. When engaging his humanoid form, maintain an aggressive approach but be ready to switch to defensive the moment he transforms into his serpent form. His serpent attacks pack a punch, requiring you to dodge and counter. To emphasize this, he can also summon the Serpent at will, while he retains his humanoid form. He will stand in place, and raise his right arm to beckon the Serpent. The Serpent will strike fast with a bite and then vanishes right after. Messmer is ready to disengage as soon as the serpent vanishes. You can roll forward as the Serpent prepares for a bite so you can get closer to Messmer.
There's also the variation of the Triple Serpent Bite where he fully transforms into a Serpent to execute 3 successive Serpent Bite attacks. During this attack, you can dodge it by rolling forward as the serpent strikes. The Serpent's head will linger after the third strike, giving an opening for you to attack, then, as Messmer reverts to his humanoid form, you can strike him again for the second time as he materializes.
One thing to note when Messmer transforms into the Serpent is when he creates a large pool of red liquid on the ground. If you can't see Messmer in the middle, this means that he will attack you with a single Serpent Bite , where you can hit him twice (similar to the ending of the Triple Serpent Bite ). However, when he casts the large pool, and you can see him in the center, there will be 3 Serpent heads floating near him and will do 3 strikes. The first one will strike fast to slam its fangs on the ground in an attempt to bite you, followed by a delayed attack from the second which deals 2 attacks, then, the third one is almost simultaneous with Messmer's attack. Messmer will follow up with a Flaming Spear Lunge attack , where he flies up, pauses mid-air, and slams his spear onto the ground to create an explosive area around him.
When dealing with this version, the first thing you will do is to run away from him. Run to your right to create a distance between you and them. Then, roll forward as the first snake bites down, then roll twice to your right with the second snake attack, and finally, roll as Messmer lands on the ground with his Flaming Spear Lunge . This gives you enough space to roll away from the explosion from his spear. However, he usually continues this move with spears erupting from the ground. Make sure to roll towards him as the ground beneath him explodes. When you are within attacking range, you can land an attack while he recovers.
Above all, remain alert when you notice Base Serpent Messmer's right hand glowing with flames it's a sign of his powerful Fire Grab. React by backing away and rolling back, giving you a high chance to evade this lethal attack.
Consider bringing heavy fire-resistant equipment for this fight. Upon entering the arena, roll twice to avoid Messmer's initial attack, and the aftershock. Opportunities for ranged attacks will be minimal without summoning spirit ashes. Given an opportunity, dragon incantations such as Borealis's Mist and Ekzykes's Decay are rather damaging, as Messmer is weak to frost and susceptible to rot. Bear in mind that Messmer's phase two transformation cleanses his rot status.
One of the safest ways to kill Messmer from a distance is with repeated casts of Impenetrable Thorns enhanced by Beloved Stardust . In addition, Greatshield Soldier ashes will hold the boss's attention for a decent amount of time, providing an opening to cast Thorns. For players that prefer DPS summons (Tiche), equipping Glovewort Crystal Tear will end the fight a lot quicker.
Voice Actor: Jack Barton
According to Messmer's Kindling , his younger sister who "bore a vision of fire" is implied to Melina .
A 46 cm figure of Messmer the Impaler comes in the Collector's Edition of Shadow of the Erdtree .
His right eye appears similar to the Marika's Scarseal talisman.
Before you take on this fight, you want to consider summoning Hornsent to progress his questline. If you fail to do so, he will feel the burden of being a failure because he couldn't exact vengeance for his people. His summon is a good call, since it deals damage Messmer is neutral to, does not count towards summons (therefore does not affect healthbar), plus he can inflict Blood Loss.
His summon is a good call, since it deals damage Messmer is neutral to, does not count towards summons (therefore does not affect healthbar), plus he can inflict Blood Loss.
During 2nd phase, The serpent and Messmer does share a single healthbar, but does not share one hurtbox. Therefore, player can essentially double their damage by hitting them at the same time. This goes for Status Effects as well.
Messmer is the only boss in Elden Ring to fight left-handed, which can make him more difficult to block and parry; Messmer tends to strike his foes on the right side of their bodies, while the Tarnished hold shields in their left hands.

Messmer the Impaler is a mandatory boss in Shadow of the Erdtree . He is a Demigod who is encountered in the Shadow Keep .
1 Overview 1.1 The Crusade
2 Boss Fight 2.1 Phase One 2.2 Phase Two
Messmer was a son of Queen Marika the Eternal . He was born with a dark serpent inside him that threatened to subsume him, which Messmer has called the Abyssal Serpent ; Marika was able to contain its influence by removing one of Messmer's eyes and replacing it with a seal of grace . Ever since his accursed birth, Messmer was in constant company of the winged serpents . [ 1 ]
Much like his younger sister , Messmer bore a vision of fire . [ 2 ] He despised this flame , and time and time again hoped to rid himself of it, but found that it ever burned. [ 3 ]
At some point, Messmer befriended Commander Gaius , an Albinauric who like him was cursed from birth. Gaius would go on to command Messmer's armies in the wars to come. They both acted as "older brothers" to General Radahn . [ 4 ]
Before the Shattering , Messmer was charged by his mother with carrying out a purge of the tower -dwelling hornsent of the Land of Shadow , who did not bear the light of grace. At some point during his Crusade , Messmer was abandoned in the Land of Shadow , but continued to zealously enforce the purge even though Marika ceased all contact with him. Messmer came to reside in a fort known as the Shadow Keep .
Key in his army were the Black Knights , commanded by Black Knight Commander Andreas and his son and second-in-command Black Knight Captain Huw . Although they remained devoutly loyal to Messmer despite his abandonment by Marika, they ultimately rebelled upon learning of his serpentine nature. The pair were imprisoned in an underground tomb , and despite his betrayal, Messmer mourned the loss of Huw, whom he had considered a brother-in-arms. [ 5 ] [ 6 ] His Fire Knights rebelled against burning the Ancient Ruins of Rauh and the destruction of the specimens in the Specimen Storehouse . [ 7 ] [ 8 ]
Eventually, an intruder invaded Messmer's stronghold, the Shadow Keep, and was noticed by the demigod's serpents, who identified them as a Tarnished Champion and a pretender to the title of Elden Lord . Though surprised that a Tarnished had come to the Land of Shadow, Messmer nonetheless attacked them to continue enforcing the purge. However, the serpentine demigod struggled overcome the Tarnished. Unwilling to accept "a Lord devoid of light" and desperate to regain the upper hand, Messmer ripped out Marika's seal and unleashed the power of the Abyssal Serpent, allowing him to frequently transform into a massive serpent. Nonetheless, the Tarnished was able to land a fatal blow upon Messmer. Realizing that his mother had indeed forsaken him, Messmer used his dying breath to curse Marika.
Messmer is not an optional boss as he must be defeated in order to acquire Messmer's Kindling that would allow the burning of the Sealing Tree.
Hornsent can be summoned for this boss. His summon sign is found inside of the boss room, and will appear if the player decides to side with him against Leda or ignore this part of Leda's quest.
Messmer's attacks are imbued with fire damage, making him a formidable opponent who relies heavily on his spear's reach and agility. To proceed in this boss fight, readiness for his swift movements is important. When fighting Messmer at a close range, you will have more chances of retaliating and chipping away at his health while he recovers. Engaging Messmer patiently is the best strategy to effectively take him down. When he finishes a string of attacks, focus on breaking his poise by pulling off heavy attacks for a chance to deal significant damage.
Because of his speed, Messmer can quickly transition between offense and defense by quickly disengaging with a leap after he finishes a move. Be aware that Messmer counters swiftly after sustaining damage, too. As his speed and precision in combat are his biggest asset, observation is required to avoid being overwhelmed. Once Messmer's health is half-depleted, a cutscene will trigger, which will start Messmer's second phase.
During the second phase, Messmer remains at 50% and not only retains his original attacks, but also introduces a set of new and more lethal attacks with the help of the Abyssal Serpent. Among the fresh additions to his move set is the ability to transform into a big red serpent that rises up to the ground to throw a fireball or do bite attacks. He also suffers a major weakness to Holy damage, likely a result of embracing the Abyssal Serpent that her mother sealed away in his eye.
Messmer, much like his siblings Malenia and Miquella , was also born cursed. Exactly when he was born remains unknown as all mention of him was erased by his mother after she abandoned him in the Land of Shadow. Messmer did, however, act as an elder brother to Radahn , who is older than the twins, making Messmer at the very least their older brother.
Messmer did, however, act as an elder brother to Radahn , who is older than the twins, making Messmer at the very least their older brother.
In one of the item descriptions of Messmer's Kindling it says that "His younger sister also bore a vision of fire", possibly relating to Melina . She serves as kindling to burn the Erdtree, and so too did Messmer have kindling capable of burning a tree.
While unconfirmed, Messmer is implied to be a child of Radagon : He is red-haired, a trait commonly seen in Radagon's children. He was born cursed, much like Malenia and Miquella, who were born "from a single god". There are four butterfly items shown in the game and its expansion: Nascent - Miquella, Aeonian - Malenia, Smouldering - Melina, and Black Pyrefly - Messmer. Radagon's motif is present in the soundtrack of Messmer's fight.
He is red-haired, a trait commonly seen in Radagon's children.
He was born cursed, much l

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Needle Knight Leda
Needle Knight Leda is an NPC in Elden Ring . Needle Knight Leda is the ringleader of Miquella's band of followers in the Realm of Shadow. She is an NPC exclusive to Shadow of the Erdtree and does not appear in the base game. She was part of an order of knights under the service of Miquella known as the needle knights.
See Needle Knight Leda Lore
Come now. Touch the withered arm, and travel to the realm of shadow.
I will not be far behind, may we meet again.
Needle Knight Leda can be found at Mohgwyn Palace , besides the cocoon of Miquella .
This NPC moves to Highroad Cross Site of Grace after entering Scadu Altus . [ Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
This NPC moves near the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom Site of Grace in Enir-Ilim : After assisting her twice, or assisting Hornsent or Sir Ansbach , she will move there. If you attack her at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace, she will vanish and move there prematurely. If the Sealing Tree in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh is burned, she will move there on her own.
After assisting her twice, or assisting Hornsent or Sir Ansbach , she will move there.
If you attack her at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace, she will vanish and move there prematurely.
If the Sealing Tree in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh is burned, she will move there on her own.
Can be summoned for the fight against Rellana, Twin Moon Knight in Castle Ensis. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ] NOTE : After Miquella's charm is broken, Leda will no longer be an optional summon against this boss.
Defeat Mohg, Lord of Blood (base-game boss). After the fight, you gain access to the Cocoon of the Empyrean Grace (the withered arm/cocoon near Mohgwyn).
Speak with Needle Knight Leda at that Cocoon and exhaust dialogue. She prompts you to touch Miquella’s withered arm to be transported to the Realm of Shadow — this begins her involvement in the DLC.
Progress through Scadu Altus / Belurat Tower Settlement and defeat the Divine Beast “Dancing Lion” in Belurat Tower Settlement . After the boss, look for Leda’s Tower-of-Shadow message in the area. Pick it up — it’s the first in-world note that points where Leda is headed.
You can find a second Leda message at Castle Ensis near the Castle Ensis Checkpoint .
You are then able to summon Leda for the Rellana, Twin Moon Knight fight in Castle Ensis (optional).
Leda appears at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace ( Scadu Altus , directly north of Castle Ensis ). Exhaust her dialogue there; you’ll usually find Hornsent nearby as well. Resting at the grace a couple of times can be necessary to advance their dialogue options.
Travel to Shadow Keep (where the “great rune” or charm is affecting NPCs). You must break Miquella’s charm — this is triggered by approaching the Shadow Keep properly or crossing the bridge east of Bonny Village ; once the charm is dispelled, return to Highroad Cross and talk to Leda again for new dialogue. This unlocks the next branch where Leda either attacks Hornsent or you defend Hornsent (or later Ansbach ).
Inside the Shadow Keep , just before the Storehouse, First Floor Site of Grace , near the Fire Knight guarding the lift, there will be two summon signs.
You can choose to: Help Hornsent ( defend him in Shadow Keep): you get Ash of War: Swift Slash as well as 1x Leda's Rune . This locks you out of the Lacerating Crossed-Tree and Retaliatory Crossed-Tree talismans. Help Leda ( attack Hornsent): you get Lacerating Crossed-Tree Talismen, but you lose the Ash of War: Swift Slash and rune.
Help Hornsent ( defend him in Shadow Keep): you get Ash of War: Swift Slash as well as 1x Leda's Rune . This locks you out of the Lacerating Crossed-Tree and Retaliatory Crossed-Tree talismans.
Help Leda ( attack Hornsent): you get Lacerating Crossed-Tree Talismen, but you lose the Ash of War: Swift Slash and rune.
After the Hornsent/Leda clash, you will now be able to choose whether to invade or assist Ansbach.
You can choose to: Help Leda (attack Ansbach ): you will get Ansbach's Longbow as well as Ansbach's Set . Help Ansbach (defend him ): you get Ansbach's Longbow and 1x Leda's Rune .
Help Leda (attack Ansbach ): you will get Ansbach's Longbow as well as Ansbach's Set .
Help Ansbach (defend him ): you get Ansbach's Longbow and 1x Leda's Rune .
If you skip the Hornsent fight (or handle it a certain way), Hornsent can be summonable for the Messmer the Impaler fight.
Leda will reappear at the top of Enir-Ilim , directly after the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom Site of Grace, where an NPC battle occurs (the big multi-ally fight). Leda's Armor and Leda's Sword will be lootable from her corpse.
Lockout conditions for Needle Knight Leda's Quest: Her summon/invasion signs in Shadow Keep for Hornsent (near the burning boats) will disappear once you enter Messmer the Impaler's arena from the Dark Chamber Entrance Site of Grace. Exercise caution as the boss fog will not be present the first time you encounter Messmer. Make sure to respond to the summon signs beforehand to prevent yourself from locking out from the rewards related to Hornsent.
Her summon/invasion signs in Shadow Keep for Hornsent (near the burning boats) will disappear once you enter Messmer the Impaler's arena from the Dark Chamber Entrance Site of Grace. Exercise caution as the boss fog will not be present the first time you encounter Messmer. Make sure to respond to the summon signs beforehand to prevent yourself from locking out from the rewards related to Hornsent.
It is possible to get all the unique items from her questline and Sir Ansbach/Hornsent's in a minimum of two playthroughs. See the " How to Optimize Rewards" section below for more info. It is NOT possible to get all of the items from the three NPCs in a single playthrough, as whatever decision you pick, you will unavoidably lock yourself out of certain items.
Leda wears a modified version of the Oathseeker Set with a unique Chest Armor signifying her allegiance to Miquella.
Leda's Rune seems to imply that Leda is a Tarnished .
NOTE: Patch 1.13 increased the stats and status of Needle Knight Leda when summoned in some areas.
The quest begins after defeating Mohg, Lord of Blood in the base game (additionally, Starscourge Radahn will have to be defeated). You will find Needle Knight Leda , a character donning a white robe standing next to Miquella's bloody cocoon in the Mohgwyn Dynasty Mausoleum. Talk to her to preface your journey to the Realm of Shadow. She introduces herself as Leda and invites you to join her, and other followers of Miquella the Kind to trace his path. To start your journey, she tells you to touch the withered arm. You will then be transported to the Shadow Realm and begin the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC .
You won't see Leda again for a while; however, you can find two of her messages which mention where she is headed and give insight to Miquella's intentions.
The first of Leda's messages ( Tower of Shadow Message ) you find is in Belurat Tower Settlement. After defeating the Divine Beast Dancing Lion , take the lift up and follow the path towards the stairs. Take the stairs up and to the right is the letter [ See the Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ].
The second of Leda's messages ( Castle Cross Message ) can be found later on after traversing through Castle Ensis. Miquella's Cross can be found, and Leda's message is right next to it [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ].
Needle Knight Leda is found at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ] near Hornsent . Speak to both of them and exhaust all dialogue options. Hornsent will provide the New Cross Map showing the location of four additional Miquella's Crosses .
(Optional) Monk's Missive and the "May the Best Win" gesture can also be found at the Site of Grace. These items are important for progressing Dryleaf Dane's questline. After meeting Dryleaf Dane, you can return to Leda to learn more about him.
To progress Leda's questline, Miquella's great rune must be broken. Triggering the event will display the message, "Somewhere a great rune has been broken. And so too, a powerful charm," . Breaking the great rune will not affect you but will advance several NPC questlines, such as Leda's. To trigger the event:
Enter Shadow Keep through the front entrance at Messmer's Army Encampment. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
Pass Bonny Village and head east in Scadu Altus , then continue north from the Bridge Leading to the Village Site of Grace. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
After breaking the great rune, return to Leda at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ] to unlock new dialogue. She will ask you who she should pursue, but your choice will not affect the game's outcome.
Suggesting Thiollier will lead Leda to conclude that he is not a threat.
Suggesting Hornsent will cause Leda to acknowledge his desire for vengeance. She will initially hesitate but will eventually agree to pursue him.
Suggesting no one will end the conversation, but Leda will ultimately decide to go after Hornsent regardless.
NOTE : It is NOT possible to get all of the unique items from Ansbach and Hornsent in a single playthrough. Whichever decisions you pick below, you will unavoidably lock yourself out of certain items. You will need a minimum of two playthroughs to acquire all items, see the "How to Optimize Rewards" section below for suggested paths.
Once Leda has informed you of her decision to pursue Hornsent , head to Shadow Keep. Just before the elevator to the Storehouse, First Floor Site of Grace in Shadow Keep (also reached by continuing up for a while from the Main Gate Plaza Site of Grace), on the battlements with the burning boats you will find two summoning signs. They'll be in the south, near a set of stairs.
If you help Leda ( the red summon ): You will kill Hornsent, ending his questline there. You will be rewarded with Falx , Hornsent's Set , and (after speaking to Leda again at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace) Lacerating Crossed-Tree . This will lock you out of getting Ash of War: Swift Slash , it will make a later boss fight easier, and it will prevent Hornsent from assisting you against Messmer the Impaler .
If you help Hornsent ( the gold summon ): Hornsent's questline will continue, and your next encounter with Leda will be in Enir-Ilim . You will be rewarded with Ash of War: Swift Slash and Leda's Rune . This will lock you out of getting Lacerating Crossed-Tree , Retaliatory Crossed-Tree , and Ansbach's Longbow .
If you help neither : Hornsent's and Leda's questlines will both continue after you defeat Messmer the Impaler . This will lock you out of getting Lacerating Crossed-Tree and Ash of War: Swift Slash .
If you helped Leda kill Hornsent , or enter the Messmer the Impaler boss room without assisting either, Leda will move on to target Sir Ansbach . Talk to Leda again in Scadu Altus and she will inform you that she is targeting him next. Return to Storehouse, First Floor and nearby Ansbach you will find two summon signs. You must give Sir Ansbach the Secret Rite Scroll and complete the portion of his quest in the Shadow Keep in order for the summon signs to appear.
If you help Leda ( the red summon ): You will kill Sir Ansbach, ending his questline there. You will be rewarded with Ansbach's Longbow , Ansbach's Set , and (after speaking to Leda again at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace) Retaliatory Crossed-Tree . This will lock you out of getting Furious Blade of Ansbach , Obsidian Lamina , and Leda's Rune , and prevent you from summoning Sir Ansbach in Enir-Ilim .
If you help Sir Ansbach ( the gold summon ): Sir Ansbach's questline will continue. You will be rewarded with Ansbach's Longbow and Leda's Rune . This will lock you out of getting Retaliatory Crossed-Tree . He will be summonable in Enir-Ilim .
If you do nothing : Sir Ansbach's questline will continue, and he will still be summonable later on in Enir-Ilim . This will lock you out of getting Retaliatory Crossed-Tree and Ansbach's Longbow .
If you aided Leda twice, speak to her again and exhaust her dialogue at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace. After resting or leaving the area, she will move to Enir-Ilim . If you aided either Hornsent or Sir Ansbach, she will move to Enir-Ilim on her own.
In your final encounter with Leda, she will try to stop you once you reach the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom Site of Grace in Enir-Ilim . After the battle with the NPC allies , you'll be able to loot Leda's Armor and Leda's Sword from her corpse.
NOTE : as of ver. 1.12.3, this encounter is bugged. If you are summoned as a furled finger and die during the battle, you will drop your runes as if you were the host. You can reclaim your runes in the place you died in your own world, but if you forget to do so before being summoned as a cooperator and dying again, the runes will be permanently lost.
It is possible to get all of the items related to Leda, Hornsent, and Sir Ansbach in a minimum of two playthroughs:
Ahh, were you guided here by Kindly Miquella? I am Leda, and like you, I was guided by faith along his honorable path. Touch the withered arm, and you too will be transported. To the realm of shadow, where Miquella the Kind now dwells. My compatriots are there already. Like us, they have heard Kindly Miquella's call. If you can find them, they are certain to lend you aid.
Come now, touch the withered arm, and travel to the realm of shadow. I will not be far behind, may we meet again.

They were drawn here by Miquella the Kind, much like you, following the path he himself trod. You must have been surprised to see such a band, each come from places far and wide…

Doubtless they’d have all come to blows at first glance… Were it not for the charm of Kindly Miquella put upon us. This is what allows us to serve together. The work of a living god.
We are all utterly captivated by Kindly Miquella. It is what binds us together.

There’s no cause for concern… Dane’s reluctance to speak to you is no fault of your own. We’ve been acquainted for quite some time, but even I am yet to hear his voice. Perhaps one day.

A cleansing by fire. It’s no wonder the hornsent holds the Erdtree in contempt. That aside, man is by nature a creature of conquest. And in this regard, the tower folk are no different. They were never saints. They just happened to be on the losing side of a war. But it’s still a wretched shame.

Right. It’s time to being ferreting out… Those of us who don’t deserve to be at Kindly Miquella’s side… (Suggest Thiollier) Thiollier, hmm. It’s true, his devotion to St. Trina is plain to see. I fear his vows to Miquella the Kind are merely secondary. But for this very reason, Thiollier is incapable of betrayal. St. Trina’s love for Kind Miquella is boundless. She is, after all, his other half. Or perhaps her feelings go beyond even that. Even if she was left behind, I doubt her heart would waver. (Suggest Hornsent) The hornsent, hmm. On one hand, he trusts Miquella the Kind to bring salvation to his people. Perhaps your concerns are well-founded after all. Allow me some time to mull it over.

Needle Knight Leda is an NPC in Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree . Standing out among her fellows for her single-minded devotion to Lord Miquella , Leda is the de-facto leader of the Empyrean's followers, guiding the Tarnished into the Land of Shadow .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
A member of the Needle Knights, an order devoted to Miquella of the Haligtree . [ 1 ] Leda was guided by Miquella to the Realm of Shadow alongside her compatriots, [ 2 ] all of whom were under the influence of a powerful charm which compelled them to follow the Empyrean. [ 3 ] While in the Realm of Shadow, Leda followed the trail of crosses left by Miquella, discovering in the process that he sought the Gate of Divinity, which was sealed away by a tree. [ 4 ]
The Tarnished encounters Leda in Mohgwyn Palace , where she urges them to touch the withered arm in Miquella's cocoon to enter the Realm of Shadow. Within the Land of Shadow, the Tarnished meets Leda and her various compatriots on their search for Miquella, seeking out the crosses to ascertain his route. However, the charm inflicted upon the compatriots is broken when Miquella's Great Rune is shattered. [ 5 ] Leda's devotion to Miquella is unshaken, but she begins to suspect her compatriots of disloyalty. [ 6 ] Leda sets about ferreting out those she believes undeserving of a place at Miquella's side, [ 7 ] with her potential targets being the Hornsent and Sir Ansbach . The Tarnished may assist her in her hunt, or side with her opponents.
After gaining access to Enir-Ilim , the Tarnished encounters Leda for a final time on the way to the Gate of Divinity. Leda deduces that the Tarnished was never guided by Miquella, and concludes that they were instead following the guidance of the Erdtree . [ 8 ] Viewing the Tarnished as a threat to Miquella's ascension, she challenges them. [ 9 ] The Tarnished triumphs over Leda and her remaining allies, and continues to the top of the tower.
1. Defeat both Starscourge Radahn and Mohg, Lord of Blood . Then rest on the nearest Site of Grace . Leda will appear in front of Miquella's cocoon at Mohgywn Dynasty Mausoleum and urge to interact with it.
2. Defeat the Divine Beast Dancing Lion , climb up the stairs in the adjacent room and collect the item on the top; a letter from Leda.
3. Defeat Rellana, Twin Moon Knight , Leda can be summoned for this fight. After the fight, you can find Leda again in the next Site of Grace.
4. Return to the same place and speak to Leda after visiting the entrance of the Shadow Keep .
5. Speaking with Leda and choose an option:
Suggest Thiollier -> Nothing happens?
Suggest Hornsent -> She goes after Hornsent
Suggest no one -> she goes after Hornsent
Rest on the site of grace and speak with Leda again after choosing an option.
6. (If she goes after Hornsent) you can find a summon signs in the Shadow Keep.
7. She can be found at the Storehouse, First Floor where Sir Ansbach is.
8. Regardless of any choices made, Leda will be finally found at Enir-Ilim , where she will be waiting for a quick chat with the player.
Leda in the Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree promotional poster.

area: Shadow Keep
chapter: 35
name: Sir Ansbach
Sir Ansbach is an NPC in Elden Ring . Sir Ansbach is a former servant of Mohg, Lord of Blood and his dynasty , but is now part of the group that follows Miquella through the Realm of Shadow. Sir Ansbach is an NPC exclusive to Shadow of the Erdtree and does not appear in the base game.
See Sir Ansbach Lore
You must be the Tarnished. I am Sir Ansbach. Formerly in service to Lord Mohg. But now, like you, guided by Miquella.
Sir Ansbach can be found at a small camp, just outside Belurat, Tower Settlement . He can be found alongside Moore . [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
This NPC moves to Shadow Keep - Storehouse, First Floor [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ].
This NPC leaves his location at Storehouse, First Floor after giving him the Secret Rite Scroll .
Sir Ansbach can be summoned to assist in the NPC battle at the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom of Enir-Ilim .
Sir Ansbach can be summoned to assist in fighting the final boss in Enir-Ilim .
Speak with Sir Ansbach at the Main Gate Cross - Site of Grace
Break Miquella's charm
Speak to Sir Ansbach at the Main Gate Cross - Site of Grace
Travel to the Shadow Keep and speak to him at the Storehouse, First Floor
Hand over the Secret Rite Scroll to Sir Ansbach
Continue Needle Knight Leda 's questline
Respond to the summons in the Storehouse
Join the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim
Defeat the final boss in Enir-Ilim
Lockout conditions for Sir Ansbach's Quest: If you do not give him the Secret Rite Scroll , he will not move from his location and exclude him from the fight in Enir-Ilim . If you give him the scroll before talking to Redmane Freyja about her decision, he will leave before giving the Letter for Freyja , potentially locking out Freyja's questline.
If you do not give him the Secret Rite Scroll , he will not move from his location and exclude him from the fight in Enir-Ilim .
If you give him the scroll before talking to Redmane Freyja about her decision, he will leave before giving the Letter for Freyja , potentially locking out Freyja's questline.
Sir Ansbach will ask you to visit Miquella's Crosses , however, it is optional to find all of Miquella's Crosses as it will not affect his questline. Additional dialogue from Sir Ansbach becomes unavailable once Miquella breaks his great rune, which is a story event that is triggered by approaching Shadow Keep or crossing the bridge east of Bonny Village. To not miss any dialogue, make sure to visit him after finding the Belurat Tower Settlement Cross , then either the Scaduview Cross , or Cerulean Coast Cross . Finding two of these crosses allows Sir Ansbach to reach a conclusion about Miquella ’s intentions.
Additional dialogue from Sir Ansbach becomes unavailable once Miquella breaks his great rune, which is a story event that is triggered by approaching Shadow Keep or crossing the bridge east of Bonny Village. To not miss any dialogue, make sure to visit him after finding the Belurat Tower Settlement Cross , then either the Scaduview Cross , or Cerulean Coast Cross . Finding two of these crosses allows Sir Ansbach to reach a conclusion about Miquella ’s intentions.
NOTE: Patch 1.13 increased the stats and status of Sir Ansbach when summoned in some areas.
Sir Ansbach is located at the Main Gate Cross Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]. Speak to him and exhaust his dialogue. He will mention Miquella's Crosses and asks you to find them, as well as introduce you to Moore .
Once you have discovered the Belurat Tower Settlement Cross , you can return to Sir Ansbach to unlock more dialogue. Additional dialogue can be unlocked after interacting with the Cerulean Coast Cross and Scaduview Cross , then talking to Sir Ansbach about them. Finding two of these crosses allows Sir Ansbach to reach a conclusion about Miquella’s intentions. N OTE : this additional dialogue becomes unavailable once Miquella breaks his great rune (see below).
Receiving the message "Somewhere a great rune has been broken. And so too, a powerful charm." will advance several NPC questlines, such as Sir Ansbach's. To break the great rune:
Approach the Shadow Keep front entrance, through Messmer's Army Encampment. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
Head past the Bonny Village to the eastern part of Scadu Altus , from the east bridge leading to the village, continue heading north. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
Once the great rune is broken head back to Sir Ansbach to unlock new dialogue with him. Once you have exhausted all of his dialogue, he will move to the Shadow Keep .
When you reach Shadow Keep , make your way to the Specimen Storehouse. You will find Sir Ansbach in the Storehouse, First Floor [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ], and Redmane Freyja in the Storehouse, Seventh Floor . Speak to Sir Ansbach and exhaust his dialogue.
It is important to follow the steps below, in order, to progress both Sir Ansbach and Redmane Freyja 's questlines, otherwise Freyja's questline may get locked out:
Speak with Redmane Freyja to learn about Freyja's decision. This must be done before giving Sir Ansbach the Secret Rite Scroll .
Speak with Sir Ansbach and tell him about Freyja's decision. Continue to exhaust his dialogue.
Give Sir Ansbach the Secret Rite Scroll and rest at a Site of Grace, speak with him to accept the Letter for Freyja . Sir Ansbach will also drop Freyja's letter as part of his loot during Needle Knight Leda 's questline, if he is sided against, and the Secret Rite Scroll was given but the dialogue to receive the letter was not initiated.
Sir Ansbach will also drop Freyja's letter as part of his loot during Needle Knight Leda 's questline, if he is sided against, and the Secret Rite Scroll was given but the dialogue to receive the letter was not initiated.
Exhaust both Freyja's and Sir Ansbach's dialogues.
The Secret Rite Scroll can be found on a table behind some tall bookshelves [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ].
If you do not give Sir Ansbach the Secret Rite Scroll , he will not leave his position, will not give you the Letter for Freyja, and will not progress his questline, therefore, you will not be able to summon him in the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim as well as for the final boss .
During Hornsent 's questline, you will be able to skip the fight against Needle Knight Leda and Hornsent , or assist Leda and kill Hornsent. This will lead to Leda going after Ansbach, when this happens, you will need to head back to the Storehouse, First Floor where you will find 2 summon signs. A red one to help Leda, and a gold one to help Sir Ansbach.
Choosing to help Leda ( the red summon ) will result in Sir Ansbach's death. This will reward you with the following items:
Choosing to help Sir Ansbach ( the gold summon ) will keep him alive. His questline can be continued, and will reward you with the following items:
It is possible to skip the fight entirely while still having him available as a summon in Enir-Ilim, however, this will lock you out of either of the above rewards.
If Sir Ansbach is alive and given the Secret Rite Scroll , he will be available as a summon for the NPC allies battle in Enir-Ilim . It is optional to summon Sir Ansbach in the fight, however you will miss additional dialogue if you don't summon him. After the battle, you can speak to him at the top of the elevator to learn more about Mohg, Miquella, and Thiollier .
NOTE: If Sir Ansbach is not summoned for the NPC allies battle, he will not be summonable for the final boss . However, he will still drop his items after the final boss is defeated.
It is optional to summon Sir Ansbach for the final boss . After the final boss is defeated, he will drop the following items near the entrance:
Furious Blade of Ansbach
You must be the Tarnished. I am Ansbach. Formerly in service to Lord Mohg. But now, like you, another soul guided by Kindly Miquella. I am hardly the steady hand I once was. My fighting days are far behind me. Which is why I have chosen to dedicate myself to investigation. To discern Tender Miquella's ultimate purpose. I'd rather not volunteer a service to a new master unless I can be of use.
May I request a service of you? Six crosses, identical to this one, have been discovered in these lands. They are akin to Tender Miquella's footprints. Or gravestones, which mark what he's left behind. If you discover any more crosses, be certain to tell me. I presume you, too, are keen to know. Just what Kind Miquella is doing here
Righteous Tarnished. What brings you here?
(Ask about the person in verdigris armor) Oh, you must mean Sir Moore. Sir Moores is a kind and stalwart ally. He provisions our little outfit. Sir Moore traffics with the forager brood. His brethren who collect supplies from all corners of the land. No doubt they find inspiration in his many virtues. These are not your foes, Tarnished. Should you spot any forager brood, be sure to mind your manners.
(Speak of the cross at the tower) Well, I am much obliged. As suspected, Kindly Miquella has visited the lands which hold the tower. And appears intent on wholly dispossessing himself of his flesh. He must be seeking entrance to the tower of shadow.
(Speak of the cross where the eye was abandoned) Well, I am much obliged. I can hardly believe it, he's divested himself of his very eye… Tender Miquella's eye is no mere morsel of flesh. It is a vessel of soaring grace. Proof of his Empyrean lineage. I wonder, does Miquella the Kind intend to sever his very birthright? His fate as a child of the Erdtree?
(Speak of the cross at the coast) Well, I am much obliged. To think there was a cross left in such a faraway place… And that deep-purple water lilies would be blooming there. This can only mean Kindly Miquella has divested himself even of that… His adoring other self, his blossom of slumber.
Righteous Tarnished. I have reasoned through my findings. Concerning Kind Miquella's intentions – why he is here. The full picture is yet to coalesce, but we do know he is bound for the tower of shadow. And that is where he intends to rise to true godhood… The tower of shadow houses a divine gateway. A well-kept secret, it was, but… The gateway was once the birthplace of a god. A god we know all too well.
After the Great Rune breaks
(If you have not met yet) You must be the Tarnished. I am Ansbach. Formerly in service to Lord Mohg. But now, like you, another soul guided by Kindly Miquella. I‘m hardly the steady hand I once was. My fighting days are far behind me.
Yes, I am well aware.
Kindly Miquella has discarded his Great Rune. The fog that vexed my aging faculties has been lifted. And follies better left forgotten haunt me once again. Once, in an attempt to free Lord Mohg from his enchantment, I challenged Tender Miquella, only to have my own heart rather artfully stolen. I knew not how weak I was. I believed that with sufficient mastery, even an Empyrean would be within reach of my blade. I could not have been more mistaken… Miquella the Kind...is a monster. Pure and radiant, he wields love to shrive clean the hearts of men. There is nothing more terrifying.
Righteous Tarnished. Miquella the Kind makes my blood run cold. I am loath to admit it, but even at this very instant I wish to run very far away indeed.
Visit in Shadow Keep §
Righteous Tarnished. It's been some time. After unburdening my soul to you, I had hoped to make a fresh start. But there was one thought I could not dislodge from my mind. So here I am, quite unable to move on.
After Lord Mohg's slaying at his dynastic palace, it appears his body has been absconded with. And taken straight to Kind Miquella. Surely you recall that I once served Lord Mohg as a Pureblood Knight. After failing him in every regard, and losing sight of my vows, I'd do anything to make amends, in whatever meagre way I can.
What could they possibly have in mind for Lord Mohg's remains? The implications are rather… unnerving.
(Tell him about Freyja's decision) Freyja, by the gods… She must be feeling the need to reaffirm her allegiance before she heads to war… A true Redmane she is, hardly troubled by the rot. But no-one is without their shortcomings… She has a hard road ahead…
Hmm, it does make one think… Perhaps our concerns are but two sides of the same coin?
(Hand over Secret Rite Scroll ) Well, what's this… … Yes, yes, I should have known. Even the truth was itself mere folly. As if using Lord Mohg to gain entrance to the land of shadow were not enough, he plans to use his corpse as the vessel of his king consort. He has forsaken Lord Mohg's soul. He desires only his empty shell. It beggars belief, but… I'm afraid Tender Miquella fails to grasp the humiliation implied by this act. One thing is certain. My dear lord deserved better.
Righteous Tarnished. You have my gratitude. You have given me the answers I needed. But I can hardly fathom it. Such folly, unto the end. I am a warrior, but I've aged. I cannot afford to act hastily. Curse it all. I know my limits. I could spend the rest of my life honing my craft, and still that creature would be out of my blade's reach.
The absolute folly of it all.
Righteous Tarnished. Could you please give this to Freyja? Thanks to you, she, too, will have the answers she requires. Freyja possesses a youth I no longer have. This knowledge will surely stoke the flames of valor within her. Yes, I can see her barreling into the fray right now... (Gives Letter for Freyja )
Perhaps I shouldn't meddle in her affairs… But this is the only way I can help…
(Pass on Freyja's message) Ha ha ha… I should have expected as much from Freyja! Some things simply never occur to her. Paralyzing fear. True despair.
That's Freyja, through and through.
(On hit) Ungh… What on earth is the matter?
There's no denying it, now. A culling appears to be on the cards. One that won't overlook even an aging soldier as myself…
(On player kill) Righteous Tarnished. You should have taken stock of your limitations.
(On death) I only wanted to play my part… Curse these aging bones...
Speak after attacking
Have you come to your senses? Now, there's no need for any hand-wringing. The way the world is, I'd say it's a wonder people don't lash out more often.
Fighting Leda in Shadow Keep
So, you would cull an old man such as I? You are thorough indeed, Lady Leda. I would expect nothing less. As you surmise, I do have my doubts. And will not simply lay down and die with them.
Assist Ansbach Have you lost your wits? By my troth, you have no such debt to this old man. Though I gladly welcome your decision. Now I might even live to see the morrow… (On victory) Lady Leda, I ask you keep Kindly Miquella in your care.
Have you lost your wits? By my troth, you have no such debt to this old man. Though I gladly welcome your decision. Now I might even live to see the morrow…
(On victory) Lady Leda, I ask you keep Kindly Miquella in your care.
Assist Leda So, you've taken Lady Leda's side. I'm glad she stands with an ally. She tends to overestimate… The burden she might carry alone. (On victory) Well, this is an unexpected victory. It seems I have my uses yet… (On defeat) I've utterly failed you both… Miquella the Kind, Lord Mohg…
