Be summoned to challenge Needle Knight Leda and her allies
I am Ansbach of the Pureblood Knights. I stand with blade loft, blood afire. For the dignity of my lord and master, Mohg.
Righteous Tarnished. It appears that our interests have aligned. A balm upon my soul it is, that we do not meet today as enemies. Now, my blade. Watch closely.
(If Freyja was summoned) Well, good Freyja. It will be my honour to test my blade against a Redmane.
Ha-hah! How readily the sensation returns! The runaway spirit of war!
Lady Leda. Your suspicions were dead-on. In the end, I could not bury the memories. Of Lord Mohg, and our pact of blood.
(Upon defeat) Lord Mohg, your honour… Was my only…
After defeating Leda
Righteous Tarnished. That was an astounding battle, to be sure. Now I suppose this leaves only one. But in truth, I cannot calm my quivering. Challenging a god is no small matter.
(About Thiollier) I am afraid I underestimated the lad. Appearing frail in both body and mind, I presumed he’d be like to stumble upon the field of battle… What a fool I was. He serves another master, but Sir Thiollier performed magnificently. Perhaps he too quivers with anticipation. As do we.
(Ask about Mohg’s death) Oh, something you want to get off your chest? Well, you needn’t worry. It was you, wasn’t it? Who defeated Lord Mohg. … Fear not, I bear no grudge against you. His Eminence was felled in an honourable duel, and such are the risks of seeking Lordship. Besides, what right have I to complain? I blame the enchantment more than anything. Righteous Tarnished. We will have our victory. I swear upon my blood.
§ When Summoned to Fight The Final Boss
(Entering the arena) General Radahn. A pleasure to see you, after all this time. But those remains do not belong to you. Lord Mohg will have his dignity.
(On defeat) Righteous Tarnished. Become our new lord. A lord not for gods, but for men.
Attire of Sir Ansbach. The embroidered crest of the Pureblood Knights has now all but faded away. Slightly enhances Blood Oath and dynastic skills. Ansbach understood that keeping his oath to his old master was not an act of logic, nor would it bear any justice, but it was for these very reasons he was unable to let go.
Incantation of Ansbach, Pureblood Knight.
Produces a bloodflame blade from the side of the hand, then lunges at enemies to cleave through them. Charge to increase the number of slashes.
Markedly different from the finessed swordplay of the dynast, this is an aggressive last resort of an incantation that gave rise to Ansbach's fearsome reputation.
The iron mask of Sir Ansbach, giving the appearance of a quiet, wise, old bearded man. But an old fear lurks beneath, together with the cold, unflinching discipline that once honed his blade, now employed to enhance Blood Oath and dynastic skills.
Signature weapon of the Pureblood Knight Ansbach. A viciously keen and slender scythe made for combat.
The obsidian edge relies not upon trickery to fulfil its violent purpose—only well-honed skill is required to rip and rend through foes, sowing blossoms of red upon the battlefield.
"Ah, there you are. I'm afraid Sir Ansbach will have to be next. He insists that he's nothing but a worn-down, over-the-hill soldier. But in his day, he was the feared commander of the Pureblood Knights, who cleaved open Miquella the Kind with his blood blade."
There is no need for concern. I will be true to my word. Though I do feel a hint of regret. Lady Leda and honourable Ansbach are of a special breed. How glorious it would have been, had we met in battle as sworn enemies.
Thank you. Now I’ve finally cast off my misgivings. Give my regards to honourable Ansbach. Tell him if we should meet in battle, to face your bow would be fine enough, but to face your blade would be an honour.
Thank you, Sir Ansbach. The mere sight of your blade sets my heart aflutter. All the more I yearn to crush it in my grasp!
Sir Ansbach is an NPC introduced in Shadow of the Erdtree . A wizened knight in service to Mohg , Sir Ansbach follows the call of Miquella into the Land of Shadow .
3 Questline Progression
Formerly the feared commander of the Pureblood Knights [ 2 ] in service of Lord Mohg , Ansbach is one of the compatriots of Needle Knight Leda who followed Miquella into the Realm of Shadow . Ansbach has dedicated himself to investigating Miquella's purpose [ 3 ] by following the trail of crosses he left behind. [ 4 ]
As with the rest who followed Miquella into the Shadow Realm , Ansbach was under the influence of a charm, which breaks when the Empyrean abandons his Miquella's Great Rune . [ 5 ] With his mind freed of the charm, Ansbach recalls that he once challenged Miquella in an attempt to free Mohg of his enchantment, which resulted in him falling under the enchantment himself. [ 6 ]
With his faculties restored, Ansbach makes his way to the Specimen Storehouse within the Shadow Keep . There, he learns that Mohg's body has been taken to Miquella, [ 7 ] and with the assistance of the Tarnished discovers that Miquella intends to use Mohg's corpse as a vessel for his king consort. [ 8 ]
Ansbach becomes one of Leda's possible targets, [ 9 ] resulting in a duel which the Tarnished may intervene in on either side. Should Ansbach survive Leda's attack, he will aid the Tarnished against Leda, Dryleaf Dane , and their allies in Enir-Ilim . Ansbach will then be available to assist the Tarnished in their battle against Promised Consort Radahn , before which he admits that he knows the Tarnished was responsible for killing Mohg, but bears no grudge against them, viewing Mohg's death as an honorable one. [ 10 ] After defeating Miquella and his consort, Ansbach dies in front of the Gate of Divinity.
1. Speak with Sir Ansbach at the Main Gate Cross Site of Grace and exhaust his dialogue.
2. Break Miquella's charm.
3. Return to Sir Ansbach at the Main Gate Cross Site of Grace and exhaust his dialogue.
4. Travel to the Shadow Keep to find him near the Storehouse, First Floor Site of Grace. Exhaust his dialogue. From the Site of Grace , head around the massive corpse and up the stairs you see after rounding the corner. Continue forward toward the dead end and take the last left. Forward and to your right is the room Ansbach will stay in.
5. Locate the Secret Rite Scroll and hand it over to Sir Ansbach. Be careful not to give him the scroll until you've talked to him about Freyja's situation, if you wish to continue Freyja's Questline .
6. Continue Needle Knight Leda's questline .
7. Side with Ansbach against Leda to continue his quest, receiving Ansbach's Longbow and Leda's Rune . Leda will remain alive and her questline will continue, though you'll lose out on the Retaliatory Crossed-Tree .
8. Sir Ansbach will be available to summon for the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim.
9. Sir Ansbach will be available to summon for the Final Boss . After the fight, Ansbach's body can be found seated near the arena's entrance, dropping Ansbach's Set , Obsidian Lamina , and Furious Blade of Ansbach .

---

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Thiollier
Thiollier is a Tarnished NPC in Elden Ring . Thiollier has an eye for crafting poison and is not a fighter. Thiollier is an NPC that can be found exclusively in the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, and can't be found in the base game.
Oh, hello there. Are you the Tarnished? Lady Leda spoke of you. Another Tarnished guided here by Miquella. My name is Thiollier. I too am Tarnished, and here on Miquella's account. So, well... here's to new beginnings, I suppose.
Thiollier can be found near Pillar Path Cross , southeast of Castle Ensis . [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map here ]. It is the bottom-right cross in the New Cross Map given by Hornsent .
This NPC moves to Garden of Deep Purple in Stone Coffin Fissure : If he is told the whereabouts of St. Trina after discovering her, he will move there after resting at a Site of Grace. If St. Trina is not yet discovered, but the Golden Hippopotamus in Shadow Keep is defeated, the next time Thiollier is talked to he will mention that he is heading south to search for St. Trina. Entering the Church District in Shadow Keep also triggers his search for St. Trina. If the Sealing Tree in Ancient Ruins of Rauh is burned, Thiollier will move to the Garden of Deep Purple on his own.
If he is told the whereabouts of St. Trina after discovering her, he will move there after resting at a Site of Grace.
If St. Trina is not yet discovered, but the Golden Hippopotamus in Shadow Keep is defeated, the next time Thiollier is talked to he will mention that he is heading south to search for St. Trina.
Entering the Church District in Shadow Keep also triggers his search for St. Trina.
If the Sealing Tree in Ancient Ruins of Rauh is burned, Thiollier will move to the Garden of Deep Purple on his own.
Thiollier can be summoned to assist in the Putrescent Knight boss if he journeys south to search for St. Trina.
Thiollier can be summoned to assist in the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim .
Thiollier can be summoned to assist in the final boss in Enir-Ilim .
This merchant can be fought
If defeated, drops: St. Trina's Smile
Speak with Thiollier at the Pillar Path Cross Site of Grace
Break Miquella's charm
Look for St. Trina in the Garden of Deep Purple
Drink and die from St. Trina 's nectar 4 times
Pass on St.Trina's words to Thiollier.
Defeat Thiollier when he invades you
Drink St. Trina 's nectar twice more and speak with Thiollier
(Optional) Summon Thiollier to defeat the NPC allies at Enir-Ili m
(Optional) Summon Thiollier to defeat the final boss
Lockout conditions involving Thiollier: You will need to obtain Thiollier's Concoction before he moves to the Garden of Deep Purple to prevent from locking out from the alternative rewards from the Dragon Communion Priestess 's questline. Moore will also not give the Black Syrup if Thiollier has left his original location. You will need to defeat Thiollier when he invades you in the Garden of Deep Purple BEFORE fighting the NPC allies in Enir-Ilim . Otherwise, he will disappear from St. Trina 's cave, and his questline will end prematurely.
You will need to obtain Thiollier's Concoction before he moves to the Garden of Deep Purple to prevent from locking out from the alternative rewards from the Dragon Communion Priestess 's questline. Moore will also not give the Black Syrup if Thiollier has left his original location.
You will need to defeat Thiollier when he invades you in the Garden of Deep Purple BEFORE fighting the NPC allies in Enir-Ilim . Otherwise, he will disappear from St. Trina 's cave, and his questline will end prematurely.
You do not need to summon him for any of the boss battles to progress his questline, including the NPC brawl at Enir-Ilim. As long as his summon sign is visible at Enir-Ilim, his items can be looted after defeating the final boss .
NOTE: Patch 1.13 increased the stats and status of Thiollier when summoned in some areas.
Thiollier is located by the Pillar Path Cross - Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]. Speak to Thiollier to access him as a merchant. His questline will not progress until Miquella's rune is broken (see below).
(Optional) Meeting Thiollier will trigger additional dialogue from Moore , who will give the Black Syrup the next time he is spoken to, which can then be given to Thiollier to receive Thiollier's Concoction , an item which is used in the Dragon Communion Priestess questline.
When you receive the message, "Somewhere a great rune has been broken. And so too, a powerful charm," it will advance several NPC questlines, including Thiollier's. It will also unlock the Stone Coffin Fissure , which is where you can find St. Trina , an NPC that is directly tied to Thiollier's questline.
To break the great rune:
Enter the Shadow Keep through the front entrance at Messmer's Army Encampment. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
Head past Bonny Village to the eastern part of Scadu Altus . From the Bridge Leading to the Village Site of Grace, continue north. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
After breaking the great rune, you can speak to Thiollier to unlock additional dialogue.
To progress Thiollier's questline you will need to do either one of the following:
Discover St. Trina on your own. She is located at the end of Stone Coffin Fissure in the Garden of Deep Purple [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]. After defeating the Putrescent Knight , you can find St. Trina at the end of the cave surrounded by purple flowers, then go back and talk to Thiollier next to the Pillar Path Cross . Choose the option " Tell him St. Trina's whereabouts ." After doing so, if you fast-travel to the Garden of Deep Purple Site of Grace, Thiollier will be there next to St. Trina.
Let Thiollier search for St. Trina . There are numerous triggers for this to occur: Defeat Golden Hippopotamus in Shadow Keep. Enter the Church District for the first time. Discover the Fissure Depths Site of Grace . Burn the Sealing Tree in Ancient Ruins of Rauh (he will move without player intervention).
Defeat Golden Hippopotamus in Shadow Keep.
Enter the Church District for the first time.
Discover the Fissure Depths Site of Grace .
Burn the Sealing Tree in Ancient Ruins of Rauh (he will move without player intervention).
Going back and speaking to Thiollier; he will mention that he will follow some clues south to search for St. Trina and will leave after resting at a Site of Grace. This will make him available as a summon for the Putrescent Knight boss. After the boss is defeated, he will be found lying next to St. Trina. He does not need to be summoned for the Putrescent Knight boss to progress his questline.
WARNING : Once Thiollier leaves his location in Gravesite Plain, you can no longer obtain nor give the Black Syrup from Moore , and Thiollier's Concoction will no longer be obtainable.
Talk to Thiollier, who is in a lulled state nearby St. Trina in her cave. If you have not done so already, talk to St. Trina and choose the option "Imbibe Nectar" which inflicts immediate death upon drinking. You will need to do this action for 4 times and keep doing it until you hear her voice on the death screen. Drinking her nectar 6 times will exhaust her dialogue. On respawn, talk to Thiollier. Choose the dialogue option " Pass on St. Trina's words ", and then " Try to pass on St. Trina's words again " and this will result in him getting angry.
Afterwards, either rest at the nearby site of grace, or drink and die from St. Trina's nectar once again to trigger Thiollier's invasion.
After defeating Thiollier, he will drop St. Trina's Smile . Talk to Thiollier again in St. Trina's room and exhaust his dialogue. He will now be available as a summon for the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim , as well as for the final boss fight .
At the NPC battle in the cleansing chamber in Enir-Ilim, and at the final boss battle, it is optional to summon Thiollier to progress his quest.
NOTE : If Thiollier is not summoned for the NPC battle, he will not be summonable for the final boss. However, he will still drop his items after the final boss is defeated.
After the final boss is defeated, he will drop all of his equipment. You can get the following items near the entrance:
Thiollier's Hidden Needle
Thiollier's Trousers

So um, something I can help you with?
Oh, what's this? I have to say, Sir Moore has an awfully sharp eye, doesn't he. It's wonderful. This ebony, as lustrous as the night sky unending. I don't think I could ever thank him enough... But I'll have to think of something...

(I am weary of life) Is that so? Then take this. This concoction can coax even the oldest of dragons to sleep. For you, it will grant the sweetest slumber... ...for all eternity. (Thiollier's Concoction)
After the Great Rune breaks
Oh, umm… Are you not affected? Even with the spell broken? I’m feeling rather lost. Haunted by memories. Of St. Trina. Her visage. Her scent. The lure of velvety sleep. I would sacrifice everything, just to gaze upon her, one last time.

I’m heading south. To the shore Sir Moore mentioned.

St. Trina...Oh, St. Trina...What velvety ambrosia is this? Your poison, and the sweet sleep that follows. Oh, please, let me hear your voice...
Ahh, then you, too, must have paid a visit...in St. Trina's garden of deepest purple. I am drifting off in her sea of poison. Soon, I will hear her voice. Let me be, I won't be long.
After imbibing nectar once
Oh, I almost forgot. You mustn’t follow my example. St. Trina’s poison would plunge you into an eternal slumber. It must be me, and only me.
I am gone, adrift in sleep. So that I may hear St. Trina’s lost voice. Let me be. I won’t be long.
After imbibing nectar twice
Why won’t you share your voice with me? Ahh, grant me more of your poison. That I might drift further into sleep. Perhaps then you’ll grace me with your voice?
After exhausting St. Trina's dialogues
Please, leave me alone.
(Pass on St. Trina's words) I'll thank you not to lie to me. I am her chosen, not you. Only I can doze off in the sea of St. Trina. Why would you hear her voice, when I cannot? If you should utter these cruel untruths to me again I...I won't be held responsible for my actions...
(Try to pass on St. Trina's words again) Never utter these cruel untruths to me again...
(Try to pass on St. Trina's words again) Please, enough...I cannot believe your cheek. And I will stand for it no longer!
I will never forgive you.
After being invaded by him

An ebony-black concoction mixed by Thiollier.
A condensed, somniferous elixir, purported to be capable of putting even an ancient dragon to sleep. For an ordinary mortal, a single sip would assuredly result in swift and certain death.
Thiollier thought of St. Trina's sleep when he created this, but the ebony sleep he concocted had not the sweetness of nectar nor the gentleness of rest's embrace.
An ebony-black syrup held in an unglazed jar. Moore asked that this be delivered to Thiollier. An unknown liquid apparently gathered and aged by the forager brood.

Thiollier is an NPC merchant in Shadow of the Erdtree .
4 Character information 4.1 Basic info 4.2 Equipment 4.3 Quest items
5 Questline Progression
This isolated young man once met St. Trina [ 1 ] and, just once, Thiollier was granted the sweet repose of velvety sleep . The remainder of his days would be spent attempting to recapture it. [ 2 ]
He seems to be a very adept poison expert, and he is in search of the means to create a unique elixir. He sees himself as utterly weak and useless, apart from his skills in the realm of mixing poisons. [ 1 ]
Can be found by himself, quite some distance from his compatriots, at the Pillar Path Cross Site of Grace on the northeastern border of the Gravesite Plain . [ 1 ]
The Tarnished (Protagonist) can help Thiollier to create the condensed, somniferous elixir , purported to be capable of putting even an ancient dragon to sleep, although fatal for any mortal. [ 3 ]
Should his questline be completed, Thiollier is able to see St. Trina once more.
He wears a mask upon which is carved a tranquil sleeping face. The silver hair is arranged in the same style as St. Trina's, increasing arcane. [ 4 ] Additionally, countless needles are sewn onto the exterior, apparently for talismanic effect. [ 5 ]
WARNING: Coming too close to the entrance of the Shadow Keep will lock you out of the first step of his quest and you will miss the Spirit Ash of another character .
Pillar Path Cross Site of Grace Find Thiollier at the Pillar Path Cross Site. Go to speak with Moore , exhausting his dialogue until he hands you the Black Syrup . Find Thiollier again and hand him the Black Syrup, then exhaust his dialogue. He will give you Thiollier's Concoction . When you get in close proximity to the Shadow Keep , a crucial event for most NPC's will trigger, advancing Thiollier's questline. This will advance most NPCs, so proceed with any you have interest in before approaching the Keep.
Find Thiollier at the Pillar Path Cross Site.
Go to speak with Moore , exhausting his dialogue until he hands you the Black Syrup .
Find Thiollier again and hand him the Black Syrup, then exhaust his dialogue. He will give you Thiollier's Concoction .
When you get in close proximity to the Shadow Keep , a crucial event for most NPC's will trigger, advancing Thiollier's questline. This will advance most NPCs, so proceed with any you have interest in before approaching the Keep.
This will advance most NPCs, so proceed with any you have interest in before approaching the Keep.
Stone Coffin Fissure Make your way to the southernmost point of the Cerulean Coast where you'll find a pit. When facing south towards the pit, venture to the right side and there will be steps for you to enter. Follow the given path and you'll come across the Stone Coffin Fissure . Proceed through the Legacy Dungeon and beat the boss, the Putrescent Knight . In the corner of the arena you'll find both the Garden of Deep Purple Site of Grace and St. Trina . There are multiple ways to have Thiollier advance to St. Trina: You can Inform him about St. Trina's location if you meet her and then talk to Thiollier. Alternatively Thiollier will inform you that he is seeking her out if you do any of the following and return to him: Defeat the Golden Hippopotamus in the Shadow Keep. Enter the Church District. Discover the Fissure Depths Grace. However, Thiollier will advance on his own if you burn the Sealing Tree in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh . Beyond the Garden of Deep Purple Grace, Exhaust Thiollier's Dialogue and then reset the area using the nearby Grace. Return and Thiollier will have new dialogue, exhaust it again and then at St. Trina. choose to imbibe her nectar. This step must be repeated 4 times for St. Trina to speak to you. You can try to pass on the words of St. Trina to Thiollier from then on. Thiollier will speak during this process, hinting the player to continue imbibing St. Trina's nectar (after 3 deaths due to imbibing). Talk to Thiollier, passing on the words of St. Trina. He will initially refuse to believe you, continue talking to him and attempt to convey her words again. After, continue to exhaust his dialogue. After this, return to the Grace. Here you will immediately be invaded by Thiollier. Defeat him and enter St. Trina's garden. Speak to the now defeated Thiollier and tell him of what St. Trina has said and exhaust his dialogue. If you can't talk to him, ensure you have heard St. Trina speak 3 times, by imbibing her nectar 6 times.

---

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Moore
Moore is an NPC in Elden Ring . Moore is one of the followers of Miquella and is a gentle giant that also acts as a Merchant . Moore is an NPC exclusive to the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC and does not appear in the base game.
Hello there. Together, we work. Together for Miquella the Kind. Many things we have found.
Moore can be found at a small camp located just outside Belurat, Tower Settlement .  [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
This NPC moves beside the Forager Brood Cookbook (4) north of the Church of the Crusade if he dies when he invades you, or when you tell him to " Remain sad forever " during a dialogue encounter with him after Miquella's rune breaks.
You can fight this NPC in the NPC group battle in Enir-Ilim . Moore's Bell Bearing , Verdigris Greatshield , and Verdigris Set can be looted from his corpse afterwards.
Meet Moore outside Belurat, Tower Settlement
Speak with Moore after breaking Miquella's charm
Lockout conditions for Moore's quest: If you kill any two of the passive Lesser Kindred of Rot (pests) you will be invaded by Moore. If you kill him, you will lose Moore, and possibly lock you out of his key items, such as the Black Syrup , if it has not already been obtained. If Thiollier leaves his original location to search for St. Trina , Moore will not give the Black Syrup anymore, making it unobtainable in the current NG cycle.
If you kill any two of the passive Lesser Kindred of Rot (pests) you will be invaded by Moore. If you kill him, you will lose Moore, and possibly lock you out of his key items, such as the Black Syrup , if it has not already been obtained.
If Thiollier leaves his original location to search for St. Trina , Moore will not give the Black Syrup anymore, making it unobtainable in the current NG cycle.
To maximize the rewards players get in completing Moore's quest, we recommend you obtain the Forager Cookbooks and the Black Syrup , then have him invade you by killing a few Lesser Kindred of Rot (Pests) to acquire his equipment earlier and exclude him from the fight in Enir-Ilim .
When defeated in his invasion "Recusant Vanquished" is displayed, but this is most likely an error as Moore has no other connection to the Volcano Manor .
Bears a striking resemblance to Siegmeyer of Catarina of Dark Souls and Lonesome Gavlan of Dark Souls 2.
You'll first encounter Moore and Sir Ansbach at the Main Gate Cross Site of Grace, near Belurat Tower Settlement . Speak to Moore to access him as a merchant. After purchasing any item from him, you can speak with him further. Meeting Moore will also unlock additional dialogue from Sir Ansbach.
(Optional) Visit Thiollier at the Pillar Path Cross Site of Grace to meet him and unlock dialogue with Moore. Return to Moore, who will give the Black Syrup to take back to Thiollier. NOTE : You must complete this step before either Moore or Thiollier leaves their initial locations, or you will be unable to obtain the Black Syrup.
Moore leads the Forager Brood , a group of friendly Pests searching for crafting materials and recipes across the Land of Shadow . Interact with all six members to collect their cookbooks. One member will need help with a Warming Stone or Sunwarmth Stone . After assisting this member, speak to Moore to receive the seventh cookbook.
NOTE : If you break Miquella's charm and then tell Moore to " Put it behind you ," " Remain sad forever ," or kill him before collecting all seven Forager Brood Cookbooks, you will lose access to any remaining cookbooks.
The following Forager Brood Cookbooks are available:
If the player kills a friendly Lesser Kindred of Rot , they will receive a warning the next time that Moore speaks to them, mentioning that he knows a forager brood was killed, and that killing one is unforgivable. This warning dialogue will not be given if Miquella's rune has been broken. If the player kills a second friendly Pest, Moore will immediately invade and try to kill them.
If you kill Moore when he invades: he will drop Moore's Bell Bearing , Verdigris Greatshield , and Verdigris Set and his quest will end. This will make a later boss fight easier, but possibly lock you out from receiving the Black Syrup if not already obtained from Moore.
If you ignore Moore or die to him when he invades: He will remain friendly, as though you didn't attack the Pest at all.
Breaking Miquella's charm will prompt Moore to ask, "Must we be sad forever?" You have three options:
" Put it behind you" : Moore will move on and confront you in a later boss fight. This choice may lock you out of the Forager Brood Cookbooks or Black Syrup if you haven’t obtained them yet and delay acquiring Moore's gear. NOTE : If you've met Thiollier but haven’t received the Black Syrup, speak to Moore immediately after this choice to obtain it.
" Remain sad forever" : Moore will fall deeper into despair and die after your next rest. You’ll find his body outside the Church of the Crusade , near the fourth Forager Brood Pest, where you can loot Moore's Bell Bearing , Verdigris Greatshield , and the Verdigris Set . This choice may also lock you out of the Forager Brood Cookbooks or Black Syrup if you haven’t received them yet, but it will make a later boss fight easier.
" I don't know" : Moore will remain where he is, allowing you to choose another option later. This is recommended until you have all the Forager Brood Cookbooks and Black Syrup.
If you burn the Sealing Tree without making a choice : Moore will remain where he is, making a later boss fight easier. You can still acquire the Verdigris Greatshield and the Verdigris Set by killing two Forager Brood and defeating Moore during his invasion. Moore's Bell Bearing will be available at the Main Gate Cross Site of Grace. NOTE : Once the Sealing Tree is burned, Thiollier will move to the Garden of Deep Purple if he hasn’t already, and you will be unable to give him the Black Syrup.
If you told Moore to "Put it behind you", he will fight against you in the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim (after the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom Site of Grace). After winning that fight, you'll be able to loot Moore's Bell Bearing , Verdigris Greatshield , and Verdigris Set from his corpse in the same room.
I like finding things. For you, Lady Leda, her allies, and the forager brood. Things bring joy to all.
Welcome… You come often. You enjoy things. I like finding things. I like you.

Oh, you must mean Sir Moore. Sir Moores is a kind and stalwart ally. He provisions our little outfit. Sir Moore traffics with the forager brood. Umm, oh… I had something I wish to tell you. I’m heading south. I’ve recalled something that Sir Moore said. He discovered deep-purple lilies blooming on the southern shore. That must be where St. Trina was left to wither away.
Sir Moore is a merchant NPC in Shadow of the Erdtree .
He likes collecting things and will share and sell goods with the Tarnished and the other followers of Miquella . He is initially located at the Main Gate Cross Site of Grace outside Belurat, Tower Settlement in the Realm of Shadow .
2 Character information 2.1 Equipment 2.2 Shop inventory
3 Quest progression 3.1 Quest items
Moore was once a pest who awaited the return of the goddess of the Scarlet Rot . [ 1 ] When her successor, Malenia , disowned and abandoned the pests, Moore and his forager brood were left without a master. He and his kin later allied with Miquella and joined his followers . The brood shared their scavenged items with Moore and his comrades out of kindness. [ 2 ] [ 3 ]

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Hornsent
Hornsent is an NPC in Elden Ring . Hornsent can be found alongside Freyja at the Three-Path Cross in Gravesite Plain. He is exclusive to the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, and can't be found in the base game. The Hornsent are a race of inhabitants from the Realm of Shadow who were purged by Messmer the Impaler , and this individual swears vengeance upon him.
Your kind are not forgiven. The Erdtree is my people's enemy. By Marika long betray'd, set aflame."
Hornsent can be found at the Three-Path Cross in Gravesite Plain.
This NPC moves nearby the Highroad Cross Site of Grace after entering Scadu Altus. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
Hornsent can be summoned for the Golden Hippopotamus boss in Shadow Keep . [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
Hornsent can be summoned for the Messmer the Impaler boss in Shadow Keep . His summon sign is in the boss arena. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
You can fight this NPC depending on your decisions during his questline: As a cooperator if choosing to aid Needle Knight Leda in Shadow Keep. In an invasion outside Romina, Saint of the Bud's arena [ Map Link ] In the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim . Drops Falx and Hornsent Set .
As a cooperator if choosing to aid Needle Knight Leda in Shadow Keep.
In an invasion outside Romina, Saint of the Bud's arena [ Map Link ]
In the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim .
Drops Falx and Hornsent Set .
Speak with Hornsent by the Three-Path Cross Site of Grace

Break Miquella's charm
Speak with Hornsent after the charm is broken
Help Leda decide who she should go after
Assist Hornsent to fight against Leda Optional, can skip both fights
Optional, can skip both fights
Summon Hornsent to fight against Messmer, The Impaler He does not have to survive the fight in order to progress.
He does not have to survive the fight in order to progress.
Speak with Hornsent in the boss room Note: if you do not summon Hornsent, and rest a second time before speaking to him in the boss room, he will move to the Highroad Cross site of grace without the player experiencing his dialogue there.
Note: if you do not summon Hornsent, and rest a second time before speaking to him in the boss room, he will move to the Highroad Cross site of grace without the player experiencing his dialogue there.
Defeat Hornsent during his invasion.
Lockout conditions involving Hornsent: When you enter Messmer the Impaler's boss room for the first time (next to the Dark Chamber Site of Grace), his and Leda's summon sign will be removed from Shadow Keep and you will be locked out of the reward for participating in that part of his quest (either the Lacerating Crossed-Tree Talisman or the Swift Slash Ash of War ).
When you enter Messmer the Impaler's boss room for the first time (next to the Dark Chamber Site of Grace), his and Leda's summon sign will be removed from Shadow Keep and you will be locked out of the reward for participating in that part of his quest (either the Lacerating Crossed-Tree Talisman or the Swift Slash Ash of War ).
Hornsent is located at the Three-Path Cross Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ], you will see Redmane Freyja there as well. After speaking to him, he will give you the Cross Map , which is a diagram depicting the location of three of Miquella's Crosses . Finding the crosses is not mandatory for Hornsent's questline, but finding certain ones will unlock additional dialogue with Sir Ansbach, and they also contain valuable items such as Scadutree Fragments so it is worth it to find them.
(Optional) If you have defeated the Divine Beast Dancing Lion you can speak with the Hornsent Grandam with the Divine Beast Head equipped to obtain the Scorpion Stew . Returning to Hornsent and giving him the stew will grant you x3 Furnace Visage . You will only be able to give him one Scorpion Stew. This can be done even after he moves to Scadu Altus.
Hornsent will move to the Highroad Cross Site of Grace once you enter Scadu Altus for the first time [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ].
Speak with Hornsent and he will provide you with the New Cross Map to discover four more of Miquella's Crosses . Exhaust his dialogue to learn more about Miquella 's intentions.
To progress Hornsent's questline, Miquella's rune must be broken. After doing so, a message will display, "Somewhere a great rune has been broken. And so too, a powerful charm." To break the great rune:
Enter the Shadow Keep front entrance, through Messmer's Army Encampment. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
Head past the Bonny Village to the eastern part of Scadu Altus , from the Bridge Leading to the Village Site of Grace continue heading north. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
After Miquella's great rune is broken, head back to Hornsent at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace to unlock new dialogue with him. Exhaust his dialogue, which will trigger him moving to Shadow Keep.
At the Shadow Keep Main Gate Site of Grace, you will be able to summon Hornsent to assist you in fighting against the Golden Hippopotamus .
NOTE (1) : It is not possible to get all the unique items from this part of Hornsent's questline in a single playthrough. You will need a minimum of two playthroughs to acquire all items.
NOTE (2) : This part of Hornsent's questline must be done before entering Messmer the Impaler 's room from the Dark Chamber Site of Grace, otherwise the summon signs will disappear. Be careful!
Speak with Needle Knight Leda once the great rune is broken. She will ask you who whether she should go after Thiollier or Hornsent. No matter what you suggest, Leda will decide to go after Hornsent in the end. This will cause two summon signs to appear in Shadow Keep.
Just before the elevator to the Storehouse, First Floor Site of Grace in Shadow Keep (also reached by continuing up for a while from the Main Gate Plaza Site of Grace), on the battlements with the the burning boats you will find two summon signs. They'll be in the south, near a set of stairs.
If you help Leda ( the red summon ): You will kill Hornsent, ending his questline there. You will be rewarded with Falx , Hornsent's Set , and (after speaking to Leda again at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace) Lacerating Crossed-Tree . This will lock you out of getting Ash of War: Swift Slash , it will make a later boss fight easier, and it will prevent Hornsent from assisting you against Messmer, the Impaler .
If you help Hornsent ( the gold summon ): Hornsent's questline will continue, and your next encounter with Leda will be in Enir-Ilim . You will be rewarded with Ash of War: Swift Slash and Leda's Rune . This will lock you out of getting Lacerating Crossed-Tree , Retaliatory Crossed-Tree , and Ansbach's Longbow .
If you help neither : Eventually, Hornsent's and Leda's questlines will both continue. This will lock you out of getting Lacerating Crossed-Tree and Ash of War: Swift Slash .
Continue exploring Shadow Keep until you encounter Messmer, the Impaler , nearby the Dark Chamber Site of Grace. Hornsent's summon sign will be in the boss arena and you can choose whether or not to summon him for this fight.
If you summon Hornsent : He will swear vengeance on all Marika 's family. You can get more dialog either in Messmer's boss room or at the Highroad Cross Site of Grace. This won't lock you out of any items, but it will make a later boss fight easier and unlock Hornsent's NPC invasion in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh .
If you do not summon Hornsent : He will sink into despair, and you will next see him in Enir-Ilim . This won't lock you out of any items, but it will make a later boss fight more difficult and prevent Hornsent's NPC invasion in Ancient Ruins of Rauh .
NOTE : Hornsent does not need to survive when summoned to continue his questline.
If you summoned Hornsent to help defeat Messmer, the Impaler and exhausted his dialog, he will invade you at the top of the staircase near the Church of the Bud, Main Entrance Site of Grace. When you defeat him, he will drop Falx , Hornsent Set , and Soiled Loincloth . [ Map Link ]
If Hornsent is not killed throughout his quest, and if he is not summoned to assist you against Messmer, he will fight against you in the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim . After the battle, you'll be able to loot Falx , Hornsent Set , and Soiled Loincloth from his corpse.
Fie, another? Treading the heels of Miquella? Then, as that woman would surely say, we are in our purposes well aligned. But understand. Your kind are not forgiven. The Erdtree is my people's enemy. By Marika long betray'd, set aflame. I believe Miquella's apologies, when he says our delivery will come. But never will I see your kind as worthy.
And yet my oath I cannot overlook. If Miquella it is whom you would seek, then comrade, allow me to give you this. These are the sites of the crosses I've found.
I urge you, follow after Miquella. As long as you abide by his footfalls, you will be no enemy to me. (gives Cross Map )
Giving Scorpion Stew
What’s this? Do you think me in need of alms? Ah, but this dish... Tis fare o' the tower. I remember fondly this kin-clad scent. ...Brings back memories I'd all but forgot. This, by my troth, is but a dismal copy. Indeed, I think it rather plain to see... Things once broken can never be the same...
Look. Take this for your troubles, won't you? I desire not to be in your debt. (Gives x3 Furnace Visage )
Giving Additional Scorpion Stew
I’ve had my fill… Indeed, I have no wish… to wax nostalgic ‘bout days gone by… Nor do I yearn to friendship kindle…
Meeting at Highroad Cross Site of Grace
You have a knack for timing, my fellow. I’ve found the site of a new cross. Take this. (Gives New Cross Map )
Miquella has said as much himself – he wishes now to throw it all away. He says the act – though undoubtedly painful – will sear clean the Erdtree’s wanton sin. The truth of his claim can be found at each cross. Tis evidence enough to earn my belief.

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Freyja
Redmane Freyja is an NPC in Elden Ring . Redmane Freyja is a former follower of Radahn , but is now a fervent follower of Miquella . She is an NPC that can be found exclusively in the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, and can't be encountered in the base game.
See Redmane Freyja Lore
Lady Leda spoke of you. You're that Tarnished, guided here by Kindly Miquella. Weren't we all. I am Redmane Freyja. I once fought alongside Radahn. I'll hold my own in battle.
Redmane Freyja can be found for the first time at the Three-Path Cross in the Realm of the Shadow [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
This NPC moves to the Shadow Keep - Storehouse, Seventh Floor Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
This NPC moves to the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom of Enir-Ilim .
Redmane Freyja can be summoned as an ally to fight the Divine Beast Dancing Lion , Rugalea the Great Red Bear , and the Golden Hippopotamus .
You can fight this NPC in the NPC allies battle in Enir-Ilim , after which Freyja's Greatsword and Freyja's Set can be looted from her corpse
Speak with Redmane Freyja at the Three-Path Cross Site of Grace .
Speak with Freyja after defeating the Divine Beast.
Break Miquella's charm.
Speak to Freyja and exhaust her dialogues at the Three-Path Cross Site of Grace.
Look for Freyja in the Seventh Floor Storehouse.
Obtain the Secret Rite Scroll .
Tell Sir Ansbach about Freyja in the First Floor Storehouse
Acquire the Letter for Freyja from Sir Ansbach.
Give the letter to Freyja and exhaust her dialogue to receive the Golden Lion Shield .
Fight the NPC allies in Enir-Ilim .
Lockout conditions for Redmane Freyja's Quest: Giving Sir Ansbach the Secret Rite Scroll before learning about Freyja's decision will potentially lock you out of Freyja's questline, unless you side with Needle Knight Leda during her invasion, after which Letter for Freyja will be dropped as part of his loot. Not giving Freyja the Letter for Freyja will exclude her from the fight in Enir-Ilim . Freyja will disappear from her spot at the Storehouse, Seventh Floor site and she will leave the Golden Lion Shield for players to loot.
Giving Sir Ansbach the Secret Rite Scroll before learning about Freyja's decision will potentially lock you out of Freyja's questline, unless you side with Needle Knight Leda during her invasion, after which Letter for Freyja will be dropped as part of his loot.
Not giving Freyja the Letter for Freyja will exclude her from the fight in Enir-Ilim . Freyja will disappear from her spot at the Storehouse, Seventh Floor site and she will leave the Golden Lion Shield for players to loot.
Can be killed after obtaining the Golden Lion Shield and still drops the rest of her equipment. This will exclude her from the fight in Enir-Ilim.
Redmane Freyja is located at the Three-Path Cross, north of the Gravesite Plain Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]. She will recognize you as the Tarnished, introduce herself, and mention Hornsent , who will give you the Cross Map , which is a map of Miquella's Crosses .
(Optional) You can unlock additional dialogue by heading to the Belurat Tower Settlement [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]. Here, Freyja can be summoned to help you defeat the Divine Beast Dancing Lion . After the battle, return to Freyja at the Three-Path Cross [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ] to talk about Hornsent and learn more about her past as a Redmane. Additionally, she can be summoned to help defeat Rugalea the Great Red Bear for this dialogue before she advances to the Shadow Keep .
To progress Freyja's questline, Miquella's rune must be broken. After doing so you will receive the message "Somewhere a great rune has been broken. And so too, a powerful charm." To break the great rune:
Enter the Shadow Keep front entrance, through Messmer's Army Encampment. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]
From the Bonny Village , head east to Scadu Altus , then proceed north from the Bridge Leading to the Village Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ].
After breaking the great rune, return to Freyja at the Three-Path Cross and exhaust her dialogue. She will now move to Shadow Keep .
When you enter Shadow Keep through the Main Gate, you will encounter the Golden Hippopotamus [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ] boss fight, and you will be able to summon Redmane Freyja to assist you in this fight.
Once the Golden Hippopotamus has been defeated, you will find Redmane Freyja again at the Shadow Keep , on the Storehouse, Seventh Floor of the Specmien Storehouse [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]. You will find her crouched low and attempting to read scriptures off a stone. Talk to her to learn that she needs help from Sir Ansbach and will not say anything new to you if you keep trying to talk to her. Then, speak with Sir Ansbach on the Storehouse, First Floor [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ].
In order to receive the Letter to Freyja from Sir Ansbach, speak to Ansbach about Freyja and exhaust all his dialogues, then you should give the Secret Rite Scroll to him. The scroll can be found at the Storehouse, Fourth Floor on a desk behind some tall bookshelves [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map Link ]. Rest at the Site of Grace and return to him to receive the letter, report back to him after you have given the letter to Freyja.
NOTE : If you give the Secret Rite Scroll to Ansbach first before telling him about Freyja, he will leave if you rest at a Site of Grace or travel somewhere else, locking you out of progressing Freyja's questline further. However , if you aid Needle Knight Leda against Ansbach during her questline, and he already left the location due to the scroll, he will drop the letter as part of his loot, allowing you to still progress Freyja's questline.
Now once you have the letter, your choice will be the determining factor of either progressing her questline or not. If you decide to give the letter (which we recommend to maximize the rewards you get for Freyja's questline), return to the Storehouse, Seventh Floor site of grace.
Give the letter to Freyja : Freyja will give you the Golden Lion Shield in return and continue her quest, moving on from Shadow Keep. (Be sure to exhaust her dialogue after giving the letter.)
Do not give the letter to Freyja : Freyja's quest will not progress, and she will remain in Shadow Keep until you defeat the final boss of the DLC. Once you've done so, return to her spot in the Shadow Keep to find the Golden Lion Shield .
If you've given Freyja the Letter to Freyja , she will fight against you in the NPC battle in Enir-Ilim (after the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom Site of Grace). After the battle, you'll be able to loot Freyja's Greatsword and Freyja's Set from her corpse in the same room.
Ahh, Lady Leda spoke of you. You're that Tarnished, guided here by Kindly Miquella. Weren't we all. I am Freyja. I once fought alongside General Radahn . In battle, you can be sure I'll hold my own.
Oh, another thing. Did you speak to our dour little friend? If you've yet to do so, have him give you a map of the crosses' whereabouts. They are Miquella's footprints.
I urge you follow after Miquella.

Long ago, I was stricken by scarlet rot in the Swamp of Aeonia . Immobile, feverish, and in great pain, I was entirely resigned to death.

We're not gods, you and I. Even if we did manage to dig up the secrets of his past... I doubt our meddling could ever amount to much

Redmane Freyja is an NPC in Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree .
2 Questline Progression
Formerly one of the most distinguished knights of Starscourge Radahn , who invited her into his ranks after witnessing her valour as a gladiator [ 1 ] , Freyja is one of the compatriots of Needle Knight Leda who followed Miquella into the Realm of Shadow .
In the past, Freyja was stricken by Scarlet Rot in the Swamp of Aeonia . Left behind and resigned to death, she was approached by Miquella, who put his lips to her wound and drained the poison from it. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Freyja was subsequently charmed by Miquella, and later entered the Realm of Shadow with Leda and her compatriots.
After Miquella's charm is broken, Freyja chooses to strike out on her own, although she remains loyal to Miquella. [ 4 ] She recalls Miquella mentioning a vow he once made with her former lord Radahn, and resolves to learn the nature of the vow. [ 5 ] To this end, she travels to the Shadow Keep , where she has difficulty reading the letters etched into the stone tablets. [ 6 ] With assistance from the The Tarnished and Sir Ansbach , Freyja learns that Miquella wishes to revive the soul of General Radahn. Freyja welcomes this, believing that endless war would suit her former master better than an honourable death. [ 7 ]
Later, Freyja joins Leda and Dryleaf Dane in Enir-Ilim , in their fight against the Tarnished. Upon her defeat, she praises the Tarnished for providing her with a glorious battle. [ 8 ]

area: Abyssal Woods
chapter: 37
name: Midra Lord of Frenzied Flame
Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame is a Legend Boss in Elden Ring . He is the embodiment of the Frenzied Flame found in Midra's Manse . This is an optional boss as players do not need to defeat it to advance in Shadow of the Erdtree .
See Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame Lore
Enough.... I have endured more than enough...I ask you forgive me, dearest Nanaya...
Midra's human form must be defeated before fighting the true boss.
This is an optional boss
Closest Site of Grace: Second Floor Chamber
Multiplayer is allowed for this boss
You can summon Spirit Ashes for this boss
Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame is the final boss of Midra's Manse . [ Map Link ]
Parryable : Yes, but 3 parries are required per stance break
Is vulnerable to a critical hit after being stance broken or parried
Damage: Standard , Pierce , Fire , Holy
Drops 410,000, Remembrance of the Lord of Frenzied Flame
The negation numbers are the % of your damage that gets blocked. For example, if a negation is 60, 40% of that damage by that type will go through and 60% will be negated. Bigger number = less damage. A negation of 100 means no damage goes through, and a negation of -100 mean the enemy takes 2x damage from that source. 0 means damage goes pretty much as is.
Poison : 726 / 1016 / 1473
Scarlet Rot : 726 / 1016 / 1473
Hemorrhage : 444 / 542 / 832 / 1289
Frostbite : 444 / 542 / 832 / 1289
The resistance numbers are the buildup amount to trigger it. For example, if a resistance is 100 you must deal 100 points of the given buildup to trigger it. Note that these go down over time, and increase each time the effect procs. The values after the "/"s indicate the increased resistances after each successive proc.
Poison : 323 / 365 / 463 / 753 / 1210
Scarlet Rot : 323 / 365 / 463 / 753 / 1210
The Best Tips for Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame:
Summons: Dung Eater Puppet , Mimic Tear to duplicate Hemorrhage effects (in your inventory)
Armaments: Rivers of Blood , Bloodfiend's Arm , Great Stars , Anvil Hammer , Staff of the Guilty , Albinauric Staff , Misericorde
Spells: Lucidity , Impenetrable Thorns , Swarm of Flies , Flame Protect Me , Ekzykes's Decay
Skills: Lion's Claw , Savage Lion's Claw , Cragblade
Talismans: Flamedrake Talisman +3 , Clarifying Horn Charm +2
Other items: Clarifying Boluses , Boiled Crab , Glovewort Crystal Tear
Prone to Hemorrhage (Bleed): Use Blood Grease on Great Katana (Can also use Drawstring Blood Grease )
Stance can be broken: Two Full Heavy Hits
Inflicting Bleed or Frost will cause him to stumble. This is not the same as Stagger, but provides a brief window to disengage, heal, or cancel his attack.
Midra is a challenging boss wielding a Greatsword covered in barbs, infused with the Frenzied Flame , making his attacks both unique and dangerous. The fight with Midra has two phases, with the second phase significantly more difficult due to his reliance on high-damage Frenzied Flame-based attacks.
When you first enter the arena, you'll face Midra's human form, a " Wailing Man " armed with a stick. This initial form is much weaker than the main boss but can still cause significant damage through a grab attack or a madness Ao E. It is crucial to deplete his health quickly to avoid these attacks. After defeating this form, there's a brief window before Midra transforms into his true form. Use this time to drink the Flask of Wondrous Physick to extend your buffs for the upcoming fight.
Once the transformation is complete, Midra's true form is revealed, and the real challenge begins. His first move will likely be to summon Flame Strands that crawl toward you. To avoid this, time your forward roll just as the attack approaches, then delay a second roll to close the distance. This positioning allows you to land a heavy attack while Midra is recovering, as he is susceptible to being staggered.
By carefully timing your moves and managing your buffs, you can turn the tide in this challenging fight against Midra.
When at close range, Midra will begin swinging his greatsword at you. While he is a slow-moving boss, his attacks are quick and yet, predictable. One of his common attack strings is the Triple Greatsword Swing . He begins with a right overhead swing, followed by a left overhead swing, and ends it again with another right swing in quick succession. This attack can be dodged by going towards his weapon, so you should dodge going to your right, then left, and right again. At the end of this attack string, you should be able to land a heavy attack on him while he's recovering.
For his second combo, he will pull off a 3-attack string that begins with 2 quick sweeps starting from the left, then right, and followed by a Dash Thrust . The final move in this combo allows him to dash forward which leaves a trail of flames. To dodge this move, roll backward twice for the quick sweeps, then roll forward as he finishes this move. This creates a distance between you and him, and from here, you can cast a spell if you have the FP for it.
Another one of his combos is a 2-attack string where he begins with a left-side swing of his weapon and is followed by a Thrust with a short delay. For this attack, you can dodge by rolling to the left, and then towards him. If you do this successfully, you will be able to get behind him and land another heavy attack. The delay is something that needs to be learned so take your time with this. For the next melee move, you will notice Midra use the nail in his left hand and stab you with it. This Nail Stab attack varies with either a single stab or a double stab with a slight delay in between. To dodge this move, roll backward to dodge the first stab, and wait for a bit before you go in with an attack since he might follow up with another stab.
One of Midra’s most powerful attacks is the Big Flame Explosion . He starts to use this once you reach the second phase when his health drops to about 70%. When you see him rise up above you, it’s a clear sign that the Big Flame Explosion is coming. Once you see him prepare this move, you want to run to the edge of the arena as there's no way of dodging it at close range. Failing to do so can result in high damage, potentially even a one-shot kill, so prioritizing distance is crucial.
In the second phase, Midra will retain his original strings of attacks and will gain new ones. He can now cast a single Flame Strand that travels the same way as his opening attack, but instead of 2 Strands, there's only one here. You can dodge the same way you did previously. The next move begins with Midra firing up his greatsword. You can see the Frenzied Flame covering his weapon and he begins to do a right-step sweep followed closely by a lunge swipe. This move also creates flames on the ground, it doesn't deal damage to you, but it does fill up the Madness bar . Make sure that you pay attention to this if you don't want to lose Focus and HP over time. Since this attack is coming from YOUR left, you can dodge this by rolling to your left, then a delayed roll to the right. Essentially, you are rolling towards the weapon to evade this attack.
He can also do Explosive Grab Plunge . He will start this attack by flying up in the air and then crashes down onto the ground in an attempt to grab you with his right hand while he plunges the greatsword with his left hand. Watch out for the aftershock where barbs will grow from underneath when he plunges his weapon deeper onto the ground. To dodge this, make sure you look up at his character while he's in mid-air. As Midra is floating, get ready to roll forward right before he hits the ground, then face toward Midra, and roll forward when you see him plunge the greatsword again to dodge the aftershock. You will be able to land a full heavy attack on him if you manage to avoid this pattern.
Finally, the longest string of attacks from Midra is a mix of quick and delayed strikes . This attack begins with a left-side sweep followed by 4 strings of delayed and quick attacks that alternate direction. Overall, you can dodge this move in any direction, but the move is more forgiving if you roll toward the origin of the weapon. So if it's coming from the right, you will dodge to the right. Your dodge pattern for this move will go like this:
Dodge to the right for the first sweep
Pause for a brief moment, roll to the left
Do 2 quick rolls to the right, and then left again
Pause here, and wait for the final sweep, then roll to the right
Moving on to his special attack, Midra can also perform Frenzied Gaze , firing multiple beams from a distance. You will notice him pull back his head and then rapidly fire lasers that track you. This move is typically slow and isn't fast enough to keep up with the Tarnished. This works best if you are close to him so make sure that you are near him when he initiates this move. Then, for his finisher, you will see Midra pause and create a ball of fire emit from his head. As soon as you see him do this, make sure to run to the edge of the arena to get out of the blast. This move will explode after some time and there's no way to dodge this aside from getting out of the Ao E. Note that the flame trails does not have any damage so you can go back in and get free hits on him.
Throughout the battle, maintain a balance between offense and defense. His ranged fire attacks are overwhelming due to their large damage area, so always be ready to dodge when you see him preparing these moves. Midra's high damage output means that rushing in recklessly will likely lead to a quick defeat. Instead, time your attacks carefully and be ready to switch to a defensive stance from time to time. Watching his movements closely will help you anticipate his attacks and react when needed.
Midra can be a tough boss to beat because his high damage can easily kill your character if you are not careful. Study his attack patterns, exploit the moments when he is open for attacks, and don't get greedy with your attacks. Sometimes, it's better to land a few hits and then retreat to a safe distance rather than risk getting killed immediately. Overall, the best strategy to defeat Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame, is to stay close to him, anticipate his moves, and go for a full heavy attack at the end of his combos.
Midra is weak to bleed effects and on NG, he is eaten alive by repeated casts of Swarm of Flies or Impenetrable Thorns . The boss has several attacks that can quickly close the gap between himself and the player. Despite this boss's extreme aggression, there is a short window of opportunity that the player can capitalize on between each of Midra's combos. As such, ranged players will find it useful to carry a light and fast melee weapon that applies bleed, such as Moonveil , Uchigatana , or Rivers of Blood .
Note that Midra is susceptible to Rotten Breath / Ekzykes's Decay . Spirit ash summons are most effective in combination with Glovewort Crystal Tear . Standing at a safe distance, this boss can be made trivial by summoning Greatshield Soldiers , and spamming casts of Impenetrable Thorns enhanced by Beloved Stardust . Not only does this spell melt the boss's health bar, but it also deals poise damage with a high chance of staggering him. If your character does not meet the minimum stat requirement to cast impenetrable thorns, acquire the necessary faith by equipping Marika's Soreseal , Two Fingers Heirloom , or Faith-knot Crystal Tear .
Voice Actor: Connor Byrne, who also voiced Father Gascoigne from Bloodborne and Laddersmith Gilligan from Dark Souls II.

Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame is an optional boss in Shadow of the Erdtree .
3 Boss Fight 3.1 Phase One 3.2 Phase Two
Long ago, the great Sage Midra attempted to become a Lord of Frenzied Flame . However, he was too weak and failed. [ 1 ] Pierced by the branching thorns of the Greatsword of Damnation , his power was suppressed by the hornsent . [ 2 ] [ 3 ] [ 4 ] Midra's Manse and the dark lands of the Abyssal Woods were forbidden by the Hornsent. [ 5 ] It is said the eyes of those who served the sage were burned by the Frenzied Flame of madness . [ 6 ] [ 7 ] Midra lingered on in perpetual suffering at the behest of his beloved Nanaya . [ 8 ] [ 9 ]
Eventually, a Tarnished Champion entered Midra's manse to explore and loot it. Midra repeatedly tried to warn the Tarnished to leave, but his pleas went unheeded. Upon the Tarnished coming upon Midra's chambers, Midra chastised the Tarnished for their foolishness before attacking them, only be brutally beaten and subdued. Finally, Midra could endure his pain no more. He pulls the sword out through his head and decapitates himself. Then, he manifests as the Lord of Frenzied Flame. The branching arms of the sword coil tightly around its central needle. [ 4 ]
A hollow man impaled with a golden barbed spear. He wearing a yellow or gold robe with a big clasp. The barbed spear went through his eye and replaced his spine. [ 10 ] He is in a lot of pain; poor guy looks like a Christmas tree. He rips his own head off by pulling out the spear. He gains a fireball head and the barbs coil around the spear to make a sword.
You fight the feeble Lord. He has low HP and defenses; the player will likely kill him with a few hits at most.
He pulls his head off and gets a fireball head of Frenzied Flame. He deals madness .
Midra's wounds have stitches. [ 11 ]
Midra is voiced by Connor Byrne, who also voiced Laddersmith Gilligan in DARK SOULS II and Father Gascoigne in Bloodborne.

area: Ancient Ruins of Rauh
chapter: 36
name: Romina Saint of the Bud
Romina, Saint of the Bud is a Legend Boss in Elden Ring . She appears humanoid, though her lower half has inexplicably transformed into a centipede with a scorpion's stinger. After her church was burned during Messmer's crusade, she discovered a twisted divine element and weaved it into the Scarlet Rot , wielding the bud she cradled as a deadly poleblade in combat. She can be found in the Church of the Bud, which you can access at the end of your exploration in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh . This is not an optional boss, as players need to defeat her to advance in Shadow of the Erdtree .
See Romina, Saint of the Bud Lore
This is not an optional boss
Closest Site of Grace: Church of the Bud, Main Entrance
Multiplayer is allowed for this boss
You can summon Spirit Ashes for this boss
You can summon Dryleaf Dane for this boss
Romina, Saint of the Bud can be found in Church of the Bud. You can get to the Church of the Bud by heading to the top floor of the western-most square building in the Rauh Ancient Ruins [ Map Link ].
From the Rauh Ancient Ruins, West Site of Grace , go southwest into the building. From the top of the stairs, go roughly straight ahead to the exit to outdoors.  Go north across the watery outdoor area. To the north is an entrance to a room with red rot and red Lesser Kindred of Rot enemies. Find the lift on the left wall a short way into the room. If the lift is raised, it will look like a solid stone wall and may be easy to miss. Take the lift up then go up the inside central stairs and you'll emerge at the The Church of the Bud.
Alternative Route: from Ancient Ruins, Grand Stairway Site of Grace. Go partly down the stairs to about midway between the site of grace and the Archer Golem . To the north you will see one of several circular shaped gazebos down on a lower landscape level far under the stairs, where there is also is water and 2 yellow armored knights. The gazebo midway between the site of grace and the Golem will allow you to double jump onto it with Torrent from the stairs without any fall damage. The correct jumping point is right near the join between the stairs and a flat section of the stairs path. Double jump from the stairs to the gazebo, and then safely to the ground. Then go north, pass the yellow knights, pass the other gazebo, and go into a red rot-filled interior. In the rot-filled room on the left wall is a block-shaped lift near some red Lesser Kindred of Rot enemies . Take the lift up, then go up the central stairs to get to the Church of the Bud.
Is vulnerable to a critical hit after being stance broken or parried
Damage: Standard , Slash , Strike , Pierce
Inflicts: Scarlet Rot
Drops 380,000, Remembrance of the Saint of the Bud
The negation numbers are the % of your damage that gets blocked. For example, if a negation is 60, 40% of that damage by that type will go through and 60% will be negated. Bigger number = less damage. A negation of 100 means no damage goes through, and a negation of -100 mean the enemy takes 2x damage from that source. 0 means damage goes pretty much as is.
Scarlet Rot : Immune
Hemorrhage : 444 / 542 / 832 / 1289
Frostbite : 323 / 365 / 463 / 753 / 1210
The resistance numbers are the buildup amount to trigger it. For example, if a resistance is 100 you must deal 100 points of the given buildup to trigger it. Note that these go down over time, and increase each time the effect procs. The values after the "/"s indicate the increased resistances after each successive proc.
The Best Tips for Romina, Saint of the Bud:
Recommended summons: Mimic Tear , Black Knife Tiche
Recommended armaments: Barbed Staff-Spear , Royal Greatsword
Recommended spells: Flame, Cleanse Me , Swarm of Flies , Impenetrable Thorns , Frozen Lightning Spear , Dragonice
Recommended talismans: Sacred Scorpion Charm , Lightning Scorpion Charm , Godfrey Icon , Immunizing Horn Charm
Bring Preserving Boluses , can be crafted by finding the Armorer's Cookbook (6)
Other items: Holy-Shrouding Cracked Tear , Lightning-Shrouding Cracked Tear , Cerulean Hidden Tear
Romina, Saint of the Bud , is a mid-sized boss with a body that combines features of a centipede, scorpion, butterfly, and human. She wields a poleblade, centipede fangs, and a scorpion stinger, making her a distinctive opponent. To defeat Romina, you need a balanced strategy of offense and defense, but being more aggressive is often advantageous.
When fighting her as a melee character, focus on her central human body as well as her centipede fangs and scorpion stinger, which act as her arms. These parts move independently, allowing her to launch multiple simultaneous attacks, such as swinging her spear while her stinger strikes from another direction. Her attacks include Heavy Spear Thrusts , Sweeping Stinger strikes, and Biting Fangs .
Romina’s common opening move is the Centipede Sweep , where her centipede body swings from right to left. You can dodge this by rolling forward just before it hits. This allows you to get close and deliver a full heavy attack. Be aware that she may follow up with a Quick Dash to reposition and swing her poleblade. This can be avoided by rolling to your left or backward.
Stay as close as possible to Romina, as some of her sweep attacks won't hit you at point-blank range. Being near her also makes her attack pattern more predictable. For instance, after her usual opening attack, closing the distance with a dodge roll will cause her follow-up attack to miss if you are right beside her.
One thing that you should do is to always look at her Poleblade , when you see it glowing red, this means that the weapon is enchanted with Scarlet Rot. If this weapon hits you while the enchantment is active, it will cause Scarlet Rot build-up. So always be wary of her weapon status . One of the combos that she does with her enchanted weapon, is the Leap Combo where she starts with a poleblade sweep, an overhead attack, and then she jumps high in the air, and then plunges her weapon down. To dodge this move you will do the following:
Roll to your left for the sweep attack
Roll forward (slightly to your right) as she swings the overhead attack
Pause here and pay attention when her weapon flashes pink for a brief moment, then roll forward at that exact moment
When this move is dodged properly, you can get a couple of free hits as she recovers. This move also has a variation where she would skip the 3rd part of the attack and will just do an enchanted weapon combo. She also has another basic move where she makes 3 quick sweeping movements with her poleblade without enchantments. Then finally, she can also do the Rolling Attack where Romina will curl up into a ball, and then roll around the arena. After she is done rolling, she will then slam the ground and do a swing with her poleblade. You can avoid the initial roll by getting out of her way by either dodging or sprinting away. Lock the camera onto her to see where she is going. At the end of the roll, dodge roll to the right to dodge both of her attacks. Then, you can retaliate with a heavy attack.
After she finishes a combo, quickly counterattack to take advantage of the brief windows of vulnerability. Her attacks, while powerful, have high recovery times, allowing you to dodge and immediately counter. When she begins a combo, dodge her attacks by rolling to the side or towards her to close the distance. Immediately after dodging, counter with heavy attacks to break her poise. Frequent usage of jumping and heavy attacks is recommended as it can stagger her, leaving her open to further attacks.
One of her special moves is the Double Spin 360 Combo . This is a move where you see her lift her human body up as her weapon glows red, then, she will do 2 full spins while swinging her poleblade. Then, she pauses after this move to recover. This move can be dodged by rolling to the left, then dodging to the left again after a slight delay. This move usually causes Romina to end up at a far position from you, but she will take time to recover and you can do at least 1 light attack or a jump attack if you sprint up to her.
During Phase 2, her attacks remain the same but she can now conjure butterflies that will explode after a short period. She will transition into the 2nd Phase when her health drops to 50%, and you will see her surround herself with pink lighting with butterflies circling her. After a brief period, this will cause a whirlwind in the small area, then she follows up this move with more butterflies in the outer area of the whirlwind that will explode randomly throughout the arena. To dodge the Butterfly Bombs move, start sprinting away as her body lights up with pink hue, then, if you can outrun the Ao E, you can just stand at the edge and you will not get hit. If you get caught, you can spam roll until you get out of the Ao E. As the fight goes on, Romina will likely redo this move again, but with a shorter preparation. So you will need to react quicker to get out of the exploding butterflies. Romina at first seems like you must wait and punish with a typical melee build. She has a glaring weakness of being weak to animations that shift the players' hurt box down to low profile common moves she performs, such as her double glowing spear lunge and swipe move. A perfect example I found was using Dryleaf Arts, the Heavy attack naturally low profiles many of her spear-swiping moves. During my run I found it more beneficial to be aggressive rather than defensive, staying on her inner right side up front will negate a lot of her attacks with a dodge as needed. I ran frost to add the slow to her animations which was a plus but not needed in fighting her. The low profiling and staying on the offensive to break stance to heal as needed or deal big damage was enough. During you have enough time to heal and use Ash of War: Divine Beast Frost Stomp for example without getting hit. Patience and aggressive play shine in this fight as long as you can consistent low profile which is the namesake of this strategy.
Romina moves around a lot, so spells with high tracking will be most useful. Flame Cleanse Me can be used to heal scarlet rot.
Incantations have a lot of options, as the boss is weak to lightning, holy, and frost. Knight's Lightning Spear is a good choice, as the follow-up spears can usually still hit even if the main spear misses. Pest-Thread Spears have high tracking and damage. Romina is weak to Swarm of Flies .
One of the best options among Sorceries is Roiling Magma . Buff it with Talisman of the Dread , cast with Gelmir Glintstone Staff or have it in the off-hand. Upon entry to the arena throw the Hefty Oil Pot . Considering your catalyst's sorcery scaling is maxed out, first cast should take most part of the boss's HP. Impenetrable Thorns is also an excellent choice.
Spirit Ash summons and the NPC furled finger Dryleaf Dane will be a great help to ranged users.
Romina was first shown in the Story Trailer for Shadow of the Erdtree, kneeling and holding her poleblade amidst the burning church.
A cloth doll depicting a dancer garbed in blue. An ancient heirloom of some sort.
Raises attack power with lower equipment load.
The dancer in blue represents a fairy, who in legend bestowed a flowing sword upon a blind swordsman. Blade in hand, the swordsman sealed away an ancient god — a god that was Rot itself.

Weapon of Romina, Saint of the Bud. A scarlet glaive with a dangling bud-like blade. Attacks cause buildup of rot. Once, in the crumbling, burning church, Romina held the bud in speechless silence. That bud would become her blade.

Romina, Saint of the Bud is a mandatory boss in Shadow of the Erdtree .
2 Boss Fight 2.1 Phase One 2.2 Phase Two
Romina is one of the many victims of Messmer the Impaler 's crusade throughout the Realm of Shadow , with her church being burned to the ground. In the crumbling, burning church, Romina held the bud in speechless silence. [ 1 ]
Within the church where everything was burned, Romina discovered a grotesque divinity, and she clung to the sinister Scarlet Rot. [ 2 ] She transformed into a parasitic creature, a hybrid of a centipede, scorpion and butterfly, while the Bud she held was converted into a poleblade that became Romina's signature weapon. [ 3 ]
Romina is not an optional boss as it must be defeated in order to burn the Sealing Tree and reach Enir-Ilim .
Romina is a distinctive opponent whose weaponry are her poleblade, centipede fangs and a scorpion stinger. It is important to have a balanced strategy of both offense and defense but it is advantageous to be more aggressive in battle.
When fighting her,  focus on her central human body as well as her centipede fangs and scorpion stinger, which act as her arms. These parts move independently, allowing her to launch multiple simultaneous attacks, such as swinging her spear while her stinger strikes from another direction. Her attacks include Heavy Spear Thrusts, Sweeping Stinger strikes, and Biting Fangs.
However, her most common opening move is her Centipede Sweep , where the centipede part of her body will swing from right to left. It can be dodged by rolling forward. This will let you get close to Romina to deliver a full heavy attack. However, you have to be aware that she may follow up by dashing quickly and swing her poleblade, which can be avoided by rolling to the left or backwards.
It is important to always look at Romina's poleblade, as when it glows red, this shows that it has become enchanted with Scarlet Rot. If hit with the enchanted weapon, it will cause a Scarlet Rot build-up. Always be wary of her weapon status. One of the combos that she does with her enchanted weapon, is the Leap Combo where she starts with a poleblade sweep, an overhead attack, and then she jumps high in the air, and then plunges her weapon down. In order to dodge this, you can either:
Roll to your left for a sweep attack
Roll forward (slightly forward to your right) when she wings the overhead attack.
Pay attention when Romina's poleblade will flash pink for a brief moment then roll forward at that exact moment.
When the leap combo is dodged properly, you can deliver a couple of free hits while she recovers. This combo also has a variation where Romina would skip the 3rd part of the attack and will just do an enchanted weapon combo. She also has another basic move where she makes 3 quick sweeping movements with her poleblade without enchantments. Finally, she can also do the Rolling Attack where she will curl up into a ball, and then roll around the arena. After she finishes rolling, she will then slam the ground and do a swing with her poleblade. This can be initially avoided by getting out of her way by either dodging or sprinting away. Lock the camera onto her to see where she is going. At the end of the roll, roll to the right to dodge both of her attacks. Then, you can retaliate with a heavy attack.
After she finishes a combo, quickly counterattack to take advantage of the time where she will be briefly vulnerable. While powerful, her attacks have high recovery times, allowing you to dodge and immediately counter. When she begins a combo, dodge her attacks by rolling to the side or towards her to close the distance. Immediately after dodging, counter with heavy attacks to break her poise. Frequent usage of jumping and heavy attacks is recommended as it can stagger her, leaving her open to further attacks.
One of her special moves is the Double Spin 360 Combo , where she will lift up her human body as her weapon glows red, and will do 2 full spins while swinging her poleblade. After performing this move, she will pause to recover. This move can be dodged by rolling to the left, then dodging to the left again after a slight delay. This move usually causes Romina to end up at a far position from you, but she will take time to recover and you can do at least 1 light attack or a jump attack if you sprint up to her.
After her health drops to 50%, she will immediately transition to her second phase; her attacks remain the same, but she now has the ability to conjure butterflies that will explode like bombs after a short period. She will be seen with pink butterflies circling around her, and after a brief period will cause a whirlwind in the small arena. Romina then follow up this move with more butterflies in the outer area of the whirlwind that will randomly explode throughout the area. To dodge this move, start sprinting away as her body lights up with pink hue, then, if you can outrun the Ao E, you can just stand at the edge and you will not get hit. If you get caught, you can spam roll until you get out of the Ao E. As the fight goes on, Romina will likely redo this move again, but with a shorter preparation. So you will need to react quicker to get out of the exploding butterflies.
Romina at first seems like you must wait and punish with a typical melee build. She has a glaring weakness of being weak to animations that shift the players' hurt box down to low profile common moves she performs, such as her double glowing spear lunge and swipe move. A perfect example I found was using Dryleaf Arts, the Heavy attack naturally low profiles many of her spear-swiping moves. During my run I found it more beneficial to be aggressive
