area: Shadow Keep
chapter: 35
name: Rellana Twin Moon Knight
Rellana, Twin Moon Knight is a Legend Boss in Elden Ring . A former princess of the Carian Royal Family , she is the younger sister of Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon . Wielding her signature Twin Blades that she can infuse with Glintstone magic and Messmer's Flame respectively, she is fought at the end of Castle Ensis . This is an optional boss as players do not need to defeat her to advance in Shadow of the Erdtree .
See Rellana, Twin Moon Knight Lore
This is an optional boss. She can be bypassed via the sealed Spritspring in Gravesite Plain to enter Scadu Altus.
Closest Site of Grace: Castle Lord's Chamber.
Multiplayer is allowed for this boss.
You can summon Spirit Ashes for this boss.
You can summon Needle Knight Leda for this boss: Note that she often uses the Needle Piercer skill, which removes buffs and debuffs. Depending on the player build, summoning her may not be recommended. If you bypassed Rellana to enter Scadu Altus and return to the boss fight later, Leda cannot be summoned if Miquella's Great Rune has broken.
Note that she often uses the Needle Piercer skill, which removes buffs and debuffs. Depending on the player build, summoning her may not be recommended.
If you bypassed Rellana to enter Scadu Altus and return to the boss fight later, Leda cannot be summoned if Miquella's Great Rune has broken.
Patch 1.13 removed Dryleaf Dane 's summon sign for this boss.
Rellana, Twin Moon Knight can be found by heading to the East Wing of Castle Ensis . Go up the stairs around the tower to reach a bridge guarded by a Black Knight with a hammer and a greatshield. After defeating this enemy, use the elevator behind it to go up and use the Castle-Lord's Chamber Site of Grace to your right. You will find the mist door to this boss in the next hallway to your left. [ Map Link ] [ Video Link ]
Parryable : Yes, but 2 parries are required per stance break
Is vulnerable to a critical hit after being stance broken or parried
Damage: Standard , Slash , Pierce , Magic , Fire
Drops 240,000, Remembrance of the Twin Moon Knight
The negation numbers are the % of your damage that gets blocked. For example, if a negation is 60, 40% of that damage by that type will go through and 60% will be negated. Bigger number = less damage. A negation of 100 means no damage goes through, and a negation of -100 mean the enemy takes 2x damage from that source. 0 means damage goes pretty much as is.
Poison : 296 / 338 / 436 / 726 / 1183
Scarlet Rot : 296 / 338 / 436 / 726 / 1183
Hemorrhage : 408 / 506 / 796 / 1253
Frostbite : 408 / 506 / 796 / 1253
Sleep : 296 / 338 / 436 / 726 / 1183
The resistance numbers are the buildup amount to trigger it. For example, if a resistance is 100 you must deal 100 points of the given buildup to trigger it. Note that these go down over time, and increase each time the effect procs. The values after the "/"s indicate the increased resistances after each successive proc.
Rellana is one of the hardest bosses that you will encounter in Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree, with a particular focus on brutal boss design. She combines swift and aggressive melee attacks, often with short reaction time, with ranged magic spells to form a very deadly arsenal for both new and veteran players. Once she enters Phase 2, her combo attacks deal extra magic and fire damage, she gets new, optional extensions to her combo strings, and can add addtional attacks to final blows, allowing her to deal large amounts of damage per hit to an already fast paced boss fight and reducing the margin for error, as one late dodge or instance of panic rolling can cost you the attempt.
The Best Tips for Rellana, Twin Moon Knight:
Recommended Spirit Ashes: Greatshield Soldiers , Black Knife Tiche , Mimic Tear , Black Knight Commander Andreas
Recommended Ash of War: Flame of the Redmanes , Giant Hunt , Wing Stance
Recommended Spells: Rotten Breath , Swarm of Flies , Lightning Spear , Barrier of Gold
Bring the Spelldrake Talisman (+3) to reduce Magic Damage, it can be found in Castle Ensis. Also recommended to craft Spellproof Dried / Pickled Liver .
Also recommended to craft Spellproof Dried / Pickled Liver .
There is a pool of water in the center of arena, so it's recommended to bring some weapons that can deal Lightning Damage .
You can use a Shield or dodge roll to either side to protect yourself from incoming physical damage. The key to winning is patience. You can summon Mimic Tear to fight alongside you or opt to summon Leda. It is recommended to only have 1 Summon so the boss will not have a dramatic increase in health pool. Beware that the NPC summon enacts a general debuff that will affect the player.
The boss's long variable combos make it difficult to get near her to launch your attack, but the player should particularly observe her Forward Thrust Combos , as they often provide the best punish windows.:
3-string attack combo: she slices each weapon once and ends with Double Thrust with both light greatswords.
6-string attack combo: she starts with an upward Thrust with each weapon, followed by one spinning attack, a quick thrust with each weapon, and ends with another Double Thrust.
The key to determining the end of her attack patterns is when she does the move slow. If it's fast, there's a big chance that it will be followed up by a slow attack, then if it's slow (or heavy) then, you can go in for an attack as she recovers.
When you see an opening, opt for a heavy attack for a chance to stagger her.
The second phase of the fight begins when her health drops below 50%. Rellana will  infuse her Greatswords  with Glintstone and Flame elements. The greatswords will glow blue and orange. The boss becomes more dangerous and the moves become more difficult to dodge. The dodge frames become shorter and the Ao E of skills become larger.
Fire. The flame element creates the widest Ao E when using the Sleight of Sword - Fire. This also creates smaller rings of fire in the outer Ao E but they don't deal as much damage in the center of the skill. Ideally, you can roll backward three times to get yourself out of the area. Make sure to pay attention to the outer rings as you run to safety.
Magic. When you see her cast the huge Magical Greatsword, be prepared for the first downward vertical slash, this is immediately followed by a giant horizontal spin slash that reaches nearly the entire room. Make sure to dodge early for this attack because it has a deceptively wide reach. Roll forward to get a free hit on her if you get the timing right.
Another spell that she can use is the Glintblade Slicer that she uses when you're far away from her. This is an attack where she swings her weapon downward and sends a blue arc that tracks your movements. The arc varies in position, others are vertical, horizontal, or slanted. To dodge the Glintblade Slicer , you can run away to the left for the first two (if you're far enough), then dodge roll twice for the 3rd and 4th slicer, and then, go for a forward roll for the 5th one. This will allow you to close the gap.
After a while, this boss will use Twin Moons , which protects them from incoming damage and is closely followed by a plunge attack. If you get hit by the falling moon, you will get stunned and you will not be able to roll away to avoid further damage. The Pearldrake Talisman will reduce incoming Elemental Damage. Sometimes she will use Twin Moons at at close range or she will jump away to execute it. The distance will not make much of a change since you still need to jump or dodge roll (any direction) at the exact time the moon hits the ground. Te move is dodgeable.
As the fight comes to an end, the boss will alternate between Physical and Elemental Attacks. Always pay attention to her light greatswords, because this will tell you what her next move is.
Sorcery users can cast Terra Magica at the door before entering, then cast two Ranni's Dark Moon back to back for great damage, chill, and to reduce the boss' magic resistance. Throughout the fight, one of the best spells to use is Night Comet , as Rellana will (most of the time) not dodge it, and it deals great damage when cast while holding a Staff of Loss on the other hand. Radagon Icon 's increase in casting speed is very useful in this fight.
Incantation users can make use of tanky spirit ash summons to keep Rellana still. Meanwhile, the summoner can cast rot breath and swarm of flies from a distance. The goal is to defeat her quickly, before she uses the more damaging abilities in her second phase. It is possible to defeat her before she summons the twin moons. If she does summon them, remember to jump three times (a third time when she lands) .
Her fighting style and enchanted swords are reminiscent of Pontiff Sulyvahn from Dark Souls III .
Silver thrusting shield embedded with glintstones. Enables the wielder to attack and guard simultaneously.
Once wielded by Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight, during her sole entry into ritual combat—a demonstration of fidelity to the Erdtree.
The hat of Count Ymir, High Priest. The circular design at the top represents the Greater Will and its lightless abyss, imparting increased intelligence and arcane to the wearer. Though Count Ymir instructed Rellana in the sorcerous arts, he abandoned his allegiance to the moon. "It was merely the closest of the celestial bodies. Nothing more."
Ornate armor of Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight, fashioned from silver steel. Adorned with the lapis-lazuli blue that is symbolic of Caria, its radiance is yet to fully fade.
Talisman featuring a gallant portrait of Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight. Enhances attacks executed after maintaining the same stance for a certain length of time. Engraved as a reminder of the unparalleled devotion of those who left their homelands to serve Rellana. "By your leave, we will accompany you wherever your lunar vessel takes you."
Ornate helm of Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight, fashioned from silver steel. Rennala, head of the royal family of Caria, was said to have given her younger sister, who renounced her lineage to chase after Messmer, a gift of lustrous black hair.
Sorcery of Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight. Associated with a Carian princess.
Incarnate overlapping twin moons which strike the ground one after the other, violently shaking the earth with moonlight.
In her childhood, she and her elder sister Rennala met these moons. Overlapping, as though nestled against one another.
Carian light greatsword embedded with blue glintstone. Weapon of Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight. Two swords as a single armament. When two-handing, a straight sword engraved with golden flame will be carried in the left hand.
Here, and here alone, were moon and fire ever together.
Remembrance of Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight, hewn into the Scadutree. The power of its namesake can be unlocked by the Finger Reader. Alternatively, it can be used to gain a great bounty of runes.
Once a Carian princess, Rellana disavowed her birthright and chose to stand at Messmer's side instead, knowing full well that not even the brilliance of the moon could grant him succor. Before long, she became known as the Sword of Messmer.
Shattered linchpin stone. Raises potency of kicking and stomping skills.
Linchpin stones are spiritual anchors said to hold the ground in place and quell the fury of earthquakes—when this one shattered, the surrounding town fell into the broken earth.
One account claimed that the moon itself had come tumbling down.

Radahn (nephew) Rykard (nephew) Radagon (ex-brother in law)
Rellana, Twin Moon Knight is a boss in Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree . She is encountered in Castle Ensis .
Born as a princess into the Carian Royal Family , Rellana is the younger sister of Rennala , who gifted her lustrous black hair [ 1 ] . In her childhood, she and her elder sister met the twin moon, which were overlapping, as though nestled against one another [ 2 ] . Rellana also had a sole entry into ritual combat—a demonstration of fidelity to the Erdtree [ 3 ] .
At some point, Count Ymir instructed Rellana in the sorcerous arts, though he later abandoned his allegiance to the moon. [ 4 ] In a similar fashion, she later disavowed her birthright and chose to stand at the side of Messmer the Impaler instead, knowing full well that not even the brilliance of the moon could grant him succor. Before long, she became known as the Sword of Messmer. [ 5 ]
During the search for Miquella, a Tarnished warrior fought through Castle Ensis and engaged in combat with Rellana, defeating her and opening a path to the Shadow Keep .
Rellana is an optional boss as she can be defeated in order to access Scadu Altus .
Needle Knight Leda can be summoned to fight this boss.
In the game's internal files, she is referred to as "Swordof Messmer". [ 6 ]
Rellana's soundtrack bears a melody similar to Rennala 's. [ 7 ]
Rellana has fully voiced cut dialogue. [ 8 ]

area: Jagged Peak
chapter: 38
name: Bayle the Dread
Bayle the Dread is a Legend Boss in Elden Ring . Bayle is the forefather of the drakes , a sworn enemy of the ancient dragons, and the target of Igon's vengeance. He is found at the summit of the Jagged Peak . This is an optional boss as players don't need to defeat it to advance in Shadow of the Erdtree .
See Bayle the Dread Lore
This is an optional boss
Closest Site of Grace: Jagged Peak Summit
Multiplayer is allowed for this boss
You can summon Spirit Ashes for this boss
NPC Summons : Igon , if you have Igon's Furled Finger from his summon sign in the arena, to the left as you enter. He does not count towards the maximum number of summoned players nor increases bayle's hp. He has unique dialogue during the boss fight, although his equipment can be obtained whether or not he was summoned.
Bayle can be found at the summit of Jagged Peak, from the Jagged Peak Summit Site of Grace, head through the stone arch that leads to the crater. [ Map Link ]
Is vulnerable to a critical hit after being stance broken
Damage: Standard , Fire , Lightning
Weak point: Head, Leg Stump, Bone at Wing stump (+50%)
Drops 490,000, Heart of Bayle

ER Shadow of the Erdtree Dragon Priestess Build Written Guide
The Best Tips for Bayle The Dread:
Recommended summons: Redmane Knight Ogha
Recommended armaments: Dragon King's Cragblade
Recommended spells: Ekzykes's Decay , Loretta's Mastery , Frenzied Burst , Meteorite of Astel
Recommended Talismans: Godfrey Icon , Blue Dancer Charm , Ritual Sword Talisman , Great-Jar's Arsenal
Bring Dragonwound Grease or preferably Dragon Communion Grease to more effectively deal damage Any weapon with bonus against Dragons will prove useful
Any weapon with bonus against Dragons will prove useful
Other items: Cerulean Hidden Tear in mixed Physick, to cast an expensive spell in order to end his first phase quicker. Other options include Opaline Bubbletear / Hardtear or Crimson Crystal Tear for survivability.
Bayle the Dread can be found in the crater of Jagged Peak. From the Jagged Peak Summit Site of Grace [ Map Link ], head straight through the stone archway, and Bayle will appear shortly after. This boss deals both Fire and Lightning damage, and when it breathes fire, the attack is a mixture of both elements. Bayle also leaps and flies around the arena, creating significant distance between you. NOTE : If you are progressing Igon's questline, be sure to use his summon sign inside Bayle's arena. Summoning Igon for this fight is crucial to completing his quest.
To tackle this fight against Bayle, you can opt to lock in on his head but your camera will follow all of its movements and make it difficult to track him. For now, there are no other lock-in options aside from its head. On the other hand, you can also focus on hitting the leg, without locking in your camera, to continuously inflict damage, but this will also prevent you from seeing what the incoming attacks are due to its massive size. This fight is a lot more difficult for melee users, but this is why you will use the Dragon Communion Grease or Dragonwound Grease to deal more damage by coating your armament.
Due to the fast movements of Bayle the Dread, it is recommended to take on this boss with Light equip load and pair it with the Blue Dancer Charm , Great-Jar's Arsenal , and Godfrey Icon . This gives you more i Frames and makes dodging easier on foot for melee users. You can also add in Erdtree's Favor +2 to further raise your maximum HP, Stamina, and Equip Load. This will let you dodge more frequently as you chip away at the boss' health. You can stay under its leg and hit him from there, as the tail doesn't have any hitbox as of the current patch (1.12.3).
Unlike other dragons, Bayle props itself up with its wings due to losing most of its left leg. This gives the dragon a hunched posture, making it harder to get underneath as you would with other large dragons. However, its head is more accessible for melee attacks, and both the head and left leg take additional damage. The wings can sometimes make it difficult to reach the leg, requiring an approach from the front or from the side. As with most dragons, using a piercing weapon like a spear, thrusting sword, pickaxe, or the Rusted Anchor is recommended.
Bayle has several attacks aside from its Fire and Lightning breath — Fatal Bites , Fatal Crush , Tail Swipe and a Lunging Claw Stab . During Maul , Bayle lunges forward to bite you repeatedly, potentially leading to instant death. To avoid this, be ready to dodge roll forward or to the side. Fatal Stomp involves Bayle hovering briefly, then gliding toward you and landing with brute force. When you see Bayle in the air, run away or dodge to avoid the impact. For the Tail Swipe , Bayle turns and swings its tail at you. When Bayle starts to turn, dodge roll forward to evade the attack. Finally, the Lunging Claw Stab occurs when Bayle hunches back slightly, and then lunges forward to stab with its claw. This attack happens almost instantly, so be prepared to roll to the side or forward, allowing you to close in for an attack.
Bayle also has several powerful Fire and Lightning breath attacks . One is an arena-wide attack, where Bayle sweeps Fire and Lightning from one side to the other. Another is an Ao E attack, where it breathes Fire and Lightning downward, covering a wide area. You can dodge roll to avoid both of these attacks. The last attack is the Straight breathing attack , where it breathes Fire and Lightning directly at you. You can easily dodge roll to the side since it doesn't track your movement. After defeating Bayle the Dread , you will receive 490,000 Runes and the Heart of Bayle .
Casters should avoid using lock-on except for spell projectiles that rely on a target lock. Bayle will frequently close the gap and position his body over you, which can make the camera erratic, making dodging difficult. Additionally, aiming for Bayle's head can cause spells to miss despite his torso or tail being in range. Sorcery users can utilize Ranni's Dark Moon to weaken Bayle with frostbite. During Bayle's phase change and large Ao E attacks, longer wind-up spells such as Comet Azur can be used to great effect. Faith casters can make use of Black Flame and Black Blade to whittle down Bayle's HP from a distance.
For Glintstone players, Shard Spiral is effective against large bosses like Bayle the Dread.
Bayle challenged Dragonlord Placidusax and they fought to a stalemate, leaving both severely wounded. This is shown in the expansion, as Bayle is missing the entirety of his left leg and also most of his wings. On Bayle's neck, one may notice two of Placidusax's heads still biting down, even after being severed
On Bayle's neck, one may notice two of Placidusax's heads still biting down, even after being severed
Bayle is part of a reoccurring draconic Boss archetype in From Software DLC content, which includes Black Dragon Kalameet , Sinh the Slumbering Dragon , and Darkeater Midir .

Bayle the Dread is an optional boss in Shadow of the Erdtree .
1 Overview 1.1 Enemy of the Dragonlord 1.2 Dragon Communion 1.3 Drake Warrior
1.1 Enemy of the Dragonlord
1.2 Dragon Communion
2 Boss Fight 2.1 Stats 2.2 Phase One 2.3 Phase Two
Bayle the Dread was a legendary black dragon who is said to be the ancestor of the drakes or lesser dragons. Long ago, Bayle betrayed the Ancient dragons and clashed with Dragonlord Placidusax . [ 1 ] Their vicious battle ended in stalemate and left both dragons with grievous injuries and loss of body parts, with Bayle losing his two winged arms, his pair of wings on his back and his left leg, with the dreaded dragon fleeing to the Jagged Peak soon afterwards. [ 2 ] [ 3 ]
As punishment for his betrayal, Placidusax created the art of Dragon Communion , sending his followers to shepherd humanity into hunting Bayle and his descendants. [ 4 ] As reward for felling the drakes, the dragon hunters consumed their hearts in order to gain their power.  Bayle was pursued by Florissax , a servant of Placidusax who sought to recruit mortals in the hunt for Bayle. [ 5 ] [ 6 ]
Dragonslayer Igon , a particularly obsessive drake warrior, was one such mortal. Igon faced Bayle, but was left broken in both body and spirit by their battle. [ 7 ] [ 8 ]
Igon was stumbled upon by a Tarnished champion , and begged them to summon his spirit to face Bayle once more. Together, the pair succeed in slaying Bayle. [ 9 ] [ 10 ]
If Bayle's heart eaten at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion , his willpower will one day consume the body and soul of the drake warrior. [ 11 ]
If the player has not previously attempted Bayle in their current playthrough, there will not be a fog wall separating the Site of Grace from his arena, Igon's summon sign will not immediately be available--even if the player has acquired his finger --and Bayle will not enter until the player goes deep enough into his arena. Upon noticing the player, he will crash down into its center and will always follow up with his pouncing-bite grab attack . On all attempts afterwards, Bayle instead starts in the center of the arena, with no guaranteed attack, and Igon's summon sign will immediately be available if unlocked. Bayle's attacks include bites, talon-bone slams, swipes, flame-lightning breath, and pounces.
Bayle is weakest against piercing damage, Dragon Communion Grease , and Igon's Harpoons .
Bayle is stuck on the ground. He commands a unique power that combines fire and lightning damage. He is a
Once a certain amount of Bayle's HP is depleted, he will transition into phase two, where he sprouts flame-lightning wings and immediately soars into the skies above the arena. He will launch fireballs as he circles the arena for a few moments, then swoop down and crash back into the arena. His talon-bone now crackles with flame-lightning, and his slams now summon orange lightning in the direction he is facing.
Two of Placidusax's lost heads can be found still clinging onto the upside of Bayle's neck.
The ancient dragons who sided with Bayle against Placidusax wielded the branching red lightning . [ 12 ]
Drake Warrior Igon may be summoned inside the area if the player has received his Furled Finger .
Igon and Florissax will reward the player after Bayle is defeated.
Bayle is the only boss in the game who displays the text 'Legend Felled' when defeated who does not drop a Remembrance . Rather, he drops the Heart of Bayle , which functions similarly, with some differences. The Heart of Bayle cannot be redeemed for an item by meeting with Finger Reader Enia . Rather, it must be redeemed at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion . The Heart cannot be consumed to receive Runes, nor duplicated.
The Heart of Bayle cannot be redeemed for an item by meeting with Finger Reader Enia . Rather, it must be redeemed at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion .
The Heart cannot be consumed to receive Runes, nor duplicated.
Bayle's name is a corruption of "bale," which refers to a destructive and evil force.
Bayle's name and moniker "The Dread" may also be a reference to the dragon Balerion , The Black Dread from author and the base game's co-writer George R. R. Martin's fantasy novel series, A Song of Ice and Fire .
Igon can be summoned for this fight.
Some of Placidussax's heads can still be seen biting his back.

area: Shadow Keep
chapter: 35
name: Metyr Mother of Fingers
Metyr, Mother of Fingers is a Legend Boss in Elden Ring . Metyr, Mother of Fingers is a large monster that is composed of several fingers with two arms in front, and has a stomach made of smaller fingers, and is found in the Finger Ruins of Miyr. This is an optional boss as players don't need to defeat it to advance in Shadows of the Erdtree.
See Metyr, Mother of Fingers Lore
This is an optional boss
Closest Site of Grace: Cathedral of Manus Metyr
Multiplayer is allowed for this boss
You can summon Spirit Ashes for this boss
Metyr, Mother of Fingers can be found in the Finger Ruins of Miyr after using the Hole-Laden Necklace to ring the bells in the Finger Ruins of Dheo and the Finger Ruins of Rhia . From the Cathedral of Manus Metyr Site of Grace, head into the cathedral and interact with the throne to find a ladder going down. Head down the ladder to reach Finger Ruins of Miyr and continue down the path to find a summoning pool and finger-shaped bell. Interact with the bell to reach Metyr, Mother of Fingers' arena. [ Map Link ]
Is vulnerable to a critical hit after being stance broken .
Damage: Standard , Slash , Strike , Magic , Holy
Weak point: Belly (+50%)
Drops 420,000, Remembrance of the Mother of Fingers
Note regarding the below absorptions: Due to the boss arena being filled with water, all sources of lightning damage receive a 10% increase, while all sources of fire damage receive a 10% damage reduction.
The negation numbers are the % of your damage that gets blocked. For example, if a negation is 60, 40% of that damage by that type will go through and 60% will be negated. Bigger number = less damage. A negation of 100 means no damage goes through, and a negation of -100 mean the enemy takes 2x damage from that source. 0 means damage goes pretty much as is.

The resistance numbers are the buildup amount to trigger it. For example, if a resistance is 100 you must deal 100 points of the given buildup to trigger it. Note that these go down over time, and increase each time the effect procs. The values after the "/"s indicate the increased resistances after each successive proc.
The Best Tips for Metyr, Mother of Fingers:
The boss does not immediately attack upon entering the arena, so use this opportunity to buff and summon.
Recommended Scadutree Blessing: Level 14 (See Scadutree Fragment locations )

Other items: Cerulean Hidden Tear , Opaline Bubbletear , Glovewort Crystal Tear ,
Metyr, Mother of Fingers can be encountered in the Finger Ruins of Miyr , you may find her by entering the Cathedral of Manus Metyr and interacting with the throne. Follow the path to reach a bell to interact with that brings you to Metyr's arena. This boss deals Standard Damage and Magic Damage , she moves around quite slowly but will sometimes leap around to gain some distance.
Metyr has a few attacks she does with her head, one of which is Kowtower's Resentment , where she raises her head until it starts to glow and then she smashes it onto the ground and after a moment, the ground will explode, this attack can be indicated when she raises her head briefly, the attack indicators show 7 lines in a cone shape from her head before they explode. You will be able to stand in between those lines or perform dodge rolls to get next to her to attack her there before she comes up. Another head attack of hers is her Gaze. While at a distance, her head will briefly glow and then shoot out a laser that covers a large range in the arena and deals heavy damage, this can be indicated when there are glowing rings around her head briefly before she shoots out her laser. You can avoid this attack by perfectly timing your dodge forward to get closer to her to attack her while you can.
Metyr's arm attacks are pretty straightforward. The first attack is where she swings her arms one at a time in front of her, this can be indicated when she spreads her arms out before she attacks. This attack can be avoided by dodging backward when she spreads her arms out. The next attack is her Single Arm smashes. Metyr will lean towards one side with her head before she smacks down her arm, this can be indicated when she leans her head towards one side, it would take her a brief moment of leaning before she attacks. You can avoid this attack by dodging to either side so that you can attack her from there or dodge backward to avoid the attack all together.
One of the Arm attack Combo is her Arm Ground Smashes. This is where Metyr will raise up both her arms and repeatedly hashing down onto the ground, the string attack will begin with her smashing the ground quickly with each arm, and then she will clasp her arms together, pause for a brief moment, and then smash the ground twice. To dodge this attack, you want to roll twice, just before the first arm hits the ground. This will dodge the first 2 quick arm smashes, then, pause here as you wait for her to clasp her arms together, and then roll backward, and then roll forward for the last 2 attacks. NOTE : There's a variation of this attack where she will do a 3rd arm smash at the end if you attack too fast!
A few attacks that she uses with her body are her Body Smash , Spawning Leap , Jumping Smash , and Finger Spawn . Her Body Smash is when she brings her arms close to her as she lifts up her body holding it up briefly then slamming it back down onto the ground, this can be indicated when she holds her arms close to her as she goes up. You can easily avoid this attack by dodging to either side and then attacking her from the sides. Her Body Leap is where she will bring her arms to open up her chest made up of smaller fingers and leap over you as she releases smaller fingers onto you which deals damage to you as well, this can be indicated when she puts her hands to her chest and quickly leaps over you. When she leaps you can quickly dodge towards the sides to avoid the small fingers. Her Leaping Attack is when she slightly crouches down before leaping up and slamming back down several times and dealing heavy damage, this attack can be indicated when you see that she slightly crouches or when her legs bend. You can avoid this attack by getting as far from her as you can. Lastly, her Finger Spawn is when she will open up her chest to release three Lesser Fingercreepers to overwhelm you, it takes her a brief moment to summon them so when you see the Fingercreepers appear, you may get as far from her as possible to deal with them first before dealing with her.
Lastly, Metyr's tail attacks, these attacks deal massive damage and can be fatal if you're not careful. One of her tail attacks is her Levitating Spin , this is where her tail orb starts to glow purple and will levitate her in the air, later using the force of her tail orb to knock you down. It takes her a while to fully levitate up with her whole body not touching the ground so it gives you time to prepare to either run away or to perfectly time your dodge roll to avoid getting knocked down. Another attack of hers is her Levitating Spin, she will levitate much higher this time and begin to spin in the air in her position then launch herself at you. Just like with her previous attack, it takes her a while to levitate up and she spins for a while as well before launching herself at you, so it is recommended to get as far as you can so the range of her launch does not hit you. Once you defeat Metyr , you will be rewarded with 420,000 Runes and her Remembrance.
Sorcery users can deal massive damage with Shard Spiral , especially if free-aiming the spell so it goes through its body. If you find the opening for it, Meteorite of Astel in melee range can kill the boss in mere seconds.
Incantation users can deal massive damage by casting Pest-Thread Spears in melee range. If you find the opening for it, Burn, O Flame can also deal massive damage. You may also use Ekzykes's Decay to inflict scarlet rot.
According to her remembrance, Metyr predates the arrival of the Elden Ring, as the Elden Stars description reads "it is said that long ago, the Greater Will sent a golden star bearing a beast into the Lands Between, which would later become the Elden Ring."
Remembrance of Metyr, the Mother of Fingers, hewn into the Scadutree.
The power of its namesake can be unlocked by the Finger Reader. Alternatively, it can be used to gain a great bounty of runes.
The mother of all Two Fingers and Fingercreepers was in turn a magnificently gleaming daughter of the Greater Will, and the first shooting star to fall upon the Lands Between.
Staff fashioned from the tail-fingers of Metyr, the Mother of Fingers, and the microcosm raised aloft over the crux they form. Catalyst for casting both sorceries and incantations.
The Mother received signs from the Greater Will from the beyond of the microcosm. Despite being broken and abandoned, she kept waiting for another message to come.
A finger sorcery of Count Ymir, High Priest. One of the secret hallowed rites.
Conjures an image of a microcosm at a foe's feet that pulses with a single wave before disappearing in a burst. Charging enhances potency.
The broken and discarded are fully willing to cling to fleeting simulacra, earning them some modicum of sympathy.
The head of Metyr, the finger-mother, wielded as a weapon without modification.
From within the center of the fingerprint that wrinkles the creature's foremost protrusion, a tiny wart-like eye gazes vacantly into the beyond.
A finger sorcery of Count Ymir, aspiring mother of fingers.
Surrounds the caster with a mesh of hefty fingers that wards all manner of projectiles, whether magical or physical in nature, away from the caster. Charging enhances potency.
The dear fingers look after their mother, or perhaps that is merely what the mother wishes to believe.
A cerulean-colored talisman patterned after an Erdtree seed. Greatly boosts HP restoration from the Flask of Cerulean Tears.
The Erdtree seed of this talisman was presumed to be an object of myth. This age-old artifact also depicts the Two Fingers, perhaps harkening back to the birth of the Erdtree.
Light-pink mushroom resembling a wizened finger. Material used for crafting items. Exceedingly rare to find.
Used to by those who wish to become fingers to induce hallucinations. They call these mushrooms the stillborn of the Two Fingers.
Bolus engraved with whorl-like lines. Craftable item.
Boosts mind, intelligence, faith, and arcane at the cost of gradual HP loss.
A secret medicine of those who attempt to turn their human bodies into fingers. Ingesting causes something to wriggle within.
Nailstone with shining fingerprints floating above. Craftable item.
Uses FP to fling a magic nail that crumbles during flight.
The lampreys that guard the finger ruins resemble fingers far more than they do humans, and so the finger-weavers imitated their sorcery.
Do you recall what I said? That Marika, and the fingers that guided her, were unsound from the start. Well, the truth lies deeper still. It is their mother who is damaged and unhinged. The fingers are but unripe children. Victims in their own right. We all need a mother, do we not? A new mother, a true mother, who will not give birth to further malady.
May you join the glimmering stars above.
[screenshots and artwork go here]
- Staff of the Great Beyond
Metyr, Mother of Fingers is an optional boss in Shadow of the Erdtree . She is encountered after ringing the bell at the Finger Ruins of Miyr , beneath the Cathedral of Manus Metyr .
1 Overview 1.1 Shooting star 1.2 High treason 1.3 A New Mother
3 Boss Fight 3.1 Phase One 3.2 Phase Two
4 Notes 4.1 Speculation
Metyr, brilliant daughter of the Greater Will , was the first of all stars to fall upon the Lands Between . [ 1 ] She possessed a microcosm which allowed her to receive messages from the Greater Will. [ 2 ] Metyr gave birth to the Two Fingers that guide the empyreans and the Tarnished . [ 1 ]
Metyr was damaged and is possibly the source of the Ringed Finger . [ 3 ] She would never again receive a signal from the Greater Will. [ 2 ] The wisdom received by the Two Fingers was severed, and Metyr began birthing the aberrant Fingercreepers . [ 1 ] Although Metyr was broken, her Two Fingers continued guiding the empyreans without a word from the Greater Will. [ 4 ] It is said they presided over the birth of Marika ’s Erdtree as she created it from the primordial matter of the Crucible . [ 5 ] [ 6 ] [ 7 ]
Count Ymir was a glintstone sorcerer who became a finger-weaver and uncovered the secrets of Metyr herself. [ 8 ] He found Metyr concealed within the Finger Ruins of Miyr, below the Cathedral of Manus Metyr, still waiting in vain for a signal from her lord. [ 2 ] Ymir uncovered that Metyr's true power, and the guidance of the Two Fingers, had been broken long ago. [ 4 ] [ 6 ] After Ymir's young child Yuri died, his grief drove him to find a way to bring him back. [ 9 ] He decided he would succeed Metyr as the next mother of Fingers. [ 6 ]
Ymir shares the wisdom of the cosmos and a Hole-Laden Necklace with the Tarnished champion . He directs them to sound the ominous hanging bells at the Finger Ruins of Dheo and Rhea . Once both bells are rung, Ymir reveals the location of the Ruins of Miyr. Ringing the third and final bell will leave one's fate guided by the stars—and face-to-face with Metyr. If the Mother of Fingers is defeated, Ymir's hope is rekindled but the prospect of achieving his dream drives him mad. [ 10 ] He achieves a partial transformation and fails to birth Yuri anew, instead creating more Fingercreepers. [ 11 ]
Metyr is a monstrous being made from fingers. Her head resembles an elongated thumb, and her face a pronounced thumbprint. At its center is a small, wart-like eye from which she blasts pink energy beams. [ 12 ] Ridge-like wrinkles, forming fingerprint-like patterns, cover Metyr's head and neck. She moves about on four sets of four-fingered hands, 16 coordinated finger legs in total. Metyr has two long-reaching arms that terminate in four-fingered hands tipped with sharp nails painted red. She may use these to pry open her abdominal cavity when birthing new Fingercreepers. Her tail forks into two tail fingers which wrap each other like a spiral. Between them rests an orb containing a microcosm. [ 2 ] [ 13 ]
Metyr's body festers with unhealed wounds. Upon her right chest is a deep laceration, and her belly has been split wide open, revealing gore and the stillborn remains of malformed fingers. Several more protrude from large lesions on her neck and tail fingers, and her microcosm appears clouded like an eye with cataracts. The iris of her bloodshot eye is quartered into an X. [ 13 ]
Metyr is an optional boss fight that can be fought by the end of Count Ymir 's questline; after blowing the two finger bells at the other finger ruins, and returning to the cathedral and examining Ymir's throne, she is found in the Finger Ruins of Miyr , just beneath the Cathedral of Manus Metyr .
Metyr crawls around and can fire a laser beam from her eye. Try fighting her head-on. She punishes flanking during this phase.
Metyr summons her damaged microcosm and uses it to channel gravity magic. Be wary that she can fly during this stage, which can be troubling for some melee players.
Metyr's hands each look like a pair of Two Fingers put together.
The finger protrusions in Metyr's wounds resemble Festering Bloody Fingers . There are several different models, but they typically only have one or two fingers.
Metyr is the only boss that does not fade away upon defeat, but instead disappears into the microcosm once held by her tail-fingers. Metyr is one of the few bosses to survive having their health depleted by the player, with the first being Rennala . However, it should also be noted that the Gazing Finger weapon is referred to as her head.
Metyr is one of the few bosses to survive having their health depleted by the player, with the first being Rennala .
However, it should also be noted that the Gazing Finger weapon is referred to as her head.
The soundtrack for the second phase of Metyr's fight is reminiscent of the first-ever recorded sound of a Black Hole . [ 14 ] [ 13 ]
Metyr is referred to as "Finger Nursery" within the game files. [ 15 ]
It is implied that the Nokron Nox used the Fingerslayer Blade against Metyr. [ 2 ] [ 16 ] [ 17 ] [ 18 ] [ 19 ]

area: Enir-Ilim
chapter: 34
name: Enir-Ilim
Enir-Ilim is a Legacy Dungeon in Elden Ring . Enir-Ilim is a dungeon exclusive to the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC. You can gain access to an area containing a sealing tree by defeating Romina, Saint of the Bud in the Church of the Bud. You can then burn the sealing tree by using Messmer's Kindling , which you can obtain by defeating Messmer the Impaler . Enir-Ilim will reveal its true form after burning the sealing tree, teleporting you inside of it.
Previous: Shadow Keep
Recommended Levels: 150 - Above
Bosses: Promised Consort Radahn / Radahn Consort of Miquella
Site of Grace: Enir-Ilim: Outer Wall [ Map Link ] First Rise [ Map Link ] Spiral Rise [ Map Link ] Cleansing Chamber Anteroom [ Map Link ]

Other Items in Belurat Tower Settlement Secret Path
x1 Scadutree Fragment
Divine Beast Warrior
In order to gain access to Enir-Ilim, you must first defeat Romina, Saint of the Bud who is located in the Church of the Bud [ See Elden Ring Map ]. After defeating Romina, you will unlock the Church of the Bud Site of Grace in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh, which is a few steps away from the entrance to Enir-Ilim. Head to the archway south of the Church of Bud Site of Grace, and you will find a sealing tree. When you interact with the sealing tree, it states that it cannot be burned without Messmer's kindling. This means that you have to defeat Messmer the Impaler , located in the Specimen Storehouse [ See Elden Ring Map ], who will then drop Messmer's Kindling along with the Remembrance of the Impaler .
Once you have Messmer's Kindling , you can head to the sealing tree to burn it, triggering a cutscene wherein Enir-Ilim's true form is revealed. After the cutscene, you will then be teleported to Enir-Ilim.
Once you arrive in Enir-Ilim, look behind you to find a body containing x1 Somber Smithing Stone [9] . You will then discover the Enir-Ilim: Outer Wall Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ], which is right next to the spot where you were teleported.
Follow the path going east, up the stairs, in which you will see the Erdtree on the northeastern side. Keep following the path, then head up the stairs. Once you head up the stairs, a hostile Divine Bird Warrior will be approaching you. This enemy can fly, so watch out for its attacks. The staircase on the right is destroyed, so it leads to a dead-end. After dealing with the Divine Bird Warrior, head up the stairs on your left which leads to the First Rise Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ].
If you go to the stairs heading up on the south side of First Rise, you will find a body that you can loot for x1 Somber Smithing Stone [5] . The staircase is destroyed, so it leads to a dead-end.
Return to the bottom of the stairs, then head to the hallway heading east-southeast of the First Rise Site of Grace. You can loot a body containing x1 Messmerfire Grease just right before the archway. Enter the room to find a lift, which you will ride going down. Go down the stairs, which then leads to Belurat, Tower Settlement [ See Elden Ring Map ].
Head southwest of the First Rise Site of Grace, and you will see an archway leading to a staircase going up. Go up the stairs, and you will end up in a courtyard. There will be a body on your right that you can loot for x1 Broken Rune . There is a room on the western corner of the courtyard, which you will see two Tree Shamans, one attacking you and the other praying to a tree. In the room, you will find a doorway to a staircase on the northwestern corner of the room. Follow it, and you will see another Tree Shaman. After dealing with the shaman, you can pick up x1 Rune Arc on the ground.
On the eastern side, you will find the continuation of the courtyard. A Greater Tree Shaman will be roaming around it, along with Tree Shamans praying in different corners of the courtyard. On the corner of the courtyard where shamans are praying, you can pick up x4 Budding Horn . In the middle of the courtyard, you can pick up a Revered Spirit Ash . On the northeast side of the courtyard where you killed the Greater Tree Shaman, you can pick up x2 Lightningproof Pickled Liver from the body.
Head down the stairs beside the body, leading you to the base of a tree with two hostile Man-Flies . Once you have killed the two enemies, you can pick up x8 Thin Beast Bones on the dead body that the two enemies were surrounding. Northeast of the tree roots is a body that you can loot for x3 Whiteflesh Mushroom . Two Man-Flies will land on you, which you can easily deal with.
Traverse through the path on the west side of the giant tree roots. When you walk down the path, a Man-Fly will be hiding behind a root, waiting to attack you. You can find x4 Redflesh Mushroom on top of a root nearby. Straight ahead will be three Man-Flies inside a room, praying to a tree. After clearing the enemies, you can pick up x2 Revered Spirit Ash lying on the remains of a humanoid tree. Go back up the courtyard to head deeper into Enir-Ilim.
Once you have found yourself back in the courtyard, head up the stairs on the southeast. You will encounter a Tree Shaman on top of the stairs. After killing the Tree Shaman, keep following the path until you encounter a Divine Beast Warrior .
After dealing with the Divine Beast Warrior , you will see a platform on the northern side with a tree shaman and an item. You can drop down to the tree branches below if you want to go there. Immediately after dropping down, you can loot x3 Furlcalling Finger Remedy behind you. Cross the branches going north to get to the platform. Kill the tree shaman, then you can pick up the incantation Spira on the altar. You can then return to where you came from, then continue on from there.
Keep following the path and stairs leading up. You can pick up x1 Dragonbolt Grease on the ground, but there will be a magic attack which seems to be coming from above. Sprint up the stairs, then kill Tree Mage responsible for the flurry of magic attacks.
After dealing with the Tree Mage, you can pick up a Drawstring Golden Grease on the ledge southwest of the platform. You'll notice another Tree Mage on the rooftop of the structure, which you can deal with by using ranged attacks or incantations. A Tree Shaman will also be patrolling the area at the top of the stairs. If you don't clear the Tree Mage on the rooftop, it will keep casting spells on you as you're going up the stairs. You can sprint forward if you have no way of dealing with the Tree Mage on the rooftop. After clearing the two enemies, head up the stairs. You will find bodies that you can loot for x10 Fire Arrow and x10 Golden Bolt .
Open the door at the top of the stairs to discover Spiral Rise Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ] and pick up the Scadutree Fragment nearby.
The area just before the Spiral Rise Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ] has a lot of loot that you shouldn't miss. From the Site of Grace, backtrack your way down the stairs, then turn left to find a set of steps going down. As you go through the archway, there will be a room on your right containing x1 Inquisitor Ashes . Go to the other archway just before the room, and you will see a staircase that you can drop down to that has an item.
Drop down to the stairs, and you can pick up x2 Fireproof Pickled Liver from the body. Head up the stairs, then look below the broken railings to find a ledge that you can drop down to. Look down on the southeastern section of the ledge, and you will find another area that you can drop down to.
Drop down to it, then follow the ledge going south. You'll come across a broken section going to an item. Jump to get to the other side, and you can pick up x1 Holyproof Pickled Liver . After looting it, drop down from the broken section of the ledge, then pick up the nearby item, which is x2 Smithing Stone [8] .
Follow the ledge going south. Be careful as there are pillars are blocking off some parts of ledge, making it tough to navigate. Keep hugging the wall just to be safe. When you go around the corner, you will find another area that you can jump down to. Jump down the area, then traverse the ledge going southwest.
Keep following it, and you will acquire x10 Lordsworn's Bolt from a dead body at the end of it. After looting the body, follow the ledge to the other direction going down, and you'll be at a broken section of the ledge. Drop down it, and you will land near a hostile Shadow Undead. Kill it, then pick up x1 Somber Smithing Stone [5] nearby. Go down the stairs, and you'll find x1 Fireproof Pickled Liver from a dead body.
To progress further, sprint jump down the window south of the dead body containing x1 Fireproof Pickled Liver .
Once you have successfully jumped on the window, head inside the structure. The section you are in will be inside Belurat, Tower Settlement. Head out of the room, and you will end up in a courtyard. Kill the Spider Scorpion and pick up the a Scadutree Fragment at the base of the covered statue. There are two other rooms that you can enter from the courtyard. The room on the east has a lift that you can take to return to Enir-Ilim, while the room on the south has a lift going down Belurat, Tower Settlement. Ride down the lift in the southern room.
After taking the lift, head outside by going through the archway on the southeastern side of the room. Follow the path by going down a staircase that leads you to a room containing Inquisitor enemies. After clearing the room, follow the path going southwest. Head down the stairs, and you'll encounter a Divine Beast Warrior patrolling the area. You can either fight it head-on, or use stealth as the Divine Beast Warrior is a formidable combatant and is patrolling in a way that you can sneak around it.
After dealing with the Divine Beast Warrior with whichever method you choose, head inside the structure then go down the stairs on the western side. Go down the lift, and you will arrive in an area with a bridge crossing to a door.
Cross the bridge, then open the door. You'll find a dead body by the altar, which you can loot for the Euporia twinblade.
After obtaining the Euporia twinblade, head back to the Spiral Rise Site of Grace to continue going up Enir-Ilim.
Head north of the room to climb up the stairs. There will be an opening on the left side of the stairs which lets you attack the Tree Mage earlier on the rooftop if you didn't get the chance to kill it. If you go back to the staircase, it will lead to a dead-end, so your only choice would be to traverse through the rooftop. Keep following the rooftop path by turning right as you exit through the opening. You'll encounter hostile large birds around the rooftop area, which you can easily dispose of. One of the birds will be hiding behind a corner, so be cautious of it. You can find x1 Smithing Stone [8] atop the roof near the part where the bird was hiding.
Keep going west until you get into an opening of a walkway. There will be another large bird guarding a room and a tree shaman patrolling the stairs. You can sneak up on the bird to easily dispose of it. Enter the room that the large bird was blocking to pick up x1 Revered Spirit Ash . Continue heading up, and if you didn't deal with the Tree Shaman earlier, it will be in this room along with another Tree Shaman. Deal with the shamans, then continue through the archway on the western side of the room and keep climbing the stairs.
As you get to the top of the stairs, you will find a room that contains x1 Scadutree Fragment lying on an altar. Keep exploring the rooftops, in which you will encounter a Divine Beast Warrior . It will use frost magic along with the greatsword they are wielding, so be cautious of those attacks. On the northeast side of the rooftop, you can loot a body to pickup x2 Sunwarmth Stone .
If you want to find more loot, go back to where you picked up the Revered Spirit Ash . In the middle of the stairs going up, you can drop down to a set of stairs. You can find x1 Silver Horn Tender on the edge of the staircase going down. Head up the staircase, and you will end up in a room containing Horned Warrior Ashes , x2 Neutralizing Boluses , and Stanching Boluses . Backtrack your way up by climbing a ladder inside the room southwest. Head out of the ladder room, and you will find a body by the ledge containing a Golden Horn Tender . Climb up another ladder to get on the top floor of the structure, then jump off of it back into the rooftop where you fought a Divine Beast Warrior using ice spells.
Keep going by jumping up on the rooftop, and you'll find your way back to the structure of the First Rise Site of Grace, bypassing the destroyed part of the staircase. If you head down the staircase, you will find a Somber Smithing Stone [6] lying on the ground near the part where the staircase has been destroyed. If you look down the destroyed part of the stairs, you will see a platform containing an item. You can go there by jumping off of the stairs. You will get to pick up x1 Gravebird Helm if you choose to go to the platform.
After looting the platform, you can jump on a staircase on the east side of the platform. Head down the stairs, and you will find an area with a lever that when pulled down, it will bring a lift to where you currently are. There will also be a doorway leading down to an empty room with a Curseblade hiding out of sight by the other doorway. The Curseblade will be quite nimble, so be patient while you're waiting for an opening to attack it. Once you have slain the Tree Dancer, there will be a broken staircase when you go through the archway leading outside. At the end of the staircase, you can pick up x1 Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone from a dead body.
Make your way back to where you pulled the lever then ride the lift going up. You will end up in a room with two Tree Shamans that you can easily dispose of. After clearing the room, head to the archway on the south of the room leading to a stairway going up. You will encounter a Divine Beast Warrior patrolling nearby the area, which is different from the one you encountered earlier. It uses wind attacks to complement their greatsword, and it can also swing its greatsword successively. Wait for your turn to attack it. You can even stagger it if you have enough Poise damage with your build. You will obtain the Horned Warrior's Greatsword after defeating the Divine Beast Warrior.
After you have slain the Divine Beast Warrior, enter the structure to discover the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ]. Southeast of the Site of Grace, you can pick up a Scadutree Fragment .
Go through the archway on the east side of the Site of Grace, going up the stairs. Keep following the path, and you will encounter Needle Knight Leda who will invite you to challenge her and her allies. You can summon Pureblood Knight Ansbach and Thiollier to help you in this fight by interacting with the summon signs beside Leda's summon sign. During the fight, Redmane Freyja will be summoned first, followed by Hornent, then Moore , then Dryleaf Dane . You will then fight Needle Knight Leda after all the NPC invaders have been summoned.
Once you have defeated Needle Knight Leda, you will receive Leda's Sword . You can pick up the equipment of her and her allies right after returning to your world. The bodies of her allies will be lying around the place, which you can then loot. You can pick up Moore's equipment - Moore's Bell Bearing , Verdigris Greatshield , and the Verdigris Set . From Freyja, you can receive Freyja's Greatsword and Freyja's Set . Hornsent's body can be looted for Falx , Hornsent's Set , and the Soiled Loincloth . Lastly, you can receive the Dane's Set and Dane's Footwork from Dryleaf.
Head east through the archway, and you will find Needle Knight Leda 's body containing Leda's Armor . After you have looted the place, head north of Needle Knight Leda's body and go up the stairs, leading you to a lift. Ride the lift going up. Once you arrive at the top of the place, you will see Ansbach right beside the lift. Go through the archway and up the stairs directly in front of you, and you will discover the Divine Gate Front Staircase [ See Elden Ring Map ].
As you reach the top of the stairs, you will find a boss fog that you can pass through. If you go through it, a cutscene will trigger, showing a familiar character that you have once fought. It is then revealed that the boss that you are going to face is Promised Consort Radahn . Radahn will be using the Starscourge Greatsword once again, even using an attack similar to the Starcaller Cry . He will also be using gravity orbs from afar, guaranteeing that you won't feel safe even if you are very far from him. He can also pull you or push you away from him using gravity. For a more-detailed guide on how to defeat Promised Consort Radahn , you can go to this page.
After you have defeated Radahn, you will receive the Remembrance of a God and a Lord . The Gate of Divinity Site of Grace [See Elden Ring Map] will also be unlocked. If you look around the area, southeast of the Site of Grace, you can pick up the equipment of those who assisted you in the fight. You can pick up Thiollier's Set along with his weapon, Thiollier's Hidden Needle , from his body. You can also pick up Ansbach's Set , his weapon Obsidian Lamina , and the incantation Furious Blade of Ansbach .
In the middle of the arena, you can obtain the emote Let Us Go Together. You can play a memory, triggering a cutscene. After the cutscene, you will obtain the Circlet of Light .
Enir-Ilim: Entrance Enir-Ilim: Cliffs
Enir-Ilim: West Side Enir-Ilim: Gate of Divinity
Enir-Ilim Walkthorugh Guide
Promised Consort Radahn Boss Guide
To gain access in Enir-Ilim, you need to defeat Messmer the Impaler to obtain Messmer's Kindling , and Romina Saint of the Bud to gain access to the area. Once you have defeated both of them, head to the sealing tree behind the Church of Bud then burn the sealing tree. You will be teleported inside of Enir-Ilim after a cutscene. After the cutscene, you can loot a body nearby for x1 Somber Smithing Stone [9] . Activate the Outer Wall Site of Grace beside you, then follow the path going through the archway. You will encounter a Gravebird Knight going down the stairs. Defeat the enemy, then go up the stairs to activate the First Rise Site of Grace.
Go up the stairs near the Site of Grace to obtain a Somber Smithing Stone [5] . Afterwards, you can head southwest then pass through the archway to get to the courtyard filled with enemies. You can loot a dead boy for x1 Broken Rune near the trees in the courtyard. Enter the room, then fight the two Cursed Erdtree Priests inside. Head up towards the doorway with a stairs leading up, then defeat another priest. You can pick up a Rune Arc on a small shrine by the priest you just defeated.
You will then face a Cursed Erdtree Shaman roaming in the bigger courtyard, along with priests scattered around the corners of the courtyard. Defeat all the hostiles in the area, then head up the stairs. You will encounter another priest which dies in two or three hits. Keep following the path, and you'll encounter a Horned Warrior approaching the area. Defeat the Horned Warrior, then look at the platform across the one you are on. Go there by traversing through the tree branches. You can obtain x3 Furlcalling Finger Remedy right where you dropped. As you get on the platform, you'll encounter a Cursed Erdtree Priest there. After dealing with the priest, you can then pick up the Spira incantation. You can head back dropping down, then going through the path you went from originally.
Once you're back from where you dropped to the branches, follow the path going up. You can pick up a Dragonbolt Grease by the stairs, but there will be a flurry of magic attacks. The one casting the spell will be on top of the stairs, so you can sprint towards the enemy, a Cursed Erdtree Mage, to stop the attack. After that, you can deal with the mage on the rooftop near the next staircase going up by using ranged attacks. If you have no ranged attack options, you can just sprint to the top of the stairs, then defeat the priest on top of it. Along the way, you can loot dead bodies for x10 Fire Arrow and x10 Golden Bolt . Open the door to gain access to the Spiral Rise Site of Grace.
Note : Right before going up the stairs to the Site of Grace, you can find a staircase going down which will eventually lead you to a room containing the twinblade Euporia .
After activating the Spiral Rise Site of Grace, head up the stairs until you find an opening to the outside. Attack the mage, then turn right to go to the rooftop. You will encounter numerous gravebirds as you explore the rooftops. You can acquire a Smithing Stone [8] from one of the rooftops on the right side. Keep going west, and you'll find an area with broken railings that you can jump to. You will encounter a gravebird guarding a room with a chest containing x1 Revered Spirit Ash . Head up the stairs, and you'll come across Cursed Erdtree Priests that you can easily dispose of. Go through the archway, then keep climbing the stairs. You can find a room with x1 Scadutree Fragment on an altar.
You will arrive in a rooftop section with a Horned Warrior that uses frost spells. After defeating the Horned Warrior, climb up the rooftop until you find an opening to the structure containing the First Rise Site of Grace. Keep heading up the stairs until you get to the top of the structure. You'll end up in an area with a lift, and Cursed Erdtree Priests on the corners of the room. After clearing the area, you can head outside through the archway. In this area, you will encounter a Horned Warrior using wind spells. After dealing with the Horned Warrior, go up the stairs to open the door leading to the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom, then activate the area's Site of Grace. You can pick up a Scadutree Fragment southeast of the Site of Grace.
Follow the path, then head up the stairs. You will encounter Needle Knight Leda, challenging you to a fight. In this fight, you can summon Pureblood Knight Ansbach and Thiollier to assist you in defeating Needle Knight Leda and her allies. After defeating Needle Knight Leda, you will receive Leda's Sword . You can then loot the bodies of her allies scattered around the arena to obtain their equipment, such as Moore's Bell Bearing , Freyja's Greatsword and Dane's Footwork , among other things. Proceed to the archway, then follow the hall until you get to a lift. Use the lift to go up, and you'll arrive in a room with Ansbach near the top of Enir-Ilim. Go outside then head up the stairs, and you can activate the Divine Gate Front Staircase Site of Grace.
As you go through the boss fog, you will fight Promised Consort Radahn . For a complete guide on how to defeat Promised Consort Radahn , you may visit this page. After defeating Radahn, you will receive the Remembrance of a God and a Lord . You can also pick up the equipment of those that helped you in the fight. You can get Thiollier's Set and Thiollier's Hidden Needle from Thiollier's remains, while you will get Obsidian Lamina , Ansbach's Set , and the Furious Blade of Ansbach incantation from Ansbach's Remains. In the middle of the arena, you can interact with a memory to play a cutscene. After the cutscene, you will obtain the Circlet of Light .
O tower of ours, hidden in shadow. O lofty spiral piercing the heavens. Lead him safely unto greater Godhood. And in divinity grant our salvation.
Enir-Ilim is a sub-region and legacy dungeon in the Land of the Tower .
4 Encounters 4.1 Bosses 4.2 Enemies 4.3 NPCs
5 Items 5.1 Notable Loot 5.2 Armaments 5.3 Armor 5.4 Spells 5.5 Spirit Ashes 5.6 Ammunition 5.7 Tools 5.8 Consumables 5.9 Crafting materials 5.10 Enemy drops
5.9 Crafting materials
The fabled spiral tower of the Hornsent and the location of the Gate of Divinity, Enir-Ilim pierced the skies with such a majesty that it even inspired a secret faith in the people sent there to destroy it. [ 1 ] When the Crusade of Messmer the Impaler took place, the tower was veiled in shadow. [ 2 ] As such, Enir-Ilim may only be accessed after using Messmer's Kindling to burn the Sealing Tree located near the Church of the Bud in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh .
The tower comprises an intricate network of spiral staircases and rises; it is the final area of the Shadow of the Erdtree main quest, armed to the teeth with Horned Warriors and Inquisitors.
After dispelling the shroud cast over Enir-Ilim and journeying downward through the tower, the main door to it can be opened from just outside the Theatre of the Divine Beast in Belurat, Tower Settlement . Enir-Ilim is the "tower" referred to both in Belurat's name and in that of the Tower Festival that took place there.

area: Shadow Keep
chapter: 35
name: Shadow Keep
Shadow Keep is a Legacy Dungeon in Elden Ring . The Shadow Keep is a legacy dungeon exclusive to the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, it accommodates the main army of Messmer the Impaler. The Shadow Keep is located at the center or the heart of the Scadu Altus region and has multiple entrances and exits, considering it to be a non-linear dungeon where adventurers will encounter many enemies such as Messmer Soldiers, Fire Knights, Giant Crabs, Giant Bats, Shadow Militia, Black Knights, and devastating creatures. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
The Shadow Keep location is divided into three sections, the Main Plaza , the Church District , and the Specimen Storehouse . Please click the links to jump to a specific section of the walkthrough.
Previous: Castle Ensis
Recommended Levels: 150 - Above
Bosses: Golden Hippopotamus , Messmer the Impaler
