Lesser Ulcerated Tree Spirit
The main entrance of the Church District of the Shadow Keep is located on the east side of the Shadow Keep. The direct path to finding it is to travel from the Church District High Road Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]. You'll know you'll arrive when you enter a large doorway and pass through a long hallway. At the end of the hallway is where you'll formally arrive at the church district and discover the Church District Entrance Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ].
The Church District is a flooded area, and the goal is to traverse the area by using high ground and finding a mechanism that drains the water. Please take note that falling into the water will result in death. Ignore the path on the left since you won't be able to use the lift until you drain the flood. Instead, jump across (using a running jump) to land on the roof of a building, then check the right side to find a small Crab and a body next to it that can be looted to acquire x2 Crab Eggs . Continue walking around the roof to find another body that's being eaten by a Crab . Loot the body to obtain x4 Thin Beast Bones . Continue south and you'll find another body being eaten by a Crab , check the body to find x2 Strip of White Flesh .
Next, jump on the floating log to reach a large broken-up circular platform. The path here is fairly straightforward, try to reach the opposite end by using the floating logs and wooden platforms, and make sure to loot the body on the circular platform to find x1 Great Grave Glovewort . Retrace your steps to the rooftop of the building where you looted bodies and killed the small Crabs , and this time, head west where you can jump across to progress (see the second image above for reference).
Once you're across, walk around the sides to reach the platform and loot the body that is guarded by Ignited Putrid Corpse enemies to obtain x1 Fireproof Pickled Liver . Jump back onto the sides of this building so you can walk to the top and proceed west where you'll have to do a running jump to safely land on top of another roof of a building. On the roof of the building, you should encounter a Fire Knight enemy walking around. Kill it and then loot the body that's near the spot where the Fire Knight enemy was walking to obtain x3 Ember of Messmer . Keep going straight until you reach a wall, then turn around to find a body sitting against one of the roof's structures. Loot it to acquire x1 Slumbering Egg [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ].
Next, face the east side of the compass and you should see a narrow ramp structure of one of the churches. Do a running jump to land on it safely, then walk to the top where you'll find two large openings or holes on the big roof. Slowly drop down onto the narrow structure that's on the right side, then you can walk down so you can safely run onto the other structure that is on the left so you can loot the body that holds x3 Stormhawk Feathers [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]. Go back and then drop down onto the opening of a ceiling (see the third image above for reference).
Below, you should immediately see yet another corpse next to a small Crab . Loot the body to find x1 Lightningproof Pickled Liver . Go around, northeast, and you'll locate a locked door, this is the Prayer Room that requires the Prayer Room Key , a key that drops upon defeating the NPC Invader Fire Knight Queelign , encountered for the second time at the Church of the Crusade [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]. If you have the key, you can unlock the door and find Fire Knight Queelign (NPC) in the flesh. Now, if you have an item called the Iris of Occultation , you can give it to the NPC, and after Queelign's dialogue, the NPC will drop a weapon you can loot. This weapon is Queelign's Greatsword . NOTE : If you give the Iris of Grace to Queelign, you'll receive the Spirit Ash " Fire Knight Queelign ". The Iris of Occultation can be acquired in many ways, one way is by obtaining it as an item drop from defeating a Lesser Ulcerated Tree Spirit located in this area after you drain the flood.
Exit the prayer room and proceed to the northwest doorway, but before proceeding further, be sure to enter the armory room on the left to find a body sitting against the wall. Loot the body to find x1 Rune of an Unsung Hero . Enter the hallway and kill the first Fire Knight enemy that you see, on the left side of the hallway, loot the body resting against the wall to find x2 Furlcalling Finger Remedy , and continue straight down the hallway where you'll encounter another Fire Knight . The room ahead is not accessible at the moment and the doorway to the right where you'll notice Marika's statue that has a glowing item in its hand is unreachable, so exit through the first doorway on the left side of the hallway.
NOTE : In the same hallway, there are two doorways on the left, the far most or second doorway will lead you to a lift that takes you to the Specimen Storehouse section of the Shadow Keep. You're free to roam here, but we highly recommend returning to the Specimen Storehouse after clearing the Main Plaza and Church District of the Shadow Keep. What you can do is enter the Specimen Storehouse, and activate the nearby Site of Grace (Storehouse, Back Section) so you can just fast travel here after exploring the Church District.
Cross the pathway where you'll encounter two Giant Bat enemies, then walk up to the short steps where you'll encounter another Giant Bat ahead. Jump over the gap, and kill the creature. Now before going straight, walk up a bit and you'll find a corner on the right. It's a dead end but there's a body that you can loot that's being protected by another Giant Bat hanging from the ceiling. Kill the enemy and loot the body to acquire x3 Hefty Beast Bone [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]. Next, climb the ladder next to a doorway to find the mechanism that drains the water.
You can also return to the hallway to get the item sitting on the hand of Marika's statue. You can get the item by jumping on the hand, it may require a few tries as the platform that you need to stand on is quite narrow. Loot the item to acquire x1 Scadutree Fragment , then prepare to fight a group of Ignited Putrid Corpse as you jump down. Behind the same statue, you can find the Fire Knight's Seal weapon [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
For some clarity, we recommend that you start by fast-traveling to the Church District Entrance checkpoint [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ] since you can now use the lift near the Site of Grace. From the checkpoint, go left, use the lever to call the lift, then step on the center to ride it down. You'll now be able to explore the open area of the Sunken Chapel. At the center, you will see a large tree, only approach the tree if you're prepared to fight because a Lesser Ulcerated Tree Spirit will emerge beneath the ground. Killing it will drop the Mantle of Thorns , and resting against the tree is a corpse holding x1 Somber Smithing Stone [5] . If you missed the Great Grave Glovewort earlier, it will have dropped to this area when the water drained.
The second Lesser Ulcerated Tree Spirit is located around the east area of the sunken chapel (see the images above for reference). Defeating it will drop x1 Iris of Occultation which (if you don't have it yet) you can give to Fire Knight Queelign ( see here ) in the Prayer Room to obtain Queelign's Greatsword . Also in the east area, you can find open buildings. One building houses two bodies that you can loot to find x4 Smithing Stone [4] and x6 Kukri [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ].
There is also building another where you'll find a pack of Crabs and Giant Crab enemies, one roaming around, and the other next to another body that you can loot to find x1 Shadow Realm Rune [5] . [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]. If you continue to go around this area, you'll reach the west side where you'll find the Sunken Chapel Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
Head north towards the entrance of the main church then immediately turn left to find a corpse by a corner. Loot the body to find x3 Shadow Sunflower . Clear out the enemies then approach the small room that has Marika's statue (the same statue where you got the Scadutree Fragment and the Fire Knight's Seal weapon. Make a right and you'll enter a large room. Straight ahead, you'll see some stairs on both ends, walk up to the upper platform and investigate the railings to discover a body hanging on top of it.
Loot the body to obtain the Battlefield Priest's Cookbook [1] , [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]. Walk back down the stairs and check under the upper platform to find a door. Open that door and you'll find another lift that takes you underground. Ride it to find and activate the Tree-Worship Passage Site of Grace [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ].
After activating the Tree-Worship Passage Site of Grace, follow the path going down, then cross the bridge. Enter the structure through the archway, and you'll find the Iris of Grace (you can give this to Fire Knight Queelign , see here ) if you still haven't obtained it. Go down the stairs on either side, and you can activate the Tree-Worship Sanctum Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ]. South of the Site of Grace, you can find x1 Marika's Rune on a corpse sitting against the wall.
North of the Tree-Worship Sanctum Site of Grace, you can go through the boss fog to fight the Scadutree Avatar at the Scadutree Base . The Scadutree Avatar uses its tree trunk-like arms to attack. It has different attacks, such as uprooting thorns over an area. There is a dedicated page here containing information on how to defeat the Scadutree Avatar .
After defeating the Scadutree Avatar , you will receive the Remembrance of the Shadow Sunflower along with Miquella's Great Rune . From here, (if you followed the tip earlier by activating the Storehouse, Back Section checkpoint in the Specimen Storehouse) you can fast travel to the Storehouse, Back Section Site of Grace to start exploring the Back Section of the Specimen Storehouse.
Upon entering, look to the right immediately to see a noble corpse against the wall. Loot it to obtain x1 Somber Smithing Stone [9] . Proceed to the left side, through the doorway, where you'll find the Storehouse, Back Section checkpoint. Continue moving straight until you walk past a ladder. Do not climb the ladder first, instead, keep going straight to find some items. First, look to your left to see, another noble corpse sitting against a desk, loot it to obtain x3 Glintslab Firefly . Then at the end, you'll see a Fire Knight and three Shadow Undead standing next to another corpse, kill the enemies then loot the body to acquire x1 Carian Thrusting Shield .
If you look over the railings from the Back Section Site of Grace, you can safely jump onto the bookcases and drop to the First Floor Site of Grace. This lets you explore the rest of Shadow Keep without the need to fight the Golden Hippopotamus that guards the Main Plaza.
Go back and climb the ladder to the top where you'll encounter another Fire Knight and two Shadow Undead reading a book. You can sneak behind the enemies to backstab them. Continue to the right side and walk carefully up the stairs to avoid aggravating the Fire Knight, roaming the floor above. At the top of the stairs, turn to the corner on the right to find a corpse to loot x3 Fire Coil . Kill the enemies on this floor, then continue moving straight. You'll discover another noble corpse sitting against a small table, loot it to obtain x4 Rada Fruit , and go straight towards the balcony to find another noble corpse that holds the Fire Serpent incantation. Go back, and look behind the bookcase to find an opening on the wall.
Through the opening, turn right and you'll find another noble corpse sitting by the edge of the narrow scaffolding. Loot the body to acquire x6 Shadow Sunflower . Continue going up, and by the ramp, you should find another noble corpse that you can loot to obtain x1 Blessed Bone Shard . Continue going up and you'll arrive at the loft section of the storehouse. Activate the Storehouse, Loft Site of Grace before you proceed to explore.
Once you have activated the Storehouse, Loft Site of Grace, proceed to the lift north of the Site of Grace to go up. You will end up on beams above the Specimen Storehouse. This level will be filled with Giant Bats roaming the area. The only path you can follow will be the one going west, so keep following it. There will be a lot of ways you can walk on the beams, but the only way you can progress through this area is through the ladder. Pick up x7 Thin Beast Bones from a body at the edge of a beam, then go up the ladder that you walk past through just before picking up the loot.
After climbing the ladder, you will arrive in a new level. If you go to the southeast section of the level, you can find x4 Smithing Stone [7] from a dead body. Following the path from the south side will lead you to a pillar blocking your way. If you look down, you can find a platform that you can jump off of, and it will contain the Wrath from Afar incantation. Next, jump on the beam to the north, then climb up the nearby ladder to the west of where you landed. You will end up on the beam that a pillar was blocking earlier. You can aggro the bats here, so be careful when attacking as you might fall off of the beams.
You can find a lift in the middle of the beam going west with an item above it, along with a Fire Knight on a nearby beam which will be hostile if you approach the lift.
If you use the lift to go down, you will end up in an area with a Fire Knight facing the other way around. You can easily dispose of the Fire Knight by backstabbing it and staggering it continuously. After dealing with the Fire Knight, you can pick up x1 Rune of an Unsung Hero on the southwestern corner of the area. The area will be clear, and you can head back up the beams using the lift. If you want to get the item above, you can ride the lift, then exit it before going down. A second lift will arrive just beside it, then do it again. Jump on the roof of the first lift just as it is arriving, and you will be able to reach the item, which is a Fire Knight Hilde summon.
If you walk across the beam going west, you can climb down a ladder. On your left, just around the corner, you can loot a body for x4 Rada Fruit . Directly northeast of the ladder you descended on, you will find another ladder you can use to descend. You will reach an area similar to the Site of Grace.
If you head outside the archway on the northwestern side of the area, you will find a lift directly on your right. Take the lift going down, then keep following the scaffolding until you reach an area with a door and a lift. You cannot open the door yet, so use the lift nearby to go down. The lift will then lead you to the Shadow Keep, Back Gate Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ].
This area leads you into two areas. There is a room on the east, just beside the Site of Grace. In this room, you can pick up x1 Scadutree Fragment . After that, you will see an archway heading outside on the north-northeast side of the room. This is a boss area in which you will be fighting Commander Gaius . For a more detailed guide on how to defeat Commander Gaius , you may go to this page.
Defeating Commander Gaius will give you the Remembrance of the Wild Boar Rider . It will also give access to Scaduview region.
The main entrance of the Shadow Keep is pretty linear, as you can follow the path directly from Castle Ensis leading to the main entrance of the Shadow Keep. Along the path, you will encounter Messmer Soldiers in encampments, so tread carefully. By the eastern section of the keep's outer walls, you can loot a fallen Furnace Golem for x1 Furnace Visage . Nearby, you can find a tent containing a corpse with x1 Furnace Keeper's Note . Return to the path, and you will find a big archway to a room containing a lift. This will take you to the Shadow Keep Main Gate Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ].
When you arrive at the top of the lift, activate the Site of Grace. The area ahead of it is a boss room. Once you are ready to fight it, enter the room and you will encounter the Golden Hippopotamus . Its attacks have a deceptively big hit box, using mostly its head to attack. For more guides and strategies on how to defeat the boss, you can visit this page. After defeating the Golden Hippopotamus, it will drop x2 Scadutree Fragment and the Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns incantation. Defeating the Golden Hippopotamus will give you access to the Main Plaza of the Shadow Keep, allowing you to explore the whole area. The Main Gate Plaza Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ] will be unlocked after the boss fight, so you can activate it now.
To have his revenge on Sir Messmer, there is only one place he will go. The black keep.
- Needle Knight Leda
Shadow Keep is a dungeon and region in the Realm of Shadow .
4 Characters 4.1 Enemies 4.2 Bosses 4.3 NPCs
5 Items 5.1 Notable Loot 5.2 Crafting Materials
5.2 Crafting Materials
The towering black keep, lair of Messmer the Impaler . Shadow Keep guards Scaduview to its north, the location of the Hinterland and the Scadutree . The keep itself has three main sections: the entrance's Main Gate Plaza, the Specimen Storehouse, and the Church District.
The outer walls tower over the Scadu Altus at a height of almost two-hundred meters. [ 1 ] Entering through the main gate guarded by a Furnace Golem leads to a lift to the Main Gate Plaza. The plaza is guarded by the Golden Hippopotamus , bristling with Crucible thorns. [ 2 ]
Shadow Keep, Church District
Shadow Keep as seen from the Castle Watering Hole.
One of Messmer's Fire Knights guarding the entrance to the Specimen Storehouse.

---

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Gravesite Plain
Gravesite Plain is a Location in Elden Ring . The Gravesite Plain region is added with the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, located in the realm of shadow. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
A somber plain covered in spiritgraves. The spiral tower of the hornsent lies to the northwest, and the entire expanse is marred by the harrowing ravages of war.
Gravesite Plain is the first region you will arrive at after touching Miquella's withered hand in the Mohgwyn Dynasty Mausoleum . You can find the map piece by following the main road heading north.
Gravesite Plain is revealed with Map: Gravesite Plain
(See our Shadow of the Erdtree Game Progress Route page for more info.)

A somber plain covered in spiritgraves. The spiral tower of the Hornsent lies to the northwest, and the entire expanse is marred by the harrowing ravages of war.
- Map: Gravesite Plain
Gravesite Plain is a region in the Realm of Shadow .
3 Locations 3.1 Dungeons 3.2 Landmarks
5 Encounters 5.1 NPCs 5.2 Invaders 5.3 Field Bosses 5.4 Enemies
A vast sepulchral plain scattered with scorched ruins and ethereal spiritgraves, located in the south of the Realm of Shadow . The spiral tower of the Hornsent lies to the north-west of the region. [ 1 ] The Gravesite Plain borders Scadu Altus to its east and the Land of the Tower to its west. The region is strewn with blackened ruins of the tower-folk, burned in the Crusade by the people of the Erdtree. The soldiers of Messmer the Impaler 's army maintain a presence around the Church of Consolation , but are for the most part confined to the northern rim of the Ellac River canyon.
On the southern shore of the region, the Cerulean Coast and Charo's Hidden Grave are scattered with luminous flowers, massive stone coffins, and spirit worms. Farther to their east towers the Jagged Peak , looming over the Finger Ruins of Rhia and the craggy foot of the mountain .
Also known as the land of spiritgraves, the Gravesite Plain is home to many forms of the deceased. [ 2 ] Shadow undead wander the ruins of Hornsent villages and towns, and a ghostflame dragon resides close to the Abandoned Ailing Village . The gravestones in the region are so old that many of them have turned to spirits and begun to fade away. [ 3 ] The gravesites are populated by gravebirds , ancient golems constructed to guard the spiritgraves. [ 4 ]
The plain itself is primarily vegetated by spiritgrave grass, which grows from the berry-like Rada Fruit . [ 5 ] These bitter fruits can be used in the fermenting recipes of the Forager Brood and, in raisin form, serve as a spiritually-enhancing medicine. [ 6 ] [ 7 ] Like the rest of the Realm of Shadow, mushrooms of the Whiteflesh and Redflesh varieties can also be found throughout, serving as a useful material for stuffing Hefty Cracked Pots . [ 8 ] [ 9 ] The plain is always at least partially overcast and experiences frequent rainstorms.
Note: for sake of clarity, these lists only include encounters found strictly in the Gravesite Plain proper, not any of its sub-regions. For details on sub-region-specific encounters, see those regions' respective pages.
Painter's Soul ("Incursion")
Logur, the Beast Claw
Ulcerated Tree Spirit

area: Abyssal Woods
chapter: 37
name: Abyssal Woods
Abyssal Woods is a Location in Elden Ring , added with the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC. The Abyss region can be found east of the Gravesite Plain, south of the Scadu Altus, and can be accessed through Darklight Catacombs at the end of the Recluses' River.
At the east of the Gravesite Plain. A thick forest sprawls at the depths of the ravine that is untouched even by sunlight. Known as the sanctum of Sage Midra, and is a region forbidden by the hornsent.
The Abyssal Woods is a region hidden behind a dungeon exploration. To get there, you need to defeat Jori, Elder Inquisitor in Darklight Catacombs. If you haven't defeated him, please see Darklight Catacombs for the directions.
After defeating Jori, Elder Inquisitor , use the freshly-spawned Forsaken Graveyard - Site of Grace. Then, head through the arch to formally arrive at the Abyssal Woods .
Abyssal Woods is revealed with Map: Abyss [ Map Link ]
(See our Shadow of the Erdtree Game Progress Route page for more info.)
Divided Falls [ Map Link ]
Abyssal Woods [ Map Link ]

Map: Abyss [ Map Link ]
Mad Craftsman's Cookbook (1) [ Map Link ]
Mad Craftsman's Cookbook (2) [ Map Link ]
Jori, Elder Inquisitor
Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame
The Abyssal Woods is an area that is very far off the beaten path.
Start at the Main Gate Plaza in Shadow Keep . Progress forward through either path until you see the first Fire Knight . Do not go up into the Specimen Storehouse, instead search for a ladder among the glowing boats. Descend it onto a narrow walkway, taking care not to fall into the water. Go under the first waterfall you see and you will enter a room with the Domain of Dragons Painting . Roll against one of the walls opposite to the painting and you will reveal a secret passageway. Walk down the waterway and rest in the coffin to be transported to the Castle Watering Hole Grace in front of the Ruins of Unte .
Dash southwest, past the awakening Furnace Golem . Hit up the Recluses' River Upstream Grace and continue along the path, using Torrent to hop across the gaps and minding the Man-Flies that may ambush you. Continuing south down this path leads you to the Recluses' River Downstream Grace. From there, turn around, hug the right wall, and cross over to the platform with Greatbirds. Carefully look for ledges to descend down, picking up the Dryleaf Seal on the way. The Teardrop Scarab you can find holds a Smithing Stone (8) . From this point, going east leads you to the Eastern Nameless Mausoleum where you can fight Rakshasa, while going west brings you to another waterfall. You can also look upstream to find another Scadutree Fragment .
Descend down the waterfall by platforming across the gravestones jutting out from the nearby cliff face. Once down there, you can tackle two Hippopotamus which each hold a Scadutree Fragment . From the hippos' area, look south, and you will find several gravestone platforms again. Descend down until you enter a poorly-lit area and are attacked by Shadow Militiamen. Dispatch them, then look for the entrance to the Darklight Catacombs at the foot of the cliffs.
Alternatively, continue south from the Recluses' River Downstream Grace until you reach the Village of Flies . Be careful as the entrance is guarded by a Curseblade . Once you're within the village premises, go downhill into a drop down onto a ledge. Continue descending to the next ledge and thereafter, taking out the Man-Flies along the way. Eventually, you will make a jump across to a platform where there are Shadow Militiamen. Go across to the cliff face to find the Darklight Catacombs.
Complete the Darklight Catacombs, taking out Jori, Elder Inquisitor at the very end. The end of the catacombs will open up into the Abyss, letting you explore the Abyssal Woods in full. You're in the Abyssal Woods proper once Torrent dismounts you and the game informs you that Torrent is too fearful to be resummoned.
Torrent is unable to be used in this region.
At the east of the Gravesite Plain . A thick forest sprawls at the depths of the ravine that is untouched even by sunlight. Known as the sanctum of Sage Midra , and is a region forbidden by the hornsent .
The Abyssal Woods , also known as the Abyss , is a sub-region of Scadu Altus .
1 Overview 1.1 Messages Left in the Woods
1.1 Messages Left in the Woods
Below the rest of the Realm of Shadow , hidden under a sprawling canopy that blots out the light of the sky, a thick swampy woodland corrupted by Madness . Jori , the elder Hornsent Inquisitor , guards the entrance to the area at the end of the Darklight Catacombs .
The forest is forbidden by the Hornsent , as is the frenzied flame contained within. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] The woods are so harrowing that even Torrent refuses to be summoned out of fear. [ 3 ] Messages on the ground warn about beings lurking in the woods, creatures that cannot be touched and can kill one with a glance.
In the heart of the woods sits the ruined manse of Sage Midra .
The Abyssal Woods can only be accessed by navigating through the Darklight Catacombs at the terminus of the Recluses' River.
A series of messages has been left in the forest, along the path to Midra's Manse.

---

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Scadutree
The Scadutree is a giant tree which looms over the Land of Shadow . It is the shadow of the Erdtree . [ 1 ]
The Scadutree is the shadow of the Erdtree, [ 2 ] and it was born of dark notions that bear no sense of Order, that twist and bend its stock, rendering it brittle. [ 1 ]
The Scadutree is holy but its gold is veiled in shadow and covered in impenetrable thorns. [ 3 ] [ 4 ] Due to its holiness the Scadutree is able to bless, although it has no influence outside of the Realm of Shadow . [ 5 ] Its splinters were collected, displayed, and prayed to in some Churches of Marika by the people of the Erdtree. [ 6 ] Even some of the remains of the warriors who perished in the realm of shadow were touched by the gold of the Scadutree, a blessing said to be the greatest honor that can be granted to the dead. [ 7 ]
For those who abandoned the practice of incantations after devout faith rewarded them with only despair, the image of the misshapen Scadutree is an edict: Spurn all that exists. Wound all that exists. For we have been abandoned. [ 8 ] Denounce their ways. Do them harm. For they have abandoned us. [ 9 ]
The scattered sap of the Scadutree pools deep within the furnace golems . [ 10 ]
Scadu is an Old English word for Shadow, meaning that the Scadutree is literally named the "Shadowtree".

area: Shadow of the Erdtree
chapter: 30
name: Scadutree Avatar
Scadutree Avatar is a Legend Boss in Elden Ring . Scadutree Avatar is the Realm of Shadow's equivalent of the Erdtree Avatar , but in design it resembles an enlarged Shadow Sunflower with two massive roots for arms and can summon thorns and conjure the Scadutree's light for offense. It is found in the Scadutree Base, accessible through a hidden path in the Shadow Keep . This is an optional boss as players don't need to defeat it in order to advance in Shadow of the Erdtree .
See Scadutree Avatar Lore
The Scadutree is the shadow of the Erdtree. Born of dark notions that bear no sense of Order, that twist and bend its stock, rendering it brittle.
This boss has three phases with three separate health bars. After each of the first two phases, it will become vulnerable to a critical hit; if you take advantage of this, it will start the next phase with 30% less HP
This is an optional boss
Closest Site of Grace: Tree-Worship Sanctum
Multiplayer is allowed for this boss
You can summon Spirit Ashes for this boss
Scadutree Avatar can be found in Scadutree Base. Scadutree Base is located East of Albinauric's Shack and Northeast of Shadow Keep . Scadutree Avatar can be found at ground level in the Scadutree Base area. [ Map Link ]
Health: 7,560 HP (phase 1), 13,608 HP (phase 2), 13,608 HP (phase 3)
Parryable : Yes (Magic parry), No (melee parry)
Is vulnerable to a critical hit after being stance broken
Damage: Standard , Strike , Magic , Holy
Inflicts: Hemorrhage
Weak point: Flower (+50%)
Strong spot: Stem Base (-66%)
Drops 260,000, Remembrance of the Shadow Sunflower , Miquella's Great Rune
The negation numbers are the % of your damage that gets blocked. For example, if a negation is 60, 40% of that damage by that type will go through and 60% will be negated. Bigger number = less damage. A negation of 100 means no damage goes through, and a negation of -100 mean the enemy takes 2x damage from that source. 0 means damage goes pretty much as is.
Poison : 421 / 519 / 809 / 1266
Scarlet Rot : 421 / 519 / 809 / 1266
Frostbite : 306 / 348 / 446 / 736 / 1193
The resistance numbers are the buildup amount to trigger it. For example, if a resistance is 100 you must deal 100 points of the given buildup to trigger it. Note that these go down over time, and increase each time the effect procs. The values after the "/"s indicate the increased resistances after each successive proc.
The Best Tips for Scadutree Avatar:
Recommended summons: Omenkiller Rollo , Bigmouth Imp , Fire Knight Hilde
Recommended armaments: Fire Knight Greatsword , Mohgwyn's Sacred Spear , Spear of the Impaler
Recommended spells: Frenzied Burst , Flame Fall Upon Them , Pest Threads
Recommended talismans: Godfrey Icon , Fire Scorpion Charm , Dragoncrest Greatshield
Bring Stanching Boluses , Holyproof Dried Liver
Use the Flame-Shrouding Cracked Tear in your Physick (if weapon/spell inflicting Fire Damage is used during the encounter) Other options include Opaline Hardtear or Bubbletear to lower damage, especially in the third phase where the explosions deal immense damage.
Other options include Opaline Hardtear or Bubbletear to lower damage, especially in the third phase where the explosions deal immense damage.
The Scadutree Avatar is a formidable boss in Elden Ring. This challenging encounter is divided into three phases, each increasing in speed and difficulty as its health depletes. As a melee character, your main objective is to get close and target its sunflower-shaped head, which is its weakest point.
In Phase 1, the Avatar is easy to handle. The battle begins with its triple tree root ranged attack, where it sends out two spiked roots toward you upon entering the Scadutree Base. Dodge these roots and close the distance to deliver heavy attacks to its head. The second spiked root will arrive a bit later than the first one, so you should be mindful of dodging it. If you can get close to the Scadutree Avatar after this attack, you can attack its head to deal a lot of damage.
This boss will continue the phase with one of its basic combos. It will start with the quick left overhead attack, a slightly delayed right overhead attack, and another left overhead. Then, it will pause for a bit, do a delayed right overhead attack, and bury it onto the ground. After a short delay, the ground will explode in the area around it. To dodge this attack your move pattern will go like this:
Dodge roll to your right
Wait for the pause. roll again to the right, then roll right again
Roll as the delayed attack hits the ground, then roll forward (slightly to the right) as the ground explodes
When this attack is dodged properly, you can retaliate with a heavy attack at the end. Throughout this phase, the Avatar will occasionally raise its head and swing at you multiple times. It can do the Sunflower Head Slam , Sunflower Head Charge , and the Sunflower Head Grab . Be ready to back up and evade these swings. These are the basic moves of the Scadutree Avatar and you can dodge them once you get the timing right. Another move is 360 Sweeps and Swing attacks. This is a move where you see the boss spin in a static location while swinging its arms. In the first move, you will only see the boss do a single 360 Sweep, and there's a variation where there's a slight delay between the two 360 Sweeps.
For the special move in Phase 1, it will do the Area of Thorns , where it summons thorns in a large area. This move is quickly followed by the Land of Shadow attack where you see its head glow bright gold and it will cock its head backwards. The sunflower appears to gather like a holy light on its head, and after a short charge, it will explode, creating orbs of holy magic. These orbs will be launched as projectiles that can track your movement. These projectiles can be dodged by sprinting sideways (either left or right) and then dodge roll just before the orbs hit you. In the first phase, there will be 3 sets of projectiles that you will need to dodge, but later on, there will only be 1 set of these.
The first phase is slow-paced, allowing you to predict and learn its attack patterns. Once you deplete its health, the Scadutree Avatar will collapse, only to resurrect a few seconds later with 100% (75%, if it is finished by a riposte) health, marking the start of Phase 2.
Phase 2 introduces a more aggressive and faster Scadutree Avatar. It retains all its previous attacks and adds a powerful thrust move. Upon resurrection, it will immediately charge at you regardless of distance. It will do this 3 times consistently and you need to time your rolls precisely to avoid this attack. Adapt to its increased speed by counter-attacking after each move it makes. If the boss ends up at a long distance, it will do either the long-branch attack or the Land of the Shadow projectile attack.
The key is to stay close, dodge its attacks, and strike its head when it is down. In this phase, you will now see the boss doing a single overhead attack, but it will now conjure thorns at the end of its arm. Even the 360 Swing and Sweep attack will now get a follow-up move, where it will do the same overhead attack that creates thorns at the end. The timing for the dodge roll is still the same during this phase. The Scadutree Avatar will often leave its head exposed after an attack, giving you opportunities to land heavy blows. Maintain your agility and continue the pattern of dodging and counter-attacking until its health is once again depleted. This triggers the final and most challenging phase (100% health without riposte as a finisher and 75% when it is a finisher).
Phase 3 is the most difficult stage of the fight. The Scadutree Avatar retains all attacks from the previous phases and introduces the devastating Charged Light Explosion . This attack is its most powerful one, capable of either one-shotting you or draining 80% of your health upon impact. This attack consists of a long charge time and once it explodes, it will shoot out laser projectiles (javelin-like) that can reach you anywhere in the arena. One way to dodge this is first, you need to get the timing right for this one; a tip here is to listen to the sound cue and roll to the side at the exact moment you hear the explosion. If dodged correctly, this boss will drop its head on the ground for an extended period, and you can get a couple of free hits.
As you are nearing the end of this fight, this boss can resolve to do the Charged Light Explosion multiple times, up to three consecutive explosions. At this point, you need to get the timing correct so you can dodge it properly. If you're having difficulty dodging this move, you can try sprinting away and rolling backward, however, doing it this way will not let you get many free hits once it slumps its head on the ground.
Its overall movements are now very fast, requiring heightened focus and quick reflexes. Watch for the telltale sign of this attack: when its chest becomes stationary and glows yellow. When this happens, run as far as possible and lock onto the foe to predict the explosion beams. Keep dodging this attack while looking for opportunities to land heavy blows on its head.
Ensure you have a melee weapon equipped so you perform a critical strike on the boss when you defeat it so that it starts the next phase with less health. Even the most basic of daggers will work.
Sorcery users can negate the entire Land of Shadow attack (the rain of golden projectiles) by casting Eternal Darkness and running back, causing all projectiles to get sucked into the black hole. If you do not run back after casting Eternal Darkness, you will still get hit. Use Comet targeting the head of the boss to deal great damage. Optionally, equip a shield with low requirements such as Carian Knight's Shield and enchant it with Scholar's Shield to negate all damage from attacks, though you will still get hemorrhage buildup from those attacks that inflict it, and the boss should offer windows of opportunity throughout the fight to reapply Scholar's Shield. Optionally, use Vow of the Indomitable on the shield to negate some dangerous attacks, such as the third phase explosions.
Frenzied Flame Incantations are a great choice for Faith users. Frenzied Burst has exceptional range and high damage, especially to the head. Ash of War: Flame of the Redmanes is another decent option.
Depleting the final phase's health does not trigger the healing effect from Blasphemous Blade and Taker's Cameo Depleting 1st/2nd phase's health does trigger the healing effect, but it is delayed (happens few seconds after, probably tied to how fight is constructed)
Depleting 1st/2nd phase's health does trigger the healing effect, but it is delayed (happens few seconds after, probably tied to how fight is constructed)
Under the arena are additional copies of Scadutree Avatars.
Bug: If a phase is finished by a riposte inflicted after stance break, the Avatar will respawn in the next with max health.

area: Enir-Ilim
chapter: 34
name: Impenetrable Thorns
Impenetrable Thorns is a Sorcery in Elden Ring . It was added with the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC . Impenetrable Thorns wounds the caster to unleash trails of Scadutree's impenetrable thorns from the ground.
Sorcery of those who abandoned the practice of incantations after devout faith rewarded them with only despair.
The caster wounds their own flesh using impenetrable thorns grown from the Scadutree, which then sprout from the earth. This sorcery can be used repeatedly.
The image of the twisted Scadutree is an edict: Denounce their ways. Do them harm. For they have abandoned us.
Where to find Impenetrable Thorns :
Shadow Keep : From the "Storehouse, Seventh Floor" Site of Grace, head the center of the storeroom, after pulling the lever to rotate the center beams. Keep climbing on the beams until you can jump to a room with a Marika Statue in it and an archway leading outside. Once outside, carefully walk off the nearest ledge (you'll see a bat below). You'll find the spell on a corpse. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
Alternatively, backtrack from the "Dark Chamber Entrance" Site of Grace, cross the bridge and down the steps on the right, and it will be directly below you next to the sitting bat enemy.
A multi-trail darker variant of Briars of Punishment , shooting three trails of thorns instead of one.
Deals ( Sorcery Scaling x 0.63 ) Magic Damage and 1.67 Stance damage per hit ( 15.03 total).
Inflicts 17 Hemorrhage buildup on hit (Each trail hits 3 times, dealing up to 153 bleed buildup per cast if standing next to target) Bleed buildup is increased by Staves with Arcane scaling, such as the Albinauric Staff or the Maternal Staff ( )
Bleed buildup is increased by Staves with Arcane scaling, such as the Albinauric Staff or the Maternal Staff ( )
Medium to long range. Each trail has moderate tracking.
Costs 50 HP to cast and adds 25 Hemorrhage buildup to the caster.
Can't be used while riding.
Alberich's Set boosts damage by 18%.
Staff of the Guilty boosts damage by 20%. Another staff copy wielded in the offhand adds further damage boost.
Compared to Briars of Punishment , Impenetrable Thorns has a wider radius and can be used for crowd control or against a single large target at medium range. However, it has a less consistent damage output and hitbox while attacking enemies from a different height or a sloped terrain.
NOTE: Patch 1.13 : Heavily changed the Spell:
All trails now track enemies (Used to be only the middle trail)
Magic Damage has been drastically decreased (125 > 63)
Bleed buildup has been drastically decreased (72 > 17)
Damage and Bleed buildup has been drastically decreased against blocking enemies.
PVP Damage Multiplier has been decreased (1.0 > 0.8)
PVE Stamina Damage has been decreased (50 > 10)
PVP Stamina Damage has been decreased (62 > 12)
PVE Poise Damage has been decreased (5 > 1.67)
PVP Poise Damage has been decreased (67.5 > 22.5)
Impenetrable Thorns (spell)

area: Gravesite Plain
chapter: 30
name: Land of Shadow
Land of Shadow is a new Location in Elden Ring . In the upcoming Elden Ring Shadow of the Erdtree DLC players will explore a new area called the Land of Shadow . This expansion introduces a fresh map separate from the familiar Lands Between, offering a distinct and immersive experience. In Shadow of the Erdtree DLC , players are led by Empyrean Miquella to the Land of Shadow , an enigmatic realm shrouded by the Erdtree's shadow. This is where the goddess Marika first made her presence known. The Land of Shadow holds many secrets that players will uncover as they meet others who, like Miquella, tread its lands with their own agendas. According to Hidetaka Miyazaki, the new area has roughly the size of Limgrave .
The Land of Shadow. A place obscured by the Erdtree.
Where the goddess Marika first set foot.
A land purged in an unsung battle.
Set ablaze by Messmer’s flame.
It was to this land that Miquella departed.
Divesting himself of his flesh, his strength, his lineage.
Of all things Golden.
And now Miquella awaits the return of his promised Lord.
Shadow of the Erdtree Release Date Trailer Impressions
The Elden Ring Land of Shadow has been confirmed to occupy the same space as the Lands Between but has been physically disconnected. Players will need to use a warp to access this area.
The entrance to the Land of Shadow is located within Mohg's blood-soaked boss arena, specifically at the iconic cocoon in the back. This entrance connects to Mohgwyn Palace , serving as the starting point for players venturing into the Land of Shadow . The Elden Ring Land of Shadow is a completely new location, different from the Lands Between, and is characterized by the unique presence of the Shadow of the Erdtree . This land is physically separated from the Lands Between, requiring a special means of travel. Players can expect a similar structure of exploration in the Shadow of the Erdtree Land of Shadow.
Land of Shadow is a mysterious and dark realm obscured by the Erdtree, where the goddess Marika first set foot. It is a place filled with secrets and dangers, with vast maps, dungeons, and enemies to encounter. The lore behind this DLC suggests that players will explore the Land of Shadow guided by Empyrean Miquella, meeting others who follow him with their own motives.

Sunlight feels all the warmer in darkened lands.
The Realm of Shadow (影の地, Kage no Chi ), also known as the Land of Shadow , is a veiled domain in the universe of ELDEN RING (2022) . It is the setting of the expansion Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree .
1 Overview 1.1 Access
A forbidden realm, hidden from view and excised from the annals of history. [ 1 ] The Realm of Shadow lies veiled in the Shadow of the Erdtree , known as the Scadutree . [ 2 ] It is the homeland of a people called the Hornsent ; before the realm was enshrouded, the vitality of the Crucible , the primordial form of the Erdtree, flourished. [ 3 ] [ 4 ] The Crucible is sacred to the Hornsent and is central to their culture. [ 5 ] The Realm of Shadow is also where the goddess Marika first set foot, and the place to which Miquella of the Haligtree departed, divesting himself of his flesh, his strength, his lineage, and all things golden.
Long ago, before the Shattering , the Realm of Shadow was set ablaze in a Crusade carried out by Messmer the Impaler on the orders of his mother, Queen Marika. [ 6 ] Messmer's forces sought to impale and immolate all those deemed impure. [ 7 ] At some point, however, Marika severed all ties with Messmer, and the Land of Shadow was hidden under the Scadutree to conceal all evidence of the purge. [ 8 ]
The Realm of Shadow is at the center of The Lands Between , a converging point for Death in all its forms. [ 9 ]
The Realm of Shadow is accessible to the Tarnished only after defeating Starscourge Radahn in the Wailing Dunes of Caelid and Mohg, Lord of Blood at the Mohgwyn Dynasty Mausoleum in the eastern Siofra River region. After defeating both of these demigods, the Needle Knight Leda appears near the Cocoon of the Empyrean in Mohg's boss arena. She tells the Tarnished to touch the withered arm in the cocoon, which will transport them to the Realm of Shadow.
After contacting the arm, the Tarnished emerges in a grotto under the Church of Benediction in the Gravesite Plain , with the Realm of Shadow free to explore.
The Realm of Shadow may be described as an island or a continent. Smaller islands are visible to the south, east, and west. Beyond those lie unnamed seas.
The topography of the realm is complex, characterized by many overlapping levels of verticality. Significant portions of the continent have no in-game map since they are located directly below others and therefore obscured. In general, the continent can be considered as a network of multi-layered plateaus separated by river canyons.
Much like its counterpart, the coasts of the Realm of Shadow consist mostly of near-vertical, extremely high cliffs. There are beaches along the Cerulean Coast , however.
Based on game engine measurements, the area depicted in the map of the Realm of Shadow covers almost twenty-nine square kilometers (about six kilometers from north to south and five kilometers from east to west.) The area of the actual landmass, not including vertical layering, is about eight square kilometers. At this size, the landmass as it stands is larger than the bay at the center of the Lands Between. [ 10 ] The lands' relative sizes have changed in the millennia since the Shattering .

area: Jagged Peak
chapter: 38
name: Jagged Peak
Jagged Peak is a Location in Elden Ring , added with the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC. The Jagged Peak is a mountainous region southeast of Gravesite Plain. You can reach this region by heading southeast from Castle Front, enter the Castle Front, and then head east and pass through Dragon's Pit . [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
An area rifled with powerful dragons.
You can reach this area after defeating the Ancient Dragon-Man in Dragon's Pit. If you haven't defeated the boss yet, please see Dragon's Pit
After defeating the boss, follow the tunnel until you reach the exit. Once you emerge, you can activate the Dragon's Pit Terminus Site of Grace [ See it in the Elden Ring Map ]. Head southeast and go past the area where the lone Jagged Peak Drake is, you can sprint past this boss while riding Torrent. Head north and pass through the pillars. This will take you to the cliff of a mountain. Use the Spiritspring at the end of this path, then veer right and cross the bridge. Then, you will arrive at the Foot of the Jagged Peak - Site of Grace, which is part of the Jagged Peak region.
Jagged Peak Map is revealed by Map: Southern Shore [ Map Link ]
(See our Shadow of the Erdtree Game Progress Route page for more info.)

[other maps go here]
To the east, the jagged peak thrusts upward into the clouds.
- Map: Southern Shore
Jagged Peak is a sub-region of the Gravesite Plain .
Visible from the rest of the Realm of Shadow, the great mountain can be seen dominating the skyline. The closer one gets to the mountain, the more dangerous and wild the storms become, and red lightning strikes the steep slopes and sharp stones.  The area is home to many powerful dragons and drakes, and their corpses can be seen in great number littering the mountain's sides. At the top is a legendary and feared dragon , whose rage against the Dragonlord remains potent and destructive.
The Jagged Peak can be reached through the Dragon's Pit after slaying the Ancient Dragon-Man and passing through the Dragon's Pit Terminus. In the foothills of the mountain is the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion , where the Dragon Communion Priestess explains the purpose and history of the occult ritual. The corpse of a colossal drake towers over the Grand Altar; walking across its body leads to Charo's Hidden Grave .
The Jagged Peak can be seen from most elevated points in the Realm of Shadow .
The peak as seen from the Ancient Ruins of Rauh .
The peak as seen from Enir-Ilim .
The peak as seen from Foot of the Jagged Peak .

area: Ancient Ruins of Rauh
chapter: 36
name: Ancient Ruins of Rauh
Ancient Ruins of Rauh (Rauh Ruins) is a Location in Elden Ring . The Ancient Ruins of Rauh region are added with the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, located north of Gravesite Plain, west of Scadu Atlus. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ] It can only be accessed to its entirely by traveling through the Shadow Keep .
These giant ruins are flooded, overgrown, and split into two by a chasm. To the southwest is the Church of the Bud, overlooking the tower of shadow.
From the Shadow Keep, West Rampart - Site of Grace, head west and enter through the small archway on the right side of the bridge. Ride Torrent here as you sprint past the enemies. Once you reach the other side, look for the spiral stairs, and then use the elevator to reach the Ancient Ruins of Rauh .
Ancient Ruins of Rauh is revealed with the Map: Rauh Ruins which can be obtained near Temple Town Ruins in Rauh Base .
(See our Shadow of the Erdtree Game Progress Route page for more info.)

To access this area, you need to unlock the West Rampart - Site of Grace in Shadow Keep. If you haven't unlocked this section, please see Shadow Keep or the Shadow of the Erdtree Walkthrough for the full walkthrough on how to reach the area.
From the Site of Grace, enter through the small archway on the right side of the bridge. It is recommended to ride Torrent here as you cross the bridge so you can move faster and loot as you travel. There will be giant bats on this bridge but you will move fast enough and they shouldn't be able to reach you. You will find bootable corpses here, the first one to the left which is a Smithing Stone [3] . Up ahead, you can loot more 3x Beast Liver , 3x Beast Blood , Smithing Stone [4] , 5x Redflesh Mushroom , 8x Thin Beast Bones , and a Somber Smithing Stone [2] .
At the other end of this bridge, you will encounter a Fire Knight wearing a red cloak. It wields dual daggers and can cast fire magic and will drop Salza's Hood and Rain of Fire upon defeating it.
Go inside the building behind the enemy you just defeated and take the Somber Smithing Stone [8] from the corpse on the corner. Take the spiral stairs next to it and you will reach an elevator at the top. This will take you to a new gate and then, interact with the Viaduct Minor Tower - Site of Grace here.
The southern area of the Ancient Ruins of Rauh covers exploring the parts near the Site of Grace. This includes the area below you that is accessible via the elevator in the small pond.
Go through the archway and go to the right side. Stick to the wall and go ride past the thick greenery here. You will go around this building and at the back, you can pick up the Golden Horn Tender . Go back the same way you came from, return to the entrance side, and head southeast past the small pond. You can see some boar creatures hanging out here and go behind this tree. Look for the corpse lying on top of a stone slab and pick up the Spirit Calculus . Then, use the elevator to go down to the lower level.
As you go down, face the southwest direction so you are ready for the Bloodfiend waiting here. Then, follow the path behind it where a group of Bloodfiends is gathered. This can be difficult since you are outnumbered, but if you have a magic build, you can use Adula's Moonblade to effectively take out the group. After fighting them, you can pick up Somber Smithing Stone [8] from the corpse behind the group. From here, face the southwest direction and exit through the small hole. Go to the right side and go past the bloodfiend. There's a corpse near the edge and you can pick up 8x Thin Beast Bones from it.
Now look behind you; you should be facing southeast here and go towards the edge and jump down on the steps below you. Look to your right and fight the lesser Golden Hippopotamus . This looks just like the boss at the Main Entrance of Shadow Keep but this one has a smaller health pool. It can also pull off the same moves just like the boss version. Upon defeating it, it will drop a Scadutree Fragment . You are now done with this section. Return to the Site of Grace and continue exploring the eastern area through the felled tree.
Upon exiting the archway, face the western direction and go through the collapsed tree. Follow the path ahead where you will see small creatures and make a right to to enter the building marked by these 2 standing lanterns. This area is filled with Shadow Undead and there are variations of them. Others are hunched on the ground, while there are some walking around carrying a jar. Take note that some of these enemies carry an important item. If you see them glimmering in the dark, eliminate them to take their item.
As you enter, go to your immediate right and climb up the overgrown roots. Head towards the northeast and turn left behind the pillar to pick up the Somber Smithing Stone [9] from the edge of this root. Go around this area and return to the entrance side and eliminate the Shadowpot . This one is carrying a Revered Spirit Ash and a Silver Horn Tender . Pick it up later after you fight the Curseblade . This behaves similarly to the boss but has a lesser health pool. After defeating these enemies, face the northeast where you see this tall door.
Don't enter through the door yet, instead turn left and you will see this burning cauldron. You can pick up the Fire Spritestone on the right side of this cauldron. Then, return to the door but make sure to defeat the nearby enemies here so they can't interrupt you. Open up the door and head down the steps. You will be fighting a Giant Scorpion here, then turn left where you will fight more of these Giant Scorpions. You can pick up the Shadow Realm Rune [5] just past this lit stone.
After picking up this item, look behind you and attack this illusory wall. Go inside and pick up the Bondstone and Antiquity Scholar's Cookbook (2) . Leave this area and turn right to progress. Go down the steps where a group of shadow enemies are gathered. Pay attention to the glimmering one carrying a jar and make sure to eliminate them to get 3x Spirit Calculus from them. You can also pick up a Broken Rune in front of this square hole to the southeast. (Take note of this location because you will be coming back here later)
Go through this square hole and once you reach the other side, you will be fighting more Giant Scorpions. Watch out for the ones hiding and sleeping on the ceiling. They will attack you as you approach this loot on the corner. Go up the stones and take the Somber Smithing Stone (6) from the corpse here. Exit this area and turn right. Look for this glimmering enemy carrying a jar and defeat it. It will drop a Scadutree Fragment and Golden Horn Tender .
Then, look behind you and face the northeast direction. You will be able to see a Curseblade on the second pillar. This one refuses to go down unless you go near it so you can just use range attacks to defeat it without getting damaged. After killing it, walk in front of you and pick up the 5x Throwing Dagger on the next alcove.
From the last alcove, turn right, then left. You will be facing a group of shadow enemies and a Spider Scorpion here. Make sure to take them out before you continue exploring here. Loot the corpse here to get Glass Shard . Continue up ahead and turn right to this room where you see a scorpion suspended in the air. You can see multiple corpses on the ground with loot on them but you need to eliminate this enemy first. Try not to the aggro of the Giant Scorpion on the other by doing ranged attacks. After doing so, pick up the 2x Sliver of Meat , 3x Scorpion Liver , and Spider Scorpion Ashes .
Head to the next area filled with white flowers and defeat the Giant Scorpions here. You can also pick up 2x Dewgem from the corpse here. Then, return to the other side of the square hole that you entered earlier. Trace your steps and return to the area where the shadow enemies are gathered. Open the door here facing southwest, then, take the 5x Rauh Burrow from the corpse to your right.
Follow this path until you reach this Spiritspring with surrounding nails. You can jump down here while riding Torrent and you will not be damaged. Face the southeast direction and go to the very end of this path. Pick up the Shadow Realm Rune (6) behind the Spiritspring . Don't go up the spiritspring and head back towards the area where you came from.
Turn right behind the 3rd pillar, and then go up the thick roots to pick up the Fine Crucible Feather Talisman . Then, head southwest and exit through the small archway at the end. Turn left and you will see an item on the top of the first archway to your left. Behind it, you can use the overgrown branch to climb this arch and then pickup the Shadow Realm Rune (7) from the corpse. Continue riding southeast and you will reach the outdoor area.
When this enemy comes into view, don't try to take its aggro right away. You want to be able to reach the nearby Site of Grace here by walking only on the right side. Stick to the edge on your right and you will see Miquella's Cross here near the edge. The Rauh Ancient Ruins, East - Site of Grace is on your right and you can pick up an Ancient Ruins Cross Message and Scadutree Fragment on the ground near the cross.
From the Site of Grace, face northeast and go towards the pillar here. Walk over the edge to find an item near the waterfall. Pick up the Ash of War: Dryleaf Whirlwind here. Then, return to the enemy that you saw earlier. This Lesser Crucible Knight will drop Devonia's Hammer and Crucible Hammer-Helm upon defeating it.
After defeating this enemy, cross the bridge facing southwest. There's a Furnace Golem at the other end of this bridge and it will start attacking you as you jump down. There are also shadow enemies along the way and you can pick up 4x Dewgem and 2x Flight Pinion . Then, jump down to the right side here. Ride up ahead but make sure to sprint so you can safely reach the covered area here. Then, go past the Furnace Golem , and don't fight it for now. The Rauh Ancient Ruins, West - Site of Grace is in the area behind it so you want to get the checkpoint first.
From the Rauh Ancient Ruins, West - Site of Grace, go down the ramp and fight the Furnace Golem . It will drop the Crimson-Sapping Cracked Tear and a Furnace Visage upon defeating it.
After fighting the Furnace Golem, teleport to the Site that you had just activated. Face the southwest direction and go toward the double archway. There are different Shadow Undead here, like shadow mage and shadow hound. Eliminate them as you go and go up the stairs to the right. You can pick up a 6x Kukri on the steps and Whiteflesh Mushroom from the enemies.
Go up the steps, then, go to the right side and eliminate the glimmering Shadowpot . It will drop 3x Spirit Calculus and a Rauh Burrow . Then, defeat the 2 Shadow Mages here. At the center of this map, there's a Curseblade . This one uses death spells and is just as dangerous as the boss version. On the northwest side, there's an altar where shadow enemies are worshipping. Go towards it and pick up the Scadutree Fragment .
Then, explore towards south and then turn right. Make sure to pick up the Rauh Burrow and 2x Spiritgrave Stone along the way. Then, use the elevator at the end of this path that will take you to the stone pillar section. Go to the left then jump down on the stone pillar below you. Try to land on the widest portion here and follow the steps and land on the area below you. Carefully walk here and try not to fall off the map. Walk forward, and jump to your right at the lower level.
This should take you to a level where, if you look behind, you will see a corpse behind you and pick up the Four-Toed Fowl Foot from the corpse.
Return to your last location and then jump between the gaps while riding Torrent. Keep following the steps here and then face southeast to where you see the edge of a building. Jump towards the left side and eliminate the 2 enemies holding a candlestick standing in front of the steps here.
Face the building and go towards the square hole to the left, then turn left again to reach the chest. You can get the Talisman of All Crucibles inside.
While facing the chest, exit through the square hole to your right. Use the elevator to go up, and you can already see the Ancient Ruins, Grand Stairway - Site of Grace from the top. Walk towards it and activate it.
Face the northwest direction, jump down the stairs, and then turn right to get loot. Stick to the wall to your right and then go to the very edge here to pick up a Spirit Calculus from a corpse. Then, return to the grand stairway where you see the archer version of the Golem Warrior. You can go up the ramp behind it and take it out. This enemy is weak to heavy attacks so make sure to take full advantage of its weakness.
Go down the broken bridge here and cross towards the northeast. Near the end of this bridge, you will find a corpse in the middle and you can take a Shadow Realm Rune (7) from it. Then, jump down on the right side. There's a Divine Beast Dancing Lion field boss here, and it's up to you if you want to fight it but you can run away from it by jumping down on the northeastern platform. This disables the fight and the boss will not chase you.
There's an elite enemy in the middle of the pool. This one wields a dual curved sword. Go up the ramp on the left side to pick up 2x Beast Bones. Then, go to the area behind the boss to pick up Verdigris Discus . Continue exploring the southern area here to pick up a Redflesh Mushroom from a corpse next to a pillar. This is your last stop in the Grand Stairway section and you will now return to the Rauh Ancient Ruins, West - Site of Grace.
This covers the rest of the exploration of the western section where you will be invaded by an NPC.
From the Rauh Ancient Ruins, West Site of Grace, face the southwest section and go up the stairs. Since you can ride Torrent in this area, you can outrun most enemies. This is the same area that you explored earlier but instead of going to the left section, you will head northwest towards the outdoor area. Ahead of you will be a gazebo, with one Horned Warrior patrolling in front of it and another one guarding the altar under the gazebo. You can pick up a Revered Spirit Ash from the altar.
Past the gazebo roughly towards the north, there's an archway that leads to a Scarlet Rot -infested area with some red Lesser Kindred of Rot patrolling around. Ignore this path for now, because you'll return here later. Instead, continue northeast, where you'll find some steps leading down. Head down the steps to reach a rocky intersection. Here, make a right (heading northeast) instead of continuing forwards.
Ahead of you will be an area with arches, Strays , and a stronger than usual Bloodfiend . The Bloodfiend drops 5x Sacred Bloody Flesh upon defeat. In the same area, there's a corpse that you can loot a Golden Horn Tender from. Head northwest and you'll encounter a patrolling Bloodfiend, and several more Rotten Strays and Bloodfiends in the area ahead.
On the upper area, while facing northwest, you might see a glimmering Shadowpot . To reach it, head through one of the short arches and jump down - riding Torrent should lessen any fall damage. Once you drop down, beware of the many small and giant Spider Scorpions in the area, as they can quickly poison you. Heading west will take you down a sloping area that leads to an elevator, but don't activate it just yet. Instead, proceed northeast.
You'll spot two flying Spider Scorpions. Fearsome as they may seem, they don't have that much health. You should also see a corpse surrounded by a few small Spider Scorpions. Loot the corpse for a Somber Smithing Stone (1) . Heading east, you should see several Miranda Sprouts , and a large ruin with a Giant Miranda Sprout . Near the Giant Miranda Sprout is a corpse that you can loot for the Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom incantation.
Now, return to the elevator and ride it up. On the top level, you will reach a Shadowpot and several other Shadow Undead . The Shadowpot drops a Revered Spirit Ash . If for whatever reason the Shadowpot is no longer here, you may have to reload the area - simply quitting to the main menu and reloading your save is such a way.
Facing southeast, jump down to the area you spotted the Shadowpot from earlier, and proceed onwards. On the path ahead, there are several pillars, behind one of which is a corpse that you can loot 3x Knot Resin from. Heading up the stairs takes you to the earlier area with the gazebo and the unexplored Scarlet Rot-infested path.
Now, proceed into the Scarlet Rot area that you skipped earlier. Simply standing in the rotten water will build up Scarlet Rot, so Soap and Flame, Cleanse Me can be very useful here. There's a scarlet version of Lesser Kindred of Rot (Pests) here, be careful of them because they can deal a high amount of damage when you get struck by them. Towards the west, there is an elevator, but ignore it for now.
Go to the northwest arch and pick up the Dappled White Cured Meat . To your right, there are more pest enemies here. There's a large group of them in the next section, so be careful to not engage them all at once and get overwhelmed. If you have any ranged or magic attacks, make use of them to eliminate the enemies before they get near you. You can obtain these items from the ground here: 3x Immunizing Cured Meat , 4x Fly Mold , and 8x Smithing Stone (2) . After fighting them, exit through the archway on the left side. Look to the stack of stones on your right and you will see a corpse here. You can pick up the Greater Potentate's Cookbook (9) from it.
Face the tower to the northeast and walk straight towards it. This is the Unseen Bridge that connects the main building to this tower. Cross to the other side and pick up the Stone-Sheathed Sword . If you already have that weapon from one of the other altars, then this is where you can convert the Sword into either the Sword of Light or Sword of Darkness .
Now, return to the elevator that you ignored earlier, and ride it to reach an upper level. There are more pest enemies here, and you will see a large staircase in the middle. Just take note of these areas for now because you will be returning here later.
To the southeast, there's another pest and an archway that leads outside. Head outside and you should see another gazebo, and past it, a patrolling Divine Bird Warrior and a corpse in the water that you can loot for the Divine Bird Feathers incantation.
Northeast of the gazebo, you can find a forested area with a rocky hill. Proceeding north through the forest, you'll find a glowing stone cairn (a pile of rocks) - break it to activate a nearby Spiritspring . The Spiritspring creates a shortcut that you can use to reach this area quickly from the Rauh Ancient Ruins, West Site of Grace.
Mount Torrent and use the Spiritspring to cushion your fall, then use it again to reach the area on the other side with some stone stairs (roughly northeast). Before going up the stairs, head right (southeast) and walk up to the edge. If you face exactly west, you should see a corpse that you can loot for 2x Beast Horn .
Then, go up the stairs that you saw earlier and open the chest at the top to get the Mottled Necklace +2 talisman. Use the Spiritspring again to return to the cliff where the stone cairn was - facing the Spiritspring, ride Torrent and jump to one of the broken pillars, then onto the Spiritspring to prevent a lethal fall. A safer alternative path is to head north from the base of the stairs to find a shorter cliff that you can use to descend, then you can find your way back to the Spiritspring.
Head back inside to the rot-infested room with the large staircase, and head left to the area behind the stairs. Once you reach the rearmost area, you can find the Pest-Thread Spears incantation on a corpse. From the corpse, head northwest and make a right to head back towards the stairs. Before reaching the stairs, on your left you should find another corpse with 6x Scarlet Buds .
Once you're at the base of the stairs, if Hornsent 's quest is still ongoing, prepare to get invaded by him as you head up. If you die, you can use the Spiritspring you should've activated earlier to reach this area relatively quickly from the Rauh Ancient Ruins, West Site of Grace.
The Spiritspring is located just northwest of the Site of Grace, in the pool area. Defeating Hornsent here will reward you with the following items:
Once you're at the top of the stairs, head southeast to reach the Church of the Bud, Main Entrance Site of Grace. Immediately south of the Site of Grace is the Church of the Bud, whose entrance is shrouded in golden fog, leading to a boss fight. Just before entering the church, there's a gold summon sign nearby to summon Dryleaf Dane to assist you in the upcoming battle.
Proceeding through the fog, you will fight Romina, Saint of the Bud. Her attacks inflict Scarlet Rot , but thankfully the arena doesn't inflict it, despite earlier rot-infested areas posing as an environmental hazard. For more information on how to defeat Romina, you can read this page. Defeating her gives you the Remembrance of the Saint of the Bud , and the golden fog blocking the archways disappears.
Exit through the southern archway of the church and head up the steps to reach the Sealing Tree, which looks almost like a miniature Scadutree. Provided you have Messmer's Kindling from defeating Messmer the Impaler , interacting with the Sealing Tree will give you the option to burn it. If you choose to do so, a cutscene will play and you will be warped to Enir-Ilim .
[other maps go here]
These giant ruins are flooded, overgrown, and split into two by a chasm. To the southwest is the Church of the Bud , overlooking the tower of shadow .
Ancient Ruins of Rauh is a sub-region of the Land of the Tower .

Ancient ruins overgrown with lush, dense vegetation. [ 1 ] The Rauh Ancient Ruins are the remains of a civilization that practiced the art of sprite magic, an art so ancient that even the Hornsent of antiquity had academics researching it. [ 2 ]
The Rauh ruins are home to the Church of the Bud , which was burnt and ruined during the Crusade. [ 3 ] Now, the crumbling church is home to Romina, Saint of the Bud , who witnessed its burning and, as a result, made contact with the Scarlet Rot . [ 4 ] [ 5 ]

area: Cerulean Coast
chapter: 31
name: Cerulean Coast
Cerulean Coast (Southern Shore) is a Location in Elden Ring . The Cerulean Coast region can be found south of the Gravesite Plain. [ See Shadow of the Erdtree Map ]
A captivating vista of brilliant blue flowers, intermingled with colossal stone coffins of unknown origin that seemingly drifted to this place. To the east, the jagged peak thrusts upward into the clouds.
Start from the Pillar Path Cross Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ] directly south of Castle Ensis . Head north of the Site of Grace, wherein you will follow the cliff until you see a part of it that you can jump on. Make your way to the bottom using the rocks, then head south to a cave opening leading to the Ellac River Cave Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ]. Keep heading south until you get to the waterfalls, then carefully jump across the rock formations. Head south, and you can activate the Ellac River Downstream Site of Grace [ See Elden Ring Map ]. From here continue traveling until you see glowing blue flowers around the area, which means that you are on the Cerulean Coast .
Cerulean Coast is revealed with Map: Southern Shore [ Map Link ]
(See our Shadow of the Erdtree Game Progress Route page for more info.)

The entrance to Stone Coffin Fissure , which is located below the cliff of The Fissure Site of Grace will be sealed until Miquella casts away his great rune, which is a story event that is triggered by either approaching Shadow Keep or crossing the bridge east of Bonny Village .
A captivating vista of brilliant blue flowers, intermingled with colossal stone coffins of unknown origin that seemingly drifted to this place.
- Map: Southern Shore
The Cerulean Coast is a sub-region of the Gravesite Plain .
A coastal plain of cerulean flowers and mist on the Southern Shore of the Realm of Shadow . Accessible by following the Ellac River downstream. The Cerulean Coast is adorned with massive stone coffins resembling ships. The region has ties to the traditions of the Lands Between from before the Age of the Erdtree regarding death; many of the roads in the Cerulean Coast are lined by spirit worms and jellyfish, and a ghostflame dragon can be found not far from the entrance to the area.
The Cerulean Coast is bordered by Charo's Hidden Grave , the Foot of the Jagged Peak , and the Abyssal Woods to its north. The southern peninsula of the coast is split by a ravine leading to the Stone Coffin Fissure .
Demi-Human Swordsman
Demi-Human Queen Marigga
