Can be summoned for the boss fight against Fire Giant . His sign will appear only after entering the area through either chain. [ Map Link ]. Summoning sign is inside arena, therefore does not affect boss healthbar. Player can summon him at any point of the fight. In this encounter, he is immune to Fire Damage and deals high Stance Damage, therefore he is a very viable summon at a cost of inability to use Torrent. Note: Alexander will not appear at the next location until you've defeated Fire Giant and progressed the story.
Summoning sign is inside arena, therefore does not affect boss healthbar. Player can summon him at any point of the fight.
In this encounter, he is immune to Fire Damage and deals high Stance Damage, therefore he is a very viable summon at a cost of inability to use Torrent.
Note: Alexander will not appear at the next location until you've defeated Fire Giant and progressed the story.
Found in Crumbling Farum Azula . From the Dragon Temple Lift grace (can be accessed by using two Stonesword Keys), go past the plaza full of undead up the flight of stairs, turn right and you'll see two square bracket shaped archways. Jump on the archway on the left (closest to the sleeping dragon) to get over to the other side. Alexander can be found directly on the flat arena after crossing the crumbling tower. [ Map Link ] He will challenge you to a warrior's duel, and if you win, he will reward you with Alexander's Innards and Shard of Alexander . If you lose, he will continue letting you challenge him until you win.

He will challenge you to a warrior's duel, and if you win, he will reward you with Alexander's Innards and Shard of Alexander .
If you lose, he will continue letting you challenge him until you win.
You can help Iron Fist Alexander out by hitting him a few times on the back with any weapon. While you hit him, he will express some pain, but then he'll tell you to keep trying.
Alexander delivers some words of encouragement.
After pushing Alexander out, he will stand up, congratulate you, and present the Triumphant Delight Gesture . He then rewards you with Exalted Flesh and hints at his next destination: the combat festival at Redmane Castle in Caelid .
Alexander's next location is Gael Tunnel , accessible from the rear entrance bordering Limgrave and Caelid , or by reaching the boss room and opening the one-way door. He will mention the Caelid festival again. You can skip this step if you have already triggered the Radahn Festival through Blaidd's questline.
At Redmane Castle , Alexander is found in the plaza with other participants and can be summoned as a cooperator during the fight against Starscourge Radahn .
After Radahn's defeat, Alexander moves south of the Starscourge Radahn Site of Grace in Wailing Dunes , scavenging fallen soldiers to strengthen himself.
Alexander can be found in the plaza of Redmane Castle with the other participants. He is one of the cooperators that can be summoned during the fight against Starscourge Radahn .
After Radahn's defeat, Alexander can be found just south of Starscourge Radahn site of grace in Wailing Dunes , scavenging the remains of fallen soldiers to strengthen himself.
Next, in Liurnia of the Lakes , south of the Carian Study Hall , Alexander will be stuck in a hole. Applying an Oil Pot and then hitting him will free him. He will reward you with Exalted Flesh and mention heading north to further strengthen himself. You can skip this step if you’ve already visited Mt. Gelmir .
In Mt. Gelmir , Alexander is found in a lava pool on the west side, behind a Magma Wyrm . You can safely talk to him from the spires jutting out of the lava pool. He will give you the Jar as a token of friendship and plans to go to the Forge of the Giants in the east.
As an NPC cooperator against the Fire Giant , Alexander’s summon sign will be located within the arena, near its entrances.
After defeating Fire Giant and progressing the main story, his final location is in Crumbling Farum Azula . He can be found standing atop a large ruin as you descend toward the Dragon Temple . If you’ve accessed the Dragon Temple Lift, you can find him by platforming across the upper debris. Alexander will challenge you to a final duel. Defeat him to receive Alexander's Innards and the Shard of Alexander .

Beyond these lands lie the scarlet rot-blighted Caelid Wilds. And upon their southern edge is Redmane Castle, in which a festival of combat is being held. The fear simply assures me the ordeal is worth undertaking! Now, be sure to get a good vantage, my friend. I, Iron Fist Alexander, do hereby vow to unflinchingly brave this ordeal.

Alexander speaks and sounds similar to Siegmeyer and Siegward of Catarina from Dark Souls and Dark Souls 3, though those 2 share a different voice actor than Alexander, therefore his character could possibly be a reference to the Catarina Knights of games past.
According to the Shard of Alexander, it would seem that Alexander had taken parts of a “red-haired champion” into himself, making him mightier than before. Radahn would easily fit this description, seeing as Alexander was busy scraping up bodies to fill himself once more, after fighting Radahn. It would be easy to assume that he had taken parts of Radahn and shoved it inside of him.
He is an available NPC Summon against Starscourge Radahn during the Radahn festival and can also be summoned against the Fire Giant .  Progress in his questline, and he will be available as a summon. Make sure he is not stuck in his first location or stuck South of the Carian Study Hall in Liurnia. His summon sign will be at the fog gate entrance close to the Site of Grace before facing the Fire Giant.
During the final encounter, he is immune to Fire Damage .
After besting him in the final duel, you can hit him while he's down to skip the post-battle dialogue and you will still get the correct rewards.
A fragment of a living jar, hardened after its death. Material used for crafting items.
Such fragments command a high price due to the magical power locked within. This leaves the living jars unfortunate targets for poachers.
Shard of a shattered warrior jar.
Boosts the attack power of skills.
Scraps of stewed flesh cling to the shard, and tatters of ornaments can be seen mingled within the slime. Relics of ancient royal warriors, perhaps.
A lump of animal flesh pickled in a medicinal solution mixed with fiery spices. Craftable item.
Temporarily boosts physical attack.
Considered a delicacy in the badlands, this invigorating repast was for the exclusive benefit of those who they deem heroes.
One of Seluvis' puppets. Use to summon the spirit of a jarwight. Spirit of a man who wished to become the innards of a livingjar. A jar-hurling specialist who throws all manner of pots and jars.
The warrior jar once told the nameless man this: "You are not yet ready to join the warriors inside. No, you must apply yourself! Better yourself, and one day I will return for you."
Jar that fits cleanly over the head when upturned. Made with pride by Iron Fist Alexander.
In a uniquely jarlike gesture of friendship, it boosts the power of throwing pot items.
A keepsake of the warrior jar Alexander. Found at the core of the dead flesh that once filled the great jar.

area: Liurnia of the Lakes
chapter: 4
name: Boggart
Blackguard Big Boggart is a Tarnished NPC in Elden Ring . Initially encountered as the thief of Rya's Necklace , he is a minor NPC and merchant.
He is involved in the questlines of Rya and the Dung Eater . Blackguard Big Boggart is available as an NPC Summon against Magma Wyrm Makar by progressing his questline.
Never met someone with a taste for prawns I couldn't trust.
Blackguard Big Boggart can initially be found in the Liurnia of the Lakes swamps at the Boilprawn Shack , just north of the Scenic Isle grace. [ Elden Ring Map Link ]
Once befriended, he can be summoned for the Magma Wyrm Makar fight at the Ruin-Strewn Precipice .
Later, he moves to Leyndell in the northern section of the moat near the Capital rampart. [ Elden Ring Map Link ] (Be cautious about fighting the giant crab in this area as it can breathe Death Blight on him.)
This merchant can be fought.
If defeated, drops: Blackguard's Iron Mask , Iron Ball , Blackguard's Bell Bearing , 1,000 runes.
If you go to the Volcano Manor without speaking to Rya at Liurnia of the Lakes , you will lose access to Blackguard Big Boggart and his questline for the rest of that playthrough. This will also render you unable to purchase Boiled Prawn and Boiled Crab .
Boggart is introduced as the thief who stole a necklace from Rya . He is not remorseful of his actions but offers to sell the necklace back. He warns the player that Rya isn't right in the head, a hint that there is something more to her character.
The player can choose to buy the necklace back from Boggart for 1,000 runes, or just kill him. Killing him this early will lock you out of purchasing Boiled Crabs . You must at least initiate the quest from Rya before reaching Volcano Manor , when she moves there. If you don't, you will be unable to progress Boggart's questline. Assuming he lives from his first interaction, he can then be summoned for the Magma Wyrm Makar fight.
Assuming he lives from his first interaction, he can then be summoned for the Magma Wyrm Makar fight.
If they bought the necklace back, the player can then buy Boiled Prawns from him, after which he remarks that he's never met someone with a taste for prawns that he couldn't trust. This befriends him and progresses his questline but will put him at risk.
If the player bought prawns from Boggart , he moves to Leyndell , in the outer moat near the capital rampart. He will be cooking and selling Boiled Crabs . Doing this before completing Dung Eater's questline may result in Boggart's death.
At the Leyndell moat, he warns the player about his time in prison with the Dung Eater, where Boggart saw him do "unspeakable acts" to corpses. Boggart warns the player to stay clear of Dung Eater.
If the Dung Eater's questline is at the point where he will be "waiting in the outer moat", and the blackguard is present in Leyndell, exhaust Boggart's dialogue here until he says he thinks the Dung Eater is lurking somewhere nearby. Killing Boggart after this dialogue but before reloading will prevent any further dialogue, but a body will still be seated near the crab pot; however, the body will not have a Seedbed Curse .
Killing Boggart after this dialogue but before reloading will prevent any further dialogue, but a body will still be seated near the crab pot; however, the body will not have a Seedbed Curse .
Then reload the area and interact with the now wounded blackguard to trigger the invasion.
A Seedbed Curse will be found on Big Boggart's body at the end of his questline, along with his drops. If you have been invaded by the Dung Eater in the moat before Boggart moves there, you will be unable to get a Seedbed Curse from his body. Alternatively, you can save Boggart by aggroing him at the Leyndell moat before freeing the Dung Eater in his cell. Once you have defeated the Dung Eater during his invasion and you have fed Dung Eater's corporeal body Seluvis's Potion and/or killed him in his cell (in which you can do both to obtain the Dung Eater Puppet and the Omen Set simultaneously), head to the Church of Vows to absolve your sin and make Boggart friendly again. [There are reports that it no longer works since 1.10, but user xbot668 reports it does work. Try at your own risk] You will miss out the Seedbed Curse, and Boggart will die the moment you use the Celestial Dew. Another way to save Boggart is to simply not buy prawns from him when he's in Boilprawn Shack , as this prompts him to move to his second location in Leyndell. Instead, you can finish Dung Eater's questline and then go back to him. Buy at least one prawn and then go find him at the moat and now he will stay alive and safe from Dung Eater, cooking crab meat.
Alternatively, you can save Boggart by aggroing him at the Leyndell moat before freeing the Dung Eater in his cell. Once you have defeated the Dung Eater during his invasion and you have fed Dung Eater's corporeal body Seluvis's Potion and/or killed him in his cell (in which you can do both to obtain the Dung Eater Puppet and the Omen Set simultaneously), head to the Church of Vows to absolve your sin and make Boggart friendly again. [There are reports that it no longer works since 1.10, but user xbot668 reports it does work. Try at your own risk] You will miss out the Seedbed Curse, and Boggart will die the moment you use the Celestial Dew.
Another way to save Boggart is to simply not buy prawns from him when he's in Boilprawn Shack , as this prompts him to move to his second location in Leyndell. Instead, you can finish Dung Eater's questline and then go back to him. Buy at least one prawn and then go find him at the moat and now he will stay alive and safe from Dung Eater, cooking crab meat.

Small Talk in Leyndell
You 'eard of the Dung Eater? E's a madman, 'as it out for everyone. Curses 'em. Goes 'round in this rank armour, an' all. You see 'im though, stay well away. I was in the same gaol as him, once, so I know first 'and. E's a god-forsaken monster. Not just some petty thug like me.

(After buying Rya's Necklace) Hmm... Alright. Take it. Thing's no damn use to anyone, anyway. Your bloody idea, mate. Don't come crying to me later.
Although he is primarily referred to as just 'Blackguard', he refers to his own name while in combat when saying, "You don't mess with Big Boggart, mate" .  Additionally, his mask states "Worn by the blackguard Big Boggart". His weapon is also described as "Big Boggart the blackguard's weapon of choice". In the credits, he is listed as 'Thug'.
After buying prawn from him, he gives the 'Spread Out' gesture .
During combat, he uses his Iron Ball fist weapons, the Braggart's Roar skill , and consumes Boiled Prawns.
His appearance and attire could be a reference to the 1998 film The Man in the Iron Mask.
Iron mask forced on convicted prisoners. Worn by the blackguard Big Boggart.
To Boggart, the mask made him all the more threatening, and helped him get what he wanted. But it was also a mirror of his emptiness.
Tattered clothing. Worn by a prisoner convicted of an appalling crime.
Never once washed, every fiber is filthy.
Iron manifer of spherical shape. Big Boggart the blackguard's weapon of choice.
Not a weapon to be taken lightly, as its weighty blows shatter bones with ease.
Boiled prawn meat. A prime specimen of plump and moist meat. True connoisseurs know how to keep from over-salting.
Boosts physical damage negation for a certain duration,
The meat actually looks to have come from a crayfish. Not that it matters, it's delicious all the same.
Boiled crab meat. A prime specimen of plump and moist meat. True connoisseurs know how to keep from over-salting.
Greatly boosts physical damage negation for a certain duration,
Unlike the "prawn" sold, this truly is crab. Not that it matters, it's delicious all the same.
Curse grown on a corpse killed and defiled by the Dung Eater. A tender pox afflicted with omen horns.
The Dung Eater cultivates the seedbed curse on corpses.
By doing so he prevents dead souls returning to the Erdtree, leaving them forever cursed. One of the most loathsome things found in the Lands Between.
Hello. It's rather chilly here, isn't it? My mistress sent me off on an errand... But I was accosted by a ruffian, and now I'm in a bind. Could I ask you to lend a hand, perhaps? That thug made off with a precious necklace. I need someone to retrieve it. Only... He, too, is Tarnished. If you've any qualms confronting your own, I shall find another...

- Blackguard Big Boggart
Blackguard Big Boggart is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
3 Character Information 3.1 Basic Info 3.2 Equipment 3.3 Quest Items
4 Questline Progression
Boggart is an ex-convict who was called to the Lands Between by the Guidance of Grace [ 1 ] . At some point in the past he was imprisoned in the same gaol as the Dung Eater [ 2 ] , and saw first hand the ways he defiles and kills people. [ 3 ]
The Tarnished first encounters him in Liurnia of the Lakes, where they confront him over the theft of a necklace belonging to Rya . Despite his foreboding first impression, Boggart gives the Tarnished the opportunity to buy back the necklace [ 4 ] , and offers to sell them some cooked crawfish.
Boggart then journeys to Altus Plateau , setting up camp beside a river outside the capital, where he catches and cooks crabs. Here, he warns the Tarnished about the Dung Eater. Should the Tarnished ignore his warnings and release the killer from his cell in the Subterranean Shunning-Grounds , Boggart will become his latest victim.
WARNING: Entering Volcano Manor before speaking to Rya causes Boggart's quest and wares to become inaccessible.
Liurnia of the Lakes (Boilprawn Shack) Head north-west from the Site of Grace to find Rya the Scout inside a gazebo and talk to her. Move to north-west again from here to find Blackguard Big Boggart at the Boilprawn Shack and talk to him: select Give me the necklace and then Deal to be able to buy Rya's Necklace from him for 1000 Runes . After purchasing the item, he'll start selling an infinite supply of Boiled Prawns. Purchase at least one to progress his questline: you'll receive the "Spread Out" gesture and, in turn, Boggart's summon sign will appear at the Ruin-Strewn Precipice Overlook to aid you against Magma Wyrm Makar.
Head north-west from the Site of Grace to find Rya the Scout inside a gazebo and talk to her.
Move to north-west again from here to find Blackguard Big Boggart at the Boilprawn Shack and talk to him: select Give me the necklace and then Deal to be able to buy Rya's Necklace from him for 1000 Runes .
After purchasing the item, he'll start selling an infinite supply of Boiled Prawns. Purchase at least one to progress his questline: you'll receive the "Spread Out" gesture and, in turn, Boggart's summon sign will appear at the Ruin-Strewn Precipice Overlook to aid you against Magma Wyrm Makar.
Altus Plateau After resting at a Site of Grace in Altus Plateau, Boggart will move to the outer moat along the northern wall of Leyndell, Royal Capital in the Capital Outskirts (due west from the Capital Rampart Site of Grace). You may now purchase an infinite supply of Boiled Crabs. Exhaust Boggart's dialogue about Dung Eater. After freeing Dung Eater from his cell in the Subterranean Shunning Grounds, Boggart will have some additional dialogue.
After resting at a Site of Grace in Altus Plateau, Boggart will move to the outer moat along the northern wall of Leyndell, Royal Capital in the Capital Outskirts (due west from the Capital Rampart Site of Grace).
You may now purchase an infinite supply of Boiled Crabs.
Exhaust Boggart's dialogue about Dung Eater. After freeing Dung Eater from his cell in the Subterranean Shunning Grounds, Boggart will have some additional dialogue.
Altus Plateau After hearing Boggart's final piece of dialogue about Dung Eater, warning you that Dung Eater is lurking nearby, reload the area to find Boggart bound to a chair. Speak to him to trigger an invasion from Dung Eater. Defeat Dung Eater and return to Boggart, who is now dead. Loot his equipment, as well as the Seedbed Curse.
After hearing Boggart's final piece of dialogue about Dung Eater, warning you that Dung Eater is lurking nearby, reload the area to find Boggart bound to a chair. Speak to him to trigger an invasion from Dung Eater.
Defeat Dung Eater and return to Boggart, who is now dead. Loot his equipment, as well as the Seedbed Curse.
Blackguard, alt. Blaggard, is an old term for scoundrel.

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Yura Hunter of Bloody Fingers
I am Yura, hunter of Bloody Fingers.
- Bloody Finger Hunter Yura
Bloody Finger Hunter Yura is an NPC in Elden Ring .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Equipment 2.3 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Yura is a warrior who dresses in the garb reminiscent of that worn in the Land of Reeds . [ 1 ] He is a self-proclaimed hunter of Bloody Fingers [ 2 ] and a prior associate of Eleonora, Violet Bloody Finger [ 3 ] , whom he likely had strong feelings for. [ 4 ]
While resting by Agheel Lake in Limgrave , Yura is met by a Tarnished . Yura warns them to stay away from the lake, where the mighty dragon Agheel roosts. [ 5 ] He will however offer his aid in hunting Agheel, and if the Tarnished slays the beast, will inform them of the Church of Dragon Communion [ 6 ] and warn them about the dangers of seeking the power of dragons. [ 7 ]
When the Tarnished is ambushed by Bloody Finger Nerijus , Yura intervenes and helps them kill him. [ 8 ] Yura then makes his way to the Academy of Raya Lucaria , where he is paid back for helping the Tarnished champion by receiving aid against a Ravenmount Assassin . [ 9 ] He warns the Tarnished to avoid Eleonora, describing her as the deadliest of all Bloody Fingers. [ 10 ]
Yura manages to track her down to the Second Church of Marika in the Altus Plateau , where he engages her in combat. Yura manages to slice off one of her fingers [ 11 ] , but receives a mortal wound. As the Tarnished champion who had aided him stumbles upon the scene, Yura begs Eleonora to no longer yield to the cessblood, before dying. [ 12 ]
After his death, his body is possessed by Shabriri , who claims that Yura gave him his body. [ 13 ]
(Optional) Limgrave The following two steps can be completed in either order. His dialogue will vary depending on where he is met first. Yura can be found under a ruin near the Seaside Ruins Site of Grace, south-west of Ahgeel Lake. Exhaust his dialogue and his summon sign will appear in Agheel Lake to assist against Flying Dragon Agheel. Speak to him again after defeating Agheel for further dialogue. Enter the ravine north of Agheel Lake and head towards Murkwater Cave . Bloody Finger Nerijus will invade you. After fighting Nerijus for a while, Yura will appear and assist you. After defeating Nerijus, continue along the ravine to find Yura under a bridge. Exhaust his dialogue.
The following two steps can be completed in either order. His dialogue will vary depending on where he is met first.

Enter the ravine north of Agheel Lake and head towards Murkwater Cave . Bloody Finger Nerijus will invade you. After fighting Nerijus for a while, Yura will appear and assist you. After defeating Nerijus, continue along the ravine to find Yura under a bridge. Exhaust his dialogue.
Liurnia of the Lakes A red summon sign can be found north-east of the Raya Lucaria Main Gate. Interact with it to assist Yura in defeating Bloody Finger Ravenmount Assassin. After defeating the enemy, Yura can be found on the bridge near where the summon sign was. Speak to him to receive a Smithing Stone [5].

Shortly after, Eleonora, Violet Bloody Finger will invade. Defeat her to receive Eleonora's Poleblade and the Purifying Crystal Tear .

Mountaintops of the Giants Once you travel to the Mountaintops of the Giants, Yura's quest will be locked regardless of which steps have been completed. Shabriri will be waiting near the Zamor Ruins Site of Grace, possessing Yura's body. The Nagakiba can be found on the ground in whichever location he would have been at depending on how far into his questline you have progressed.

Shabriri will be waiting near the Zamor Ruins Site of Grace, possessing Yura's body. The Nagakiba can be found on the ground in whichever location he would have been at depending on how far into his questline you have progressed.
Curiously, even though his Nagakiba is the source of the Ash of War: Piercing Fang he himself doesn't use the associated skill, instead using the Skill: Impaling Thrust .

name: Eleonora Violet Bloody Finger
Eleonora, Violet Bloody Finger is an NPC invader in Elden Ring .
4 Questline Progression
Hailing from the Land of Reeds [ 1 ] , Eleonora is the deadliest Bloody Finger [ 2 ] and a partaker of Dragon Communion [ 3 ] . She's a proud swordstress who felled many old hands [ 1 ] , but now her legacy has been tainted by corrupted blood [ 1 ] . Yura has been tracking Eleonora for a long time hoping of cleansing the knight of her cessblood. [ 2 ]
Altus Plateau (Altus Highway Junction)
Invades inside the Second Church of Marika , north-west of the Site of Grace .
Altus Plateau (Altus Highway Junction) After helping Yura defeat Bloody Finger Ravenmount Assassin at the Main Academy Gate of Raya Lucaria , you'll find him moribund inside the Second Church of Marika; exhausting his dialogues, he'll die leaving behind his Nagakiba (with the Ash of War: Piercing Fang ). Shortly after, Eleonora will invade you: defeating her will award you with her Poleblade and the Purifying Crystal Tear that counters Mohg, Lord of Blood 's phase transitioning curse-attack.
After helping Yura defeat Bloody Finger Ravenmount Assassin at the Main Academy Gate of Raya Lucaria , you'll find him moribund inside the Second Church of Marika; exhausting his dialogues, he'll die leaving behind his Nagakiba (with the Ash of War: Piercing Fang ). Shortly after, Eleonora will invade you: defeating her will award you with her Poleblade and the Purifying Crystal Tear that counters Mohg, Lord of Blood 's phase transitioning curse-attack.
If the player enters the church before Yura moves inside, instead of Eleonora, they'll find a Sanguine Noble .
Eleonora will invade regardless at her usual location if the Grand Lift of Rold is reached before Yura advances to the Second Church of Marika. Encountering Eleonora this way will end Yura's questline prematurely resulting in his death, which in turn allows Shabriri to appear by Zamor Ruins Site of Grace. The Black Blade Kindred guarding the Grand Lift of Rold does not need to be defeated for Eleonora to invade, getting to and beyond the Grand Lift of Rold suffices.

area: Leyndell Royal Capital
chapter: 15
name: Mohg the Omen
Omen Illusion by Shoi Miyazawa
Mohg, Lord of Blood:
Mohg, Lord of Blood by Shoi Miyazawa
Welcome, honoured guest. To the birthplace of our dynasty!
- Mohg, Lord of Blood
Mohg, Lord of Blood and Mohg, the Omen is a boss in ELDEN RING (2022) . He is a Demigod and a Shardbearer who is encountered in the Subterranean Shunning-Grounds and Mohgwyn Palace .
Mohg or Mohgwyn, also known as the Lord of Blood, was born of the Golden Lineage, the offspring of the first Elden Lord, Godfrey and Queen Marika , and was the twin brother of Morgott . [ 1 ] The twins were both born as accursed Omen , and as a result were confined and shackled to the Subterranean Shunning-Grounds below Leyndell . [ 2 ] [ 3 ]
Unlike his brother, Mohg would grow to embrace his Omen blood. Deep underground, Mohg made contact with an Outer God known as the Formless Mother . The Formless Mother ignited his accursed blood with fire, prompting him to develop powerful bloodflame magic. [ 4 ]
After Queen Marika shattered the Elden Ring , Mohg escaped confinement and claimed one of the shards of the Elden Ring. During this period, he began establishing his domain from Mohgwyn Palace and recruiting followers, including his Sanguine Nobles [ 5 ] and a band of Tarnished known as the Bloody Fingers. Among these followers was Varré , a war surgeon who was able to successfully tame Mohg's accursed blood. [ 6 ]
The specific timing of Mohg assuming the title of Lord of Blood and the exact sequence of his activities relative to the Shattering are not well-documented, making the precise timeline of these events unclear.
Miquella the Unalloyed later embedded himself within his Haligtree in an attempt to shed his affliction. In this vulnerable and unguarded state, Mohg seized the opportunity to abduct him from the tree. [ 7 ] Miquella was an Empyrean , thus having the potential of ascending to godhood; Mohg intended to raise Miquella to full godhood so that he may rule at his side as consort and take the role of monarch. [ 8 ] At some point during or after the abduction, Mohg was unknowingly enchanted by Miquella. The circumstances and timeline of this enchantment remain unclear.
After abducting Miquella, Mohg hibernated with him within a cocoon and had his followers render offerings of blood, drenching its chamber in order to facilitate Miquella's awakening. [ 9 ] But no matter how much of Miquella's bloody bedchamber Mohg tried to share, he would receive no response from the young empyrean. [ 8 ]
During this time, Mohg largely escaped the notice of the Roundtable Hold , who were searching for clues as to his whereabouts. [ 10 ] Eventually, a Tarnished came to learn the location of Mohg's palace, where he had hidden alongside the stolen Miquella. When the Tarnished stumbles upon his chambers, Mohg emerges from a pool of blood, holds Miquella's hand and apologizes for leaving him, ready to confront their "guests". Despite unleashing the full power of his accursed blood, Mohg is unable to overcome the Tarnished, falling at their hands.
Following Mohg's death, his corpse was stolen and transported to the Land of Shadow where Miquella utilized it for an ancient ritual . At the Gate of Divinity , Miquella used Mohg's body as a vessel to revive the soul of Radahn , his promised consort, to honor their solemn vow.
Mohg, the Omen is encountered in the Subterranean Shunning-Grounds, in a chapel beyond the Forsaken Depths Site of Grace. He must be defeated in order to access the Frenzied Flame Proscription.
Dung Eater is available as a summon for this fight, provided he has been released from his cell.
Mohg's trident attacks have long delays, and cover a wide range. He uses a number of bloodflame-based attacks, which deal blood loss as well as fire damage. Mohg's Shackle can be used to stun him for a few seconds, allowing for some additional hits.
Mohg, Lord of Blood is encountered at the end of Mohgwyn Palace. This fight plays much like the Mohg, the Omen fight, until the second phase when his moveset is expanded. He must be defeated to unlock the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion, and enter the Land of Shadow.
Phase 1 is largely the same as the fight against Mohg, the Omen. The key difference is the introduction of a countdown. When his health drops to certain levels, he will chrust his spear into the air and chant, causing a red ring to appear around the player. He will do this three times, and once his health reaches 50% he will perform Bloodboon Ritual, dealing huge amounts of damage for each ring and inflicting blood loss, while simultaneously recovering his own health. The Purifying Crystal Tear can be added to the Flask of Wondrous Physick to significantly decrease the amount of damage dealt by this move.
After performing Bloodboon Ritual and recovering much of his health, Mohg will sprout wings and his spear will be imbued with bloodflame. The majority of his attacks will spray bloodflame on the ground, leaving patches of fire that last for a long time. He also now has abilities that utilise his wings to get out of range or to cover distance easily.
Upon release, Mohg, Lord of Blood could easily be defeated by jumping into the arena from the side instead of entering through the fog wall. This would prevent his cutscene from playing, leaving him completely immobile and unable to attack. This was patched in version 1.03.3 .
Mohg carrying the slumbering Miquella

name: Bloody Finger Okina
Bloody Finger Okina is an Invading NPC Phantom in Elden Ring . He wields the Rivers of Blood , a unique Katana , as well as the Wakizashi in his off hand. Okina can also be available as an NPC Summon against Starscourge Radahn . He will use different moves as a summon.
The more he sharpened his mind, the more the absurdities of the world came into stark relief. And so Okina renounced it all, and rose to a higher plane. There was only himself. His katana. And its mastery. Before long, Okina became a demon of a swordsman.
Where to find Bloody Finger Okina :
Bloody Finger Okina spawns near the Church of Repose at Mountaintops of the Giants . [ Elden Ring Map here ]
Furlcalling Finger Remedy
Wields the Rivers of Blood on his right hand, and the Wakizashi on his left. His armor is the White Reed Set with his signature Mask . His attacks apply Hemorrhage when struck.
He appears to unable to heal with a Crimson Flask, unlike most Invaders. (Needs further testing)
Bloody Finger Okina will use the Corpse Piler skill tied to the Rivers of Blood to slash at the player with huge slashes with additional range.
Prior to patch 1.04 this invader would not spawn if boss of the area, Fire Giant, has been killed. This issue can be fixed by updating to patch 1.04.
According to Bloody Finger Hunter Yura , Bloody Fingers are " Tarnished held in thrall by cessblood ", implying this character is a Tarnished.
He is the former master of the Inaba , who were warriors that wished for death in combat.
A wooden mask that depicts an old man baring his teeth. The source of the sobriquet of the great swordman of the Land of Reeds. Raises dexterity as it lowers focus.
The more he sharpened his mind, the more the absurdities of the world came into stark relief. And so Okina renounced it all, and rose to a higher plane. There was only himself. His katana. And its mastery.
Before long, Okina became a demon of a swordsman.
Weapon of Okina, swordsman from the Land of Reeds. A cursed weapon that has felled countless men.
When Mohg, the Lord of Blood, first felt Okina's sword, and madness, upon his flesh, he had a proposal, to offer Okina the life of a demon, whose thirst would never go unsated.
Short sword with a tempering pattern on the blade. A weapon unique to warriors of the Land of Reeds. Afflicts targets with blood loss.
These swords are considered auxiliary weapons. Yet, one man is known to wield this weapon in his left hand while using a full-sized katana in his right.
A demonic swordsman known only as Okina.
Armor of the White Reeds, fashioned from banded iron plates. Worn by the Inaba, head disciples of the great swordsman Okina.
The Inaba traveled in pursuit of not only the master who abandoned them, but also proper deaths, won honorably through hard-fought combat. To be a White Reed is to seek death; fitting for the Inaba, who desired nothing more than to die by the sword.
Helm made from strips of iron fastened together. Worn by warriors of the Land of Reeds.
The Land of Reeds has long been locked in a miserable civil war, during which time it has remained alienated from the cultures of its neighbors. Little wonder that the entire nation has succumbed to blood-soaked madness, or so it is said.
This Ash of War grants an armament the Blood and the following skill:
"Seppuku: A forbidden technique used by swordsmen from the Land of Reeds. Plunge the blade into your stomach to stain it with blood. Increases attack power and improves ability to inflict blood loss."
Usable on swords as well as polearms capable of thrusting (small and colossal weapons not included).
Bloody Finger Okina (血の指、翁) is an NPC cooperator and invader in Elden Ring .
3 Character Information
Okina (meaning "Old Man" in Japanese) is a great swordsman from the Land of Reeds whose nickname came from the wooden mask that depicts an old man baring his teeth. [ 1 ] He had several disciples in a group called the White Reeds, combatants who seek  proper deaths won honorably through hard-fought combat. The head disciples of Okina were called the Inaba (Japanese name for the leaves of the rice plant). [ 2 ]
As time passed, the more Okina sharpened his mind, the more the absurdities of the world came into stark relief. And so he renounced it all, and rose to a higher plane. There was only himself. His katana. And its mastery. [ 3 ] This eventually led him to Mohg, Lord of Blood , and, when Mohg first felt Okina's sword, and madness, upon his flesh, he had a proposal: to offer Okina the life of a demon, whose thirst would never go unsated. [ 4 ]
Before long, Okina became a demon of a swordsman, [ 5 ] known as the only to wield an auxiliary weapon in his left hand while using a full-sized cursed katana in his right. [ 6 ]
The Inaba traveled to the Lands Between in pursuit of the master who abandoned them and, desiring nothing more than to die by the sword, also sought proper deaths. [ 7 ] And so, all that was known of them was that their armor and spirit were left lingering in the Spiritcaller Cave .
Caelid (Chamber Outside the Plaza)
His summon sign is found within Starscourge Radahn 's arena.
Mountaintops of the Giants ( Church of Repose )
Invades at the entrance.
Okina spawning at the Church of Repose

area: Liurnia of the Lakes
chapter: 4
name: Latenna the Albinauric
Latenna the Albinauric is a Spirit and Summon in Elden Ring . Latenna the Albinauric is a ranged support unit that deals magic damage with a bow. She acts as a "turret" as she is not able to move from the location she is summoned, unless near an enemy Direwolf which she has the ability to use as a mount. She is able to cast Freezing Mist while mounted, similar to the Albinauric Wolfback Archer enemies encountered in the Consecrated Snowfield .
Latenna the Albinauric can also be found as an NPC in Elden Ring . For more information, see Latenna .
NOTE: Patch 1.13 Will no longer stagger as easily. Increased HP, physical attack power, and magic attack power.
Ashen remains in which spirits yet dwell. Use to summon the spirit of Latenna the Albinauric.
A rare case of one who chose to become a spirit voluntarily.
Latenna was renowned as a deft magic archer. But having lost her beloved wolf companion, Lobo, she cannot move from the place where she was summoned.
Where to Find Latenna the Albinauric
Liurnia of the Lakes, Slumbering Wolf's Shack , found at the end of Lakeside Crystal Cave upon defeating the boss.
In order for Latenna to be friendly and become a spirit, one has to have the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right) , given to the player by Albus at the Village of the Albinaurics . [ Map Link ]
Alternatively, Latenna may be found dead if one progressed too far and missed out her quest. For this instance, one can still obtain her spirit ashes at the same location by simply picking up the item, but her quest would no longer be doable.
Upgrades with Ghost Gloveworts .
Consumes Medium FP (74)
You must be near a rebirth monument to summon spirits from ashes.
When you are close to a rebirth monument and able to summon, a monument icon will appear on the left side of the screen.
When she finds a target, she shoots three magical arrows at a time, repeatedly and can shoot them very far away.
Ideally, you should find a sniping perch or vantage point to plop her down on and let her provide sniper fire as you clear the area.
Though mostly stationary, she does have a tiny amount of mobility. She can inch her way around in response to being attacked, though in rare circumstances she will decide to shuffle around on her own accord. This can make her fall off of a vantage point you've summoned her to.
Her ability to mount Direwolves is likely an oversight from sharing the same AI as the Albinauric Archers, as she tends to also shoot the wolf due to it still counting as a hostile enemy.
Because she stays in the same spot she was summoned and has one of the longest ranges in the game (probably second only to the archer golem ), she is very useful in boss fights where the arena is large enough. Latenna will constantly deal magic damage to the boss while you fight normally, as the boss's aggro cannot be changed to Latenna due to her low HP. Just make sure to summon her in a spot where she is not the boss's target, and you must constantly deal damage to the boss so that its aggro does not change to Latenna.
She is labeled situational, mostly good against dragons or in arenas that have elevation. Note: She is not tanky, therefore not good against hyper aggressive enemies.
Can be useful if one uses Shabriri's Woe in situations where elevation or an otherwise safe spot isn't present. Keep in mind that you will have to stay aggressive towards the boss in order to prevent her from drawing aggro, however.
I'm Latenna. An Albinauric, the same as Old Albus. My apologies for my coarse words earlier. I presumed the worst, seeing that you were another Tarnished, like that all-hearing brute.
Latenna the Albinauric is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Character Information 2.1 Basic Info 2.2 Quest Items
3 Questline Progression
Latenna is an Albinauric woman and a deft archer. Latenna and her wolf companion Lobo began their journey in the lands of the Haligtree , a sanctuary for Albinaurics. [ 1 ] She journeyed to the far south, eventually coming to Liurnia of the Lakes .
Here, Latenna was accosted by the servants of Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing , [ 2 ] a Tarnished wishing to gain access to the hidden lands of the Haligtree. Believing Latenna had a secret medallion that opened the way to the Haligtree, [ 3 ] Ofnir's servants attacked her and killed her wolf, leaving her crippled, but Latenna still refused to divulge the location of the medallion, feigning ignorance.
After the Tarnished receives a half of the secret medallion from the elderly Albinauric Albus , Latenna tells them the location of the other half, [ 4 ] and asks them to bring her along to the lands of the Haligtree, where she might fulfill her duty. [ 5 ] The Tarnished agrees, and Latenna volontarily becomes Spirit Ash , [ 6 ] so they might carry her with them.
In the Mountaintops of the Giants , Latenna alerts the Tarnished that the medallion is near when they approach Castle Sol . [ 7 ] With the second half retrieved, the path to the Haligtree is opened. She urges them to bring her to the liturgical town of Ordina . [ 8 ] Here, a towering young Albinauric known as Phillia slumbers. [ 9 ] Latenna grants her the birthing droplet, allowing her to create new life for all the Albinaurics. [ 10 ] She thanks the Tarnished for their aid, promising to fight alongside them until the bitter end, requesting that they lay her to rest by Lobo once the fighting is done. [ 11 ]
Liurnia of the Lakes (Slumbering Wolf's Shack) Latenna can be found at the Slumbering Wolf's Shack, accessible by clearing the Lakeside Crystal Cave . If you have the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right), obtained from Old Albus in the Village of the Albinaurics , she will die and you will be able to pick up Latenna the Albinauric (Spirit Ashes). If you have already obtained Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) from Castle Sol, Latenna will already be dead, and you can pick up her Spirit Ashes.
Latenna can be found at the Slumbering Wolf's Shack, accessible by clearing the Lakeside Crystal Cave . If you have the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right), obtained from Old Albus in the Village of the Albinaurics , she will die and you will be able to pick up Latenna the Albinauric (Spirit Ashes).
If you have already obtained Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) from Castle Sol, Latenna will already be dead, and you can pick up her Spirit Ashes.
Mountaintops of the Giants (Castle Sol) After defeating Commander Niall , continue past his arena and pick up the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left).
After defeating Commander Niall , continue past his arena and pick up the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left).
Consecrated Snowfields ( Apostate Derelict ) Travel to the Apostate Derelict in the northernmost part of the Consecrated Snowfields and approach the giant Albinauric woman to summon Latenna. Exhaust Latenna's dialogue to obtain a Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone.
Travel to the Apostate Derelict in the northernmost part of the Consecrated Snowfields and approach the giant Albinauric woman to summon Latenna. Exhaust Latenna's dialogue to obtain a Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone.

area: Liurnia of the Lakes
chapter: 4
name: Albus
Albus (aka Old Albus) is an NPC in Elden Ring . Albus is an Albinauric who is trying to hide from the calamity that befell Village of the Albinaurics in Liurnia of the Lakes. He has an important item that he will entrust to you.
Please, no. Dear me! I haven't a clue. No secrets lie with me, not a one.
Albus can be found disguised as a pot by following the path up from the Village of the Albinaurics site of grace in western Liurnia of the Lakes . You must hit the pot located past the Depraved Perfumer to get him to show up. Then talk to him twice. Map Link
This NPC does not move
This NPC can be fought
If defeated, drops: Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right)
This is an extremely important NPC for a few questlines and end game locations. After you reveal his true form, talk to him again to trigger a second dialogue and obtain the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right) . This item will create a pair with Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) found in Mountaintops of the Giants (see Castle Sol page) and give you access to an entire secret area in the northern lands. He will perish on his own after a few dialogue exchanges.
Please, no. Dear me!. I haven't a clue. No secrets lie with me, not a one. Oh please, leave me be.
Wait, then, you're not one of them? Well, what a relief... Oh, goodness me.
I am Albus. An Albinauric, as you can see. We are finished. The whole village is finished. The cursemongers have destroyed everything. Not one that remains has their wits about them
I beg you. Would you look after this medallion?
You must keep it out of the cursemonger's hands.
And if you should meet the young Albinauric Latenna, then please, give it to her.
A chosen land awaits us Albinaurics. The medallion is the key that leads to the city. It's only a quaint treasure, for we who cannot make the journey. But for dear Latenna, it is needed. To fulfill her purpose.
My legs will soon fade, and with them, my life. Alas, this is the immovable fate of all Albinaurics...
We are Albinaurics. We have caused you no harm!
(On player kill) I will never give this up. Not to your kind.
You merciless brutes. Let the curse take everything.
Required to complete the questline for Latenna .
Required to reach a few end-game locations after Mountaintops of the Giants .
I am Albus. An Albinauric, as you can see. We're finished. The whole village is finished.
Old Albus is an NPC in ELDEN RING (2022) .
2 Questline Progression
3 Character Information 3.1 Quest Items
An elderly resident of the Village of the Albinaurics , Albus was one of the few survivors of the massacre of the village at the hands of Gideon's men, and the only one to retain his sanity. [ 1 ] He was in possession of the right half of a medallion , which would grant access to Miquella's Haligtree if united with the left half, and in order to protect it he disguised himself as a pot.
The Tarnished encounters him in the village, breaking the illusion to reveal his true Albinauric form. After ascertaining that the Tarnished was not among the cursemongers, Albus bestows his half of the medallion to them, beseeching them to give it to an Albinauric woman named Latenna [ 2 ] , claiming that she would need it to fulfil her purpose of seeking out the Albinaurics' chosen land. [ 3 ] With this done, he fades away, meeting the fate faced by all Albinaurics. [ 4 ]
Village of the Albinaurics Albus can be found, disguised as a pot, at the top of the slope past the Village of the Albinaurics Site of Grace, near the Depraved Perfumer . Roll or attack the pot to break the illusion and reveal Albus. Exhaust his dialogue to obtain the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right), which can be shown to Latenna the Albinauric at the Slumbering Wolf's Shack in order to progress her questline.
Albus can be found, disguised as a pot, at the top of the slope past the Village of the Albinaurics Site of Grace, near the Depraved Perfumer . Roll or attack the pot to break the illusion and reveal Albus. Exhaust his dialogue to obtain the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right), which can be shown to Latenna the Albinauric at the Slumbering Wolf's Shack in order to progress her questline.

area: Limgrave
chapter: 1
name: Deathtouched Tarnished
Deathtouched Catacombs is a Location in Elden Ring . The Deathtouched Catacombs is found in Stormhill , Limgrave . It can be reached by heading west from Saintsbridge.
[ Elden Ring Map here ]
x5 Grave Glovewort (1)
Assassin's Crimson Dagger
Black Knife Assassin
At the entrance of the catacombs, you will find a tormented spirit. Open the wooden door, and enter. There is a Site of Grace immediately after. Head down and turn to the left, prepare to fight some Skeletons. Some are melee combatants, while others are ranged ones. Remember that skeletons need to be “killed again” once they are down to be put to rest, if not, they will keep returning to life. You will find 2 Grave Glovewort (1) in the first big room.
Keep moving further down, there are more skeletons in the next room. Beware of the Ranged ones that are on the platform as you turn around., get to safety quickly. There is a Grave Glovewort (1) to the right side of the room.
There is a body against a wall that contains Bloodrose in the next room. Also in that room, you will find a statue with a lever, activate it. Retrace your steps, and as you reach the stairs when you fought the first skeletons. Drop down and enter a narrow corridor, now open. More skeletons await, as well as Grave Violet and Grave Glovewort (1) . Kill them and take the left exit, you will reach the elevated platform from where the ranged skeletons were shooting you previously. Deal with them. At the end of the platform you will find another Grave Glovewort (1) and a Uchigatana on a hanging body.
Get back to the beginning of the Catacombs, to the room after the Site of Grace. You will notice that the big door is now open. Enter to fight the Black Knife Assassin . Once you have dealt with her, you will obtain an Assassin's Crimson Dagger and the dungeon will be completed. Before leaving, don't forget to loot the chest to obtain a Deathroot .
[other images go here]
Deathtouched Catacombs (死に触れた地下墓 ( Shi ni Fureta Chika Haka ) is a dungeon in ELDEN RING (2022) .
6 Crafting Materials
Deathtouched Catacombs is located to the west of Saintsbridge in the northern part of Stormhill . It is currently inhabited by skeletons, the remains of champions who currently live in death. A Black Knife Assassin hides within the farthest chamber of the catacombs, alongside a Deathroot.
Assassin's Crimson Dagger (obtained from Black Knife Assassin)

---

area: Liurnia of the Lakes
chapter: 4
name: Carian Royal Family
The Carian Royal Family is a powerful magic-based faction in Elden Ring . Founded by Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon , the Carian Royals lead the Raya Lucaria Academy and their study of glintstone sorceries . Under their employ are select servants, soldiers, preceptors and a group of renowned knights. See Carian Sorceries for the related spell class.
The fate of the Carian royal family is guided by the stars. - War Counselor Iji
Queen Rennala was once an astrologer who studied the stars. She then came into contact with the full moon, and learned powerful magic that can absorb other spells, and this allowed her to eventually lead the Raya Lucaria Academy . She then founded the Carian royal house, with an order of sworn knights to protect it and servants such as preceptors to help guide it. Included in her ranks were trolls who wielded magic and were also treated as knights . At an indeterminate point, the First Liurnian War broke out between Raya Lucaria and the Golden Order of Leyndell, Royal Capital . As a Sword Monument reads, Radagon of the Golden Order took part in this conflict. After the Second Liurnian War, from which no clear victor arose, atonement mended the relationship between Rennala and Radagon and the two were wed at the Church of Vows , which brought an end to the conflict between Leyndell and Raya Lucaria. Rennala was not universally accepted at the academy, as many disliked seeing the moon as equal to the stars. According to the Lazuli Robe , those outside the Lazuli Conspectus saw this robe in the hue of the full moon as a signifier of their heresy. Though being responsible for forbidding the study of the Primeval Current , her use of the spell Comet Azur likely implies Rennala had some connection to it.
Lord Radagon was an enigmatic figure in the Lands Between. He despised his red locks for which he was known for, and which could have been a curse from the long since defeated Fire Giants . Radagon first gained recognition in the Liurnian Wars, and later used Celestial Dew to reconcile with the Carian Queen Rennala of the Full Moon. After their reconciliation, Radagon wed Rennala and received a greatsword as a gift from her. Together they had three demigod children, Rykard, Radahn, and Ranni. When Radagon married Rennala, he ordered the Carian preceptors to don the mask of confidence . To make it clear that all of their matters were to be kept strictly private. When Godfrey, First Elden Lord defeated his last worthy foe, he was divested of grace and banished from the land along with his tarnished army. Radagon then left Rennala to return to Leyndell and become the second Elden Lord with Queen Marika . Before departing he left Rennala with an amber egg and his loyal red wolf . His children with Rennala became demigod stepchildren, and he had the Twin Prodigies Miquella and Malenia with Queen Marika. As the husband of Rennala of Caria, the red-haired Radagon studied sorcery , and as the husband of Queen Marika, he studied incantations . Thus did the hero aspire to be complete. To symbolize the tenets of the Golden Order, Radagon reforged the greatsword given to him by Rennala into a greatsword of light modeled after the Elden Ring.
Rellana is the sister of Queen Rennala, and a Carian princess by birthright. They were close since childhood, and Rellana learned powerful sorcery from Count Ymir . She was also a formidable fighter who at one point used the Carian Thrusting Shield in ritual combat to show fidelity to the Erdtree. Those that followed Messmer's crusade against the Belurat Tower , even knights hailing from renowned families of the Erdtree's upper echelons, were shunned and chased from their homes after pledging allegiance to Messmer. Among those that chased after Messmer included Rellana, who disavowed her birthright and chose to stand at Messmer's side instead. After some time she became known as the Sword of Messmer and received a straight sword engraved with golden flame. Carian knights who served as direct retainers to Carian princesses such as Rellana received a Carian Filigreed Crest . Then there were those who left their homelands to serve Rellana, recounted by the Rellana's Cameo talisman; " By your leave, we will accompany you wherever your lunar vessel takes you ." Before Rellana departed, Rennala was said to have given her younger sister a gift of lustrous black hair.
Rykard is the son of Queen Rennala and Radagon, and was adept at sorceries just like his mother. After discovering the ancient hexes of Mt. Gelmir , Rykard brought them back into practical use as new forms of sorcery. At some point Rykard struck out on his own and raised his own banners, becoming the figurehead of the Volcano Manor . This could be because Caria is a matriarchy, and according to War Counselor Iji, Lady Ranni is the first heir in the Carian royal line. Rykard and his inquisitors committed numerous atrocities so that he could wage war with Leyndell. At some point he met Lady Tanith , a dancer from a foreign land, who became his consort at Volcano Manor. During the Shattering Rykard bore a Great Rune , but fed it and himself to an ancient serpent. After this, the birth of the man-serpents soon followed. Rykard was rewarded with a tool after the Night of the Black Knives, which would allow him to challenge Maliketh, the Black Blade . Rykard would gather champions to the Volcano Manor to become recusants and hunt down the tarnished and rebel against the forces of the Erdtree. According to the Taker's Cameo , when Rykard turned to heresy, taking by force became the rule. To him the gods themselves were no different, after all.
Radahn is the son of Queen Rennala and Radagon, and has flowing red hair just like his father. Radahn was fond of his heritage, letting his hair poke out behind his helmet and taking the Redmane moniker for himself. According to Radahn; " I was born a champion's cub. Now I am the Lord of the Battlefield's lion. " Inspired by heroes of the battlefield such as Godfrey, Serosh, and his father, he modeled his armor and likeness after them. At some point Radahn struck out on his own and raised his own banners, leading the Redmanes. This could be because Caria is a matriarchy, and according to War Counselor Iji, Lady Ranni is the first heir in the Carian royal line. Radahn learnt powerful gravity sorceries from an Alabaster Lord in Sellia, Town of Sorcery so that he could challenge the stars and continue to ride beloved but scrawny steed. After succeeding in locking the stars into place, he became known as the Starscourge. During the Shattering Radahn bore a Great Rune and took part in many conflicts, ultimately fighting against Malenia in one final confrontation in Caelid . Now mindless from scarlet rot, he still roams the battlefield.
Ranni is the self-described daughter of Queen Rennala and Radagon, and a princess of Caria. Ranni was an Empyrean chosen by the Two Fingers, and as such was a potential candidate to succeed Queen Marika and usher in a new age. Due to this she received Blaidd as a shadow-bound beast who would protect her, and along with Iji they have been together since childhood. Rennala imparted onto her powerful sorceries including her dark moon, which was veiled in occult mystery. Her true mentor though was a mysterious snow witch who taught her cold sorcery . Ranni came to despise her eventual fate of being controlled by the Two Fingers, and endeavored to free herself with the aid of her companions. She is the one predominately responsible for the Night of the Black Knives, stealing a fragment of the Rune of Death from Malekith and imbuing it onto a series of daggers , after which a group of assassins used them to kill Godwyn the Golden in spirit while Ranni herself discarded her flesh. According to the Cursemark of Death , two demigods perished at the same time, breaking the cursemark into two half-wheels. Ranni was the first of the demigods whose flesh perished, while the Prince of Death perished in soul alone. Ranni then possessed a doll modeled after her mentor and returned to the Carian Manor where she had grown up, and continues to work in secret.
The Carian Knights were a group of magic-wielding knights sworn to the Carian Royal Family. According to the Carian Knight's Sword , these knights' swords could serve as catalysts, letting them wield sorcerous battle skills. Despite numbering fewer than twenty, this power made them a match for even the champions of gold in battle. Called into service when the Queen invoked an oath they swore, even trolls were in the ranks of the knights of Caria, and fought arm in arm with their human comrades. The enchanted knights, anointed by the Lunar Queen, were heroes of the highest honors, but fell into disarray with the decline of the royal family. The Raya Lucaria Academy turned on the Carians with the aid of the Cuckoo Knights , and laid an unsuccessful siege on the Carian Manor. The Carians however knew of the academy's disloyalty, and developed the Carian Retaliation spell as a countermeasure. The moon and stars would one day go their separate ways. Notable members of the knights included Moongrum who guards Queen Rennala, Bols who was captured by the Cuckoos, Moonrithyll who was the Twin Moon Knight's chamberlain, and Royal Knight Loretta who was a former personal guard. Loretta left the Carian royals to seek out a place where the Albinaurics could live in peace, eventually finding the secretive Haligtree .
Jerren is an old knight who once preferred a nomadic existence, but after spending time as a guest of the Carian royals, he became a guest commander for General Radahn. And for the first time, the restless tumbleweed would be bound by an honorable oath. This oath would be one of honorable death between him and Radahn while Jerren was in his service. At the end of the Shattering, when Malenia and Radahn battled in Caelid leading to a Scarlet Aeonia that devastated the land, General Radahn who was known as the strongest of the demigods did not perish there on the battlefield. Instead he was infected by the scarlet rot , becoming more animalistic and as Jerren describes, his wits are long gone. Wishing to give his old friend a proper death, Jerren plans to host a grand festival at Castle Redmane , gathering all the champions from across the land for a chance to slay Radahn and lay claim to his Great Rune. At the castle scarlet rot is kept at bay with flame, and as such, Jerren's beloved sword featuring a flame-like undulation eventually became the symbol of the castle itself. Jerren is also known as the Witch-Hunter, and Iji recounts that he made an old promise to hunt down the one known as the Graven Witch , that they may finally be rid of a longstanding Carian weed.
Blaidd is the stepbrother and sworn protector of Ranni, and possesses a large wolf-like appearance. He was gifted to Ranni by the Two Fingers, and according to Iji earned the approval of Queen Rennala. According to the old legends, wolves are the shadows of Empyrean which could explain some of Blaidd's features. Once there was an assassin who assumed the guise of Ranni the Witch's loyal shadow using a black wolf mask . In defiance of the fate he was born to, Blaidd swore to serve no master but Ranni. As proof, the sword he received was imbued with a cold magic at the moment the oath was sworn. Bothered by the cold, Blaidd wears a pelt fashioned as a cape. Iji warns that if Lady Ranni as an Empyrean continues to resist being an instrument of the Two Fingers, her shadow Blaidd will go mad, transforming from a follower into a horrid curse. But such is his destiny, and Blaidd would defy destiny itself if it would have him turn upon his Lady. Iji, who had long served the Carian Royals as a blacksmith, became Ranni's war counselor when she decided to turn against the Two Fingers. He wears a mirrorhelm to protect against the Fingers' language of light as he is terribly frightened by his own treachery. It seems fear guides some of his actions, as he later imprisons Blaidd. He reflects on this mistake, hoping to meet Blaidd again and give him an apology.
Seluvis is a preceptor of the Carian Royals, similar to Preceptor Miriam in the Carian Study Hall . He is a teacher of the sorcerous arts and aids Ranni along with Blaidd and Iji, though Blaidd is not very fond of him. The movements of the stars are drawn inside the brim of his hat and on his gown, and he has a particular obsession with Starlight Shards . He can begrudgingly help find the pathway that leads to the Eternal City of Nokron through his connection to the Sorceress Sellen. Notably, one of his puppets Therolina also takes part in the festival at Castle Redmane to defeat General Radahn. Seluvis's secret lair can be discovered not far from Ranni's Rise , where he keeps a collection of puppets. By using a special potion Seluvis is able to fashion a puppet out of the person who drank the potion, which also leads to the death of said person. One of his victims includes Dolores the Sleeping Arrow , who once belonged to the Roundtable Hold , where she was both a critic and a friend of Gideon the All-Knowing . It was because of her that he and Seluvis went their separate ways. Gideon holds a deep distain for Seluvis, referring to him as a twisted dolly botherer. Whether by coincidence, Seluvis has also taken an interest in Nepheli Loux who also has a connection to Sir Gideon. Pidia is a servant of the Carian Royal Family and resides at the Carian Manor. He is a first-generation albinauric , and as such has gray skin and immobile legs. Albinaurics are lifeforms made by human hands. Thus, many believe them to live impure lives, untouched by the Erdtree's grace. He and Seluvis seem to work together since Pidia looks after some puppets and possesses a map that is part of Seluvis's plan. His plan involves betraying Princess Ranni, but Ranni may know this as she says " heed not their peculiarities; feel secure in gaining from them what advantage thou canst. I am sure the others will be doing just the same. " After retrieving the Fingerslayer Blade for Ranni, Seluvis is found dead and Pidia is attacked by his own puppets. It could be inferred that Pidia puppeteered Seluvis from the start, and after their usefulness came to an end Ranni had them both killed.
A talisman engraved with the legend of a queen. Raises intelligence.
The young astrologer gazed at the night sky as she walked. She had always chased the stars every step of her journey. Then she met the full moon — and, in time, the astrologer became a queen .
Astrology tool used by members of the Carian royal family. A stolen part of a larger instrument.
Allows the viewer to better see faraway things.
During the age of the Erdtree, Carian astrology withered on the vine. The fate once writ in the night skies had been fettered by the Golden Order.
A tall crown depicting the moon. Worn by Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon. Increases intelligence.
When Rennala, head of both the Academy of Raya Lucaria and the Carian royal family, lost her husband Radagon, her heart went along with him.
And then, those at the academy realized. That Rennala was no champion, after all.
Greatsword made of light, modeled after the Elden Ring itself. Forged by King Consort Radagon to proudly symbolize the tenets of the Golden Order. One of the legendary armaments.
Telltale signs betray that this was once the greatsword bequeathed to him by his first wife, Rennala.
Ornate helm of Rellana, the Twin Moon Knight, fashioned from silver steel. Rennala, head of the royal family of Caria, was said to have given her younger sister, who renounced her lineage to chase after Messmer, a gift of lustrous black hair.
A Great Rune of the shardbearer Rykard. Its blessing restores HP on attacking enemies.
Rykard was amongst the children of Rennala and Radagon, who became demigod stepchildren after Radagon's union with Queen Marika.
But Rykard fed himself to the blasphemous serpent, Great Rune and all.
Helm of the golden lion, with flowing red hair. Worn by General Radahn.
Radahn inherited the furious, flaming red hair of his father Radagon, and is fond of its heroic implications.
"I was born a champion's cub. Now I am the Lord of the Battlefield's lion."
Legendary sorcery associated with the Carian queen.
Uses the caster as a vessel to incarnate a cold, dark moon, then sends it floating toward foes. The dark moon dispels all sorcery that touches it, and temporarily reduces magic damage negation for those it strikes.
This moon was encountered by a young Ranni, led by the hand of her mother, Rennala. What she beheld was cold, dark and veiled in occult mystery.
A Moon Greatsword, bestowed by a Carian queen upon her spouse to honor long-standing tradition. One of the legendary armaments.
Ranni's sigil is a full moon, cold and leaden, and this sword is but a beam of its light.
Intricately crafted silver war sickle wielded by Loretta, Knight of the Haligtree.
Originally given for service as a personal guard to Carian royalty, the weapon's blue glintstone has been replaced with unalloyed gold.
Helm of the enchanted knights that once served the Carian royal family.
The enchanted knights, anointed by the Lunar Queen, were heroes of the highest honors, but fell into disarray with the decline of the royal family.
Unusually colorful armor. Worn by the knight Jerren.
Jerren preferred a nomadic existence, but after spending time as a guest of the Carian royals, he became a guest commander for General Radahn. And for the first time, the restless tumbleweed would be bound by honorable oath.
Greatsword decorated in royal Carian Style. Favored weapon of Blaidd the Half-Wolf.
In defiance of the fate he was born to, Blaidd swore to serve no master but Ranni. As proof the sword was imbued with a cold magic at the moment oath was sworn.
Helm fashioned from a crystal looking-glass, said to have never left War Counselor Iji's head. Easily broken and weak against striking attacks.
Worn by those committed to high treason, it wards off the intervention of the Greater Will and its vassal Fingers.
Iji was afraid. Terrified of his own treachery.
Large hat with the movements of the stars drawn on the inside of the brim. Worn by the magic preceptors who served the Carian royals.
Increases mind to the detriment of stamina.
Glintstone sorcerers are the descendants of astrologers, a fact that the Carians remain of aware of. Even if their fate has been long severed from the stars.
You wish to know more of Lady Rennala? She is Queen, head of the Carian royal family, and governor of the Academy of Raya Lucaria. The great and beautiful Full Moon Witch. Sadly, her heart was broken when Lord Radagon left her. And then, when the academy rebelled against the royals, she was locked away in the grand library.
In the end, Lady Rennala was left alone, cradling the amber egg Lord Radagon bequeathed her. Now she devotes herself to it through forbidden rite; the grim art of reincarnation. You would do well to remember... Severing a vow, strongest of bonds, has consequences ever more dire.
You wish to know more of Lord Radagon? Lord Radagon was a great champion, possessed of flowing red locks. He came to these lands at the head of a great golden host, when he met Lady Rennala in battle. He soon repented his territorial aggressions though, and became husband to the Carian Queen.
However, when Godfrey, first Elden Lord, was hounded from the Lands Between, Radagon left Rennala to return to the Erdtree Capital, becoming Queen Marika's second husband and King Consort, taking the title... of second Elden Lord. The mystery endures, to this day... As to why Lord Radagon would cast Lady Rennala aside... and moreover...why a mere champion would be chosen for the seat of Elden Lord.
Rennala is the queen of the Carian royals, who govern the academy. But Rennala herself is no demigod. Her beloved, Radagon, left her to become Queen Marika's second husband, taking the title of King Consort. The Great Rune dwells within the amber egg that was Radagon's gift to her.
General Radahn, the famed Red Lion and scourge of the stars, is a ferocious warrior. He fought Malenia and her rot to a standstill in the Caelid Wilds to Limgrave's east. And now Caelid has been engulfed by the scarlet rot, even approaching the region is no mean feat. I've heard survivors of Radahn's army are still in the wilds, staving off the rot with fire. And if it's true, I suspect Radahn is still there as well, in Caelid. Though I doubt he much resembles his former self anymore...
Praetor Rykard is the Lord of the Volcano Manor on Mt. Gelmir. He is a ruthless justiciar who commands a company of inquisitors, reviled for his serpentine demeanour. The volcano, Mt. Gelmir, lies in the west of the Altus Plateau; the realm of the Erdtree. It was the stage of the most appalling battle in the entirety of the Shattering. Rykard has commited the grave sin of blasphemy. Marking himself as an enemy, never to be forgiven.
Lunar Princess Ranni, daughter to Rennala. Ranni is said to have cast aside her Great Rune, so here at the Hold, we seek the whereabouts of the remaining three shardbearers.
This territory once belonged to the Carian royal family. Their manor lies not far beyond this point. When the Raya Lucaria Academy turned on the Carians, the Knights of the Cuckoo descended on this tract. After leveling it, they carried on to the manor. The Carians were taken off guard, but their strength had not waned, and they repelled the knights' onslaught...By conjuring an enchanted snare that remains potent to this day.
Lady Ranni has explained everything. Again, I am Iji. The Carian royal family's dedicated blacksmith, and Lady Ranni's war counsellor.
Blaidd is Lady Ranni's stepbrother. Ranni's mother, Queen Rennala, approved of him, and they played like siblings from childhood. They were always happy to have me tag along, as well. When Lady Ranni renounced her flesh, and chose the dark path of the Empyrean, Blaidd and I swore allegiance as vassals, but none of us will ever forget our earliest days together.
Jerren. Now, that's a name I haven't heard for a while. Before taking up the banner of General Radahn, he was a guest of the Carian royal family. An expert swordsman, to be sure, but ever the eccentric.
The fate of the Carian royal family is guided by the stars. As is the fate of Lady Ranni, first heir in the Carian royal line.
The Carian Royal Family was a faction in ELDEN RING (2022) .
Members of the Carian Royal Family were prolific magic users and erstwhile heads of the Academy of Raya Lucaria , who were famous for developing their own glintstone sorceries. [ 1 ] They originated from the mist-laden region in the west of the Lands Between , known as Liurnia of the Lakes .
The Carian Royal Family would reach its peak under Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon . In her youth, she was a prominent champion who would go on to charm the academy with her lunar magic, becoming its master. She also led the Glintstone Knights and established the house of Caria as royalty. [ 2 ]
The Carian Royals have always been closely associated with the western coast of Liurnia, [ 3 ] which is the location of their estate, the Caria Manor , as well as the Three Sisters region immediately behind it. Another location of importance was the Carian Study Hall , a large library in eastern Liurnia that connects to the region's Divine Tower . [ 4 ]
The Carian royals were served by magic preceptors notable for large hats with the movements of the stars drawn on the inside of the brim, [ 5 ] as well as on their bright blue gowns. [ 6 ] All glintstone sorcerers are the descendants of astrologers , a fact that the Carians remained aware of. [ 5 ] The astrologers preceded the sorcerers and had established themselves in mountaintops that nearly touched the sky, and considered the Fire Giants their neighbors. [ 7 ] They would read fate in the stars, but alas, the night sky no longer cradles fate. [ 8 ]
However, the Carian royal family were mostly known for the famous enchanted knights that served them. These enchanted knights, anointed by the Lunar Queen, were heroes of the highest honors, [ 9 ] and served as direct retainers to the kingdom's princesses. [ 10 ] Their swords were embedded with blue glintstone, and could serve as catalysts, letting them wield sorcerous battle skills. Despite numbering fewer than twenty, this power made them a match for even the champions of gold in battle. [ 11 ] They combined this art with their swordsmanship to maintain pressure upon their foes, striking in waves of steel and sorcery. [ 12 ] The Carian knights never wavered, and even had sorceries that could alleviate the buildup of sleep or madness. [ 13 ] There were also enchanted troll knights among their number, comrades of young Rennala, bound by oath. [ 14 ] They were called into service when the Queen invoked an oath they swore. These trolls were treated as true knights of Caria, and would fight arm in arm with their human comrades. [ 15 ]
The famous Loretta was once a royal Carian knight, [ 16 ] and served as a personal guard to Carian royalty. [ 17 ] Despite fighting mounted and using a war sickle , her favored weapon was said to be the bow, and she developed sorceries imitating the power of a greatbow. [ 18 ] She would leave her service in search of a haven for Albinaurics , [ 19 ] but an apparition of her remains to defend Caria Manor to this day. Several Albinauric mages, such as Pidia , remained as servants to the Carian Royal family, [ 20 ] seemingly facing less persecution there then in other parts of the Lands Between.
The oldest sorcery devised by the Carians would conjure an enemy-seeking glintblade , and it would serve as a prototype for more enhanced sword-phalanx sorceries wielded by royal enchanted knights. [ 21 ] They also created sorceries that would conjure magic swords that could sweep , slice and pierce their enemies. Some sorceries were said to have been taught to them by the enigmatic Crystalians , to mark the swearing of an old concord . [ 22 ]
Due to the influence of the Carians, [ 23 ] the Lazuli Conspectus would eventually developed in the Academy. The scholars of this conspectus sought to master Carian sorcery, and viewed the moon as equal to the stars. [ 24 ] This would be seen as a heterodox pursuit, or even outright heresy, [ 25 ] by their colleagues at the academy.
When Queen Marika the Eternal established her new Age of the Erdtree , she dispatched a champion known as Radagon to lead her armies against the lands of Liurnia. Not much was known about Radagon before he joined the ranks in the Liurnian Wars, [ 26 ] but he was seen as a great champion with flowing red locks, and he would lead a great golden host to Liurnia, where he would meet Rennala in battle. [ 27 ]
Radagon would fight two wars against Rennala and the Academy, [ 28 ] both of which saw no clear victor. [ 29 ] Instead, Radagon eventually repented his territorial aggressions by cleansing himself with Celestial Dew , [ 30 ] swore his love to Rennala and became the husband of the Carian Queen. [ 31 ]
He married her at the Church of Vows , [ 32 ] and it would be this matrimonial union, and reconciliation between the houses of the Erdtree and the Full Moon [ 33 ] that would eventually have grave consequences for the Carians, as it was during the age of the Erdtree, that Carian astrology withered on the vine, as the fate once writ in the night skies had been fettered by the Golden Order . [ 34 ]
Rennala would bequeath Radagon with a greatsword , [ 35 ] to honor a long standing tradition in which a Moon Greatsword is bestowed by a Carian queen upon her spouse. [ 36 ] Upon entering into marriage with Rennala and joining the Carian line, [ 37 ] Radagon would order Carian magic preceptors to don masks of confidence, to make it clear that all of their matters were to be kept strictly private. [ 38 ] While husband to Rennala, he studied glintstone sorcery taught at the Academy. [ 39 ] Together, Radagon and Rennala would go on to have three children: Radahn , Rykard , and Ranni .
After Godfrey , the first Elden Lord , was hounded from the Lands Between , Radagon left Rennala to become the second husband and King Consort of Queen Marika, and would receive the title of second Elden Lord. [ 40 ] Queen Marika made Radagon's and Rennala's children her demigod stepchildren. [ 41 ] When
